Following the AI classification update, there are no changes in the thresholds and quality process.

What are the benefits of selling Premium grade devices?

Quality is the primary reason why customers hesitate to buy refurbished devices. With the Premium grade program, customers will have access to the best quality devices proven to deliver best-in-class performance and longevity. 

Selling Premium devices will help you sell more products (around 10-15%) and at a price +10–15% higher than Excellent grade, as customers will cover the higher-quality costs. 

 

What devices can be sold as Premium grade?

Premium devices follow high quality requirements: 

 

  • Excellent appearance condition with flawless screens and barely visible scratches on the bodies (not visible at arm's length),

  • Only official manufacturer (genuine) spare parts,

  • “Open-box” (following a change of mind) and unopened devices (demo devices for instance),

  • A battery capacity of at least 90% (as shown in the device and/or test reports) and less than 500 cycle counts. Devices with reprogrammed or booster batteries can’t be sold as Premium grade.

For MacBooks:

 

  • Only ISO keyboard
  • Power delivery chargers/cables (and magsafe to USB-C cable for MagSafe 3 enabled devices)

Premium devices are typically as-is stock (for Apple devices) 

Repaired devices can be sold as Premium only if the repair was performed by a manufacturer-approved repair partner (using official manufacturer parts and following manufacturer procedures). If a device’s repair status can’t be guaranteed, the device must be listed as Excellent.

Charger cables have to be original or MFI certified, when applicable.

 

What are the requirements to be eligible to sell Premium grade devices? 

As each customer pays more  to buy a Premium smartphone, high quality is critical. 

Only sellers complying with high quality standards (as defined below) will be able to sell Premium grade devices.

 How to track the quality results of the Premium Offer?

In your Back Office, follow the tabs: Insights > Quality
Once your Quality Insights Dashboard is open, select the condition filter and choose “Premium”
You can apply this filter both on the overview page and within the detailed views for Long-Term Defective and Appearance.

 

What are the specific warranty and applicable after-sales conditions for Premium grade devices?

All after-sales conditions of regular products sold on Back Market apply to Premium grade devices. 

In addition, the following conditions apply: 

  • If the origin of a single part can’t be guaranteed and your customer receives a warning message about an uncertified part, your customer is entitled to a refund or replacement, at your cost.

  • If a battery falls below 89% during the cancellation period (change of mind), your customer will be offered a refund or replacement, at your cost

  • If a battery falls below 85% after the cancellation period (change of mind) and within 1 year of purchase, your customer will be offered a repair or replacement, at your cost.

 

What are the specific quality standards for Premium grade devices?

Quality KPIs apply to Premium-grade devices sold on the Back Market platform. If you do not meet these thresholds, you will enter an alert period and will have two months to improve your performance. Failure to meet the required standards within this timeframe will result in suspension from the program for a minimum of three months. After this period, you must re-apply and demonstrate compliance with all the requirements outlined above.

Can I be exited from the Premium grade program?

If you fail to meet any of the Premium grade program’s requirements, after a prior notice, you will be exited from the Premium grade program for at least 3 months. For instance : 

  • 2 non compliant devices (battery below 90%, reprogrammed/booster battery or non genuine spare parts) within 30 days,

  • Not sharing the testing reports,

  • Non compliant Defective D90 rate and/or Appearance rate on Premium devices

Back Market also reserves the right to pause or modify the conditions of the Premium grade program or change any conditions at any time. You’ll be notified of any change by email.
Title: What’s the Premium grade offer?

Details__c:
Starting October 6th., all your customer requests will be managed directly by our customer support team (Customer Care by Back Market - more information here ) and the design of the after-sales platform will therefore be adapted. Requests will only be visible to you whenever your intervention is needed, except customer messages which will not be displayed. However, you can still search for all requests by order number.

The feature is already live since middle of September for sellers using Back Care.
 


 

Back to basics

When going to the "Customer Care" tab, you will still have the four main topics available, as usual:
  • Open tickets
  • Product returns
  • Closed
  • Invoices
The most important thing to keep in mind is that Back Market is taking over all after-sales requests and that you will no longer intervene directly with the customer.
 

What we are expecting from you

You will be able to see all automatic messages and those we consider important for you. However, the request will always be managed by our Care agents, mainly without your intervention. If your help is needed, the information will be displayed to you, so that you easily know what to do. Please note that this page will continue to change in the coming weeks, as we're currently developing a new project (3-click resolution) that will be deployed by end of the year and allow you to manage those requests even faster.


Let's go through the different tabs!
 

1. Open requests


This tab is the most important for you! When Back Market agents send you messages asking for your assistance, you’ll receive an email notification and the customer request will show in this tab.

A customer request in “Open tickets” signals to you that your intervention is necessary and that you need to provide a response within 24 hours. Here you will see all customer requests, but without having all details.

We're displaying the most important information to you, the rest will be managed by our customer care agents.
Therefore, when an agent sends an informative message, the customer request won’t show up in “Open tickets,” and you won’t receive an email because no action is required from you.
 


How to see photos and attachments?
 

Our customer care agents will have the possibility to share photos and attachments with you. You will be able to see those shared elements in the "Details" section, just on the top of a request. Here, you will also find all customer details and related information.

 

I do not agree with a decision
 

Same than before October 6th., you will not be able to complain every taken decision. However, if your complaint concerns a situation stated in the Charter (example: contestation of a warranty exclusion, after the customer have received a solution), you can reach out to the Seller Support Center. Please note that the process is not changing and that the Seller Support Team will therefore not receive any request that is not linked to a justified complaint.


 

2. Product returns

This tab is also crucial for you because it displays all the items that have been returned to your location and require action. Each ticket in this tab will show the total amount of time the returned item has been at your returned location. 
 

 

3. Closed

This tab shows all customer requests that are closed or pending. Back Market agents will ensure that customers are answered and will escalate requests to you if necessary, so there’s no need to monitor this tab.

 

4. Invoices

This tab shows all customer invoice requests. You can upload invoices directly here. Make sure to verify this tab regularly, to avoid having customers waiting for too long.

 

Additional information
 

We recommend that you check the “Open tickets” and “Item returns” tabs once per business day to understand what’s required of you to resolve customer requests. 
 

Within a customer request, you’ll see the following: 
 

  • An information bar displaying the customer’s request and issue, along with the return label if applicable.

  • Messages from Back Market agents: All Back Market agents have been trained on when to escalate customer requests to you, and what information’s required from you to process the customer’s request (note that messages between the Back Market agent and customer will not be shown within the request — you’ll only see necessary information to save time) 

  • Automatic messages sent in the customer request: Most commonly, automatic messages show up when a return is on its way, when a return is delivered to you, and when a refund has been issued.

  • Details about the order and customer, including all attachments sent by the customer (e.g., photos of their item).

  • Various buttons for sending messages to agents, refunding orders, and closing tickets.
Title: How to use the Back Office to manage customer requests

Details__c:
Back Market maintains strict guidelines on sellable items to ensure quality, safety, and customer satisfaction. This article outlines which items are prohibited or restricted on our platform.

 

Prohibited Items

The following items are not allowed on Back Market for reasons including reliability, compatibility, customer experience, or market value:

 

  • Non-electronic items or items not considered electronic accessories
  • Health-related devices (reliability cannot be fully guaranteed)
  • Devices with unsupported or unupgradable operating systems, as they cannot deliver a reliable, up-to-date user experience
  • Devices with high return rates or persistent issues (e.g., certain LG devices)
  • Items priced above market value (including shipping, e.g., old video games)


 

For the full, most up-to-date list, please refer to the attached document.

The latest updates to this list include mostly devices that are too obsolete to function well or products with characteristics that could mislead customers or be perceived as counterfeit, such as:

 

  • iPad mini 2s and mini 3s
  • All brands of Apple-like earphones
  • Wired earphones with lightning connectors
  • Low performing Desktops and the ones that do not support Windows 10.

Restricted categories of items that require approval

Some categories are restricted due to quality and/or legal concerns. Only sellers who have completed a specific approval process can list the products in these categories.


To access the restricted category, you need to submit a request through the Seller Support Center and provide, depending on the category you’re applying for: photos of the devices, samples to the lab for testing, testing reports, and invoices proving your sourcing. 
Only a limited number of sellers are selected for strategic partnerships. We require a proven quality track record and reliable sourcing. Please reach out to your dedicated point of contact for more information.

 

More details are available here
For questions, contact your Back Market representative. Once you are ready, submit your request via the Seller Support Center.

 

Note: Sellers in onboarding or launch phases are not eligible to sell in these categories.

 

Restricted categoriesCategories limited to strategic partnerships
Apple earphones (AirPods)Brand New smartphone accessories (Cases, tempered glass screen protectors, smartphone chargers)

Requiring special attention

Apple Earphones: Special Restrictions prior to approval

Due to a high risk of counterfeiting, to apply for Apple Earphones you must:

 

  • Have total sales of at least €650,000 in the 90 days before your application
  • Have been actively selling on Back Market for over 6 months

 

MacBook Chargers and Apple earphones: Special restriction post-approval

 

  • You must reach at least €5,000 GMV (Gross Merchandise Volume) in the category within 90 days
  • No counterfeit alerts in the last quarter (for any device)

Failure to meet these criteria will lead to deactivation of your selling privileges in the approved category.

 

Safety Compliance

For hazardous items (like chargers), you must comply with safety regulations. For cables, you are required to provide an Underwriter Laboratories (UL) certification or equivalent within 5 business days upon request.
Title: What items does Back Market restrict or prohibit?

Jump to:

  • What do I need to do? 
  • What information do I need to provide?
  • What information does Back Market report to tax authorities? 
  • Do I need to pay taxes on my income? 
  • Where can I find my Tax Identification Number (TIN)? 
  • What happens if I don’t provide my information? 
  • What's the legal basis for data collection under the DAC7 directive?
  • Who will receive my information? 
  • How long will my information be kept on record?

 


DAC7 is a European Union (EU) directive that requires online marketplaces like Back Market to: 

 

  • Collect and verify certain tax information concerning sellers that are established in the EU
  • Report the income of these sellers who reach a specific sales or revenue limit to the tax authorities


If this applies to you, you'll need to provide your taxpayer details and confirm your business information in the Back Office.

Back Market must report tax information by 31 January of each year, for sales of the previous calendar year.
 

What do I need to do? 

If your business is established in the EU and meet one of the following conditions, are in the scope of DAC7: 

  • You made 30 or more sales in the past calendar year.
  • You've earned more than 2,000.00 € from your Back Market sales over the past calendar year.


If you are in the scope of DAC7, you are required to verify that your seller information in the Legal Details and Tax Details sections of your Back Office is correct, accurate and up to date.

 

What information do I need to provide?

You need to provide and verify the information on your seller Back Office about your business details and most importantly, the following information:

 

  • Legal name/registered company name
  • Main address
  • Company identification number 
  • Tax identification number (NIF/TIN of your country of establishment)
  • VAT number (if applicable)
  • Presence of a permanent establishment in another country within the EU.  

 

What information does Back Market report to tax authorities? 

Back Market is required to report your business details (such as name, address, TIN, VAT number) and to verify this information. Back Market is also required to report, for each seller : 

 

  • Amount of consideration paid for sales of products on Back Market, per quarter 
  • Number of sales per quarter 
  • Back Market fees and potential taxes paid by Back Market on your behalf. 

 

Do I need to pay taxes on my income? 

If you sell products on Back Market, you may be subject to tax and social security requirements in one or several countries of the EU, particularly in regard to Value-Added Tax (VAT) and income/corporate tax. 

It's your responsibility to check your tax and social security requirements and comply with any applicable regulations.

For more information, please contact the tax authorities, your accountant, or your tax advisor.

The DAC7 report does not determine your tax liability. Even if Back Market reports your sales under DAC7 requirement, you still have to report and pay your taxes. 

 

Where can I find my Tax Identification Number (TIN)? 

Your Tax Identification Number (TIN) is assigned to your business by the tax authorities in your country of establishment. 

If you don't know where to find your Tax Identification Number (TIN), contact the tax authorities in your country of establishment. For information purposes only, we've compiled some information about Tax Identification Numbers (TINs) in each country within the EU:

 

CountryName of Tax Identification Number (TIN)Format
FranceSIREN number9 digits (example: 999 999 999)
GermanySteuernummer (Tax Identification)10–13 digits (most often 10 digits)
SpainNIF (Numero de Identification Fiscal)9 digits or letters starting with a letter 
IrelandTax Reference Number (TRN)7 digits + either 1 or 2 letters 
CyprusTax Identification Number (same as VAT)

9 digits or letters
(8 numbers followed by a letter) 

NetherlandsTax Identification Number9 digits 
Italy Codice Fiscale 
Tax Identification Number		11 digits 
LithuaniaTax Identification Number 9 or 10 digits 
Estonia Registration Code 
Tax Identification Number		8 digits 
BelgiumNuméro d’Identification de l’Entreprise 
Numéro d’Entreprise Belge (Ondernemingsnummer)		10 digits 
AustriaSteuernummer9 digits (example: 999 999 999)

 

What happens if I don’t provide my information? 

If you provide incorrect or incomplete information or fail to provide it at all, Back Market may have to suspend your payouts and your activity on the platform.

 

What's the legal basis for data collection under the DAC7 directive?

The data collected by Back Market to comply with the DAC7 Directive (Council Directive (EU) 2021/514) is required by law (article 1649 ter A et seq. of the French General Tax Code).

 

Who will receive my information? 

Back Market report all information collected to French tax authorities, in accordance with a secure procedure imposed by those authorities.

The French tax authorities may exchange this information with tax authorities in other EU countries.

 

How long will my information be kept on record?

Your information will be kept for as long as is necessary for Back Market to comply with the DAC7 directive.
Title: What's the DAC7 directive?

Details__c:
1. How do I change the bank account I have on file?

If you wish to change your bank account details, please submit a request to the Seller Support Center under “Finance or legal.” Updates of bank details can be processed by the Seller Support Center only.  

 

  • Bank account in € (euro currency), £ (pound sterling currency)

Bear in mind that the change is not immediate, you need to allow our security payments partner enough time to verify and validate your documents. 

 

2. What is the documentation required?

In order for your request to be processed, you must make sure the Certificate of Good Standing and the Owner’s ID uploaded to your Back-office is up-to-date. 

In order to verify which documents are uploaded for your seller account, here are the steps you need to follow:

 

  • Click on the icon on the top right of your Back-office, then go to “My profile”
  • Then go to the line “Good standing certificate from the Secretary of State” and check if the document uploaded is up-to-date
  • Check the document uploaded for the option “State ID or Passport of Ultimate Business Owner (Front and Back)” as well.

 

3. Which bank document should be provided?

If the information listed above is up-to-date, you can go ahead and create your request in the Seller Support Center with a document issued by your bank attached to your case. 

The document can be: 

 

  • Your account opening letter 
  • Bank statement issued no longer than one year ago

Only an official PDF document extracted from the website of the bank will be accepted (please, do not submit pictures of bank statements you received by post as our security payments partner requires a document where the information available can be screened by the system).

No matter the type of bank document you decide to send, the following information should be shown:

 

  • Account name: should show the name of the company as the account holder (personal bank accounts will not be accepted)
  • IBAN and SWIFT BIC code (if your request concerns the UK market, alternatively you can provide the account and sort code number instead)

After reviewing the above documents, the Seller Support Advisors team will get back to you in short delays with the confirmation that the bank details have been updated. 

 

4. I am registered with Storfund: What should I do? 

The documentation required is the same, all you need to do is make sure the information provided is compliant with the guidelines mentioned above.
Title: How do I change the bank account I have on file?

Details__c:
What’s the New Battery offer? 

The New Battery offer is an option that allows customers to buy smartphones (Samsung Galaxy and iPhones) and MacBooks (MacBooks and MacBook M1) that have been refurbished with new batteries.

 

The New Battery offer is currently available in Europe, Japan and the UK.

We’re actively looking to expand into new markets and categories, so stay tuned for more! 

 

What are the benefits? 

Benefits for sellers 

As a refurbished device marketplace, battery concerns are the primary reason why customers are hesitant to buy refurbished. Selling smartphones and/or MacBooks with brand-new batteries will help you sell more products. 

 

Benefits for customers 

Your customers will have access to first-rate products, so they can rest assured that they have a brand-new battery.

 

What are the requirements to be eligible to sell devices with New Batteries? 

As customer often pays more to buy a device with a new battery, high quality is critical. 

 

Only sellers who are refurbishers, who are in stage RUN and who comply with high quality standards (as defined below) will be able to be onboarded and sell devices under the New Battery program. 

 

Requirements for onboarding:

In addition to compliance with the above stated metrics, we also require the following in order to assess your onboarding request:

 

1. A repair video

 

  • The video must show the full battery replacement process, from start to finish:
    • Show the battery percentage in the settings (before replacing the battery)
    • Screen removal
    • Battery removal
    • Installation of the new battery
    • Reassembly
    • Final screen sealing
    • Then, show the battery percentage in the settings again after all these steps have been completed.
  • In case of re-onboarding following a previous exit due to safety concerns, we also require that these safety concerns are addressed in the new video.

2. Tests reports

 

  • Test report before and after the battery replacement for the device shown in the video.
  • 2 to 3 additional reports for other models to be sold under the program, and no older than 30 days from a testing software that is in compliance with our vetted and listed solutions.

3. Battery specific reports

 

  • EMC / IEC62133-2 / MSDS report
  • Battery supplier certificate

How can I apply? 

Reach out to the Seller Support Center or your Back Market point of contact. 

If your application is rejected because you don’t meet the above requirements for onboarding, or because you replace batteries in a dangerous manner (e.g. by using tools that bend batteries in half, or failing to remove the adhesive from the old battery), you will need to wait at least one month before reapplying to the New Battery program.

 

What are the technical New Battery requirements?

There are a few battery requirements you’ll need to meet to onboard and to respect after onboarding:

 

Battery origin

  • We recommend sourcing through a major supplier such as REWA. . 
  • If you’re using a compatible third party battery, you need to share battery performance reports to guarantee quality. 

New batteries must also have: 

  • 100% capacity  (for iOS and BMS for iPhones)
  • Less than 30 cycles 

Battery warning messages:

  • Since the iOS 18.1 update, we now accept warning messages related to new batteries on iPhones, as long as the customer can see the State of Health (SOH) at 100% on their device (indicating the iOS is up to date). In these cases, please ensure you include the leaflet provided at the bottom of this page with the device, in the customer’s local language. This leaflet should reassure the customer that these warning messages are normal. 

Prohibited practices

  • Tin-soldered batteries are strictly prohibited.

Recommendations

  • We recommend to avoid battery soldering to:
    • Simplify processes
    • Reduce safety risks
  • We also recommend to avoid high-capacity batteries
    • High-capacity batteries generate more heat during operation. It can create intermittent issues that are challenging to diagnose since they only manifest when the device is under load or experience elevated temperatures.
  • Avoid using low-capacity batteries
    • Best stick to original specifications
  • Use diagnostic batteries for iPhone 15 and later, in order to:
    • Reduce number of safety issues
    • Eliminate the visibility of the cycle count for iPhone 15 and later when changing the battery
    • Simplify battery change process

What are the quality standards to respect after onboarding? 

The following Quality KPIs apply to the ongoing participation in the New Battery program.  

If you do not meet these thresholds, you will enter an alert period and will have two months to improve your performance. Failure to meet the required standards within this timeframe will result in suspension from the program for a minimum of three months. After this period, you must re-apply and demonstrate compliance with all the requirements outlined under the onboarding conditions.

 

Smartphones

MacBook M1

< 5.2%

< 10% EU

< 8.5% UK

< 6.5% EU

How to track the quality performance of your New Battery offer? 

In your Back Office, follow the tabs: Insights > Quality 

 

Once your Quality Insights Dashboard is open, select the condition filter and choose “New Battery.” You can apply this filter both on the overview page and within the detailed views for Long-Term Defective and Appearance Rates. 

 

What are the after-sales conditions? 

For Samsung Galaxy phones, if a battery is not satisfactory during the cancellation period, your customer will be offered a repair, exchange, or refund. 

 

For iPhones and MacBooks, if a battery falls below 99% during the cancellation period, your customer will be offered a repair, exchange, or refund. 

 

For iPhones and MacBooks, if a battery falls below 90% after the cancellation period and within 1 year of purchase, your customer will be offered a repair or exchange. 

 

There’s an exception around the seller warranty for products sold in Spain and Portugal, where only the first warranty year is covered by this condition. 

 

If your customer receives a warning message on their iPhone with a new battery, they’re entitled to a 10 €/£ 10 goodwill gesture. 

 

Can I be exited from the New Battery Program? 

If you fail to meet any of the New Battery program’s requirements, after prior notice, you will be exited from the program for at least 3 months.

 

For instance: 

 

  • 3 consecutive months of non-compliance against the long-term defective rate in New Battery devices, as outlined in section “What are the quality standards to respect after onboarding?”
    • We do take into account your short-term defective rate and evolution when evaluating your performance and any potential program exit; however, the long-term defective rate is the most reliable measure for evaluating the continuous program participation.
  • 2 non compliant devices such as devices not holding a new battery and/or reprogramed/boosted batteries
  • Multiple failed mystery orders

Back Market also reserves the right to pause or modify the conditions of the New Battery program, or change any conditions at any time. You’ll be notified of any change by email.
Title: Information on the New Battery offer

Details__c:
In this article we will explain to you, step by step, how to update your listings on the Back Office. There are three ways to do so.


Manually

  • Go on the 'Offers' tab in your Back Office or use directly this link.
  • Click on 'View details'
  • You can directly update the quantity and the price of your listings by filling the corresponding fields.
  • You can also click on 'Edit offer' to update the warranty period, the SKU, the comment, the price, the warranty period and the shipping method of the listing.
  • Please note that shipping fees are included in the price since October 1st 2020. More information here.

These methods allow you to modify your listings one by one, while keeping a pricing strategy specific to each country in which you sell. The prices per country are easily changeable and adaptable thanks to our international pricing tool. 


Using a CSV file

You can update your listing using CSV files. 

In your CSV file, you should have a column titled 'sku' and a column for each field you want to update.

Among the fields you can change, there are:

  • the price
  • the quantity
  • the warranty delay
  • the comment
  • the shipper
  • the shipping delay
  • the shipping price

Once your CSV file is ready to be imported, go in your Back Office on the 'Offer' tab and click on: 'Offer update (stock/price)' and upload your file.

For European sellers only (or US Sellers selling in Europe): if you want to have your listing listed in several countries, you can read the article How to put my listing live in other countries?

Please watch this video  to know how to update your listing using the CSV file

 

For more BackBox wins, you can now export the “price to win the BackBox” for all your listings. To do so, navigate to your Listings page. Choose “Import or Export Listings button,” followed by “Download Existing Listings via CSV.” You’ll find the download in your File Library at the top right corner. Within the CSV file, you will find a column listing all prices to win the BackBox.


Using API

If you want to update several listings, the endpoint of the request should be https://www.backmarket.fr/ws/listings.

In the Body of your request, you have to put in the catalog field the equivalent of the CSV file used to update listings.

In this field, you must provide:

  • the SKU
  • the fields you want to update

If you want more information on the listing's fields you can update, you can read our documentation here.
Title: How can I update my listing on the Back Market's Back Office?

VAT specificity to sell in the UK
 

What is VAT? 

Value Added Tax (VAT) is a consumption tax applied to most goods and services sold in Europe and in the UK. Suppliers are required to charge their customers VAT.

Under certain conditions, businesses can deduct or recover the VAT paid to their suppliers, so VAT is generally not a cost for businesses. However, VAT constitutes a cost for the final consumers.

If your business is selling goods to customers within the European Union or the UK, you have VAT obligations. These obligations depend on your specific situation and the nature of your sales. Generally, you must:

 

  • Register for VAT
  • Submit VAT returns
  • Issue invoices to customers that comply with VAT regulations including e-invoicing / e-reporting requirements where applicable
  • Pay the collected VAT to the tax authorities


It’s your responsibility to comply with the current tax regulations for your sales made on Back Market.

Back Market doesn't provide tax services or advices to third-party sellers. However, ensuring the tax compliance of sellers on our platform is a priority, and our partners Avask or Taxually can assist you. You can also get advice from your tax advisor or accountant.

 

What are the VAT rates? 

Each European country has its own VAT rates. There are standard rates and reduced rates relevant to certain types of products. Products sold on the Back Market platform are generally subject to the standard rates. It’s your responsibility to ensure that you apply the correct VAT rate.

Standard VAT rates applied by country*:

 

CountryCountry code Standard rate
AustriaAT20%
BelgiumBE21%
FinlandFI25,5%
FranceFR20%
GermanyDE19%
GreeceEL24%
IrelandIE23%
ItalyIT22%
NetherlandsNL21%
Portugal PT23%
SlovakiaSK23%
SpainES21%
SwedenSE25%

 

*Last updated: February 2026. (Europa.eu)

 

How is VAT calculated? 

When selling a product on Back Market, you are required to list a total tax inclusive price.

It’s your responsibility to calculate the amount of VAT that you must charge and declare.

For example, the VAT rate for a product sold for 1,000 € to a customer in France is 20%. Here’s the breakdown:

 

  • 1,000 € is the total price including taxes paid by the customer
  • 166.67 € is the VAT amount
  • 833.33 € is the price excluding taxes.
  • The VAT amount equals 20% of the tax exclusive price. 

On the invoice you issue to the customer, you must include the price excluding taxes, the VAT amount, the VAT rate, and the total price including taxes. 

 

How does VAT work for cross-border sales in the EU?

As a platform, Back Market connects sellers with individual customers (non VAT registered). The platform is only available for B2C. We do not allow B2B sales. 

When making a distance sale in the EU, you are required to collect VAT in the destination country of the goods ; country where the product is delivered to the customer.

For example, if your business is located in France and you ship products from France to customers in Germany, you are required to collect and report German VAT. If you ship the products domestically to customers located in France, you are required to collect and report French VAT. 

There’s an exception: When a business makes distance sales to other EU countries with a total value of less than 10,000 € per year, the business can apply the VAT rate of the country of dispatch rather than the VAT of the destination country.

For example, a French business shipping products from France to a customer located in Germany. If the business's sales outside of France don’t exceed 10,000 € per year, they can apply the French VAT rate or choose to apply the German VAT rate.

 

When do I need to register for VAT?

You need to register if you’re a business selling goods to individual consumers located in the EU.

As an exception, small businesses making only domestic sales are allowed to trade without a VAT registration if they are below the VAT registration threshold. See below threshold in some EU countries. The VAT thresholds may be complex to navigate, we encourage you to refer to your accountant or tax advisor.

 

Country

Exceptions for small businesses ( in EUR currency) 

Austria

35,000 € - 55,000€

Belgium

25,000 €

France

85 000€ (prior year) and 93 500€ (current year)

Germany

25 000€ (prior year) and 100 000€ (current year)

Ireland

85 000€ or 42 500€

Italy

85 000€

Netherlands 

20,000€

Romania 

Around 80,000€

Poland 

Around 56,000€

Spain

None

 

* Last updated: February 2026.

As explained above, the VAT applicable is the one of the country of destination of the products. If you sell products to customers in different countries of the EU, you have to charge and report VAT of these different countries. 

To invoice, declare, and pay this VAT, you have 2 options:

 

1. You can register for VAT in each of the relevant countries. In this case, you’ll be required to submit VAT returns in each of these countries.

2. You can register with the European Union's One Stop Shop ("OSS"). This OSS allows you to report and pay VAT on all your sales to EU customers through a single VAT return and a single VAT registration. Using the OSS is not mandatory, but it is a simplification measure for businesses, it allows you to sell in all EU countries with one single VAT number.

 

Warning: If you ship products to individual customers located in the same EU country, domestically from an EU country, to customers located in the same EU country these sales are considered domestic sales and may not be reported to the OSS. You must report them in your normal VAT return. 



If you import from a non-EU country or if you hold inventory in an EU country:

If your business imports products within the EU, you may need to register for VAT in the EU. We recommend consulting your tax advisor or accountant to understand your VAT requirements.

If you hold inventory in an EU country, you must register for VAT in each country where you have storage locations.

 

What is margin VAT scheme for second-hand goods? 

Margin VAT scheme for second-hand goods is a special VAT regime that can be applied to certain second-hand goods, subject to strict conditions.

  • The second hand goods must have been acquired from a person who did not charge VAT, such as an unregistered individual.
  • The second hand goods are not imported from a country outside the European Union.
  • Your business keeps the required records such as a police and stock register.


It’s important to consult an accountant to understand the specific conditions and document retention requirements, which may vary by country.

This scheme allows VAT to be applied only on the margin realized by the seller of the second-hand product, rather than the total selling price. It’s therefore a VAT regime that allows customers to access lower prices, encouraging the circular economy.

Tax authorities frequently carry out controls on this scheme to prevent abuses or misuse. It’s your responsibility to inform yourself about your eligibility and comply with tax regulations.

If you sell products applying the VAT margin scheme, you’re not required to include VAT on the customer invoice. You must report VAT on your margin in the country of dispatch of the second hand goods. Therefore you are required to register for VAT in the country of dispatch and not in the country of delivery. You may not use the OSS to report these sales.  
 

When is Back Market required to collect VAT on my sales? 

Since July 1, 2021, there are certain cases where Back Market, as an online marketplace, is required to report and pay VAT on behalf of third-party sellers as a deemed supplier.

If you are concerned, you must still display prices including VAT. It’s your responsibility to calculate VAT when setting your prices, knowing that Back Market deducts this VAT from your payment at each invoice cycle and reports and pays this VAT to the tax authorities.



Case #1: Non-EU business shipping goods from the EU to an individual customer located within the EU (either in the same country or in another EU country).

For example, if a Chinese entity ships goods from stock located in France to customers in Germany, Back Market handles the collection of German VAT and the reporting to the tax authorities on behalf of the seller.

Back Market collects VAT for the sales of sellers who aren’t “established within the EU” for VAT purposes. An EU legal entity is not systematically considered as “established within the EU for VAT purposes”. Please note that Back Market may ask for evidence of your EU establishment to determine whether Back Market is liable to VAT. 



Case #2: Any business, regardless of its place of establishment, shipping low value goods (less than 150 €) from a non-EU country to a customer.

For example, a British seller shipping AirPods from the UK to a customer in Germany with the value of the shipment being less than 150 €.

The seller must ensure that Back Market's Import One-Stop-Shop number (IOSS) is included on the customs documents accompanying the shipped goods. Otherwise, there’s a risk of double taxation.

If you find yourself in this situation, we encourage you to contact Back Market to obtain the IOSS number and for further details on the customs procedures to be followed.



Case #3: Any business, regardless of its establishment, shipping goods from a non-EU country to a customer in France when the value of the shipment exceeds 150 €. 

For example, a British company shipping products from Britain to a customer in France with the value of the shipment exceeding 150 €.

In this case, the shipment must include Back Market's VAT number and the mention of "sale via Back Market - electronic interface". There’s a risk of double taxation if this requirement is not met. If you find yourself in this situation, we advise you to contact Back Market as well as your shipping provider/freight forwarder.

To comply with its legal obligations, Back Market may ask you for additional information regarding the justification of your place of establishment and the country of dispatch of the goods.

 

Warning: since Brexit, the UK is no longer part of the territory of the European Union. As a consequence, UK sellers or sellers shipping products from UK to EU may be in the scope of the marketplace VAT collection (deemed-supplier). If you find yourself in this situation, we recommend consulting your tax advisor or accountant to understand your VAT obligations and customs formalities related to shipping goods and potential return flows. You may need to obtain an EORI number and 1 or more intra-community VAT numbers, depending on your import flows.
 

VAT specificity to sell in the UK

VAT in the UK operates in a similar way to VAT within the European Union. The standard VAT rate in the UK is 20%. The VAT registration threshold is set at £90,000.

Online marketplaces like Back Market are required to collect and report UK VAT on behalf of sellers  in 2 situations: 

 

  • Non UK established sellers, shipping domestically from the UK to UK customers, 
  • Sellers who import low value goods to UK customers (£135 or less). ct VAT on your sales. In these cases, the VAT amount will be withheld from your Back Market account and deducted from your payments.

If you wish to register as a seller on the UK platform, you must:

 

  • Be a business established in the UK and, therefore, registered for VAT in the UK if you exceed the VAT registration threshold 
  • Ship goods from inventory located in the UK
Title: The VAT guide to selling in Europe

Details__c:
As a seller on Back Market, you have to provide invoices to your customers after their purchases. Of course, those invoices must contain specific information to be compliant with the law. In this regard, we would like to provide you with all the necessary elements to make sure you know how to establish correctly an invoice.

 

What should appear an all invoices, no matters if you're in marginal VAT or not:

 
Seller Information
  • Registration Number
  • Place of establishment
  • VAT registration Number
Customer Information
  • Name
  • Address
Bill Information
  • Number
  • Date
 

What should appear on the invoice if you're in marginal VAT:

Seller's legal notices
  • Corporate name
  • Head office or legal domicile
  • Registration/incorporation number
  • VAT number
Client's legal notices (individual consumer)
  • Name & last name
  • Address
Invoicing legal notices
  • Date
  • Invoice Number
Legal notice regarding the service provision
  • Operation date if different from the one on the invoice
  • Quantity and precise of the goods delivered
  • Price excluding taxes
  • Discount if appropriate
  • Delivery fees
  • State: VAT Non-applicable special scheme, second hand goods)
  • Total amount including VAT

 

What should appear on the invoice if you're not in marginal VAT:

Seller's legal notices
  • Corporate name
  • Head office or legal domicile
  • Registration/incorporation number
  • VAT number
Client's legal notices (individual consumer)
  • Name & last name
  • Address
Invoicing legal notices
  • Date
  • Invoice Number
Legal notice regarding the service provision
  • Operation date if different from the one on the invoice
  • Quantity and precise of the goods delivered
  • Price excluding taxes
  • Discount if appropriate
  • Delivery fees
  • VAT rate of country of arrival
  • VAT amount of country of arrival
  • Total amount including VAT

 

What language do I need to provide the receipt in?


If your customer asks for a receipt in the language of the market they purchased in, you must provide it. (You can find this in article 6.1 of the seller T&Cs: The seller must communicate with any buyer in the language of the country where the buyer accessed the marketplace).
 

How long am I required to provide the receipt for?

Your customer can also ask for the receipt up to 2 years after the purchase in France and Spain, for 6 years in the UK, and 3 years in Germany. Keep in mind that after you’re no longer legally required to provide the customer receipt, you may be asked for it for up to 10 years by authorities for accounting purposes.

 

Please note that in some countries, e-invoicing or e-reporting obligations may apply. It is the seller’s responsibility to ensure compliance with local regulations.

 

Check out this article for more information on how to upload a receipt for your customer

 

➡️  If you're facing issues in establishing your invoices or if you still have some questions, please feel free to contact the Seller Support via the forms in the SSC.

 

Back Market in no way guarantees the accuracy or completeness of the information contained in this article and disclaims all liability in this respect.
Title: How to establish a B2C invoice that is compliant with the law?

Details__c:
The Late Processing Rate measures the percentage of orders that have been processed too late. It’s an important indicator of customer satisfaction, so keep a close eye on yours.

 

Jump to

How is my Late Processing Rate calculated?
What happens if my Late Processing Rate is too high?
How do I keep my Late Processing Rate low?
​​​​​​​​​​​​​​How should I monitor my performance indicators?
​​​​​​​

How is my Late Processing Rate calculated?

The Late Processing Rate measures the percentage of orders that have been processed too late. You’ve got 48 hours to process your Trade-in order from the time the item is received. This timeline excludes weekends and bank holidays (in the country where you have your warehouse).

“Processing” the order means taking 1 of the following actions upon receiving the order:

  • Paying the customer the initial price offered
  • Making a counteroffer if you feel that the item's condition doesn't match how the customer assessed it (you’ll need to provide photo or video evidence to back this up)
  • Putting the order on hold if the transaction can’t be completed due to a blocked account or missing information (such as a customer ID)

This is the Late Processing Rate formula: the number of Trade-in orders that were processed too late (after 48 hours) in the last 30 days, divided by the number of Trade-in orders received in the last 30 days. The Late Processing Rate is calculated every day.
 

What happens if my Late Processing Rate is too high?

If your Late Processing Rate is too high, you’ll enter a “monitoring period" where you have the opportunity to improve your rate. If your rate doesn’t improve enough, your daily Trade-in limit will be decreased. If the issue persists, you won’t be able to use the Trade-in service.

​​​​​​​Learn more about Late Processing Rate limits and how the monitoring period works
 

How do I keep my Late Processing Rate low?

Here are some quick tips on processing your orders on time.

Check your Back Office frequently
Check your Back Office a few times a day to see if you’ve received any new Trade-in items that need to be processed. It's quick and easy to see the Trade-in orders that still need processing by going to your Back Office > "Trade-in" > "Orders" > "New orders".

You'll also see a timer showing how much time you have left to process each order.

Make sure you’re getting email notifications
Make sure you’re receiving email notifications when an item has arrived at your chosen delivery location. If you’re not getting email notifications, reach out to the Seller Support Center to get them set up.

Make a plan to tackle big issues
If you’re anticipating or encountering an issue, consider the following options to solve it:

  • Adjust your process for handling orders received (including prioritisation of orders or a more organised workflow).
  • Review guidelines given to your employees.
  • Increase your resources temporarily.


Don’t forget to plan your holidays
If you’re planning to go on vacation or be away from your business, we suggest you update all your Trade-in order listings to £0 about 2 weeks before your departure.

That said, you’ll always need to process Trade-in order deliveries within 48 hours. Make sure you have the resources needed to process them while you’re away.
 

How should I monitor my performance indicators?

You can check the progress of your Late Processing Rate and other performance indicators in your Trade-in insights dashboard. You can access these from your Sales Back Office, then “Insights” > “Trade-in”.

If you have any questions, contact your Back Market point of contact or reach out via the Seller Support Center.
Title: What’s my Late Processing Rate, and how do I keep it low?

Details__c:
Overview

Starting mid-November, Back Market is launching a new Obsolete Laptop category — designed to help you move aging inventory while fighting electronic waste (e-waste).

 

This category lets you resell high-quality laptops that are not compatible with Windows 11, provided they’ve been refreshed with ChromeOS Flex.

 

Why This Matters

Microsoft officially ended support for Windows 10 on October 14, 2025.
Windows 10 devices no longer receive security updates, making them less secure to use, even if the hardware is still perfectly functional.

 

By pre-installing ChromeOS Flex, you give these laptops a second life, making them safe, fast, and sustainable for new users.

 

What Qualifies

The “Obsolete Laptop” category is open to:

 

  • Common business models not compatible with Windows 11
    (for example: HP EliteBook 840 G4, Lenovo ThinkPad T470, Dell Latitude 5480, and similar models)

  • Devices that have been fully tested, cleaned, and refurbished

  • ChromeOS Flex must be pre-installed before listing

⚠️ Important: Laptops sold without ChromeOS Flex (or another compatible OS) are at the seller’s own risk.
If a customer opens a claim related to functionality or compatibility, it will be handled as part of the warranty, and Back Market will refund the customer accordingly.

 

Step-by-Step: How to prepare your devices

1. Check Compatibility

Before installing ChromeOS Flex, verify your models are supported.

Google maintains a Certified Devices List. Most business-grade laptops from the last decade are compatible.

 

2. Install ChromeOS Flex

ChromeOS Flex is a free, lightweight operating system from Google designed to give older PCs and Macs a second life.

You’ll need:

 

  • A USB key (at least 8GB)

  • An internet connection

  • The ChromeOS Flex installer, created via the Chrome Recovery Utility extension

Quick install steps:

 

  1. Download and launch the Chrome Recovery Utility

  2. Select ChromeOS Flex as your target image

  3. Write it to your USB key

  4. Boot the target laptop from the USB drive

  5. Follow on-screen instructions to install

Need help? See the full tutorial here:


https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4si1HyBY570&t=1s

 

How to Apply

To join the launch of this category, please send your application to your usual Back Market point of contact or via the Seller Support Center.

Include the following information:

  • Laptop models you can supply(ThinkPad, EliteBook or Latitude series only)

  • Quantities available for launch

  • Product grades

  • Estimated selling price

Quality & Testing Requirements

To ensure the best customer experience, please verify:

 

  • All hardware components are functional (keyboard, screen, battery, ports, Wi-Fi, webcam)

  • ChromeOS Flex boots properly and updates automatically

  • Devices are cleaned and meet Back Market’s cosmetic grading standards

Why It Matters

By selling laptops in this new category, you’re helping:

 

  • Reduce e-waste by keeping perfectly functional tech in use

  • Extend device lifecycles beyond planned software limits

  • Offer affordable, sustainable alternatives to new devices

This is a key step in fighting planned obsolescence and promoting circular tech.
Title: How to prepare and sell Laptops in the New “Obsolete Laptop” Category

Details__c:
The seller portal is where you can view, update, and follow along with your requests in the SSC
 


How to answer or update my requests?

There are two ways to answer SSC requests:

 

  • After receiving an answer to your initial request, you can follow up via email. To do this, just reply to the email you receive from the SSC. This will also update your case in your portal.
  • You can also reply in your portal by adding a comment and clicking “Save”.
Title: What is the Seller Portal?

Details__c:
The Seller Support Center contains articles that tackle frequently asked questions to streamline your experience selling on Back Market, so you can get straight to selling. It also gives you more information about how Back Market works and what our expectations are for sellers.

There are 2 ways to check out our FAQs.

  • Select a topic category on the home page to learn more about selling on Back Market — from Trade-in to customer care and legal questions.
  • Search directly in the “Ask a question…” bar to find FAQs related to your search.

If you still have questions, reach out to an expert at Back Market by selecting “Submit new request”. Learn more about how to contact Back Market with questions

If you think of a topic that we haven’t covered and that could benefit other sellers, please feel free to let us know.

You can also let us know if an article was useful or not by using the thumbs up and thumbs down button under each article.
Title: How should I use the Seller Support Center?

Details__c:
If your question isn’t answered in our Seller Support Center FAQs, get in touch with an expert at Back Market by filling out a contact form
 

Jump to

How does it work?
Tips for using the contact form
 

How does it work?

Just select “Submit new request” from the menu bar in the Seller Support Center and fill out each section of the contact form. The contact form streamlines the support process and makes sure your question is sent to the most relevant expert at Back Market.
 

Tips for using the contact form

Have your order ID on hand for order-related questions.
When filling out the contact form, you’ll first be asked if your question is related to an order. If so, make sure to have the order ID on hand — you’ll need to include it before you can send your message. If your request is related to multiple order IDs, you’ll need to share an Excel file with the order IDs. You can’t add an attachment to the contact form, so go to “My existing requests” after you've submitted your inquiry. There, you can upload the file to your request.

Confirm next steps via the confirmation message.
In a few cases, you’ll be asked to provide additional information at the time you’re submitting your request. You might be asked to complete a Typeform or fill out an attached PDF for instance. These next steps will be listed on the page after you’ve submitted your request — so keep your eyes peeled.

View your request later and add details as needed.
Once you've submitted your request, you can select "See my request" to see all the details of your request. You can also upload supporting documents and add additional details.
Title: How do I contact Back Market if I have a question?

Details__c:
How to fill in the IMEI / Serial Number?

You must provide the IMEI / Serial number of every smartphone, tablet, iPad, Macbook and laptop sold on Back Market through Back Office within 1 business day of shipping. This information must also be included in every invoice.

 

New Feature: How to know which orderlines are missing an IMEI?

- Please go in the order page:

 

    - Select a start date within a time range below 6 months

    - Click on the button “export missing IMEI”

    - Check the notification in your file library (top left corner)

    - the exported CSV will include 3 columns: order_id, orderline_id, shipping_date.

 

This CSV will list every orderline which requires an IMEI but doesn't have any (only smartphone orders with quantity = 1 for now). To remain compliant with our quality guidelines, please note that you need to provide the IMEIs for the above orderlines within 24hr after shipment.

 

 

The Back Office allows you to provide the IMEI number for all smartphones as well as the serial number for all other products. Providing the IMEI number is mandatory on Back Market. This means, the IMEI number will have to appear systematically on your invoices. You can provide the IMEI number as soon as you validate an order and at latest, 24 hours after shipping. The same applies for the serial numbers and orders for tablets, iPads, Macbooks and laptops.

 

Note that if an order has 2 or more of the same device, you won't be able to input both IMEI numbers. But do not panic, because you will not be penalized for not doing so, and your metrics will also not be affected, as long as you include one of the item’s IMEIs.

 

 

Why do you need to do this?

Tracking the IMEI / Serial Number enables product traceability and helps improve both customer and seller experience. It is an essential enabler:

 

  1. For the customer care team to check iCloud account deactivation autonomously before a product return.

  2. To save you time by killing customer contacts who specifically reach out to request the IMEI number.

  3. To protect you from fraud and act as supporting evidence in claim resolution, such as when a different product is returned to you from the one you originally sent out.


How can you provide the IMEI / serial number?

You have different ways to provide us with the IMEI / serial number:

 

  • Back Office connection – manually for each order

When you prepare the shipping of an order by clicking on "send" on the Back Office order page, you will be required to provide the IMEI / serial number of the product alongside the tracking number of your package.

 

 

 

  • API connection

Dedicated endpoint

If you cannot provide the IMEI information whilst shipping the order, you can use the endpoint “Update Orderline” and send the value IMEI. This endpoint can be used at all stages of the order lifecycle, from state 1 to 6. 

 

A new IMEI / serial number field is available in the Back Market API. All the information related to this field is communicated via via Back Market API Documentation under the section API Generics → Update orderline.

 

The following Back Market limitations apply, regardless of the platform you use:

  • It is only possible to add IMEIs to order lines where the quantity is equal to 1 (one). E.g.: if you sell one Samsung Galaxy S21 and one iPhone 13, each of those items falls within their own order line and the respective IMEIs can be added. If you sell two iPhone 13s, both fall within the same order line – and adding the IMEIs is not possible.

 

 

This field will be filled in the same way as "tracking_url" or "shipper".

 

  • Manual Option: Back Office connection - CSV import

You are able to import IMEI numbers in bulk via CSV from your Back Office. To do this, go to your orders page and click on the 'Import my tracking / IMEI' section. Please check the attached file for a CSV template. 

 

  • Adding IMEI by the integrators

    • ChannelEngine: Check out this guide if you're using ChannelEngine.
    • Infiplex: Please follow the instructions in this guide if you're using Infiplex. Also note that users with other WMS/ERP or shipping systems can work with them to connect to pull the information if it’s available.
    • Neteven: Please contact your Neteven account manager if you need assistance.
    • SellerCloud: Please check out this guide on how to fill serial numbers in SellerCloud.
    • Wholecell: WholeCell can send your inventory's IMEI to Back Market if this is enabled in your Back Market integration settings. Please follow the instructions in this guide.
    • Linworks - it happens automatically - no documentation.
    • Shipstation - supports this feature through our “Internal Notes” field on order records. Merchants must manually enter the IMEI number into the Internal Notes field on the Order Details window before creating a label.


Integrator without IMEI feature that are being developed:

 

  • Shopify
  • Channel Advisor
  • BrightPearl
  • SKU Vault

 

How can you track your IMEI / serial number filling rate?

We are working on making the IMEI filling rate available in your Quality Dashboard. Once activated, you will be able to follow the IMEI filling rate there. Keep you posted!

Feel free to reach out to your point of contact at Back Market should you face any difficulties or if you want to give your feedback.

 

What happens if you don't provide the IMEI or serial number?

If a customer requests the IMEI or serial number at any time, you will have 2 business days to provide it. If you haven't provided the number after 2 business days hours, Back Market may offer your customer a goodwill gesture valued up to €50 / $50 or the customer can choose to cancel their order.

 

Any seller protections because of a wrong item returned, warranty exclusion, or more will not apply if you didn't provide the IMEI or the serial number by the required deadline.
Title: How to fill in the IMEI / Serial Number?

Details__c:
The newest version of the Quality Charter went into effect on 4 August 2025. Article 3.6 and Appendix 1 of the Quality Charter were slightly modified from January 1, 2026.

The purpose of the Charter is to outline the quality requirements and customer service management rules for Back Market sellers, as well as including the key elements of customer satisfaction.

 

Charter updates: Sales Part

  • Category S items must be shipped the same day if the order is placed before cut-off
  • You must provide IMEIs for smartphones and the serial numbers for iPads, tablettes, MacBooks and Laptops
  • If your multiple defects rate is deemed too high by Back Market, we reserve the right to deactivate the repair and/or replacement options for your account.
  • If you repeatedly fail to meet the repair or replacement deadlines—after receiving at least two warnings from Back Market—we may deactivate the repair option for your account.
  • In cases where accessories are missing (not returned by the customer), you must issue a full refund and submit a refund request through the Seller Support Center.
  • If you believe a warranty exclusion applies, you must…return the product to your customer only after the confirmation of Warranty exclusion by Back Market and within 2 business days after this confirmation. Paid repairs are forbidden in this case.
  • To increase the probability that the customer will keep the device, for all partial refunds or goodwill gestures, the amount will be rounded up to the nearest 5€ or £5. For example, a refund of 7€ will be rounded up to 10€. This does not apply to partial refunds for late express shipping, which will be refunded at the exact shipping cost.
  • The minimum commercial gesture is 5 EUR/GBP/USD.In case of the dirty item or item worse than fair, the Agent may propose 5% commercial gesture.

 

Charter updates: Trade-In Part

  • Engraved MacBook or side with stickers are considered as cracked.
  • In case of transport issue, you need to provide the Certificate of non-receip in the chat before any request from a Back Market agent.
  • For damaged packages received, if you have a listing for the product in the received grade, you must do a counteroffer and the counteroffer price must, at least, either match the listing price for that grade or be 80% of the current Backbox price.
  • For Non functional devices (Grade 4 and 5  devices),
    • You must pay the order at initial price if you cannot find the IMEI
    • You can request customer action or refuse the device only if you are able to prove the issue to the customer (with IMEI check report or alternatives). 
  • If you’re unable to return products by the deadline (1 business day), Back Market reserves the right to pay the initial offer price  on your behalf.
Title: The Quality Charter of Back Market

Details__c:
What is a Mystery Order?

A mystery order is an order anonymously placed by the Innovation Lab, our expert team, on which a batch of tests is performed to ensure the product’s quality is compliant with our Seller's Terms and Conditions, in particular the quality standards provided for in the Quality Charter.

 

What is assessed exactly?

Both product quality and customer experience are assessed, notably but not exhaustively:

 

  • Order compliance (Right product, expected Grade)
  • Quality of parts: Battery (health & capacity), Screen
  • Functionality of parts: Sensors (Screen rotation, Cameras, Biometric), Buttons, Flash, Microphones & speakers, GPS etc.
  • Quality of assembly (e.g. sealing) & Cleanliness
  • Compatibility (e.g. Network, Charger plug & cable)
  • Accessibility (e.g. pin/iCloud lock) and Absence of User’s Data
  • Compliance with the rules on prohibited refurbishment practices

 

How the result is worked out?

For each check, a score is assigned upon success, partial success or failure. If the overall score is below 80/100, the Mystery Order is failed (KO). Besides, some quality requirements provided in the Quality Charter are considered as critical in the refurbishing or testing process to ensure the product's quality and performance. If a breach to a critical quality requirement is observed, the Mystery Order is automatically failed with a special warning (KO No-Go).

 

Find some examples of breach here below:

 

  • Counterfeit (product, packaging)
  • Leaflet for incentivization to bypass Back Market after-sales platform
  • Specifications do not match - Wrong model/capacity is provided
  • Blancco* Tests are failed (*software testing solution)
  • Battery requirements are not met: Battery cycle counts are above 500 and Battery health is below 85%
  • Very bad quality assembly (missing screws, wrong screen technology etc.)
  • Network is not compatible
  • Device contains personal user data (Device has not been erased)
  • Device is part of a potentially problematic type/series (A1660, Not identified Japanese Phone, Device reported stolen/lost...) forbidden on the platform

Finally, the Innovation Lab delivers an audit report with the final score, along with the documentation of tests and detailed results, that is shared with you with the Mystery Order outcome.

 

What is the Mystery Order Process?

In case of Mystery Order failure, you enter a monitoring period : reasons for failure are shared by email and for each of them you can find detailed recommendations. You have therefore a dedicated time for support to implement required actions to improve your refurbishing and/or testing process in order to meet our quality requirements.

 

Note that a new Mystery Order will be triggered at least within the 90 days following the first failed Mystery order, to ensure compliance with our quality requirements. If the Mystery Order fails a second time, you will have a last chance period to meet our quality standards, your account will also be limited to 50% of your average daily sales on the last 30 days for 21 day.

 

A new Mystery Order will be triggered after this last chance period and in the event it fails again, your account will be limited to 20% of our average daily sales (based on last 30 days) until business arbitration. Following this arbitration we will inform you if you can continue to sell on our platform and under what conditions or if we will have to close your account and our business relationship shall terminate, as provided for under Articles 12 and 18.2 of the Terms and Conditions.
Title: The Mystery Order Process Explained

Details__c:
Selling well on Back Market 

On Back Market, quality is essential. The key to speeding up your sales is to get as many BackBoxes as possible, and this is only possible with optimum quality. That being said, here are some additional tips to help you grow on our platform.

 

Offer, Offer, Offer!

Your business volume (GMV) on Back Market is directly linked to the scope of your offer. The more ads you have (especially positioned on BackBoxes), the more visibility you gain with customers, the more you sell.

 

Go international!

  • Back Market is currently present in several markets, in addition to France (our home country) and a lot of others will be open during the next months and years. 
  • On these markets, our growth is exponential and the competition is less (your chances of getting the BackBox are therefore higher). 
  • Expanding your operations to all available markets, rather than limiting your presence to a single country, has the potential to double your average sales.

If you don't have the resources to handle foreign language after-sales service or if you don't have a competitive transport contract to deliver and issue vouchers abroad, our CCBM and BackShip services are available!
 


IT integration!

  • This is the most scalable way to update your offer on the Back Market, in API or via FTP. 
  • You can do the developments yourself internally, or use the services of an external service provider (such as Channel Advisor or other). 
  • The integration allows you to benefit from an automatic update of your stocks on all the marketplaces where you are present. 

This is essential if you want to pursue an ambitious and effective omnichannel strategy.

 

Diversification on all Back Market grades!

  • The GMV Back Market is evenly distributed between the grades. If you do not offer one of the grades, you miss sales opportunities on the product in question. 
  • You therefore maximize your chances of obtaining the BackBox by positioning yourself on the entire range. A useful tip: if you have a lot of stock on a product for a given grade (> 50 units - ex: 50 iPhone 7 16Gb Sidereal Grey Mint) but you don't have the other grades in stock, you can "lower" the grade of part of the stock (ex: pass 25 units in Good). 
  • Of course, this depends on your margin (since by lowering the grade, you will also have to lower your selling price). But in most cases, this is a winning strategy and the additional sales compensate for the slightly reduced margins.

 Please note that beyond the grades, you can also apply to our Premium and New Battery Programs.

 

Diversification across product categories!

  • Our flagship category is, of course, smartphones (the historical vertical of Back Market). But the fastest growing categories today are tablets and laptops (Apple in particular). 
  • We therefore recommend that you position yourself in at least one of these 3 categories. Beyond these, we are gaining market share on other products (speakers, headphones, game consoles, home appliances, ...). 
  • On these categories, the competition is less strong, which improves your chances to get the BackBox. Our vision is that everything that is plugged in can be repackaged and therefore sold on the Back Market. 
  • The opportunities for you are therefore limitless. Please refer to the article "Sourcing opportunities on the Back Market" for more advice on this subject.



Brand diversification!

If you see a significant price differential on a flagship product such as the iPhone 8, even though you have a good quality score (around Back Market average), then don't hesitate to source on other brand products with less competition but more chances of getting BackBoxes. These products include Huawei, Xiaomi or Google Pixel. 



Additional services!

Some services are significant conversion boosters. 
Among them: 

  • Express delivery
  • PayPal
  • Extended warranty


When the customer is at the end of the buying process, hesitating whether to click on "buy", these are the services that make the difference. 
Some of our sellers have increased their sales by 58% thanks to PayPal from one month to another. As a marketplace, we have the advantage of having access to millions of pieces of data from 6 different markets, allowing us to provide you with valuable customer conversion figures.



Pricing!

  • With our BackPricer, you can set up a minimum price and optimize your listings in real-time to the highest selling price possible without losing the BackBox. 
  • Unlike other marketplaces, Back Market fights against price erosion. Our interest is the same as yours: the higher the average basket on Back Market, the higher our commission.
  • The objective of the BackBox algorithm is to reward sellers with better margins for better quality. 
  • A low price is not enough to win the BackBox. It's all about quality. Nevertheless, your price must, of course, be at a new market level. 
  • Take the time to compare your selling price with the price of the new product, and with the price of your competitors in other marketplaces. 
  • Ads with prices higher than new will be automatically taken offline. In order to better assist you, we have created a pricing tool directly accessible in the Back Office.
  • There is generally less competition on weekends and prices are often higher, allowing you to access the BackBox more easily and sell at higher prices. So do not forget to update your stock and prices on Fridays!

All the explanations are here
Title: What's the secret to selling well on Back Market?

Details__c:
Each of the following points is essential for a successful start on the platform and will help you answer the form at the end to increase your current daily sales limit from 6 to 10.

You have now acquired the basics for managing your business on Back Market. Nevertheless, some key tools can help you to improve your activity on the platform!
 

BackBox algorithm

Quality is crucial to winning the BackBox. The BackBox system is based on a proprietary algorithm that calculates your price for a given listing using 10 different quality metrics that we track on a weekly basis.

The better your quality performance, the higher you can charge for an item and still get the BackBox. 
For more information, check out this article

In addition, you have access to a dashboard on your Back Office that allows you to see the impact of your quality on your prices, per KPI. If your quality is good, you are able to price your devices higher than your competitors and still win the backbox.
 


Quality performance metrics

In addition to the quality performance dashboards, find our quality technical guides here

 

Testing software programs

To ensure consistency and high standards, all sellers will be required to test their smartphones before shipping with one of the approved testing software solutions for smartphone diagnostics listed below.
