Learn more about the approved testing softwares. 


Learn more about testing requirements. 

 

Please note that according to our quality charter, you must be able to provide us with a full test report if requested.

In any case, we advise you to test your product before putting it online, as well as before each order.
 

New Battery offer

Customers can now choose to upgrade their smartphones and MacBooks with new batteries.

Read more about this new offer here

If your quality KPIs match the outlined expectations, and if you know how to replace batteries with new ones, feel free to reach out through the SSC contact forms to apply.

 

Premium grade

Customers can also buy smartphones, iPads and MacBooks under a new Premium grade, with higher expectations in terms of functionality and aesthetical conditions. If you can source high-quality supply with original parts and high battery health (CPO, ASIS/ASIS+, open-box supply), feel free to contact us through the SSC contact forms and apply to sell this grade.

Read more about Premium grade here.


 

Secret to selling well on Back Market

International markets 

The strength of Back Market lies in the fact that it opens up many European markets, allowing you to be visible to tens of millions of consumers. Our platform has been created in such a way that you can easily sell in one or 13 countries in Europe, all on a turnkey basis, without having to make any investment or effort.

As you know, one of the main levers for developing sales is to sell in all these countries.

You just need to configure compliant shipping methods and define pricing rules in every country you want to sell in, and you’re all set!
And check out our International Pricing Tool here


Use integrations to optimize your efficiency

Integrations are the most scalable way to update your offers on Back Market and allow you to manage your workflow by transferring data via API, managing listings, orders, shipping, and automating Back Office tasks.

There’s a wide range of marketplace integrations to select from, allowing you to connect to our platform via API.


How can you select the right integration?
First, analyse your needs and look for an integration that meets them (creating listings, managing inventory, pricing, invoices, shipments, etc.).

You can either develop integrations yourself internally (first-party integrations) or use integrations from an external service provider (third-party integrations). 

Here are some names of integrations we work with: ChannelAdvisor, ChannelEngine, Linnworks, SellerCloud, ShipStation, ShoppingFeed, and Wholecell.

Add a new integration directly from your Back-Office here


How can Back Market help you?
If Back Market has access to the code of the integration software you're using, you can contact the Seller Support Center for help.

You can find more details here.
 

 


Grow your sales volume

Within your Back Office account you have access to a number of business reports which can be used to identify your sales trends, recognize top performing products and discover new opportunities for growing your business. 

 


Back Market services

You’ll find all our articles associated with our services here
 

BackShip

BackShip is a service that allows you to issue pre-filled shipping labels in 1 click, directly from your Back Office. This service allows you to benefit from competitive prices with our international shipping provider partners and will increase your On-Time Delivery Rate.
In addition, the return logistics are fully managed by our service and at no extra cost.

For more general information on BackShip, check out this article

Find pricing information here

You can subscribe to BackShip, via the Seller Support Center contact form, then check out how to set up your Back Ship shipping options in this article.

Thanks to our Back Ship service, you can set up regular pick-ups and delegate shipping provider investigations, directly handled by Back Market. Here is how to set up a regular pickup 

 

BackPack

Discover our packaging offer for smartphones and laptops here

The service has many advantages. Indeed, the packaging is very functional, takes only a few seconds to assemble and can be reused. Moreover, there is no extra cost as it is included in the CCBM fees.

You can place an order directly from your Back Office, from the delivery options tab.
 

Our Trade-in service 

Trade-in is a simple tool that allows refurbishers to source devices directly from Back Market customers. More than 150,000 products are bought back every month! You can access this service once your launch phase has been completed and validated.

You can use this service if:

  • You already sell on our platform
  • You are live in France, Spain, Germany or the UK


You can find more information directly here

 

Our tools

BackPricer

Our BackPricer works 24/7 to optimize your listings in real-time to the highest selling price possible without losing the BackBox. 

Here’s how it works: simply set your minimum and target prices and BackPricer uses its secret sauce to find the best possible price for you (based on your quality score), either by hitting your target price or the optimal price between your target price and your minimum price, this is the highest price at which you can win the BackBox.
More information about this tool here
 

Customer Care tools

To save you time, we’ve reduced your after-sales management to a few buttons. Back Market agents will let you know if you need to get involved in a customer contact, and you can easily select different options for returns, replacements, repairs, adding tracking information, resolving shipping provider investigations, and more. Using these buttons is the only way for you to take action in your after-sales interface.

 


How do I complete this module?

We know that this is a lot of information. To make sure you’ve got all of this, take 3 minutes to fill out this form. It will give you a chance to increase your sales limit from 6 to 10 sales per day.
 

Thank you for completing the four trainings, please make sure you comply with our quality standards to validate your Launch Phase, if you have any questions you can contact us via the SSC contact forms.
Title: #5 - New to Back Market? Everything you need to know about the key factors to success

Details__c:
All products sold on our sites must be delivered with compatible accessories:

Smartphones: 

  • Wall plug charger is no longer mandatory, only the charger cable is mandatory
  • Headphones are no longer mandatory since 19. December 2020)

Tablets/Macbooks/PCs: Charger
iMac: Keyboard and Mouse

If an accessory is not supplied or is not compatible, you must offer the customer a commercial gesture (see table below) or send him the missing accessory. If the accessory is not compatible, you can provide a return voucher to retrieve it before refunding / returning a new accessory.

Charger and charging dock

Apple

Below is a list of basic checks to know to ensure the quality of the original Apple charger and charging dock. 

You should always check:

  • OS compatibility (depending on the age of the models, the type of charger may be different).
  • The marking on the dock located below the USB port (serial number)

  • Marking on charging cable (marking along the cable).
  • Respect for the original manufacturer's colors.
  • The "MFI" // "CE" // "RoHS" certification.
  • That the charger can operate in both directions.
  • The result of a technical test of the charger (voltage, amperage, watt, charging speed). Please select a viable sample. You can also refer to the "Charger and lightning testers" section of this guide. 
  • The Lightning to Apple USB cable is marked "Designed by Apple in California". As well as the mention on the cable, "Assembled in China", "Assembled in Vietnam" or "Indústria Brasileira" will be visible about 17 cm from the USB connector. A serial number with 12 digits follows this mention.

Test on chargers and lightning

You can test OS compatibility by manually connecting the device to the charger. Your accessories will not be compatible if this message appears: "This accessory is not compatible with the device".

It is also advisable to use a Lightning cable tester to test a significant sample when in doubt about the originality or quality of the products offered to sellers.

If the cable is original, the display will show "original, as well as the code engraved on the cable". If the cable is not original, the display will show "copycat". 

The "score" number corresponds to the quality of the cable, 100 corresponding to an original cable.

In the case of a generic, the closer the number is to 100, the better the quality of the cable.

 

Samsung / Android

Samsung chargers and charging docks and Android, in general, must comply with the manufacturers' recommendations in terms of voltage, amperage and watts. These characteristics vary according to the manufacturer.

The chargers specify the current intensity (in amperes) and voltage (in volts) at the output, for the smartphone. To easily calculate the charger's output power (in watts), simply multiply the current by the voltage.

For example, in the case of a conventional charger capable of delivering a current of 1 ampere on 5 volts, we will be allowed 5 watts at the output. 

 SUMMARY TABLE - MANUFACTURER RECOMMENDATIONS



Regarding the quality of the chargers offered to the end customer, we recommend that you use the original manufacturer accessories.  However, the price of the original accessories is sometimes very expensive, so you can use accessories certified by the manufacturer.
Title: The quality of accessories

Details__c:
What happens if a customer returned the wrong product to you?

As soon as your customer opens a service request, an automatic message with return instructions is sent. This message contains a link to this FAQ article, where step 5 requires them to include the order details inside the return package after they’ve provided the product's IMEI or serial number. 

While this should be enough information for you to check in advance that the correct device is being returned to you, it’s possible that your customer could return the wrong product.

Here’s how to solve this situation efficiently.
 
First, report a problem in the open service request by selecting the “Report a problem” button. This will block an automatic refund and allow you to complete the following steps.

Next, provide the returned product's correct IMEI or serial number as soon as you realize that your customer has provided information that doesn’t match your records. You can do this in the open service request. This will notify your customer that the package will either be refused upon delivery at your warehouse or shipped back within 72 business hours.

Next, provide evidence if you realise that the wrong product was shipped to you. You can do this in the open service request within 72 business hours of receiving the item. 

Accepted evidence can be:

  • Screenshot of your database showing the returned product's correct IMEI or serial number 
  • Customer receipt for this order
  • Photos from all angles of the device you received (at least one should show the IMEI from the settings or the serial number)

You can also blocklist the device you originally sold to the customer (this action may lead to a quick resolution of the problem if the customer is attempting to commit fraud).

Always add products’ IMEIs or serial numbers in your Back Office, so you and Back Market agents have visibility in case a service request is opened.

You don’t need to provide the Back Market delivery note as this is automatically available for all orders directly in the after-sales services platform.  

 

If you found this article clear and helpful, hit the like button to let us know.
Title: What do I do if I received the wrong product?

Details__c:
On-hold rate definition

On-hold rate on trade-in orders is an important KPI of how satisfied your customers are with the quality of your Trade-in order management.

As a reminder, putting an order on-hold is 1 of 3 actions you can take when receiving a Trade-in order. You can also opt to pay the customer or submit a counter offer.

It is currently possible to put an order on hold when:

  • An action is needed from the customer for the transaction to continue (Disconnect iCloud/Google account, send a copy of their identity card or communicate their access code)
  • The package seems to have incurred damages during transport.
  • It is not possible to authenticate the product (Blocklisted / Greylisted / Rooted (only for Android products) / counterfeit)

However, and as indicated in our Back Market Charter, you undertake to:

  • Use this option in a legitimate and justified manner
  • Follow up with the Back Market customer in the event of no response from them.
  • Respond and help the customer as long as the terms of purchase are not respected.

The on-hold rate is calculated on a 30-day basis and takes into account the number of orders currently on-hold out of all orders received.
 

How to keep your on-hold order rate low

To keep your on-hold order rate low, you must:

  • Perform the following tasks upon receipt of the product: (where relevant)
  1. When the product is blocked by an iCloud / Google account: click here
  2. When an Android device is blocked by an access code: click here
  3. When an Apple device is blocked by an access code: click here  
  4. When you want to restore multiple Apple products simultaneously: click here  
  • Respond to all customer messages within 24 hours  (see Late Response rate action plan for details)
  • Justify ''putting an order on hold'' with evidence (when possible)
  • Help the customer to resolve an issue as quickly as possible 
  • Put an order on hold only when justified. It is strictly forbidden to put an order on hold for no reason.

 

How to monitor your KPIs

You can track your on-hold rate in your Trade-in quality dashboard. 

If you have any questions, do not hesitate to reach out to your Back Market point of contact.
Title: What's the On-Hold Rate in Trade-in?

Details__c:
Back Case 

Concerning the reconditioning in general, all screws and other holding plates must be present, all screws in the right place. No traces of glue on the screen or frame should be present. The markings and colours shown on the device must comply with the manufacturer's graphic charter.

Frames with even slight twist/twist should be discarded from sale.

  • It is recommended to avoid replacing the frame as much as possible because a non-original frame will be subject to various problems:

  • Since the torsion measurement is not carried out during the production of "copy" of frames, the torsion resistance is low or even non-existent depending on the quality of the "copy". 

  • The aluminium alloys used in the frame were not chosen in terms of strength, but in terms of economy. This leads to poor heat loss, internal electrical insulation, and also and especially to low or even no resistance to impact, and or immersion (even if the refurbished equipment is no longer certified waterproof).

  • In order to sell a refurbished device on the Back Market, the assembly must be compliant (the screen must not extend beyond the frame even slightly). This is practically impossible on a "copy".

 Screen

Touchscreen

We recommend that you carry out a complete test (manual + test software). This will allow you to perform a complete scan of the screen. You can also press and hold an application to move it to the four corners of the screen and from above perform a grid to test the touchlines to do it in both directions (switch screen sideways).

We remind you that the resistance of the screen is closely related to the thickness of the touch screen used to recondition the screen.  If the glass meets low impact resistance, the life of the unit is biased.

On the same basis, there are 3 types of touch screen quality:

  • Cheap: 2 kg max. pressure (drop less than 50 cm = crack)
  • Toughened glass: 3 max. pressure (drop less than 60-80 cm = crack)
  • Gorilla glass: 12 max. pressure (drop less than 1 m-1m40 = crack)

Gorilla Glass is present on more than 900 smartphone models, representing 6 billion devices from 45 different manufacturers, including Samsung, Huawei, Apple and Xiaomi.

 

Backlight

The backlighting must not show even slight traces of reconditioning of the screen, such as traces of cutter blades. You should also check for any white spots resulting from dust between the LCD and touch panel.

The backlight must not show traces of dust on a white background (blank point symptom).

 

Screen

As the screen is in several parts (LCD/tactile/backlight/frame), it is important to check that the alignment of its different parts is correct. There must be no unplanned gap/spacing at this location.

For the refurbisher, it is necessary to check the torsional resistance as well as the rigidity of the different layers composing the screen.

There are different types of tablecloths depending on the quality of the screen (strength/flexibility). The quality must be equivalent to that of the original screen for better durability.

The exterior colour of the screen must be in accordance with the original colour.  

It is important to ensure that the glue between the screen and the screen frame (bezel) is of good quality. You can, therefore, perform tests on a significant sample of the screen batch that will be used for future reconditioning of the devices.

At the same time, we advise you to check the plastic resistance of the screen bezel. Indeed, if a weakness persists, the screen will detach from the frame and cause a service opening even if the screen is still functional.

The alignment between the FaceTime camera and the location provided for this purpose must be perfect. When sourcing your screens, make sure that the additional transparent plastic part to "fix" the lens is present. The same applies to the proximity sensor.

For cleaning, a simple antibacterial treatment on the screen is sufficient. Also remember to check that the lower and upper screens of the screen are free of dust and other contaminants.

 

Battery

Batteries must be tested upon receipt of stock in two ways:

  1. Perform a first diagnosis with test software such as Blancco, Cellebrite, Picasoft or Phone Check.

  2. Perform a physical test (Number of load cycles, Production date, Percentage of wear and tear).

It is important to store/transport the batteries in a dry place and away from temperature fluctuations. Above 45 °C, the internal pressure of the battery is at its maximum and may implode or degas. 

We recommend that you prepare a hermetic container in a separate room for used batteries.

These tips are very important since the battery of a device sold on the Back Market must have a minimum capacity of still 85%. (80% for the UK market) 

The battery must be charged at least 50% when the end customer receives his device, so it is advisable to work on a stock rotation (PEPS) allowing the easy management of the charging problem.  Equipping yourself with one or more load banks can be a solution if you sell a large quantity of equipment at the same time.

 

Charging connector

It is imperative to ensure that the charging connector is clean to avoid any presence of "lint" or other accumulation.

The charging connector must be compatible, this means that no error messages saying that the cable is not recognized should appear. It must be possible to load in both directions. 

When replacing the charging connector, use only original parts, as the "copy" charging connectors tend to overheat and can cause the device (battery or motherboard) to fail. A "copy" charging connector may also not work with an original cable.
Title: The quality of the refurbishment and the components used

Details__c:
Introducing a new quality metric in the BackBox algorithm: the delivery deadline.

At Back Market, we understand that offering fast and reliable delivery is a commitment that can impact your operational costs. However, we also recognize the significant effect it has on customer satisfaction and conversion. 

To recognize your effort and encourage excellence in service, we’re implementing a bonus system for sellers who set up the fastest delivery deadlines and fulfil them.

How does it work? The bonus amount depends on the:
  • BackBox price 
    • For a 350 € product, the bonus in the BackBox algorithm is 3.30 € if you reduce the standard from 3 to 2 days. This means you can sell the product for 3.30 € more and still win the BackBox.
    • For a 700 € product, the bonus in the BackBox algorithm is 6.60 €. You can sell the product for 6.60 € more and still win the BackBox.
  • Deadline
    • When you set up a 24-hour delivery deadline instead of the usual 3-day delivery for a product that’s 350 €, the bonus is 6.60 €. You can sell the product for 6.60 € more and still win the BackBox.

This new metric is designed to reward you when you reduce delivery deadlines and help you maintain a high standard of punctuality. 

You’re rewarded if you keep your usual delivery deadline and ship on time instead of indicating a shorter delivery deadline but shipping late.

If you have the capability to reduce your delivery deadlines and keep up with on-time shipping, we encourage you to do so!
Title: New quality metric in the BackBox algorithm: the delivery deadline

Details__c:
As outlined in Article 15.1 of the Sellers Terms and Conditions, only returns classified as "change of mind" qualify for commission refunds.

Eligibility conditions:

  • The order is "refunded".
  • The product is returned within the contractual or legal cancellation period for “Change of mind” reason.
  • The After-Sales discussion is not classified with anything else than “change of mind” reason

=> All eligible commissions will be automatically refunded to you after the order refund. You don't need to do anything.

Frequently asked questions:

What if I notice a missed refund on an eligible claim?

Contact the Seller Support Center using the "Claim Misclassification / Commissions Refund" tile. Our team will review your case.

 

How can I ask for clarification on how a claim is categorized?

If you believe there’s such an error, you may :

 

  • Ask the Back Market Customer Care agent directly in the customer service interface for access to the full customer conversation
  • Submit a request to the Seller Support Center using the "Claim Misclassification / Commissions Refund" tile

Remember: For all demands, please make sure to submit your request to Back Market within 3 months of the cancellation, as per the Sellers Terms and Conditions.
Title: If my customer returns an item, does Back Market keep the commission?

Details__c:
Shipment standards at Back Market ✅ 

  • The packaging must be adapted to the product in order to avoid any deterioration during transport,
  • The Seller undertakes not to insert flyers in its packages, or even display its telephone number or email address on the packaging,
  • The Seller must respect the preparation time defined in the Quality Charter. The end of the preparation time corresponds to the moment when the carrier scans the product. Until this scan, the product remains under the Seller's responsibility,
  • The tracking number and the name of the shipping provider are mandatory,
  • 🏆 Packages shall be properly insured,
  • It is recommended to require the customer's signature upon delivery for high-value products,
  • The Seller must always offer a free delivery option. An additional paid delivery option can be added if the free option does not meet the delivery timeframes defined in the Quality Charter.

In the event of non-compliance with these commitments, Back Market may act in the place of the Seller and the costs generated (return coupons, refunds ...) will be borne by the Seller.

 

Best practices packaging your product 👍🏼

The transportation of lithium-ion batteries is subject to strict domestic and international shipping regulations. Contact your shipping provider for additional information.

Material & Size of the package

  • For all electronic devices ideally use a dual-wall corrugated box which is rated to the size and weight of the item being transported.

  • ⚠️ Under-filled boxes may collapse and over-filled boxes may burst. Prevent this by choosing a box with the right size for the content being shipped.

  • ⚠️ Avoid reusing old boxes as they may have lost some of their rigidity

 

Packing the content 

  • For added protection use the box-in-box packing method.

  • Pack the contents so that they are well cushioned with at least 6 cm of separation from the external walls of the box.

  • Use a suitable filler material such as bubble wrap, inflated airbags, packing peanuts, or kraft paper.

  • Ensure the pins/plugs are covered in 1 cm of cushioning to prevent damage to contents and outer packaging.

  • If shipping multiple items in a single box, ensure they are well separated from one another.

  • Ensure power button is protected from accidental activation with tape.

  • Packaging and wrapping must ensure an even distribution of the masses to secure the stability of the package and prevent any overturning.

  • You have to make sure, your products will be able to withstand typical impacts during handling and shipping (package will most likely be turned upside down for example).

 

Sealing the content 

  • Protect with a Good Seal: both the adhesive tapes you choose and how you seal the shipment together makes a difference in protecting your shipment contents from being exposed or damaged during transit.
  • H-Taping : ensure all seams are sealed using the H-taping method:

- Apply 1 strip of tape along the center seam
- Apply 2 strips of tape across both edge seams

  • Anti-theft sealing with stretch shrink wrap for example is a plus.
  • Use of recognizable tape could help identify if a package has been opened during transport.

 

Labelling

  • Fix the shipment label securely on the top surface of the shipment box (the position of the label is the best incentive for the shipping provider to handle your package upside up).

  • Ensure the label is wholly visible on one surface and that the label does not cover any seams.

  • Do not let other labels, tapes or paperwork cover the shipment label.

  • Placing a spare label inside the package will help the shipping provider identify your shipment if the original label becomes detached or damaged.

  • If you need to apply other labels on your shipment, please avoid placing them on the same surface as the shipment label.
Title: Preparation of the order - Best practices to prepare your parcel for transport 📯

Details__c:
Sometimes you need to double-check item specifications or compare different listings of the same item.

To make this easier for you, we’ve added 2 tabs on the “Listing detail” page:
  • Item specifications
  • Similar listings
To find these tabs, log into your Back Office > “Listings” > select a listing to see the “Listing detail” page > “Item specifications” or “Similar listings.”

Under the “Item specifications” tab, you’ll be able to see the device details like its category ID and name, brand, EAN, height, width, etc.

Under the “Similar listings” tab, you’ll be able to keep track of your inventory and see the appearance grades of all similar listings. You can access each similar listing by selecting the “See listing” button.
Title: How can I check item specifications and/or compare different listings of the same item?

Details__c:
Our catalogue definitely uses EANs, and they are required per listings, but the BMID is the number we use internally as the "source/number of truth". EANs are required, but that is not the number we use to manage and clean the catalogue.
 

So where does that leave us? And how can you create listings on your own?


You can use the 'create listings' feature to get a specific product's BMID. In the photo below, we used our search bar to get the BMID of a particular watch by looking up the features of the watch. The corresponding BMID is 422501. This is essentially what our BMID matching service does, just at scale.



 


If there is no matching BMID then you can create the listing by adding a price, pictures, description etc and when created the product will get a new BMID you can attribute to the listing and push via API. In the case there are duplicate listings, we choose the BMID with the lower value as that means it has been created earlier.

In summary; yes Back Market uses EANs, but they are not accurate internally, for this we use BMIDs as the source of truth. If EANs is what BuyBay uses, then that is great because it means the attributes for the products are consistent. We need to match those attributes to BMIDs to upload the listings to the Back Office using APIs.
Title: Why is the EAN number not accurate for this listing?

Details__c:
The Back Office order page collects all the information and tools you need to process orders in one place. Learn how to maximise your use of the page for quicker, better results. 

 

Jump to
How do I find the order page?
All you need to know about the order page
All you need to know about looking at individual orders
How do I process or modify an order?

 

How do I find the order page?

After logging into the Back office, select “Orders” in the menu bar to get to the orders page. 

 

All you need to know about the order page

The order page is divided into 4 main sections: 

  • All orders
  • New orders 
  • Pending orders
  • Orders with open tasks



Using filters

Once you’ve got orders coming in, you’ll be able to find information using a number of filters. Here’s a breakdown of the filters and where you can find them

All pages

Order status

“All orders” page


 

Importing and exporting

You can always import and export information from the orders page. The results of your imports/exports will be available in the file library page. 

 

Here’s an overview of the information you can export: 

 

Name

Action associated

Export all orders

Export all orders within a chosen time frame.

Export missing IMEIs

Export all orders for which you still need to register an IMEI number.

Export packing slips

Export all packing slips for selected orders within a chosen time frame.

Export BackShip label if BackShip seller

Export all of your BackShip labels within a chosen time frame.

 

When exporting, you’ll have the option to limit or filter the information you export by things like date, language, order status, and more. 

 

Here’s an overview of the information you can import:

 

Name

Action associated

Import tracking number

Import tracking numbers for specific orders in bulk.

Import IMEIs

Import IMEIs for specific orders in bulk.

 

Keep in mind that if you’ve applied a filter to your orders page, the filter will still apply when you go to import or export information. 

 

Understanding order statuses

Your orders will be labelled with a status that reflects its progress. When your order moves from one status to another, it will include different sets of relevant information about the order.

When your order is “To be processed,” it will look like this:  

 

Here’s a breakdown of the information that you’ll see when your order is “To be processed.”

Meaning

“Confirm” button

Select this button to confirm your intend to process and ship this order

Will change the order status from “To be processed” to “To be shipped”

 

Once the order status is “To be shipped” it should look like this:

 

 

Here’s a quick outline of the new information you’ll see on the order page.

 

Name

Meaning

“Print packing slip” button

This button is used open and download your packing slip

“Ship” button

This button is used to indicate you’re shipping a specific order

Will redirect you to the order shipping page

 

Once the order status is “Shipped”, nothing new will appear. 

 

Updating multiple orders

You can update more than 1 order by selecting the empty square above the individual order listings. You can also choose your orders by batch by selecting the empty squares next to each individual order.

 

Once the orders are selected, you can:

  • Confirm selected orders
  • Print packing slips
  • Create BackShip labels (if you’re a BackShip seller)



 

All you need to know about looking at individual orders

When you select an order ID, you’ll be redirected to a page with detailed information about the order in question. 

 

Here’s what you’ll find on the page:

 

Name

None



 

How do I process or modify an order?

As you progress through the process of completing an order, you’ll be able to select buttons to indicate you’re ready to move on to the next step. These buttons give you the ability to confirm the order, indicate you’re ready to ship the order, or cancel the order.  
 


Confirming an order

To confirm an order, go to the “New orders” page and select “Confirm”. You can also select multiple orders and confirm them all by using the “Confirm selected orders” button.

Now, you’ll be able to ship the order and add an IMEI number to the device. To do so, simply select “Ship.”

 

After indicating you’re ready to ship the order, you’ll see the following screen:

 

 

Here’s a brief explanation of each field:

 

Name

Action associated

Shipping provider

You can choose between all the different shipping providers available on our platform. If you’re a BackShip seller, you’ll find all BackShip options at the top of the list.

Tracking number

You can enter the tracking number of your parcel.

IMEI

You can enter the IMEI number for the device you’re shipping.

“Ship items” button

Once all the information has been entered, you can select this button to ship the order.

 

Once you’ve progressed past this screen, you’ll need to ship your order. You’ll still have the option to contact a Back Market agent by selecting the three dots and then “Contact agent”. 

 

Cancelling an order before it’s been shipped

If you need to cancel an order, here’s how to do it. Go to the “New orders” page and select the 3 dots. Then, select “Cancel order”. 

 

Refunding an order after it’s been shipped

There are 2 different scenarios when it comes to refunding an order — depending on if you’ve refunded the order before or after it’s been shipped. 

 

In order to make a refund before the order has been shipped, select the 3 dots next to the order > “Refund this order”. You’ll be able to choose the amount you want to refund. 

 

To refund an order that’s already been shipped, you’ll need to contact Back Market customer service. Select the 3 dots next to the order > "Contact agent" to open a request.



 

If you found this article clear and helpful, hit the like button to let us know.
Title: How to maximise the Back Office order page

Details__c:
As its name indicates, the Seller Support Center (SSC) is linked to all support requests, which have no direct business impact and therefore do not need the immediate attention of your Seller Success Manager.

To know who to reach out to and why, you should ask yourself this simple question:

Is my request related to a business-oriented topic or is my request related to a support topic not needing a direct intervention from my usual point of contact?

To help you better understand the difference between those requests, we prepared some examples:

Support requests that you should submit through the Seller Support Center:

  • I do not agree with a decision taken in one of my after-sales requests.

  • I don't understand the fee indicated in one of my invoices.

  • My listings are offline, I don't understand why.

  • There seems to be a bug in the algorithm, the BackBox is not displaying my products.

  • I want to subscribe to a Back Market Service

 

Business requests that you should send directly to your Seller Success Manager:

  • I don't understand my Quality Dashboard and need help in improving one of my KPIs.

  • I require help in developing my sales.

  • My sales are limited, I don't understand why and would like to remove this limitation.

  • I want to get the BackBox, but I don't know how to do so.


**If you don't have a Seller Success Manager, please use the "Account performance" option when filling out the SSC contact form to submit your request. 

All 'support' requests should be submitted to the Seller Support Center to optimize efficiency. The tool was built for you, we want it to become very useful for your day to day on Back Market! The success of the Support Center depends on your good will to use it accordingly.
 

Using this tool will give you a better vision on your ongoing requests, give us better visibility on your needs and on things that are not working well and need improvement. Our main objective is to improve our processes and seller experience in general based on your requests.
 

No worries, if you don't know if your request is related to a support or a business topic, please give priority to the Seller Support Center. If you're wrong and the support team is not the correct team to reach, they will simply let you know! We're sure you will become an expert in no time.
 


By submitting your request, you send a ticket to our inbox that will automatically be forwarded to the corresponding team. The person who will manage your request will be able to see your choices and therefore be more efficient in answering your request.

You will be informed by an update or an answer per mail. Moreover, you will also be able to follow up with all your requests thanks to the dedicated seller portal.
Title: How to know if I have to contact my SSM or the Seller Support?

Details__c:
As refurbishers, you receive customers’ trade-in devices before paying for them. Therefore, Back Market must take specific precautions to protect Trade-in customers against failed and refused payments.

 

Trade-in Deferred Payout is one way we protect customers — it also helps you run and grow your Trade-in activities while having lower Sales activities. 

 

Learn more about our processes to protect Trade-in customers’ payments

 

What is the Trade-in Deferred Payout?

The Trade-in Deferred Payout is additional to your Deferred Payout (Sales) and is separate from the amount retained based on your sales volume.

 

Also known as a “Trade-in Security Payment”, it aims to retain money from your payout to guarantee the payment of your Trade-in orders.

 

Learn more about Sale deferred payouts

 

How is your Trade-in Deferred Payout computed? 

The amount of your Trade-in Deferred Payout is calculated based on your:

 

  • Trade-in Exposure
  • Tiering (useful for calculating your Deferred Payout linked to Sales activities)
  • Wallet balance, as disclosed in your Back Office
  • Trade-in history (including any past failed payments)

 

If your Trade-in and Sales activities are well balanced (i.e. not generating risks for customers’ payments), your Trade-in Deferred Payout will equal 0.

 

If applicable, the Trade-in Deferred Payout is retained and reevaluated at each payout date. The Trade-in Deferred Payout amount can increase gradually based on your Trade-in Exposure, but will not exceed your Trade-in Exposure. Where a Trade-in Deferred Payout applies on top of a sales deferred payout, Back Market still guarantees 85% of each payout (compared to 95% when only a sales deferred payout is applied). The maximum amount retained at each payout to build to the Trade-in Deferred Payout also will not exceed €15,000.

 

Note that in case of suspension or limitation on sales or Trade-in, as well as in case of an increase of your Deferred Payout (Sales), your Trade-in Deferred Payout will be adjusted up to 50% of your payout (for Trade-in Deferred Payout only, notwithstanding any additional Sales Deferred Payout). 

 

How to track your Trade-in Deferred Payout?

Our team is working to ensure that the Trade-in Deferred Payout amount is visible and easily identifiable separately from your Deferred Payout (Sales). This would be displayed on both your invoice and in your Back Office. This feature will be available as soon as possible.

 

Until then, note that the Deferred Payout amount displayed on your invoices and in your Back Office includes both your Sales and your Trade-in Deferred Payout. 


When may your Trade-in Deferred Payout be used?

Your Trade-in Deferred Payout will only be used to pay your Trade-in customers if:

 

  • You have failed payments
  • Neither your Sales nor a wire transfer can cover the debt generated by your failed payments

 

When will my Trade-in Deferred Payout be released?

At each payout, your Trade-in Deferred Payout is reassessed and compared to your Trade-in Exposure. Any euro withheld above your Trade-in Exposure is released instantly.

 

If you exit the marketplace, your Trade-in Deferred Payout will be released within a maximum of 60 days of your exit, provided that you complied with your payment obligations to your Trade-in customers.

 

How can I reduce my Trade-in Deferred Payout?

The only way is to boost your Sales on Back Market. By doing so, your Trade-in Exposure will decrease and therefore your Trade-in Deferred Payout will also.
Title: All about the Trade-in Deferred Payout

Details__c:
Disclaimer: Note that Back Market doesn’t provide legal advice, and that it’s your responsibility to remain up to date with existing regulations. For additional information, contact your legal advisors.

 

Here are some recommended practices to follow in the EU and UK when you sell eco-friendly items such as those made with recycled or compostable materials.

 

If an item or packaging is listed as compostable, it must be suitable for home composting. Items that can only be composted in industrial units cannot be listed as compostable.

 

If an item or packaging is listed as being made from recycled material, the percentage of recycled material used in production or incorporated in the item or its packaging must be specified.

 

Avoid using terms such as "eco-friendly", "sustainable" or "green”.

 

These requirements mostly concern accessories such as smartphone cases, but you need to follow the above requirements when listing any new item on the platform. 

 

To make this easy for you, we’ve added questions that ask about compostable material and the percentage of recycled material to the listing creation process.

 

Here are a couple other requirements to keep in mind:

 

  • Add “Do not dispose of outdoors” to the item description if your item or packaging is compostable at home.
  • Don’t label your item or packaging as “biodegradable” or “respectful of the environment” unless you have robust evidence. Carbon offset claims are particularly restricted under the EU and the UK legislation. 
  • Claims must be clear about what they refer to. For example, if only the packaging is recycled, don't imply the entire product is.
  • Claims must be justified. Don't make claims you cannot back up with evidence.
Title: Environmental requirements for recycled and/or compostable materials in the EU and UK

Details__c:
No, you can set a different price in each country where your listing is online. 

Note that the price isn't the only field that can be different from one country to another. 

The mandatory fields that can be different in each country are:

  • the comment (as it needs to be translated)
  • the selling price
  • the warranty delay
  • the shipping method

The quantity would remain the same for all countries though. For instance: it is not possible to have a quantity of 2 in Spain and 0 in France for the same listing/SKU.
Title: Is my listing's price the same for all countries?

Details__c:
Creating listings 1 by 1 is time consuming.

To boost your work efficiency, we’ve added a new “Batch Listing Creation” tab that will allow you to create multiple listings and add multiple new items to Back Market’s catalog all at once.

To find this new feature, log into your Back Office > “Options” > “Batch Listing Creation.”

You can choose to create a listing for an existing item in Back Market’s catalog or submit a new item for approval.

Here’s how it works:
  1. Generate the CSV file template. You’ll see detailed instructions for the fields that you must fill in to create accurate listings.
  2. Download the CSV file.
  3. Enter your listing details in the correct file fields.
  4. Upload the final CSV file on the “Listings” page.
Title: How do I create multiple listings all at the same time?

Details__c:
The Counteroffer Acceptance rate is an important indicator of how satisfied your Back Market customers are with the quality of your Trade-in order management.

The Counteroffer Acceptance Rate represents the number of counteroffers that are accepted by Back Market customers versus all counteroffers. It’s calculated on a 30-day basis.
 

Why is it important to have a high Counteroffer Acceptance Rate?

If a Back Market customer rejects your counteroffer, it’s bad for both you and the customer. 

The Back Market customer trusted Back Market enough to trade their item in, put in a lot of effort to assess and send the item to you, and in the end, never got paid. And they won’t be able to resell their item somewhere else until you return it to them. Less than 1% of Back Market customers are satisfied after rejecting a counteroffer.

From your perspective, you spent a lot of time processing the order, only to be unable to repair and resell the item.

It's in everyone's interest to avoid ending up in this situation.
 

How to keep your Counteroffer Acceptance Rate high

Please read the “How to make a counteroffer?” section in this article carefully.

 

How to monitor your performance indicators

You can monitor your Counteroffer Acceptance Rate in your Trade-in quality dashboard accessible from the sales part of your Back Office.

Each of your Trade-in performance indicators has a limit. Check out this article on monitoring periods to learn more. If you enter a monitoring period, we'll let you know by email and provide you with the next steps.

If you have any questions, reach out to the Seller Support Center or your Back Market point of contact.
Title: What’s the Counteroffer Acceptance Rate?

Details__c:
Along with Quality Charter , your Quality KPIs (Key Performance Indicator) are one of the most important tools to help manage your product quality and customer satisfaction on Back Market.


You can access your Quality metrics simply via Back Office with the ability to filter on specific metrics, and can export the raw data.

 

How to access this Quality Insights?

From the Back Office, click on the 'Insights' tab. You will see the 'Quality Dashboard' section, where you will be able to have a deeper look into your metrics. 


Troubleshooting: If you see a connection page instead of your Dashboard, that means that cross-site tracking is disabled. No worries, here is what you should do to get access to your Dashboard:


If you use Safari, you have two options:

  • Use another browser (Chrome or Firefox for example).
  • Enable cross-site tracking in Safari. Here is the way to change your system preferences in Safari.

If you use Chrome or Firefox, and it still does not work:

  • Turn cookies on.

 

How exactly is it working and how to read my quality indicators?

You will have access to your five KPIs:

 

  • Defective rate
  • On-Time Delivery rate / On-Time Shipping rate
  • Appearance rate
  • Order Cancellation rate
  • Wrong Item rate

Note that this page is only for Sales monitoring, for BuyBack, please, refer to Insights - BuyBox Dashboard in your Back Office.

 

Here is an overview of the homepage:

 

 

From here, you will be able to dive deep into the 5 metrics.

 

Defective Rate

Definition

This metric represents the level of contacts linked to the functionality of the product and accessories ordered and delivered in the last 90 days. It impacts your ability to win BackBoxes and may lead to a 30-day sales suspension at a device model level if you are among the lowest performing ones.

 

Cohort: 90 days.

On-Time Delivery Rate / On-Time Shipping rate

Definition

This metric represents the share of the orders that were supposed to be shipped and delivered in the past 30 days that were tracked by Back Market's tools and delivered on time. In a nutshell, were you able to ship and deliver the tracked product in the promised time?

 

Cohort: 30 days.

Appearance Rate

Definition

This metric represents the share of the orders delivered in the past 90 days that generated no contact related to a functionality issue (technical claim only so far)

 

Cohort: 30 days.

Order Cancellation Rate

Definition

This metric represents the number of orders placed that you canceled in the last 7 days. It impacts your ability to win BackBoxes and, in case of a significant increase of orders you canceled within a short period of time, Back Market reserves the right to suspend your account until we receive clarification and a solution for their situation.

 

Cohort: 7 days.

Wrong Item Rate 

Definition

This metric reflects the customer contacts linked to wrong items received, including incompatible accessories, for orders delivered in the last 30 days. It impacts your ability to win BackBoxes.

 

Cohort: 30 days.

 

Raw data can be exported, and we advise you to do so, this will give you constructive insights on customer requests and satisfaction. With all this information you are able to monitor and improve your quality to ensure the best customer experience and the better sales at our platform.

 

How to download raw data?

  • Click on the "Download Excel or CSV file" button in the bottom left corner of your screen.
  • Select the information you would like to download (for raw data, for example, select "Raw data" little square.)
  • You can select CSV or excel extension
  • Click on "Download". Wait a few seconds and the file will be automatically downloaded on your computer.

 

Below is an excerpt from one of our webinars designed to help you navigate the Quality Insight Dashboard. Feel free to check it out for more detailed explanations.
Title: Insights tab - Quality in the Back Office

Details__c:
Around 60% of Back Market customers are not only new to Back Market but also refurbished tech. That’s why ensuring a frictionless customer experience is the best way to convince new customers to permanently switch to using renewed devices.

A disappointing customer experience may cause them to go back to buying new tech.  
 
Our goal is to give our customers no factual reasons to buy new by ensuring a perfect customer journey at all levels. 

We also aim to improve our quality processes. This is why beginning of October 2023, we have launched fairer quality processes called sales suspensions to replace monitoring and last-chance periods.
 
To ensure the process is fair, we follow these 5 metrics: 
 

  • Defective Rate (over 90 days): This metric represents the level of contacts linked to the functionality of the item and accessories ordered and delivered in the last 90 days. It impacts your ability to win BackBoxes and may lead to a 30-day sales suspension at a device model level if you’re among the lowest performing sellers.  

 

  • Appearance Rate (over 30 days): This metric represents the contacts linked to your customers’ dissatisfaction with the appearance of the items delivered in the last 30 days. It impacts your ability to win BackBoxes and may lead to a 7-day sales suspension at an item category level if you’re among the lowest performing sellers.  

 

  • On-Time Delivery Rate (over 30 days) / On-Time Shipping Rate: These 2 metrics have been bundled together in a Shipping & Delivery rates block. These metrics represent the number of orders  that were properly tracked and delivered on time / shipped on time in the last 30 days.  It impacts your ability to win BackBoxes and may lead to a 7-day sales suspension at a market level if you’re among the lowest performing sellers.  

 

  • Order Cancellation Rate (over 7 days): This metric represents the number of orders placed that were cancelled by you in the last 7 days. It impacts your ability to win BackBoxes and, in case of a significant increase of orders cancelled by you within a short period of time, Back Market reserves the right to suspend your account until we receive clarification and a solution for your situation. 

 

  • Wrong Item Rate (over 30 days): This metric reflects the customer contacts linked to wrong items received, including incompatible accessories, for orders delivered in the last 30 days. It impacts your ability to win BackBoxes.

 

To check out your quality key performance indicators, log into your Back Office > "Insights" > "Quality".

For more information, check out the Back Market Quality Charter. Or read this article on how the new quality processes will affect your business.
Title: What are the quality key performance indicators, and how do they work?

Details__c:
As per our Back Market policies and guidelines, any claims you may want to make, whether related to a refund or to disputing an action taken by a Back Market agent, must be submitted within three months from the date when the refund appeared in your invoice, or from when our Back market agent took the action you wish to dispute.

We understand that claim denials can be disappointing, and we want to assure you that we are committed to providing a great service to you, and we encourage you to reach out to us promptly for any claims that fall within the specified timeframes.

We are here to assist you and address any concerns you may have, however, beware that we will not be able to make any exceptions to this as all sellers are bound to follow the same rules.
Title: When can I make a claim to the Seller Support Center?

Details__c:
Have you ever wanted to see the costs associated with each order you sell on Back Market? We’re making it easier for you to do this!

Now you can see the cost breakdown of each order.

To see the cost breakdown, go to “Orders” > “More details” > “Cost breakdown”.

You’ll see all costs here, from the sale price to refund amount and more — all in one place.

Note that for technical reasons, some costs won't be available in the cost breakdown section including sales fee refunds, BackShip fee cost corrections, Customer Care by Back Market fee cost corrections, and more. We’re doing our best to include these costs in the future.
Title: What’s the order cost breakdown?

Details__c:
Back Market has negotiated exclusive prices with different shipping providers, and we’re always renegotiating to provide our sellers with the best prices. Below you’ll find the European shipping rates with our current providers: DHL Express, Chronopost, Colissimo, DPD, UPS, and Correos Express.

In order for you to read the rate cards properly, remember that: 

  • Rates don’t include VAT
  • Rates vary depending on the package pickup location, destination, and weight (please search for your country grid and look at the rates for each destination)
  • Pickup varies per shipping provider, but most of the time it’s included in the price 


See the table below for available pickups:

  • Rates don’t include fuel surcharges, and they vary depending on the package pickup, destination, and shipping provider


Back Market is charged at the end of each month. We’ll look at the difference between sellers’ and shipping providers' bills, so we can charge the correct amount to sellers. For more information about this, please read this section.

Please note that most of the shipping providers calculate the package weight and take its volumetric weight into account, so there are no discrepancies between the actual weight and estimated one.

For heavy or bulky items of more than 30 kg, our current shipping providers aren’t yet able to handle these items properly. We’re working on a future solution for these items. To be safe, check out the items considered heavy or bulky

If you have any questions, please contact the Seller Support Center (SSC) here.
Title: BackShip - How much does the service cost?

Details__c:
Tired of warning messages showing up when you’ve installed brand-new batteries in an iPhone? Back Market has a new solution to get rid of these once and for all!

Meet the Battery Refurbishing System.

Here is a short video about it: 
 

What’s the Battery Refurbishing System?

Back Market’s Innovation Lab has built a patented welding protocol to safely and efficiently change an Apple battery cell to put an end to warning messages.


This industrialized welding protocol differs from the manual ones currently used in the industry, including: 

  • Its success rate: 99% of refurbished batteries won’t generate an Apple warning message with this protocol.
  • Its quality: Batteries fail less than 0.1% of the time in the following 12 months versus 1–2% for manual protocols.
  • Its efficiency: A single trained operator can successfully process 40–50 batteries per day.

While the Battery Refurbishing System takes about 10 minutes per battery, the welding portion takes only 15 seconds. Up to 3 operators can use the same machine at a time.


How do you use this system?

Just follow these 5 steps:

  1. Disassemble the battery.
  2. Prepare the cell and insert the jig.
  3. Weld.
  4. Activate and program the battery.
  5. Assemble the battery.
The full protocol with detailed instructions on how to use the system is attached to this article.

The Battery Refurbishing System includes jigs that fit the exact dimensions of BackParts battery cells, guaranteeing a high-quality weld. 

For more information on using BackParts, read this article on how BackParts can help your business grow.
 

What tools do I need for this?

The Battery Refurbishing System comes with:
  • Battery welding machine
  • 3 jigs for battery cells

You’ll also need these tools that aren’t provided:
  • Mini grinder and grinder bits
  • Plastic spatula
  • Nail emery boards
  • Ceramic scissors
  • Electronic precision pliers
  • Activation and programming board
  • Generator for arc welding

For a visual look at the tools you'll need, check out this slide deck.
 

How do I order it?

To get this system in your shop, follow these steps to submit an order through the Seller Support Center: 
 

  1. Submit a new support request
  2. Select “Back Market services” > “BackParts” > “Place a BackParts order”

How much does it cost?

Use this spreadsheet to figure out the cost

The Battery Refurbishing System includes the welding machine and 3 jigs for models iPhone XS, XR, and 11.


Can I trial this system first?

Yes! Trial the Battery Refurbishing System by ordering at least 150 BackParts cells along with the system.

If you return the system before the invoice due date (1 month after you place the order), the invoice will be canceled, and you won’t have to pay for it as long as the welding machine and its components aren’t damaged. 


What size cells can be used with this system?

This system’s jigs were made based on BackParts cell dimensions. The jigs dimensions are available here , so you can check if your cells fit the jigs.
 

What battery models can I use with this system?

We currently have jigs ready to be used with the iPhone XS, XR, and 11 models. For more recent battery cells, Back Market can provide you with the correct jigs, but know that it will take up to 3 weeks to produce these.
Title: How to get rid of the battery warning message on iPhones

Details__c:
Why is this change happening?

We’re streamlining the Smartphone Case category to enhance quality, performance, and customer satisfaction. By focusing on high-performing products and sellers, we’re making it easier for customers to find reliable, durable, and competitively priced cases—while boosting visibility and sales potential for top-performing sellers.


What products can remain in the category?

Only high-quality products that meet strict performance criteria will remain. Cases must demonstrate:

  • Durability & sturdiness to ensure long-term protection.
  • Unique value to prevent redundancy and enhance customer choice.
  • Strong sales performance within the category.

Check the list of removed products here


What are the specific quality standards for smartphone cases?

Quality standards include:

  • maintaining low defect rates within 30 and 90 days,
  • ensuring products have a high level of sturdiness
  • and avoiding listings that are different yet too similar to others, to avoid confusion.

These criteria ensure a better shopping experience and higher product reliability.

 

Do I need to apply to remain in the category?

No application is needed. However, only sellers meeting the required performance criteria will retain their listings. We encourage you to review your current listings and optimize product quality and sales.

 

Can I be removed from the category?

Yes. If a seller’s listings don’t meet the necessary quality standards or sales thresholds, they will be removed from the category.

 

How does this benefit customers?

Customers will enjoy a curated selection of high-quality, durable smartphone cases, leading to a better shopping experience and increased trust in the category.

 

What should you do next?

  • Review your current listings to ensure they meet the category’s quality and performance standards.
  • Optimize your product quality & sales strategy to retain visibility in the category.

We’re here to support you through these changes! If you have any questions or need assistance, don’t hesitate to submit a ticket.
Title: Changes to the Smartphone Case category: What you need to know

Details__c:
This process helps you closely monitor your underperforming quality indicators (KPIs), available in the Quality Dashboard, in order to ensure long-term quality and improve the customer experience.
 

For each KPI referenced in the table at the end of the article, we have determined a threshold above which we consider that you are well below the Quality expectations as mentioned in our Charter. We therefore expect you to improve this indicator so that you can continue to buy products on our Platform. This process applies if you have a significant number of cases in the last 30 days or if your indicator remained above threshold over a significant time period of up to 6 months.


When you reach the quality threshold of a KPI, you are informed by email of your entry into the Monitoring Period process for the indicator in question. You have a set number of days to improve against the cutoff while your sales capacity is capped at 150% of your average daily trade volume over the past 30 days. 

 

During this period, your Back Market point of contact is at your disposal to help you set up an action plan to improve the concerned indicator.
 

If you do not meet the goal at the end of the period, you will be given one last chance ("Last Chance") before proceeding to terminate our collaboration and close your account. During this last chance, the same duration as the Monitoring Period process, you will be limited to 30% of your average daily resume volume over the last 30 days.
 

In addition, in the event that your performance continues to deteriorate, below our critical rates defined in the table below, you will enter Last Chance early and your sales capacity will be limited accordingly.

 

Monitoring Period thresholds and duration for each KPI

 
KPIThresholdCritical ThresholdDuration
Counter-offer rate≥33%≥50%28 days
Late processing rate≥15%≥40%28 days
Late response rate≥15%≥40%28 days
Counteroffer acceptance rate ≤60%/28 days



For example, if, on the last 30 days, you have an average Late processing rate above 15% you are eligible for the monitoring period process You will then get a formal notification and have 28 days to improve your quality metric.
Title: What is the Monitoring Period process in Trade-in?

Details__c:
This article is a bit technical and particularly useful for Sellers choosing a Technical connection (such as API or FTP). You can also find more information here: https://doc.backmarket.fr/#taxonomy 

 

From its creation to the moment it's totally processed, an order passes by several states. 

Order states

When an order is created, it's in state 0. The payment validation is pending. Back Market is doing verifications to check the customer identity. Orders in this state must not be shipped.

If the payment process fails, the order passes to state 8. You must ignore this order. Actually, it should never arrive in your system, as we filter them out when sending you the order list.

If the payment is checked and validated, the order passes to state 1. You must process the orderlines.

When all the orderlines are managed (accepted or cancelled), the order passes to state 3. You must ship the accepted orderlines and refund the cancelled ones.

When all the orderlines are shipped or refunded, the order passes to state 9: the order is processed.
 

Orderlines states

When an order is created, all its orderlines are in state 0. You have to wait for payment confirmation.

If the payment process fails, all the orderlines pass to state 7.

When the order passes to state 1, all its orderlines pass to state 1. You have received the payment, and you must now validate or cancel each orderline.

If you cancel an orderline, it passes to state 4. The customer will be refunded for this orderline.

If you accept an orderline, it passes to state 2. You must now prepare the corresponding product for shipment.

When all the orderlines are in state 2 or 4, the order passes to state 3.

Even if you accepted an orderline, you can cancel it afterward. If you or the customer cancel it after it has been validated, the orderline passes from state 2 to state 5. The customer will be refunded for this orderline.

Once you have shipped the orderline, you need to update the orderline from the state 2 to the state 3. 

Note that, since all orderlines are gathered in the same package, you don’t need to update every orderlines to the state 3 but just once for the whole order. Indeed, every orderline of this order will be set to this state automatically.

Finally, if the customer makes a refund request after shipping, the orderline passes from state 3 to state 6. The customer will be refunded for this orderline.
Title: The Orders & Orderlines "states" 🗃

Details__c:
Starting April 2026, you can offer customers delivery to pickup points across the UK.

 

Note: This option is only available with BackShip.

 

Why offer collection point delivery ?

Benefits for your business

  • Lower shipping costs - £3.39 per label (compared to standard home shipping)
  • Fewer failed deliveries - customers don't need to be home (about 50% fewer failed deliveries than home delivery)
  • Higher conversion - about 1 in 4 customers choose pickup points when available
  • Fewer incidents - pickup points are more secure (fewer lost or stolen parcels)

BackShip handles the complexity

  • Claims management - we handle shipping provider investigations.
  • Automated pickups - regular scheduled collections from your location.
  • Fewer address errors - automated tracking with no manual input.
  • Time savings - focus on sourcing and refurbishing, not logistics.

Coverage & availability

  • Where: UK to UK deliveries only
  • Shipping provider: DPD UK
  • Network: 12,000+ pickup locations across the UK
  • Max weight: 20 kg per product
  • Max dimensions: No 2 sides longer than 120 cm (example: 60 cm × 60 cm × 60 cm)

How to set up collection point delivery

Option 1: I already use BackShip

 

  1. Log in to your Back Office.
  2. Go to Shipping settings.
  3. Add a new shipping option: DPD UK Pickup Point.
  4. Save and activate.

Option 2: I don't use BackShip yet

 

First, set up a pickup with the shipping provider:

 

  1. Go to the Seller Support Center and submit a support request:
    • Order related? → No
    • What does your question concern? → Back Market services
    • Which service? → BackShip
    • What do you want to do? → Set up a regular pickup

Label Generation

Scenario A: I already use BackShip

No changes needed. Keep your current process.

 

Scenario B: I use an integrator with BackShip API

No action needed. Your integrator already supports BackShip.

 

Integrators with BackShip API:

 

  • ChannelEngine
  • Infiplex
  • Linnworks
  • Sellermania

Scenario C: I use an integrator without the BackShip API

Action needed: integrate the BackShip API. More details here.

 

Scenario D: I generate labels manually

Use the same process as usual.

 

Customer experience

How do customers collect their parcels?

 

  1. The customer chooses a Pickup point at checkout and selects a location.
  2. You ship with the generated DPD label.
  3. DPD delivers to the pickup point.
  4. The customer gets a QR code by email and SMS.
  5. The customer shows the QR code at the pickup point to collect the parcel.


Pickup deadline: 7 days. Customers get 2 reminders by email and SMS.

 

Frequently Asked Questions

How does this affect my on-time delivery (OTD)?

No negative impact. OTD is counted when DPD delivers to the pickup point (the first delivery attempt), not when the customer collects it.

 

How does insurance work?

Optional and the same as standard BackShip insurance:

 

  • All parcels include basic ad valorem coverage (up to the product value).
  • You can add extra coverage if needed.

How does billing work?

Same as other BackShip options:

 

  • £3.39 per label for pickup point delivery.
  • Invoices are issued monthly.
  • Learn more here
Title: Pickup point delivery now available in the UK

Details__c:
Your wallet, displayed under the ‘Money’ section of your Back Office, shows the real-time total of your sales earnings on Back Market that haven’t been paid to you yet.
On the same page, you can also view a schedule of your upcoming payouts.

 

Now, payouts are sent by Back Market every Wednesday. 

 

Each payout covers all sales made and shipped during the previous days. In practical terms, if we call your most recent payout day as D, the amount paid includes the net amounts for the orders shipped between D-14 and D-8. The next payout, which will be sent on D+7, will include the net amounts for the orders placed between D-7 and D-1, and so on.

 

 

To check your wallet balance, you need to follow these steps:

 

  1. Log in to your Back Office
  2. Go to ‘Money’ page
  3. Select the ‘Your wallet’ section
  4. See ‘Your wallet balance’

In the top-left box, labeled ‘Your wallet balance’, you can see the total accumulated amount per currency. In the middle box, named ‘Upcoming payouts’, there’s a breakdown of the payments due to you from Back Market over the next two payout cycles.

In the right-hand box, you can see the amount of the deposit currently held by Back Market.

 

 

Deferred payouts appear in your wallet as well. For more information on the deferred payout, please check out the dedicated What's a deferred payout? article.
Title: What is my virtual wallet and where can I see the balance?

Providing a tracking link in the Back Office gives Back Market agents and the customer visibility into your actions, and handles your customer service requests more efficiently. 

 

How to provide a tracking link via the Back Office ?
