Details__c:
The Late Response Rate reflects how quickly you act on customer service requests related to Trade-in orders. It’s an important indicator of customer satisfaction.
 

Jump to

How is my Late Response Rate calculated?
What happens if my Late Response Rate is too high?
​​​​​​​​​​​​​​How can I keep my Late Response Rate low?
​​​​​​​How should I monitor my performance indicators?
 

How is my Late Response Rate calculated?

Your Late Response Rate directly reflects how quickly you respond to requests from your customers. Sellers have 24 hours to respond to customer requests via the Quality Charter. This timeline excludes weekends and bank holidays (in the country where you have your warehouse).

This is the Late Response Rate formula: the number of Trade-in orders with a late seller response, divided by all Trade-in orders with a customer message.

A valid response to a customer message includes:

  • An action like confirming an order, making a counteroffer, indicating that an item can't be processed, contacting a customer if their action is needed, or indicating a shipping issue
  • An answer posted in the chat

Your Late Response Rate is calculated every day, and it’s based on orders received in the last 30 days.
 

What happens if my Late Response Rate is too high?

If your Late Response Rate is too high, you’ll enter a “monitoring period" where you have the opportunity to improve your rate. If your rate doesn’t improve enough, your daily Trade-in limit will be decreased. If the issue persists, you won’t be able to use the Trade-in service.

​​​​​​​Learn more about Late Response Rate limits and how the monitoring period works
 

How can I keep my Late Response Rate low?

We want you to keep killing it — so here’s our advice on keeping your Late Response Rate low.

Stay on top of your Back Office
Check your Back Office several times a day to make sure you’re responding to messages as they come in — and meeting the 24 hour response deadline. Just go to "Trade-in" > "Orders" > "Orders to reply to" and check if you’ve got any messages.

Note that orders in this list are sorted from the oldest to the newest.

Make sure your “Orders to reply to” inbox stays clean
To do so, provide a valid response to all orders — even if it means only saying “Thank you.” You should also answer older orders, even if you’re already late.

Double check that you’re receiving email notifications
Make sure you’re receiving email notifications when a customer or agent contacts you about a Trade-in order. If you’re not, reach out to the Seller Support Center to get them set up .

Make a plan to tackle big issues

If you’re anticipating or encountering a big issue, you can consider the following options to solve it:
  • Adjust your process to handle orders received (including prioritisation of orders or a more organised workflow).
  • Check your “Orders” section more often.
  • Increase your resources temporarily.


​​​​​​​Don’t forget to plan your holidays
If you’re planning to go on vacation or be away from your business, we suggest you update all your listings to £0 about 2 weeks before your departure. However, all customer messages must continue to be managed, so make sure you have resources to handle them during this period before you go.
 

How should I monitor my performance indicators?

You can check the progress of your Late Response Rate and other performance indicators in your Trade-in insights dashboard. You can access these from your Sales Back Office, then “Insights” > “Trade-in”.

If you have any questions, contact your Back Market point of contact or reach out via the Seller Support Center .
Title: What’s my Late Response Rate, and how do I keep it low?

Details__c:
Jump to
What’s the On-Time Shipping / Delivery Rate?
How do we calculate this rate?
Where can I find my rate?
How can I improve my On-Time Shipping / Delivery Rate?  


 

What’s the On-Time Shipping Rate / Delivery Rate?

The On-Time Shipping (OTS) & On-Time Delivery (OTD) KPIs have been bundled together in a Shipping & Delivery rates block.

On-Time Shipping (OTS) reflects your ability to prepare an order within the required timeframe.

On-Time Delivery Rate (OTD)  reflects your ability to deliver an order to your customer within the required timeframe. 

These rates track the number of orders tracked by Back Market tools within the last 30 days.

If your rate within a particular market is among the lowest-performing sellers on the platform, Back Market will suspend your listings in this market for 1 week.
 

How do we calculate this rate?

OTS : We divide the number of orders supposed to be shipped in the past 30 days by the total number of orders supposed to be shipped in the last 30 days. 


OTD : We divide the number of orders supposed to be delivered in the past 30 days by the total number of orders supposed to be delivered in the last 30 days. 
 

Where can I find my rate?

To see your rate, log into your Back Office > “Insights” > “Quality.”

Here you’ll see your results and how you compare to the Back Market seller average.


 

To better understand your performance, select the “See details” button. 

Your BackBox performance is currently only based on On Time Delivery. Keep in mind that the better your On-Time Delivery Rate is, the higher the prices you’ll be able to set to win BackBoxes, thus increasing your margins.
Please also note that On Time Shipping has an important impact on Customer Delivery Contacts and therefore on CCBM fees. 

 

How can I improve my On-Time Delivery Rate? 

Please refer to the Quality Charter to have visibility on Shipping / Delivery deadlines that are required per product categories. 

 

Here are some tips :

  • You can change your delivery speeds directly in your Back Office.
  • When you ship an order, you must add the tracking number and shipping provider in your Back Office. Make sure you add the correct shipping provider (the one also registered in your options).
  • If you add a shipping provider that doesn’t comply with your delivery options or the tracking number isn’t compatible with the shipping provider indicated, then our system will not be able to track your order. This will increase your Untracked Rate within the On-Time Delivery Rate and reduce your ability to win BackBoxes.
  • Remember that Back Market is here to support you and help your business perform as best as possible. We offer BackShip shipping services that can improve your delivery performance.
Title: What’s the On-Time Delivery Rate?

Details__c:
Jump to
Why is Back Market suspending sales?
How does this process work?
How will I be informed of a sales suspension?
What if I sell items that have a higher risk of defects, such as laptops with setup issues?
What about false defects or appearance issues? Will you recalculate my scores?
Will I be informed about the listing suspensions?
How can I track the listings offline? And the listings at risk of permanent suspension?
Have monitoring periods completely disappeared?​​​​​​​
What if I am in worst 20% but still very close to average?


In October 2023, Back Market introduced a new, improved process of sales suspensions that replaced monitoring and last-chance periods. 

Here are the 5 key performance metrics that we track:

  • Defective Rate over 90 days

  • Appearance Rate over 30 days

  • On-Time Delivery Rate over 30 days

  • Order Cancellation Rate over 7 days

  • Wrong Item Rate over 30 days

These metrics, except for Cancellation Rate, have a direct impact on your ability to win BackBoxes. Two of them have the potential to suspend your sales: the Defective, Appearance, On-Time Delivery Rates can lead to sales limitations.


 

Why is Back Market suspending sales?

We want to give you time to analyse the root cause of your issues, develop an action plan, and implement it. This is why the suspension periods differ based on the type of issue. 


Starting October 2024, we permanently suspend models that have already been suspended 3 times for Defective Rate issues. If the same model has been suspended 3 times within 12 months, the 4th suspension will be permanent.
 

How does this process work?

Defective Rate over 90 days

For this rate, we’ll compare your performance with competitors at a model level (for example, iPhone 11, iPhone 12, etc.). If you’re significantly lagging behind the seller average and fall within the lowest-performing 20% of sellers, your listings for that specific model will be suspended for 30 days.


Appearance Rate

We’ll compare your performance with competitors at an item category level (for example, smartphones, laptops, etc). If you’re significantly lagging behind the seller average and fall within the lowest-performing 20% of sellers, all of your listings in the item category will be suspended for 7 days.


On-Time Delivery Rate

If you are underperforming on On-time delivery rate, you will receive a notice giving you two weeks to fix the issue. If after two weeks your results are still lower than average, we will have to limit your daily sales volumes. 

 

Every suspension period is followed by the Trial period of the same duration. During this period, we will not suspend the same item for the KPI that initially led to suspension. We give you this time to see the efficiency of the actions implemented during the suspension period and clean up the cohort.

 

How will I be informed of a sales suspension? 

You’ll receive the first warning at least 1 week before the sales suspension goes into effect. If you don’t improve, you’ll be notified at least 24 hours before the sales suspension goes into effect. A warning notice doesn’t mean that your sales be suspended. If you manage to improve your results, your sales may never be suspended. 




You’ll be able to monitor your key performance indicators daily on your Back Office Quality Insights dashboard. You’ll also be able to monitor any suspension directly there. 


Your Quality Insights dashboard and key performance indicators tracked will be updated accordingly well in advance of the new sales suspension process launch.

You may also see which models were suspended 2 or more times, meaning they’re at risk of permanent suspension. 
 

What if I sell items that have a higher risk of defects, such as laptops with setup issues?

Good news: With the new process, we’ll compare defective rates at the model level. This means that your laptop listings will be compared to the same laptop listings of your competitors, rather than being compared to a general limit as in the current monitoring-period process.


 

What about false defects or appearance issues? Will you recalculate my scores?

No, we won’t make any changes to this process. We assume that there will always be some customer claims of false defects or appearance issues, and all sellers are equally at risk. Additionally, with the AI claims classification, we aim to minimize the false defective. 

 

Will I be informed about the listing suspensions?

Yes, you’ll receive emails notifying you of listings at risk of suspension and the listings that have been suspended. The warning notice aims to alert you to the risk of suspension. We recommend you take it seriously and take the necessary measures to improve your results and potentially avoid suspension. If not, you’ll receive the suspension notice, and 24 hours later, the affected listings will be suspended for 7 or 30 days depending on the rate in question. 
 

How can I track the listings offline? And the listings at risk of permanent suspension?

As part of the Quality Insights update, you’ll be able to see the listings at risk and the suspended listings in your Back Office > “Insights” > "Quality" > “Quality performance overview."


 

Have monitoring periods completely disappeared?

Yes, there are no longer monitoring or last-chance periods associated with sales quality key performance indicators. You may still be subject to other limitations or suspensions, relating to On-mystery orders or safety and legal processes. 

 

What if I am in the worst 20% but still very close to average?

If the average results of the sellers are very tight (no significant gap between the average and worst 10%) there will be no sales suspension for this model/category/market.
Title: How do the quality processes work and impact my business

Details__c:
Each of the following points is essential for a successful start on the platform and will help you answer the form at the end to increase your current daily sales limit from 3 to 4. 

Quality products and customer service management are crucial to Back Market. 
Complying with our quality requirements will help you during your Launch Phase and increase your chances of winning BackBoxes. 
 

The Quality Charter

Start by reading our Quality Charter
 

Key quality performance indicators 

It’s important to understand what key quality performance indicators are, how they work, and how they can help you grow your business on Back Market.

There are 5 quality indicators:

 

  • Defective Rate (over 90 days)
  • Appearance Rate (over 30 days)
  • On-Time Delivery Rate (over 30 days)
  • Wrong Product Rate (over 30 days)
  • Order Cancellation Rate (over 7 days)

Read more about the quality indicators here

 

Customer claims are assigned to the relevant quality KPIs based on an AI-powered request classification system, analyzing the entire discussion between customers and agents, therefore maximizing the accuracy of your metrics.

As a result, there is no possibility to remove claims from your dashboards and your metrics.

Read more about the AI help request classification here

 

Insights tab - Quality in the Back Office

You can access your 5 quality indicators in the Back Office and filter specific indicators, export raw data, and monitor your performance.

  


On-Time Delivery Rate (over 30 days)

This rate reflects your processing, shipping, and delivery management. It shows that you deliver products to customers as planned. This rate represents the number of your orders delivered on time and correctly tracked over the last 30 days.

To pass your Launch Phase, your On-Time Delivery Rate must be 90% or more.

Learn more about the On-Time Delivery Rate
 

Defective Rate (over 90 days)

This metric keeps track of the number of customer contacts linked to the functionality of your products and accessories ordered and delivered in the last 90 days. It impacts your ability to win BackBoxes and may lead to a 30-day sales suspension at a device-model level, meaning that the suspension will apply only to the model(s) affected if you’re among the lowest-performing sellers.

To pass your Launch Phase, your Defective Rate must be 5% or lower.

Learn more about the Defective Rate
 

 

Appearance Rate (over 30 days)

This rate measures the number of customer contacts linked to their dissatisfaction with the esthetical condition of the products delivered in the last 30 days. It impacts your ability to win BackBoxes and may lead to a 7-day sales suspension at a category level if you’re among the lowest-performing sellers.

To pass your Launch Phase, your Appearance Rate must be 1.5% or lower.

Learn more about the Appearance Rate
 

Physical appearance grades

One of the best things you can do for your business is to make sure that the grade displayed on your listing corresponds to the grade of the product the customer receives. Otherwise, you risk a high return rate and loss of customer trust in refurbished, which will impact your quality performance indicators. 

Learn more about physical appearance grades

 

Order Cancellation Rate (over 7 days)

This metric keeps track of the number of orders placed that were cancelled by you in the last 7 days. It impacts your ability to win BackBoxes and, in case of a significant increase of orders cancelled by you within a short period of time, Back Market reserves the right to suspend your account until we receive clarification and a solution for your situation.

To pass your LaunchPhase, your Order Cancellation Rate must be 2% or lower.

Learn more about the Order Cancellation Rate
 

Wrong Product Rate (over 30 days)

This metric reflects the number of customer contacts linked to wrong products received, including incompatible accessories, for orders delivered in the last 30 days. It impacts your ability to win BackBoxes.

To pass your Launch Phase, your Wrong Product Rate must be 2% or lower.

Learn more about the Wrong Product Rate


Sales suspension

To ensure that all products listed on our platform meet these standards, Back Market has implemented a comprehensive Sales Suspension process. This process is designed to identify and address listings that may not comply with our quality expectations, thereby safeguarding the interests of our consumers and upholding the integrity of our marketplace.
 

Compliance with Sellers T&Cs and Quality Charter 


We want to remind you of the importance of following the Seller T&Cs and Quality Charter. 

  • You should ensure all customer communications take place within the platform, with no personal contact details shared on invoices or flyers.

  • Refund disputes should be handled through the Seller Support Center. 

  • Devices must be erased, unlocked, and free of personal data, and only locally compatible phones should be sold. 

  • It's essential to maintain polite and respectful communication at all times, and offering paid repairs outside the platform is strictly prohibited. 

 

Charter breaches and non-compliance with any of the points mentioned above could result in account restrictions or closure.


Customer Care by Back Market (CCBM)

Customer Care by Back Market combines customer service, returns logistics, and packaging options to create a complete after-sales experience management solution. It aims to provide a consistent and trustworthy experience for all customers and sellers. 

Learn more about Customer Care by Back Market

One of the biggest challenges for new sellers can be return management. 

Learn more about returns
 

 


How do I complete this module?

To complete this module, please answer this 3-minute form. If you pass, your sales limit will automatically increase from 3 to 4 sales per day.

To continue with the next training, click here Finance and Legal.
Title: #3 - Everything you need to know about quality during the Launch Phase

Details__c:
In this guide, we will briefly summarize how legal guaranties, commercial warranties and the right of withdrawal are generally working. Please refer to this article if you're facing an issue on a specific case related to a warranty exclusion.

TABLE OF CONTENT

 

  1. Legal guarantees
  2. Sellers’ Commercial Warranties
  3. Exclusions
  4. Right of withdrawal & damage related to transportation

 

1. Legal guarantees

In the EU

There are 2 types of legal guarantees in the EU: the legal guarantee of conformity & the legal guarantee of hidden defects.

 

  • The legal guarantee of conformity

Under the legal guarantee of conformity, the product must match its expected use and description. This guarantee applies in all EU countries for all customers. It is mandatory, so the seller cannot opt out of it. It’s free and automatic.

It starts on the day the item is delivered, and its duration varies by country (see table attached). It is divided into 2 periods :

 

1st period: Presumption of non-conformity

During this period, the burden of proof is reversed in the customer's favour.. The customer does not have to prove that the item was defective at the time of delivery; this is presumed by law. The seller may, in principle, rebut this legal presumption. If the seller considers the guarantee does not apply, the seller must prove it.

 

2nd period: The burden of proof is on the customer = no more presumption

The customer must demonstrate (e.g., with an expert report) that the defect already existed when the product was delivered.

 

  • The legal guarantee of hidden defect

This guarantee applies when a defect is hidden and renders the product unfit for the use for which it was purchased.

 

It does not exist in every EU country (only in France, Belgium, Spain, Italy and the Netherlands)and its duration and conditions varies by country.


In the UK

In the United Kingdom, the Consumer Rights Act 2015 (CRA 2015) provides similar protections to EU legal guarantees. 

 

Under the CRA 2015, all goods supplied to consumers must be of satisfactory quality, fit for purpose, and as described. These rights apply automatically and cannot be excluded or restricted by the seller. 

 

The CRA 2015 provides a tiered remedy system:

 

  • Within 30 days of delivery: the customer has a short-term right to reject the goods and receive a full refund.
  • From 30 days to 6 months: the customer must give the seller one opportunity to repair or replace the goods before claiming a refund. During this period, there is a presumption that the fault existed at delivery - the seller must prove otherwise.
  • After 6 months: the burden of proof shifts to the customer, who must demonstrate that the product was faulty at the time of delivery. 

Customers have up to six years (five years in Scotland) to bring a claim for faulty goods.

 

2. Sellers’ Commercial Warranties

All items sold on Back Market must be covered by seller commercial warranties, which must last at least 12 months after delivery (except in Portugal & Spain, where the minimum duration is 24 months). Sellers may choose to offer a longer commercial warranty. Accessories and batteries are covered for the same period as the main item.

 

The seller’s commercial warranty is supplementary to legal guarantees

 

This seller's commercial warranty is free, and offers better terms than the legal guarantee. Under the commercial warranty, Buyers: 

 

  • Benefit from a presumption of non-conformity: any defects that appear during the warranty period are assumed to have existed at the time of the delivery, unless the Seller can prove otherwise;
  • Receive a responses within 24 hours (excluding weekends and public holidays); 
  • Receive a return label for returning the defective product, free of charge within 1 business day once they have completed the form available via their customer account. The shipping cost is borne by the Seller; 
  • Are offered, as quickly as possible and in any case within 3 business days from receipt of the Product(s), a repair or replacement solution. In case of replacement or repair, the Seller pays the cost of shipping the replaced or repaired product to the buyer. For logistical reasons, for large or heavy products (i.e. products weighing more than 30 kg or with dimensions of more than 1,5 m) and electric bikes, the Seller has 30 days from the receipt of the product(s) to offer a replacement or repair solution. A reimbursement is offered only when neither repair nor replacement is possible. In such a case, the buyer may return the product and receive a total reimbursement, or keep the product and receive a partial reimbursement.  

Note: If your customer opens a technical service request within 6 months of receiving their item and the item’s battery health is below 80%, you must replace the battery on top of any other necessary repairs.

 

In addition, if the battery of the returned product swells or overheats, the seller's commercial warranty applies, even when the battery issue leads to broken screens. You must replace the battery and repair any resulting damage.

 

3. Exclusions

Exclusions are events for which the Seller does not provide coverage. In such cases, legal guarantees and commercial warranties won't apply. Examples: theft, oxidation, accidental breakage, buyer’s negligence, and abnormal usage of the product.

 

Please note that the burden of proof lies with you, and the warranty exclusion must be proven by you.

If your customer disputes the exclusion for example, by claiming that it was not properly explained or documented - or if there is any doubt about the customer's responsibility for the exclusion, you must process the return as if no warranty exclusion applied.

 

4. Right of withdrawal & damage related to transportation

  • Right of Withdrawal

The right of withdrawal allows customers to change their minds and request the return and refund of their product (> the only remedy is a refund).

 

At Back Market, the right of withdrawal may be exercised within 30 days of delivery of the product; this is called the withdrawal period. When a customer exercises their right of withdrawal, they must return the product within 14 days of their decision to withdraw.

 

NOTE: Exceptions for end-of-year period 

 

The withdrawal period is extended during the year-end holiday season: all orders placed from the Monday of the week of Black Friday are covered by a right of withdrawal until the 31st of January (inclusive).

 

  • Transport related damage

In the event of damage related to transportation (e.g. package is damaged or has been opened, or the product is missing), the customer must report the damage within 15 business days of delivery.
Title: Getting to know legal guarantees and commercial warranties & withdrawal right

Details__c:
An integrator is a third-party tool that connects your systems to Back Market. It can help you automate listings, order management, stock updates, shipping flows, and, in some cases, warehouse or ERP processes.

 

If you sell at scale, or across several channels, an integrator can help reduce manual work and improve operational accuracy.

 

What an integrator can help with

Depending on the provider, an integrator can help you:

 

  • Publish and update listings
  • Sync prices and quantities
  • Collect and manage orders
  • Send tracking and IMEI information
  • Support invoicing and returns
  • Connect warehouse, ERP, or shipping tools

Not all integrators offer the same features, so it is important to choose one based on your business needs.

 

Main types of integrators

Back Market integrators usually fall into a few main categories:

 

  • Feed and order management, for listings, prices, quantities, and order workflows
  • Feed, order, and warehouse management, for stock control and warehouse operations
  • Feed, order management, and ERP, for broader business management such as accounting, procurement, reporting, or CRM
  • Fulfillment and logistics, for storage, picking, packing, shipping, and returns
  • Shipping automation, for label generation, shipping provider selection, and tracking
  • Specialized tools, for specific needs such as helpdesk aggregation or returns management

How to choose the right integrator

Start by identifying what your business actually needs.

 

If you mainly want to manage inventory and prices across different marketplaces, a lighter integration may be enough. If you need more advanced workflows, such as invoice uploads after shipping, IMEI tracking, or shipping automation, you may need a more specialized provider.

 

When comparing options, focus on the features that matter most to your business, such as:

 

  • Quantity and price sync
  • IMEI support
  • Invoice upload
  • Return label automation
  • Shipping provider integration
  • BackPricer or repricing support
  • Level of support offered by the provider

Need the full comparison?

For a detailed view of features supported by each provider, check the integrator comparison table right here:

 

What to do if you have an issue

If you use a third-party marketplace integrator, contact the integrator's support team first. They maintain the integration and are usually best placed to investigate connection or workflow issues.

 

If you use a direct API integration, or another integration supported directly by Back Market, you can contact us through the Seller Support Center by selecting Submit new request.
Title: Understanding integrators on Back Market

Details__c:
We've updated our Trade-in grading guidelines to improve clarity between customer declarations and refurbisher expectations, and to better standardize counteroffer practices.

 

The complete guidelines are attached to this article. They include:

 

  • Clear grading definitions and rules
  • Visual examples
  • Tips to decrease the Counteroffer Rate and increase the Counteroffer Acceptance Rate
  • Definition of non-spottable issues

Grade definitions for refurbishers

 

Grade numberGrade nameCosmetic condition for refurbishersFunctional condition for refurbishers
0Functional - FlawlessA few faint signs of wear, not noticeable from 8 inches awayAll features are functional with non-spottable issues
1Functional - Very goodSigns of wear, including deep scratches and/or dents
2Functional - Used
3Functional - CrackedOne or more cracks and the screen can have defective pixels, ghost touch, screen burn-in, dead pixels, touch issues
4Not functional - Broken parts with scratchesSigns of wear, including deep scratches and/or dents on the outside of the deviceNot 100% Functional
5Not functional - Broken parts with cracksOne or more cracks and the screen can have defective pixels, ghost touch, screen burn-in, dead pixels, touch issues

 

Key Updates & information

Different Grade Definitions

Important: There are now different grading definitions for customers and refurbishers.

Most customers aren't grading experts and are likely to overestimate their device's condition. We have therefore differentiated customers’ and refurbishers’ grade definitions for you to have more realistic expectations.

This includes:

 

  • When customers declare a device “Functional - Flawless”, it can have a few faint signs of wear
  • When customers declare a device “Functional - Very good”, it can have deep scratches, like a “Functional - Used” device.
  • When customers declare "All features functional," refurbishers should expect "All features functional with non-spottable issues"

Note: We are keeping stricter definitions for customers to maximize the quality of the phones you will receive on each grade.

Counteroffer Restrictions

Important: This comes as a consequence of the updated Grade Definitions

You CANNOT make a counteroffer in these situations:

 

  • Grade 0 (Functional - Flawless): Devices received with a few faint signs of wear
  • Grade 1 (Functional - Very Good): Devices with signs of wear or deep scratches
  • Non-spottable issues (weak vibration, muffled audio, false contacts, etc.)
  • Battery condition, unofficial parts, non-EU products, or locked devices
Title: Grading guidelines for Trade-in

Details__c:
What’s the Onboarding Phase? 

The Onboarding Phase is the first step for sellers on Back Market.

 

 

It involves preparing your account to start selling on the platform, including setting up your legal documents, payment methods, listings, and shipping methods. 

 

Once you achieve 100% completion during setup, please send an email to activation-eu@backmarket.com and  the Activation Team will review it. 

 

If everything is in order, your account will proceed to the legal and risk assessment (also known as KYB), once completed and your account confirmed, you will be ready to go live and into the Launch Phase.

 

Please note that after 30 days in Launch Phase, there will be a final evaluation of your performance, where you can succeed or fail the Launch Phase.

Learn more about how your account will be evaluated. 

 

In this section, you’ll find all the necessary information you need to get your account to 100% as quickly and accurately as possible.

 

This is what we call the Back Office, and this is how your account will look in the Onboarding Phase. 

 

  

There are 10 steps to complete to reach 100% account completion.

 

To learn more about how to set up your Back Office account, go to the next article here. 

 

REMINDER: 

Your listings and your account will appear as “Offline” until your account will be validated by the Activation team.

 

Once you have completed all the sections of the onboarding, please send an email to activation-eu@backmarket.com

 

Need help? Don't hesitate to reach out to the Back Market Team at activation-eu@backmarket.com
Title: #1 - Onboarding Phase presentation

Details__c:
Jump to:

 

  1. What is the Visibility Boost? 
  2. How would my business benefit from applying the Visibility Boost? 
  3. Where can I find the Visibility Boost? 
  4. How does the visibility work? 

What is Visibility Boost?

The Visibility Boost is a new marketing tool that offers you extra visibility on Back Market & on Google Shopping when you implement price drops. It’s available across select listings to ensure maximum visibility and traffic.

How would my business benefit from Visibility Boost?

By opting in, your eligible listings will appear across:

 

  • The Great Deals page
  • The Product page
  • Google Shopping
  • The Back Market app

A specific “price drop” tag will appear on your products on the customer-facing page. This increased exposure helps you drive more traffic and ultimately, more sales.

 

Where to find it?

You can find the Visibility Boost on your Listings page, in the pricing strategy column. The tool suggests a discounted price. If you decide to apply that price, you’ll benefit from the Visibility Boost.

 

It will only appear for eligible listings and be visible across approximately 5-10% of listings. Eligibility is intentionally limited to ensure strong and high-quality placement.

 

Please note: As a seller on Back Market, you are always free to set your own prices. The Visibility Boost is entirely optional and non-binding. 

 

How does it work?

  1. Go to your Back Office → Listings page
  2. Look for the pricing strategy column
  3. Select an eligible listing and apply the Visibility Boost

The beta is live now; feel free to try it on an eligible listing and monitor the impact on your sales.

 

 

Updates: 

 

The eligible listings are updated every Monday.
Title: How to boost your visibility with Visibility Boost

Details__c:
A Platform Adjustment is a smart feature powered by Back Market’s artificial intelligence tool. It allows us to optimize marketplace effectiveness by applying targeted fees to the prices you set, while continuing to deliver exceptional service and powerful marketing that continues to lift the refurbished category.

 

This balanced strategy enables us to better respond to demand, drive overall sales, and support broader opportunities like deals.

 

How the Platform Adjustment feature improves your business on Back Market: 

Key benefits that directly support your business:

  1. No increase to seller commission fees: the  Platform Adjustment feature allows Back Market to maintain premium marketplace services without raising your commission rate.
  2. Reinvestment in your growth: Additional revenue generated will be directly reinvested into initiatives that drive your sales, including commission-reduction deals and volume bonuses.

What you need to know about the Platform Adjustment feature in your day-to-day workflow:

  • There are no changes to your daily workflow. List your products at your desired prices as usual (via the Back Office or API).
  • Issue each customer a receipt for the total order price (this includes your sales price + the Platform Adjustment amount), which can be found on the “Orders page” and on the after-sales platform. 

Key details:

  • Our AI-powered engine automatically applies the Platform Adjustment in the background based on external market data.
  • The Platform Adjustment is applied selectively on a case-by-case basis to specific products and timeframes. It will not be systematically applied to all of your listings. It won’t appear on your Listings page or API. The final price customers pay will always be visible on the “Orders page” and on the after-sales platform.
  • We’ll only charge you a commission fee based on your listing price, not the total price including Platform Adjustment.

Who’s paying for it? 

A Platform Adjustment increase is reflected in the final price paid by the customer on behalf of the seller. It remains margin-neutral for sellers in the vast majority of cases. 

The Platform Adjustment will vary depending on several factors, and we’re unable to provide visibility or estimates in advance. All adjustments will be clearly visible on your invoice.

 

What to know about your invoice:

  • You’ll invoice customers for the total price, including any Platform Adjustment, which you can find on the “Orders” page.
  • On your Back Market invoice, Platform Adjustment will appear as 2 separate lines that balance to 0:
    • Platform Adjustment amount / Frais dynamiques
    • Platform Adjustment fee / Remboursement des frais dynamiques

 

For more information, check out this article, which breaks down your bills step by step.

 

Please note that our updated General Terms and Conditions went into effect on November 15, 2025. If you have any questions, please reach out to the SSC.
Title: What is a Platform Adjustment on Back Market?

Details__c:
Managing returns is a key part of customer service and can be painful for both sellers and customers when the process is unclear. This article will walk you through the return workflow in the Back Office. 
 

What does my customer see when returning an item?

Customers receive return labels in 1 of 2 ways:

 

  • At contact creation, for eligible customers (30-day cancellation period*, repair, replacement cover by the warranty)
  • Through a Back Market agent if a customer opens a service request 

*For orders delivered between the Monday before Black Friday and December 31, the cancellation period is extended until January 31 of the following year.

 

What will I see when my customer returns an item?

After your customer receives a return label, they’ll be guided through the return process, including  being asked to remove their personal account from the device, certify that the device is still covered by the warranty (no damage, no oxidation or open by third party) and packaging their item for shipping. 

As a seller, you’ll see you incoming return on your back office, customer care section. You will also have access to the customer first message describing the issue and the customer’s resolution choice ( repair, replace, refund).



Note that messages that are purely informational don't trigger a notification or open a service request and don't require a response from you. The service request will remain closed until the returned item has been delivered to your warehouse.

Also note that the recap message will only be sent if your customer sends additional questions or information to the agent or when your customer doesn’t receive an auto-prepaid label. If you need clarification, message the Back Market agent in the conversation.



When the item arrives at your return address, the service request will move into the “Item returns” section of the Back Office. Here, you’ll also be able to view your customer’s initial message and photos to understand the context and what your customer’s asking for.



Once the item arrives, you can use the buttons on the bottom of the customer service request to provide a solution for your customer. Each of the options below will trigger an automatic message to the agent and/or your customer to notify them of the action taken. 

You must use the preset buttons to declare any issues with returns by the deadline or the order can be automatically refunded. These features will allow you to manage all your returns via buttons instead of sending messages to agents. This will include product repair, product replacement, order refund, and notice of an issue with the return (such as product locked, product out of warranty, package damaged, etc.)

If your customer asked for a refund, you have 2 business days to issue the refund. In case of failure to process the return or declare an issue with the return (such as an account-locked or warranty exclusion) within the required deadline, Back Market will refund the customer on behalf of the seller as soon as the deadline has passed.

 

If your customer requests a repair or replacement, you have up to 3 business days from the receipt of the item to repair or replace it and upload the tracking number in the Back Office. The item must be shipped back to the customer no later than the end of the 4th business day ; the sooner, the better. For laptops and home appliances, you have up to 5 business days to complete the repair or replacement and upload the tracking number, and the item must be shipped the next business day at the latest.
Title: How do item returns work in Customer Care by Back Market?

Details__c:
What’s Trade-in?

Trade-in is a simple tool that allows refurbishers to source devices directly from Back Market customers. 
 

How does it work?

  1. Create listings in the Trade-in section of your Back Office. Choose the items you want and the price you’ll pay for each grade (there are 4 functional and 2 non-functional grades).
  2. Customers assess their items on Back Market. We offer them the Trade-in BackBox price to the customer according to the corresponding grading of the item. Learn more about Trade-in Backbox here.
  3. If you offer the BackBox price and the customer accepts the offer, an order is created in your Back Office. 
  4. Back Market takes care of the logistics. You'll receive the items at the address you provided.
  5. Once received, you audit the items and trigger the payment on the transaction from your account. 

Why should you take advantage of Trade-in?

1. Wide range of items:

 

5 categories of products: 

 

  • Smartphones 
  • Tablets 
  • Consoles 
  • Audio
  • MacBooks

2. Significant business opportunity:

 

  • High volumes: There are 100,000 items traded-in in the EU & UK and 40,000 items traded-in in the US per month
  • Strong growth: + 40% per year
  • Margin VAT eligible
  • You can choose your payment method by default: Direct debit (through SEPA in the EU, BACS in the UK) or credit card

3. Easy to manage:

 

  • Same Back Office, same Back Market contact
  • Back Market handles the logistics part and the logistics fees are fixed
  • Trade-in is subscription-free and commitment-free
  • Logistics costs and commission are paid by the seller only if the order is completed

4. Fees

 

ActivityCompensationDetailsAmount
TRADE-INCommission on Trade-in (total amount paid by Refurbisher incl. taxes and shipping fees)10% (excl. tax) on all Products-

in France, Spain, and Germany		Smartphones and audio11.90 €

Main rule

1. Processing time:

 

In order to meet our customers' expectations, we ask you to respect the processing deadlines of orders received:

  • 24 hours to respond to the customer
  • 48 hours upon receiving an item to process the order


2. Eligibility:

 

You can use this service if:

 

  • You already sell on our platform
  • You have a physical location (e.g. warehouse) to handle your Trade-in operations in one of the following countries: France, Germany, Spain, Italy, Belgium, Netherlands, Poland & UK.

 

Request to join Trade-in here
Title: What's trade-in? And why should I use it?

Details__c:
KPIs are calculated based on items received in the last 30 days. You must be below the average of all Back Market sellers for each one. Here are the quality KPIs that are monitored on the Trade-in quality dashboard:
 

  • Late processing rate (LPR)

This KPI highlights the percentage of orders you did not process on time (i.e. within 48 hours of receiving it).
Calculation: Number of unprocessed orders after 48 hours / number of orders received (in the last 30 days prior to the observation date) that were received more than 2 days ago
 

  • Payment rate 

This KPI gives you a global vision of your quality by comparing the gross merchandise volume (GMV) you actually paid over the GMV promised. 
Calculation: Paid GMV/Promised GMV = the initial price for all orders received
 

  • Counteroffer rate

This KPI is related to the number of counteroffers given.
Calculation: Number of counteroffers/number of orders received in the last 30 days prior to the observation date
 

  • Cancellation rate

This KPI is related to the number of counteroffers that have been refused.
Calculation: Number of canceled orders/number of orders received in the last 30 days prior to the observation date
 

  • Late response rate

This KPI indicates the percentage of transactions where you didn’t respond within 24 hours.

This can include 1 or more of the following:

  • You didn’t complete an action such as confirming payment or any other action under “See more options”
  • You didn’t answer the Back Market customer directly in the chat

How do we calculate this? We divide the number of orders with at least 1 late response by the total number of orders that involved a discussion between you and the Back Market customer.
 

  • On-hold rate

This KPI highlights the percentage of orders that still have the status on-hold in your Back Office.

Calculation: Number of orders on-hold / number of orders received in the last 30 days prior to the observation date.

 

  • STAR rate

The Satisfaction Ticket After Resolution (STAR) rate is a KPI that measures customer satisfaction after Trade-in interactions.

STAR rate is then calculated by dividing the number of positive survey responses by the total number of survey responses in the last 30 days before the observation date.
Title: Our Trade-in quality performance indicators and how they work

Details__c:
Each of the following points is essential for a successful start on the platform and will help you answer the form at the end to increase your current daily sales limit from 4 to 6. 

Joining a new marketplace platform can be overwhelming. We want to help you onboard as easily as possible, so you can get your high-quality items to Back Market customers as quickly as possible. 

Here’s a roundup of all the articles you’ll need about payouts, payments, taxes, and legal requirements.
 

Invoice compliance

As a seller on Back Market, you have to provide invoices to your customers after their purchases.

For more information on how to create compliance invoices, check out our article "How to establish a B2C invoice that is compliant with the law?"

 

Seller invoices

Your payouts are issued on a weekly basis, every Wednesday. Each invoice covers the 7 days between the last payout date (D-8) and the previous one (D-14).
You’ll find more information here and in our step-by-step guide to understanding your invoices.

To find your invoices, log into your Back Office > “Money”

For more information about your wallet, check out this article
 

 

Price of Back Market’s service

Please refer to article “Financial Terms” of the Sellers Terms and Conditions to learn more about Back Market commission and applicable fees.
 


Regarding accessories, find out more information about the commission for accessories here.

If your customer changes their mind about an item during the 30-day cancellation period,  Back Market will automatically refund the commission. Check out this article about commission fee refund requirements here.

Regarding CCBM fees, find out more information about the CCBM fees here.


 

Banking information

Need to change the banking information you have on file? Read this article for more details

 

Blended payment fees

 Back Market partners with Oney, and Klarna to provide customers with installment payment plans. Similarly, other regular payment methods are available on the website (such as credit card, PayPal, Apple Pay, Google Pay, …), Back Market offers a wide payment ecosystem to maximize conversion, especially on your high-value items.
Starting March 31, 2026, a single blended payment fee of 1% will be applied to all orders, therefore replacing the previous scheme with variable and unpredictable fees by payment methods.

Read more about the blended payment fees here

 

Deferred payout

At least 95% of the total amount available in your Back Office wallet is guaranteed to pay out every week as long as you meet our quality  standards and maintain low volatility in your sales.

The deferred payout is a small percentage of your sales that is kept in your virtual wallet to ensure you meet your obligations towards your customers and Back Market (for instance to cover any issues that may happen while your items are still under warranty). Read more about the deferred payout here

Your deferred payout depends on the following factors:

  • Your volume of sales and trade-ins. You’re expected to avoid sudden decreases in sales
  • Your quality performance (number of claims & refund rate)
  • Your financial data


You can follow the evolution of your deferred payout on your invoices, every week, in your Back Office.
 


Read these articles for more information:

Where can I find the deferred payout amount?

What is my virtual wallet and where can I see the balance?

Your deferred payout can be partially released in your future payouts if your performance improves.
Read more about it here

 

Important note ! If you don’t validate the Launch Phase and your account is closed, the entire amount of your wallet is temporarily withheld as a deferred payout as long as you still have items under warranty, to cover for any future refunds that might occur with your products.

Read more about what happens when you stop selling on Back Market.  

 

Legal

Since you’ve signed our Terms & Conditions, you’re legally required to comply with them. You can access the most recent version through SSC. 

Find all our legal articles in this section 

 

EPR registration

If you sell in France, Germany, or Spain, you have to provide Back Market with your EPR registration numbers and pay fees to the Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs). Read more here:

Read more here.

 

Taxes

Find all our articles on taxes here (DAC-7, VAT guidelines, …)


How do I complete this module?

To complete this module, please answer this 3-minute form. If you pass, your sales limit will automatically increase from 4 to 6 sales per day.
 

To continue with the next training, click here Key success factors.
Title: #4 - New to Back Market? Read these articles on payouts, payments, taxes, and legal requirements
