Title: How much does it cost to sell on Back Market?

Details__c:
What legal documents do I need to provide?

Uploading your legal documents is one of the most important parts of the onboarding process since you will have to provide proof of your commercial activities.

 

Let’s go step by step: 

 

Step 1: Fill in your legal details.

In this part you’ll need to add the legal name of your company, and you’ll need to make sure that the name of your company matches the name in your company registration document.

 

You’ll also need to add your company registration number which may be completely different from your VAT number. 

You’ll find this number in your company registration document.

 

Here’s an example:

 

Step 2: Fill in your tax details.

You’ll need to indicate whether your company is located in the EU or not.

 

If your company is located in the EU:

 

  • Indicate which country you’re shipping from.

  • Add your company VAT number or indicate if you’re exempt from the VAT number

  • Specify which VAT scheme applies to the products you want to sell.

 

If your company is located outside of the EU:

 

  • Indicate which country you’re shipping from.

  • Add your VAT, TIN, EIN, or Tax ID number. If you’re exempt, you may leave the field empty, but in this case we’ll follow up to ask why. Reminder: If you are VAT exempt, please upload the UTR document or any other document proving that you are eligible for VAT exemption.

  • Indicate if you have a permanent establishment in the EU.

 

Now that you’ve filled in all of your info, select “Add tax information”. If you select “save” the task will show as “To do” instead of “Done”.

 

Step 3: Add your EPR documents.

What are these documents? Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is an environmental policy that makes the party who first places a product on the market subject to EPR requirements. This party is also known as the “producer”.

 

 

Germany

If you want to sell in Germany, an EPR registration is mandatory.

 

Here are the 3 numbers you’ll need:

 

  • Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE)
  • Packaging
  • Batteries


To get your numbers for Germany, go to these third parties:

 

  • For Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE), get your number here: https://www.stiftung-ear.de/en/home
  • For Packaging, get your number here: https://lucid.verpackungsregister.org/
  • For Batteries, get your number here: https://www.stiftung-ear.de/en/home

If you are exempt from WEEE or Batteries, please tell Back Market by ticking the exemption box. Please note you cannot be exempt from Packaging.

 

France

If you sell in France, you will need EPR registrations for:

 

  • Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE)
  • Packaging
  • Batteries

If you are exempt from WEEE or Batteries, please tell Back Market by ticking the exemption box. Please note you cannot be exempt from Packaging. 

 

But if you do not input your EPR registration numbers or, if applicable, your exemption status, Back Market will report EPR fees on your behalf and charge you for the associated fees in your invoices.

 

Spain

If you are a Spanish business, you must comply with Spain Packaging EPR regulations ; BM cannot collect on behalf of Spanish sellers. You may provide your EPR number in your Back Office but it’s not mandatory. 

 

If you are not a Spanish business, you must provide your EPR packaging registration number if you hold one. If you do not hold this registration, Back Market will report EPR packaging on your behalf and will charge the corresponding amounts to your account. 

 

UK 

Currently Back Market only authorities UK sellers to sell on the UK platform. UK sellers must comply with applicable EPR regulations and notably WEEE. If you are a UK seller, you may provide us with your EPR registration numbers in your Back Office but it’s not mandatory.

 

Please note Back Market is not allowed to collect and report UK EPR fees on your behalf.

 

Austria

Sellers active on the Austria platform must comply with applicable EPR regulations and notably WEEE. You may provide us with your EPR registration numbers in your Back Office but it’s not mandatory. Please note Back Market is not allowed to collect and report Austria EPR fees on your behalf. 

 

Learn more about EPR documents in France here  

 

Learn more about EPR documents in Germany here

 

If you need more information about EPR numbers, contact the Activation Team. 

 

Step 4: For EU sellers only: Add your country of tax residence. If you’re a seller from the UK, leave this section blank.

 

 

Choose your country from the countries displayed and add other permanent establishments (if you have any). Then add your Tax Identification Number (TIN). Remember that this is for EU sellers only.

 

 

Step 5: Upload your legal documents.

This part is the most important of the legal section. All your documents will be confirmed by our own internal team.

 

This is how the section looks before uploading the documents.

 

 

Here’s what we ask for:

 

  • Banking information 

Make sure that you upload a proper bank document. We won’t accept a screenshot from your smartphone, it has to be a document that clearly shows both the BIC and the IBAN numbers, and that are under the name of the company registering.

You’ll need to add the same IBAN and BIC in your payment methods so that the information in your Back Office and Hyperwallet (transfer methods) matches (we’ll see this in the payment section).

 

  • Memorandum and Articles of Association

The Memorandum of Association document sets out the company's fundamental characteristics and objectives. It includes details such as the company's name, registered office address, objectives or purposes for which the company is formed, and details of the initial shareholders or members. The memorandum defines the scope of the company's activities and serves as a constitutional document outlining the company's legal capacity and powers.

 

The Articles of Association are the internal rules and regulations governing the management and operation of the company. They detail how the company will be run, including procedures for shareholder meetings, appointment and powers of directors, issuance and transfer of shares, and distribution of profits. Articles of Association complement the Memorandum of Association by providing specific guidelines for the company's internal operations and administration.

These documents are usually created at the same time the company is created. In France, it’s known as “Status de la societé”.

 

  • Legal owner’s government-issued ID

You’ll need to upload a picture of the official ID (front and back) of one of the owners of the company. Make sure that the ID isn’t expired. An official ID can be a passport, driver’s license, national identity card, visa, or residence permit. Back Market will ask the ID owner to perform an ID check later in the process.

 

  • Certificate of Incorporation

This is a legal document that establishes the existence of a corporation or company. It includes important information about the company, such as its legal name, address, business purpose, ownership structure, and often sets out the rights and responsibilities of shareholders and directors.

 

This certificate is issued by the government agency responsible for registering companies in the jurisdiction where the company is incorporated.

 

You’ll need to make sure that your certificate of incorporation is not less than 3 months old.

 

 

REMINDER: You need to upload the complete document and not only the first page 

 

Here are examples of how your certificate of incorporation should look like depending on the country your company is based:

 

In France, this document is called the KBIS. (Note that freelancers or companies with no legal entity can provide an INSEE.)

 

 

In Spain, this document is called the Extracto de Registro Mercantil.

 

 

In Germany, this document is called the Auszug aus dem Handelsregister.

 

 

In the Netherlands, this document is called the KVK - Kamer van Koophandel.

 

In Belgium, this document is called the KBO/BCE.

 

 

In Italy, this document is called the Visura Camerale - “Visura Ordinaria Societa’ Di Capitale”.

 

In the UK, this document is called the certified copy of the company certificate from the Companies House. We can also ask for the confirmation statement CS01 for a more recent proof of business.

 

 

To learn more about how to set up your Back Office account, go to the next article.

 

REMINDER: 

Your listings and your account will appear as “Offline” until your account will be validated by the Activation team.

 

Once you have completed all the sections of the onboarding, please send an email to activation-eu@backmarket.com

 

Need help? Don't hesitate to reach out to the Back Market team at activation-eu@backmarket.com
Title: #2 - What legal documents do I need to provide?

Details__c:
BackPack is our sustainable packaging offer included in Customer Care by Back Market (CCBM).

After online checkout, the first thing your customers pay attention to is delivery, and more specifically, the unboxing experience. Packaging products in a way that matches or exceeds your customers’ expectations is important.
 
We know that finding the right packaging is challenging, and your time could be better spent focusing on product quality. So, we’ve done the work of finding the packaging for you.

Our boxes are made with recycled materials and are reusable for returns, replacements, and Trade-in purchases. Plus, with no additional packing material needed for shipping protection, you can save time, money, and the planet all at the same time.

 

Jump to:

 

  1. Type of BackPack & cost

Can't find what you're looking for?

 

Get in touch with us via backmarket@packhelp.com, we will be happy to help you.

 

Type of BackPack & Cost

How much does BackPack cost?

BackPack for smartphones and laptops is included in your CCBM fees. This includes shipping costs, too. 

All you have to do is place your order on the BackPack platform. More information about how to place an order, please click HERE.


Which products can be shipped in BackPack packaging?

You can use our packaging for smartphones and laptops. 

The smartphone box is suitable for all smartphone models and their accessories. 

The laptop box is suitable for all laptop and MacBook models from 13–16 inches and their accessories

 

Order Process

How do I order BackPack?

Order your packaging here

 

You’ll use your Back Office admin email address to log in, but your BackPack password is unique to this platform (it’s not your Back Office admin password).

If you forget your BackPack password, reset it by clicking “Forgot your password?”. 

 

Here’s the process step by step for first-time orders:

 

  • Step 1: Visit backpack.backmarket.com. Or access the platform directly from your Back Office "Options" > "Delivery".
  • Step 2: Use your Back Office admin email address to log in. 
  • Step 3: You’ve received a temporary password by email. Use it to log in.
  • Step 4: Reset the temporary password.
  • Step 5: Fill in your account details and shipping address.
  • Step 6: Choose the packaging you need and the quantity, and add the order to your shopping cart.
    • Note: Your packaging order will be limited based on your sales and is predicted to cover up to 3 months of sales.
  • Step 7: Confirm your shipping address and place the order. You’ll receive email confirmation and shipping details in the following days.

 

Is the packaging safe for shipping?

The BackPack packaging was rigorously tested by our Innovation Lab to withstand any potential shipping damage.

The packaging employed is specifically engineered to ensure product safety. This entails shipping smartphones exclusively in designated smartphone packaging and laptops solely in laptop packaging.

Minor external damage during transit won't affect the device, ensuring it arrives in pristine condition.

 

Shipment Information

How long will shipping take?

Once your order is confirmed, you’ll receive an order confirmation email. 

  • All orders placed before 4:00 PM on business days will be processed and prepared for shipment within 2 business days.
  • After the parcel is picked up, shipping will take 7–9 business days, depending on the shipment location.
  • The total process will take up 9 - 11 business days  

How can I track my order status?

Once your order has shipped, you’ll receive a tracking link and be able to view it directly in your BackPack account. 

You will go to “My Account” -> “Orders” on the packaging platform.

Or you can use Order number and track through the carrier website. 

 

Example:

 

  • Step 1: If your carrier is K&N, you can access: https://mykn.kuehne-nagel.com/public-tracking/
  • Step 2: Type your Order number in the search bar, and you will see the shipment details.

 

Assemble guideline

How do I assemble the Smartphone packaging?

It’s simple. Follow these assembly steps in the YouTube video here.

 

How do I assemble the Laptop packaging?

It’s simple. Follow these assembly steps in the YouTube video here.
Title: Everything you need to know about BackPack

Details__c:
What are the shipping requirements in Back Market ?

In order to create your shipping methods you first need to create your listings. Delivery options are key when purchasing online, and a better delivery experience for our customers means more sales for you. Rules are defined by size of products (size XS, S, M, L or XL), please find more details in the table below.

 

 

Included shipping fees are a standard of e-commerce, although you have the freedom to determine your own shipping costs. 

 

For a product listing to be published, you must offer a standard home shipping option included in the price of the product (i.e., free of charge for the customer). You may offer additional paid options alongside the mandatory free one, as long as you respect the price limits per category.

 

A maximum shipping time applies to the standard home shipping option. 

 

Scenario: Customer orders a smartphone on Monday at 11:00 AM (before 1 PM cut-off)

 

  • Standard delivery: You must ship the same day (Monday), and the customer receives it by Wednesday at the latest (≤ 2 business days transit).
  • Express delivery: You must ship the same day (Monday), and the customer receives it by Tuesday at the latest (≤ 1 business day transit), for a max fee of 5 €.

How is preparation time calculated?

Please note that:

 

  • The default cut-off time is 1:00 PM. You may update this setting in your Back-Office; however, it can only be set after 1:00 PM.

Here’s how shipping deadlines are calculated:

 

  1. The shipping process begins when your customer’s order is confirmed by Back Market and ends when the first attempt to deliver the product is made. 
  2. Shipping is advertised to your customers in business days, but as the Back Office operates in hours for maximum accuracy, shipping timeframes are rounded up to the next whole number.

Learn more about setting up your shipping methods

Learn more about recommended shipping providers

 

How to add new shipping methods ? 

Now that you know the requirements, add as many markets as possible to your shipping methods, click on the section ‘Options’ then ‘Shipping’ and you’ll be able to add your options for delivery in each country you intend to sell.

 

You’ll be able to choose the carrier for each category you are listing, if you don’t have listings in that category it won’t show as an option. 

 

 

Back Market provides sellers with shipping and packaging services, known as BackShip and BackPack.

 

Don't worry if you don't see them yet ! These services become available immediately after you complete the onboarding process and your account is validated.

 

REMINDER: 

Your listings and your account will appear as “Offline” until your account will be validated by the Activation team.

 

Once you have completed all the sections of the onboarding, please send an email to activation-eu@backmarket.com

 

Need help? Don't hesitate to reach out to the Back Market team at activation-eu@backmarket.com
Title: #5 - Everything you need to know about your shipping methods.

Details__c:
How to manage Trade-in orders from Back Market customers under 18

Trade-in is only available for Back Market customers 18 and older.

However, you may still receive a device from a younger Back Market customer who didn’t reveal their age at the time of the offer.

In this case, ask the underage Back Market customer to get their legal guardian involved to change the following details to their own:

  • Copy of their government-issued ID 
  • Date of birth
  • Bank details, including the IBAN and BIC

What to do with a device that shouldn’t have been sent to you

Sometimes Back Market customers sell 2 or more items simultaneously via Trade-in, and it’s possible for them to get their shipping labels mixed up. In a case like this, you might receive an item that was intended for another seller. 

If this happens to you, contact the Seller Support Center as soon as possible with the following information:

  • Order number
  • Relevant information about the item you received

Back Market will then send you a free shipping label to send the item to the correct seller.


How to manage a shipping issue with a Trade-in order

Trade-in orders are shipped with Back Market-provided shipping labels. This means that you won’t have to manage a shipping provider investigation if a shipping issue occurs.

However, to open an investigation on our end, we’ll need some documents from you and the Back Market customer.
 
See below for the documents we’ll need from you in different situations:

 

  1. For “FORWARD” deliveries (from the customer to your warehouse):

 

Type of shipping issue

Documents required to you

  1. For “RETURN” deliveries (from you to the customer, in case of a cancelled Trade-in)

 

Type of shipping issue

EU sellers: How to process non-EU orders

Starting June 13, 2022, the location of the Trade-in item is no longer a sufficient reason to refuse it or make a counteroffer (see 2.5.2 of the Trade-in charter).

As a result, you’re encouraged to process these orders as any other order.

However, there’s 1 exception: If you receive an item without the CE logo on 1 side as well as within the software. 

If this happens to you, follow these steps:

1. Process the order as any other order

If you counteroffer and the Back Market customer refuses it, please follow the normal item return process
If the customer accepts the offer, continue with these steps:

2. Verify that the item doesn’t have a CE logo on 1 side of the item and within the device’s software

For the software part, follow these steps depending on the brands:

  • Apple: Settings > General > Disclaimers & Certifications
  • Samsung: Use the model number to check directly on this website
  • Other: Settings > Device certification


3. Take photos of the sides of the item and take screenshots to prove the absence of CE markings within the software

4. Create a return slip and return the item to Back Market at the following address:

123 rue Lucien Faure
Back Market Service Retours
Bâtiment Cap Horn, Floor 1
33300 Bordeaux
 
5. Submit a support request for reimbursement in the Seller Support Center by providing the following:

  • Proof that no CE logo is present on the item or within the software
  • Order number
  • Tracking number with proof of postage


If all the elements provided are compliant, the Seller Support Centre will reimburse you for the price including tax of the item and compensation of 5 € for the return packing slip. This refund will be indicated on your upcoming invoice.
Title: How to deal with special Trade-in cases

Details__c:
How do I set up my payment methods?

First, fill out your personal information to confirm the identity of one of the company’s representatives. Add your name, date of birth, country of residence, phone number, and home address. Then select “confirm”.

 

 

Next step is to provide “proof of business registration”. Add your legal business name and DBA/trade name. A DBA (Doing Business As) or trade name is a name that a business uses for trading goods or services, which is different from its legal, registered name.

 

 

 

 

Next, enter your business registration number. This is a unique identifier assigned to your company by the government agency responsible for business registration in your country. It's used for legal, tax, and administrative purposes. The format and name of this number vary by country, so refer to the country where your company is registered.

 

Then, enter your business phone number and business address.

 

After that, you'll need to provide information about the company director(s) and beneficial owner(s). Enter your name, date of birth, country of residence, phone number, and home address, then select "Confirm." Under the beneficial owner(s) section, fill in the relevant details for each beneficial owner.

 

The last step is to submit the authorization letter of the company. Make sure it’s an official document.

 

Don’t forget to select “submit”: you’ll  need to click submit both after each stage and at the final stage “Submit.

 

 

You’ll be directed to another tab where you’ll need to add your business bank account.

 

N.B: Please, add always your business bank account (the IBAN stated in the official Bank document in the “legal details” section needs to be the same you will upload in HyperWallet) and not your personal one. 

 

Note that once we send your account to be confirmed by our legal team, it’s required that you have at least a bank account that matches the bank info document (BIC and IBAN) you submitted before in your Back Office.

 

Additionally, you can also set up your PayPal account ,which means you'll have 2 payment methods — the PayPal account and the bank account that corresponds with your legal documentation.

 

 

To learn more about how to set up your Back Office account, go to the next article.

 

REMINDER: 

Your listings and your account will appear as “Offline” until your account will be validated by the Activation team.

 

Once you have completed all the sections of the onboarding, please send an email to activation-eu@backmarket.com

 

Need help? Don't hesitate to reach out to the Back Market team at activation-eu@backmarket.com
Title: #3 - Adding my payment methods.

Details__c:
If a device is returned due to a change of mind (withdrawal) in a worse condition than when you shipped it, you can offer a partial refund. To do this:

 

  • Within 2 business days after you receive the product:
    • Use the preset button to report a problem.
    • Take clear, detailed photos of the damage.
    • Be transparent about how you calculate the deductions you’ll propose to the buyer:
      • Deduct the cost of the identified damage from the refund amount.
      • If the grade difference is attributable to the buyer, apply the reductions set out in the Quality Charter (art. 3.6.3).
      • If a repair is needed due to the buyer, apply the costs listed in the table in the “Declare and manage a warranty exclusion” article, based on the specific part to repair.
      • Make sure the deductions are reasonable and proportionate to the actual loss in value.
  • If degradation is confirmed, offer the buyer either a partial refund (with the deductions above) or to keep the product with no refund.

If a full refund was issued even though you followed the process above and degradation is confirmed, contact the Seller Support Center to request compensation:

 

  • Provide the photos showing the damage.
  • Explain the nature and extent of the damage.
  • State the loss in value:
    • For a grade difference, apply the reductions set out in the Quality Charter (art. 3.6.3).
    • For repairs, apply the costs listed in the table in the “Declare and manage a warranty exclusion” article, based on the specific part to repair.

The Seller Support Center will review your request and may issue a refund under the rules above if the conditions are met.
Title: Degraded products returned for a Change of Mind (withdrawal) - What can you do?

Details__c:
In this article, we’ll include all the information you need to set up BackShip correctly in your Back Office.  


What shipping providers are available in each country?


 
 

How do I add BackShip as my shipping method?


Make sure you’ve added your return address before setting up the shipping methods. Once that’s done, follow the instructions below:

1. Go to the “Options” section in your Back Office

 

2. Switch to the “Delivery” tab 

 


3. Scroll down and select the country you want to ship to

 


4. Enter the shipping information by choosing the Backship carrier you will use for the shipment.

 

Can I use BackShip for returns?


If your customer requests a return for a change of mind, technical defect, etc., go to the after-sales service conversation, click the "Generate a return label" button, which will generate a BackShip return label. This label needs to be attached to the return package and is automatically sent to the customer with the necessary instructions like packaging, drop-off point, etc. The package tracking URL is automatically loaded in your Back Office for both you and your customer to track.
 

How do I set up a regular pickup?


One of BackShip’s main benefits is that you can opt to have a regular pickup directly to your address in addition to the automatic pickup included on the service for some shipping providers.

Follow the instructions in this article

Details__c:
If a product’s battery swells or overheats, it falls under the seller’s contractual warranty. The battery must be replaced, and any resulting damage from an exploded battery should be repaired.

 

Important Note: You cannot ship a product with a swollen battery due to the risk of explosion. In cases where a swollen battery is suspected, customers will be directed to diagnose the issue at a local repair shop.

 

Procedure:

 

  1. Diagnosis and Repair:

    • Customers will be provided with a maximum repair budget 

      • Within the guidelines of the table at the end of the article

      • Below the price paid for the device.

    • If the repair cost is within the budget:

      • The customer proceeds with the repair.

      • The repair cost will be refunded to the customer upon submission of the repair invoice

      • The cost of repair will be invoiced to you

    • If the repair is not possible or its cost exceeds the budget:

      • The customer is asked to pay for the extraction of the swollen battery at the repair shop

      • The customer will be fully refunded for both the item and the extraction cost.

      • The device will be returned to you and you will be invoiced for the extraction cost (30€ maximum). 

 

Post-Repair Actions recap:

 

  • If the device can be repaired, you are responsible for covering the cost invoiced by the repair shop

  • If the device cannot be repaired, the product will be fully refunded and returned to you.


Conclusion: This process ensures customer safety and satisfaction while maintaining compliance with warranty obligations. Always prioritize safety when dealing with potentially hazardous battery issues.

Details__c:
This article covers the “Processing your first Trade-in orders” module of the Trade-in Launch Phase. To complete this section and get 5 Trade-in orders added to your daily limit, you need to your quality metrics below the thresholds 2 weeks after you generated your first 15 Trade-in orders:

 

  • Counteroffer Rate below 33%
  • Late Processing Rate below 15%
  • Late Response Rate below 15%

Learn more about Trade-in Launch Phase

 

Customer service management is as crucial for Trade-in as it is for sales. As a Back Market partner, you have to deliver a seamless experience to the customer when processing the order and answering requests.

Keep in mind that Trade-in isn’t included in Customer Care by Back Market, which means you’re the one chatting with and supporting Back Market customers.

Here are the golden rules you need to respect to succeed in Trade-in.

 

Keep a low number of counteroffers

Counteroffers are critical to customer satisfaction and require extra effort from you, which is why you need to avoid them whenever possible. It’s recommended that your counteroffer rate remains around 20% of received orders. To achieve this, adjust your quality checks to be less strict and overlook some small defects that have low-cost impacts for you based on your area of expertise (for example, microphone issues, minor screen scratches when none are expected, etc.).

Quality metric this impacts: Counteroffer Rate

How this rate is calculated: We count all the CO you made for the orders received in the last 30 days. We add an estimation of still-undeclared CO for your orders pending processing or pending a customer action by applying your raw CO rate* to them. We divide by the number of orders you received in the last 30 days.
The Counteroffer rate is calculated every day.

*raw CO rate is the old definition of the CO rate, computed as: Number of CO you made for the orders received in the last 30 days, divided by the number of orders you received in the last 30 days.

Where to track this rate: You can access it from your sales Back Office > “Insights” > “Trade-in”.

How to keep this rate low: Adjust your decision-making on counteroffers and listing pricing knowing that phones traded-in are never brand new and Back Market customers tend to overestimate their value. Also, regularly check your Counteroffer Rate. Get more advice here

What happens if this rate is too high:

 

  • The higher your counteroffer rate is, the higher the prices for sourcing will be for you. Indeed, since the launch of the Trade-in BackBox as of June 2025, you are rewarded for having a lower counteroffer rate than your competitors by being able to access the BackBox for a lower price. Learn more about the Trade-in BackBox.
  • Also, Learn about the monitoring period and limits here

Process all orders within 48 hours of receiving them

Processing an order means taking action on the order (confirming payment, making a counteroffer, asking for more information, or reporting the product unprocessable). This timeline excludes weekends and official holidays.

Quality metric this impacts: Late Processing Rate

How this rate is calculated: Number of Trade-in orders that were processed too late (after 48 hours) in the last 30 days divided by the number of Trade-in orders in the last 30 days. This rate is updated every day.

Where to track this rate: You can access it from your sales Back Office > “Insights” > “Trade-in”.

How to keep this rate low: Check your Back Office every day by going to "Trade-in" > "Orders" > "New orders". You'll also see a timer there showing how much time you have left to process each order. Find more advice here

What happens if this rate is too high: If your Late Processing Rate goes over 15%, you’ll enter a monitoring period. Learn about the monitoring period and limits here

 

Answer all customer requests within 24 hours

A valid response includes any action on the order (like the ones for processing the order) or an answer in the order chat. This timeline excludes weekends and official holidays.

Quality metric this impacts: Late Response Rate

How this rate is calculated: Number of Trade-in orders with a late seller response divided by all Trade-in orders with a customer message. This rate is updated every day based on all orders received in the last 30 days.

Where to track this rate: You can access it from your sales Back Office > “Insights” > “Trade-in”.

How to keep this rate low: Start with staying on top of your Back Office and check your “Orders to reply to” inbox several times a day. Find more advice here

What happens if this rate is too high: If your Late Response Rate goes over 15%, you’ll enter a monitoring period. Learn about the monitoring period and limits here

 

More best practices and keys to success

You’ll be off to a good start with these rules, but we strongly recommend you follow these practices too to ensure good quality.

Read the Back Market Quality Charter from beginning to end and respect it.
Start by reading the Trade-in part of the Quality Charter (starting from page 36)
And check out the summary in Part 2: Trade-in

If we notice a serious breach with our Quality Charter, supported by at least 3 cases, you’ll be immediately placed in a monitoring period. If you don’t respond, your daily Trade-in limit will decrease, and you might be removed from Trade-in.

Handle your counteroffers properly and keep your Counteroffer Acceptance Rate high.
If a Back Market customer rejects your counteroffer, it’s bad for both you and the customer. Also, less than 1% of Back Market customers are satisfied after rejecting a counteroffer. That’s why you need to avoid this situation.

To help you monitor this, your Counteroffer Acceptance Rate measures the percentage of orders with counteroffers that were accepted in the last 30 days.

To keep this rate high, here are some tips:

 

  • Explain the issue clearly and thoroughly to avoid questions and/or exchanges with the Back Market customer.
  • Justify each appearance issue identified with a photo, video, or test report for technical defects as the Back Market customer will expect this from you to accept your counteroffer.
  • Specify the repair cost for each issue identified, so the Back Market customer will understand the new amount.

Get more advice here

Anticipate holidays and vacation.
If you plan to go on vacation or be away from your business, make sure to pause your Trade-in listings at least 2 weeks before your departure, to have almost no new orders received while you’re offline. Here’s how to do it from your Back Office or with a CSV file upload

Remember all orders received during this time and messages from Back Market customers must continue to be managed, so make sure you have resources to handle them during this period before you go.

Ship back products from declined offers in less than 48 hours.
If your counteroffer is declined, you have to do the following in the next 48 hours:

 

  • Generate a shipping label.
  • Send the product back to the Back Market customer within 2 business days. 
  • Provide the Back Market customer with the tracking number in the order chat.

Note that you can request a refund for the cost of shipping a product back in the Seller Support Center.

Learn about the return process for Trade-in order cancellations

Take advantage of our quality processes.
Monitoring and last-chance periods

When 1 of your quality metrics goes over the limit, you’ll be placed in a monitoring period, giving you 28 days to work on getting the metric below the allowed limit. If your rate doesn’t improve enough, your daily Trade-in limit will decrease in a second phase called the last-chance period, where you’re at risk of leaving Trade-in.

Learn about the monitoring period and limits here

Proactive interventions
To improve the Trade-in experience, we sometimes help resolve painful situations by intervening in order chats (or via email). This may include reminding you to act on Trade-in orders. These interventions only apply to the most painful situations identified by Back Market.

Learn about these interventions here
Title: Getting started with first-class quality on Trade-in

Details__c:
Return processing deadlines

Return processing starts as soon as return tracking says the parcel has been delivered.

 

You must always comply with the return processing deadline, which is the time between the reception of the return and the implementation of the solution. This deadline is the same for all Sellers but varies according to the situation and product :

 

Situation leading to the return

Processing time 

Withdrawal of the customer 

2 business days to refund

Repair or exchange an item (not laptop or home appliance)

3 business days

Repair or exchange for Laptops, MacBooks and home appliances

5 business days

 

For all repairs or exchanges, the tracking number (do not input the tracking link in the field) must be filled by the deadline, and the return shipment must be picked up by the carrier no later than the following business day to be considered on-time.


For large items, your customer can choose between 3 options: a home-repair appointment, an appointment to pick up the defective item and deliver a replacement, or an appointment to pick up the defective item and receive a refund.

Details__c:
Good communication between your company and Back Market is key to building a successful Trade-in service. This means knowing where to look for information and how to raise a question or issue with us. It also means making sure you receive our communications.

 

Submitting a request to the SSC

If your question isn’t answered in our FAQs, get in touch with an expert at Back Market by filling out our contact form. Go to the contact form.

Check out these articles to learn more:

 

  • How to know if I have to contact my SSM or SSC?
  • How do I contact Back Market if I have a question?

Trade-in communications

There are three types of important emails you’ll receive from us regularly: general communications, order-related emails, and quality-related emails.


General communications

These include updates about your Back Office, market opportunities, notices about daily limit increases, and any general news we find valuable to share with you.

These communications are sent to your Main contact email appointed in your Back Office.


Order-related emails

These relate to the status of orders you’re currently processing (e.g. Orders, Counteroffers, Customer communications).

 

You’ll receive these emails in addition to the order information you can access directly from your Back Office in the “Orders” section.

 

It’s important that the relevant people receive and read these, so please setup the emails addresses which should receive the different Trade-in order emails through your Back Office (Business profile > Contact details > Additional contacts)
 

Quality-related emails

You’ll receive these when we need to report a quality issue (e.g. entering a monitoring period, the ending of a monitoring period, etc.). Learn more about how to achieve first class-quality (so you can avoid these emails)

 

These communications are sent to your historic Quality contact in your Back Office. Submit an SSC request  if you wish to update the email contacts for thisMain contact email appointed in your Back Office.
Title: Back Market communications for Trade-in

Details__c:
This article will help you understand your BackShip invoices. If you want to understand your general invoices, check out this article

Your general invoices are uploaded to your Back Office on the 6th, 16th, and 26th day of every month. Depending on the invoice, most will include 7 sections: 

  1. Summary of the invoice for that period
  2. Number of orders confirmed during that period
  3. Number of orders refunded during that period
  4. Total support service for each order during that period
  5. Number of goodwill gestures for that period
  6. Number of BackShip orders during that period
  7. Total adjustments for previous BackShip invoices


This article focuses on sections 6 and 7. 

You’re billed based on an estimation of BackShip transportation fees. We use our in-house data to best calculate this price.  The shipping fees appear in the 6th section of your bill (under the “DETAILS DES BACKSHIP SCANNÉS AVANT LE DD/MM/YYY” headline), just as shown in the image below:



It’s possible that the estimated price will not match the final price, and Back Market will adjust the price by crediting or debiting the difference on your next invoice. You’ll find the adjustments in section 7 of your invoice, under the banner “DÉTAILS DES AVOIRS SUR COMMISSIONS BACK MARKET” or “DÉTAILS DES RÉGULARISATIONS DE FRAIS DE SERVICE BACKSHIP.” 





The credit or debit will appear on a future invoice as the estimated price that was billed to you has to be compared to the actual cost of shipping. It usually appears the month after shipping. The adjustment can be found on the invoice with the order number. See the timeline below:

 

BackShip Billing Update


In order to further improve your BackShip experience, we’re changing our billing process to be more simple, transparent, and easy to manage. As mentioned in this article, you’re billed when you ship your package via BackShip. If the charges are over- or underestimated, an adjustment is made the following month. 

Starting in October, you’ll be charged the exact amount only after the exact price of your shipment is known. You’ll be charged on a monthly basis after all shipping is confirmed. Beginning in September, these changes will be piloted with DHL Express and in the future, other shipping providers will be brought onto the new invoicing process.

If you have any questions, don’t hesitate to reach out to your Back Market point of contact.
 

Frequently Asked Questions


How does Back Market measure and charge per package?

Packages are billed based on the shipping provider’s measurements
Most shipping providers use the highest measurement between the weight and the volumetric weight
 

How do I reconcile estimated shipping costs with the potential adjustments?

You can search your orders by order number. The order amount, the shipping cost in section 6, and any potential adjustment in section 7 will all show the order number.









Tip: Use the CTRL+F shortcut to reconcile the different credits and debits in your bill associated to the same order number.

Because the adjustment is only calculated after the first invoice, the shipping charge and its adjustment will appear on different invoices (usually 2 or 3 10-day periods or invoices apart). 


What can I expect to be adjusted?

The number of items to be adjusted depends on the seller and the type of item. Larger items often have greater variations in weight measurement. However, we’re consistently working on reducing adjustments.


How many adjustments per order should I expect?

There’s 1 adjustment per order at the most. 

In some exceptional cases, you may see a second adjustment if you have claimed the price of a package after it had been adjusted and Back Market still needs to reconcile the price due to a shipping provider bill error. 


What can I do to reduce my adjustment rate?

We recommend that you keep in mind that the packages sent are billed based on the weight measurement from the shipping providers.

You should pay attention to both the actual weight and the volumetric weight to be consistently billed the same amount and avoid adjustments. This is particularly true for large items. 

We ask that you avoid calling the shipping provider for unnecessary collections as these may be also billed. 


Where can I see the weight used to charge me for my package?

Shipping providers don’t share the weight of each package, meaning that Back Market isn’t able to show it on your BackShip invoices. But we’re working on getting this data to consistently show it on invoices.
Title: How to understand your BackShip invoices

Details__c:
Customers request receipts frequently, and you should promptly provide them. You must always provide them within 1 business day of shipping the items via the after-sales platform. Each receipt must also be in the language of your customer’s market. 

 

Your receipt must contain all the mandatory information as required by laws and regulations. For all smartphone sales, the IMEIs must be included in the receipts. For tablets, iPads, MacBooks, and laptops, the serial numbers must be included in the receipts.

 

What happens if I don't send an invoice to my customer?

If you don't send the invoice within 2 business days following Back Market's reminder, your customer is entitled to a free cancellation of their order or a goodwill gesture valued at up to €50/$50/£50.

Keep in mind that you’re legally required to provide an invoice up to 2 years after the purchase in France and Spain, up to 6 years in the UK, and up to 3 years in Germany.

Don’t display your phone number or email address on the invoice. Any communication between you and your customer must take place on Back Market’s platform and via an agent as noted in the general Terms and Conditions.

 

What should you do if a customers asks you to add their VAT number on the invoice?

As a B2C-only marketplace, all our customers are individuals and not taxable entities. Therefore, you don't need to issue B2B invoices. However, if a customer is acting as a taxable entity, they can request a return and refund.


In some countries, like Spain, the law requires the customer's tax ID (NIF) to be included on the invoice, even for non-taxable individuals. If a customer requests this, you must include it within 24 hours.

 

How do I send an invoice to my customer?

You can send the invoice as a PDF to your customer 4 different ways:

  • Via an integration (recommended)
  • Via the Back Office 
  • Via the email address associated with every order
  • NEW: Via the Orders page


For more information on sending invoices via an integration, check out the detailed documentation here.

To send an invoice via the Back Office, go to “Customer Care” > “Invoice requests” > “Send invoice.”




To send an invoice via email, start by finding the alternate email address associated with the order. To do this, go to the order details. Then, attach the invoice to an email and send it to the alternate email address provided. The invoice will automatically be added to your customer’s account, and they’ll be notified. Please attach only 1 PDF document per email.

If you have a management tool that generates invoices automatically, you can use the same process as with the alternate email address. 






To send an invoice proactively via the Orders page, select “Send invoice” to upload it directly. 


 

 

In the file here under, you will find a template for invoices as well as a template in case a customer asks for a proof of refund.

If you have any questions, please reach out to your Back Market point of contact or contact the Seller Support Center.
Title: How do I create an invoice for my customer?

Details__c:
Let's dive deeper by addressing your questions!

 

Warning: This article only addresses security payments related to sellers' sales activities on the marketplace. security payments may also apply to their trade-in activities, which are not covered in this article.

For more information on the trade-in security payment, please refer to the following article:

 

Learn more about Trade-in security payment.


Jump to:

 

  1. What is the security payment?
  2. What impact does security payment have on sellers?
  3. How is the amount of the security payment amount defined and how does it vary?
  4. What about Callouts?
  5. When will the security payment deposit be released after an exit?
  6. Where can I consult the amount of the security payment? 

 

What is the security payment?

To begin, let's define what security payment (also referred as “Deferred Payout”) means in the context of your partnership with Back Market.

As per Article 17 of the Sellers T&Cs, the security payment is a sum of money that sellers agree to be held in their  wallet, aiming at guaranteeing customers’ rights, in particular as a result of legal guarantees and commercial warranties, cancellation rights, etc. 

 

When sellers refund customers, Back Market processes the refund on their behalf and deducts the amounts from the seller’s wallet, as it appears on the invoices (consult the My invoice step by step article for more information). Therefore, sellers must have sufficient funds available to cover any issues that may arise with the products under warranty.

 

What impact does security payment have on sellers?

There are two key implications to consider:

 

  • Predictability: you can expect that at least 95% of a seller’s wallet balance is available at each payment due date, unless you're impacted by a Callout (see section 4 below for more details);
  • Cash Flow Improvement: The higher the product quality, the lower the amount held as security payment. This quality improvement frees up more funds to reinvest in new inventory, creating a virtuous circle for sellers' business and product quality.

 

How is the amount of the security payment amount defined and how does it vary?

The security payment amount is progressively adjusted for each seller, based on (i) the estimate of their future refunds, and (ii) their tier.

 

⇒ Estimated Future Refunds amount: This calculation relies on machine learning technology developed by Back Market to predict the amount that will be refunded to customers by a seller under legal and commercial warranties. It considers the following factors:

 

  • Sales and Trade-in performance and quantity: you are expected to sell in a steady way and avoid sudden drops in sales,
  • Quality performance(consult the How do quality KPIs work? article for more information),
  • Risk assessment: utilizing financial data obtained from independent databases,
  • Refunds rate:
    • the number and frequency of claims;
    • the quality of the Seller’s processing of requests and complaints, including the rate of refunds made in respect of complaints.
  • Amount of GMV under warranty:sum of prices of products with ongoing warranty.

 

⇒ Tiering: we apply a tiering to each seller : 

 

  • Tier 1: All sellers start at this tier. The security payment is set to cover 100% of the estimated future refunds amount, while ensuring that 95% of the wallet amount remains available to the seller at each payment due date (unless you're impacted by a Callout - see section 4 below);
  • Tier 2: After six months of activity with Back Market,if a seller achieves revenue exceeding €30,000, maintains a defective items rate below 7% and a refunded items rate below 12%, the security payment amount is progressively reduced. It will then cover 50% of the estimated future refunds amount, while ensuring that 95% of the wallet amount remains available to the seller at each payment due date (unless you're impacted by a Callout - see section 4 below);
  • Tier 3: After six months of activity with Back Market, if a seller achieves revenue exceeding €30,000, maintains a defective items rate below 5% and a refund rate below 10%, the security payment amount is further reduced. It will then cover  25% of the estimated future refunds, with 98% of the wallet amount remains available to the seller at each payment due date (unless you're impacted by a Callout - see section 4 below);
  • Tier 4: After two years of activity on Back Market, if a seller maintains a defective items rate below 5% and a refunded items rate below 10% over the last 6 months, and if they have achieved commissioned GMV of more than €500,000 per month over the last 3 months, then they may be exempt from security payment.

 

Tiers are reviewed and updated quarterly. 

 

What about Callouts?

A Callout triggers a reevaluation of the security payment to cover the assessed risk level. In practical terms, this means that following a Callout, the seller's wallet amount may be fully retained as security deposit. The security deposit may also be retained over several consecutive payment due dates to cover the risk level. 

 

It occurs if any of the following event happens:

 

  • Significant decrease in sales or in the number of orders fulfilled
  • Detection of atypical behaviors (such as high rate of declaration of OOW or of transportation issues)
  • Fraud detection
  • Counterfeit alert
  • Safety alert
  • Quality performance issues (see How do quality KPIs work?), including Defective rate 30 days >9%
  • Significant discrepancy between security payment amount and estimated future refunds after the Launch Phase
  • Exit / Seller Account is closed

 

If two Callouts occur within 30 days, the seller’s Tier may be downgraded to Tier 1.

 

Regarding the release of funds after an alert is lifted:

 

  • If a seller receives an alert but remains active on the marketplace, funds are retained as long as the risk persists. Once the alert is lifted (for example: fraud suspicion lifted, counterfeit report resolved followed by three months without new incident, return to acceptable service quality, etc.), the exceptional security payment is progressively released. This release is carried out at 10% of the total security payment amount at each payment due date, until the security payment amount returns to its usual level, in accordance with section 3 above. However, a seller who has received two alerts over a 30-day period may be downgraded to Tier 1 so that the release is then carried out within the limit of the estimated future refunds amount.
  • If a seller receives an alert when they are no longer active on the marketplace, the release conditions for the exceptional security payment are detailed in section 5 below.

 

When will the security payment deposit be released after an exit?

If a seller account is closed for any reason, payment due dates following the closure will be blocked as security deposit, up to a maximum amount of 1.5 times the estimated future refunds. This measure manages the increased risks of returns and warranty claims related to account closure.

 

For a period of six months following the closure of your account, during which most refunds and repairs occur, the security payment will be used to cover the costs of repairs, replacements, or refunds to customers.

After this six-month period, any remaining balance of the security payment that has not yet been used will be released in increments of 10%, with the full remaining amount being released 13 months after your account is closed (or 24 months for sales to buyers located in Spain and Portugal).

 

As an exception, if your security payment is 2,000€/£/$ or less, the incremental payment will not apply, and the balance will be refunded to you in full in a single payment 13 months after your account is closed (or 24 months if sales have been made to buyers located in Spain and Portugal).

 

Where can I consult the amount of the security payment? 

There are two ways of consulting your security payment amount:

 

  • Back Office: by going to the ‘Money’ section and then to the 'Your wallet' subsection. The security payment amount is displayed on the right side.
  • Invoice (line “Sub-Total current net financial deposit”) as for the below screenshot:


 

In case you are active in Trade-in and have exposure to Back Market, please note that the security payment amount might include a Trade-in security payment. Our team is working to ensure that the Trade-in security payment amount is visible separately from your security payment (Sales). This feature will be available as soon as possible.

 

Learn more about Trade-in security payment


For any questions regarding the security payment after your account is closed, you can contact legal-sellers@backmarket.com
Title: What's a security payment (Deferred Payout)?

Details__c:
Launched in 2021, BackFunds accelerates your payouts and powers your growth on Back Market. We started with Storfund, a long-standing finance leader and have now expanded the offer with two new partners: LinkLogis and Hero, giving you more ways to get paid, faster.

 

Since then, we haven’t stopped making improvements: payouts now come weekly instead of every ten days. But we know some of you need cash even sooner. 


That’s why, with BackFunds, you can replenish stock quicker, keep inventory moving and maximize your profits.

 

On average, sellers using BackFunds grow 45% faster each year. It’s not just a service, it’s a real revenue driver.

 

Want to know how much faster you can get paid with BackFunds, or what it costs? 
Check out this comprehensive article to get all the details!

 

 

Jump to

  1.  What you should know about BackFunds
  2.  How does it work?
  3.  Our BackFunds partners and their main features
  4.  What does it cost?
  5.  Can I apply for BackFunds?
  6.  BackFunds Toolkit: Key Resources
  7.  Get started with BackFunds!
    ​​​

 

1. What you should know about BackFunds

As of today, the service offered by BackFunds (with the support of our partners) follows an advance payment model based on net sales.

 

Let’s clarify this financial model by highlighting 2 important points:

 

  • Advance payment models based on net sales can often be offered without specific lending or banking authorizations, because they are structured as a purchase of receivables, NOT as a loan. This distinction enables many financial providers to offer these services with fewer regulatory constraints.
  • Both legally and commercially, the commission applied by the BackFunds provider is structured as a pure service fee, NOT as interest.
    • Regulation: if the cost were classified as interest, the service could be regulated as a loan, triggering lending license and disclosure requirements.
    • Accounting & Tax: commissions are generally treated differently from interest in financial statements.

 A few additional details that relate to BackFunds and all our partners:

 

  • The BackFunds partner charges a fixed commission of your net sales. The rate will be negotiated directly between you and the BackFunds provider after you sign up.
  • No minimum contract length: you can stop the service whenever it suits you (as long as you don’t owe any amount to the BackFunds partner).
  • Multi-currency support: receive, hold, and pay out funds in multiple currencies at a low FX rate.
  • Transparent tracking: easily access a dedicated interface from the BackFunds provider with full details about your payment advance.

 

 

2. How does it work?

In 5 steps, here’s the path to access the BackFunds service.

 

Step 1 
Just keep selling as you normally do on Back Market, and once you sign up for the service (see 7. Get started with BackFunds!) and give your approval, we’ll take care of sending your sales and payment data to the BackFunds provider.


Step 2
You can receive up to 80% of the net value of your sales at the end of every day! The net value is calculated from the items you’ve shipped, minus any refunds and the Back Market commission.


Just a heads up: the percentage of 80% is shown because it’s the most common financing granted on BackFunds but it can vary based on the risk assessment from the BackFunds partner.

 

Step 3
Obtain the remaining 20% of your net earnings during the weekly payout cycle managed by Back Market. From this amount, the BackFunds provider will deduct its service fee for the advance payment services. The rate (get to know more about it in 4. What does it cost?) is negotiated directly between you and the BackFunds provider after you sign up.


Step 4
Once the rate is agreed and the risk team at our BackFunds provider has validated your onboarding (they may request some documents), our partner will open a joint bank account to collect your payments. The BackFunds provider will keep us updated every step of the way!

 

Step 5
We’ll update your account with the new banking details so the BackFunds provider can be reimbursed. Your onboarding with BackFunds is now complete.

 

If you ever wish to leave the service, we’ll simply switch your default bank account back.

 

You’re now all set to start receiving daily payments!

 

 

3. Our BackFunds partners and their main features

Below, you can find a table outlining the main features of our partners:

**The advance percentage rate determines the portion of your net sales from the previous day (D-1) that you receive the next day.

 

Ready to start? Go to Get started with BackFunds! and reach out to our partners.

 

 

4. What does it cost?

The BackFunds partner charges a fixed commission of your net sales. The exact rate will be negotiated directly between you and the BackFunds provider after you sign up.

 

As shown in the table above (see last table row in Our BackFunds partners and their main features), we’ve put together a simulation tool so you can easily get an idea of what the service might cost you.

 

Below are the pricing grids with daily rates, so you can get an idea of the commission that may apply to your situation.

 

 

BackFunds powered by Storfund

 

No subscription, no setup fee: pay for what you receive and stop whenever you want.

 

Storfund charges a fixed fee — equivalent to 0.3% or less per day — on the running total of your advances (see Advance Rate within the table shown in 3. Our BackFunds partners and their main features). This is the total amount of advances you’ve received that haven’t yet been repaid by Back Market payouts.

 

Click here to watch how it works!

 

On average, BackFunds users pay between 1% and 2% of their net sales annually (Net sales = Gross sales minus Back Market fees and refunds).

 

Sync your store data with Storfund, and they’ll run a fee simulation with your sales data so you can see exactly what you’ll pay.

 

To get in touch with Storfund, go to 7. Get started with BackFunds! 

 

BackFunds powered by Hero

Note that the commission applied above is daily and is calculated on net sales after Back Market commissions, generated on day D-1.

 

In addition to the service fees, there is a monthly fee of €39 for the creation of a Hero Pro bank account to collect Marketplace payments. Get more details here.

 

Concrete example (for a 12-day average payment delay for an order)
€1,000 incl. VAT in sales on Back Market
€850 incl. VAT in net sales after Back Market commissions
80% (financing rate) x €850 = €680 advanced the next day
For a cost equal to 0.063% x 12 days x €850 = €6.40

 

To get in touch with Hero, go to 7. Get started with BackFunds! 

 

 

 

BackFunds powered by LinkLogis

 

The BackFunds’ fee with LinkLogis ranges from 1% to 0.6%, depending on the monthly GMV generated.


Note that the above commission is applied to the net sales generated on D-1.

 

To get in touch with LinkLogis, go to 7. Get started with BackFunds! 

 

 

 

 

5. Can I apply for BackFunds?

There are a few basic criteria you’ll need to meet in order to use BackFunds. You must:

 

  • Be a registered company, a limited liability partnership, or a similar incorporated entity;
  • Have at least six months of business history on Back Market;
  • Achieve a monthly turnover of €10,000/£35,000/$40,000 on Back Market (based on the previous month’s figures);
  • Demonstrate solid qualitative KPIs, such as a low defect rate* (below 12%) and no suspensions due to breaches of the Back Market’s Quality Chart.
  • Be registered in the US, EEA or UK.

 

If you meet these criteria, you’re eligible for BackFunds!

 

 

All that’s left is to contact the right BackFunds provider (see section 7. Get started with BackFunds!); please use the table in 3. Our BackFunds partners and their main features to guide your choice.


Please note that these criteria might evolve over time. If anything changes, Back Market will promptly update this article to keep you informed.


*Don’t forget, you can always check your own defect rate (for ‘All conditions’ products) directly in your Back Office: just head to the ‘Insights’ page and look under the ‘Quality’ section (see screenshot below).

 

 

6. BackFunds Toolkit: Key Resources

In this section, you'll find two key assets to help you better evaluate BackFunds and discover just how valuable it can be for your business.

 

  • BackFunds Deck : everything you need to know about BackFunds!
  • BackFunds Simulation Tool : get a clear, upbeat view of the service’s cost plan, monthly and yearly! coming soon!

 

 

7. Get started with BackFunds!

Reach out to our BackFunds partners ​​​​​​​by using the following links:

 

  • Get in touch with Storfund via the specific landing page
  • Connect with Hero through their dedicated landing page
  • Reach out to LinkLogis via your SSM or the SSC Form


For any questions regarding BackFunds, you can contact the BackFunds provider directly (see above links).
Title: BackFunds: Boost your cash flow now

Details__c:
How do I declare a warranty exclusion?

Your role

Watch out for this

⚠️ Frequent mistakes⚠️

1. Run tests that diagnose all components of the device 

🕑 Processing delay from the reception of the product: 

- 2 business days if the customer asked for a refund

- 3 business days if the customer asked for a repair or replacement

Do not attempt to diagnose the device before having received it as the information provided by customers before shipping back the device is often incomplete.

2. Declare the issue using the “Report a Problem” feature and attach photos and relevant test reports to your message

📸 Relevant evidence: 

- Ensure the proofs you provide are relevant to the issue declared 

- Document the warranty exclusion respecting the processing delay

- Providing a photo of a scratched screen is not a relevant proof if the customer declared that the phone switches on and off randomly. 

- Documenting the issue long after you sent you declared the warranty exclusion by message is also not correct. 

3. Ship back the device to the customer

🕑 Processing delay after having declared the warranty exclusion: 

- 2 business days

You are expected to ship back the device to the customer soon after having declared the warranty exclusion, without waiting for the feedback of the SSC. 

 

You MUST use the preset buttons to report any warranty exclusion issues. Any declaration done without using the preset buttons will not be accepted


What are the issues that classify as a warranty exclusion?

What if your warranty exclusion is contested? 

All warranty exclusions are checked by Back Market, if it appears that there’s a doubt regarding the legitimacy of the exclusion you’ve reported, the agent will ask you to proceed either with the repair or replacement within the next 2 business days or with immediate refund as if no warranty exclusion ever applied.

 

There are potentially 2 cases: 

 

  1. Your warranty exclusion declaration is not related to the defect claimed by the customer. For example, customers complain about an audio defect and at the same time the camera is damaged.
    If the customer does not want their device back, they will receive a partial refund calculated as follows = A (the initial value of the device) - B (the seller’s cost to repair the damaged part). The value of B is determined by a fixed grid established by the iLab, based on the specific model of the device. If the customer wants to keep the device, the seller will ship back the device to the customer after repairing only the defect claimed.

  2. There is uncertainty on the reasons for the warranty exclusion. In this case, the doubts shall benefit the customer and Back Market will refund the customer. Yet you can request the compensation via SSC and no later than 3 months following the OOW declaration. If there is any doubt regarding the authenticity of the device and no IMEI (or serial number) has been provided in the Back Office, Back Market will side with the customer.

 

 

 

If you have received a device that is blocked by a customer account, please remember that this does not constitute a warranty exclusion. You can find the steps to follow for this situation in this article.

If you have received the wrong product, this does not constitute a warranty exclusion. In order to not what to do in this situation, please see this article.
Title: Declare and manage a warranty exclusion

Details__c:
What you pay for each Trade-in order

Every time you complete a Trade-in order, the amount that will be debited from your account equals the amount you paid for the Trade-in item plus commission and logistics fees. This comes either after you select “Validate order” or after a counteroffer you have made is accepted by the customer.

Learn more about commission and logistics fees

Pro tips:

  • If your headquarters are located in France, a 20% VAT (Value-Added Tax) will apply on top of your commission and logistics fees for each order you pay.
  • For each Trade-in order you generate, you pay everything you owe Back Market for this order at the same moment you are paying customers.

 

How to access your Trade-in invoices

In your Back Office, go to “Sales” > “Money” > “Past invoices” > “Trade-in” to download any of your invoices in PDF format.

Pro tips:

  • You’ll receive a new Trade-in invoice on the first day of each month that will cover all Trade-in orders paid during the prior month.
    • For example, an invoice created on 1 April 2026, will display orders you paid between 1-31 March (inclusive).
    • An order is considered paid once it has been confirmed that the customer will be paid for the order. This usually happens a couple of days after you’ve selected “Validate order” or a couple of days after your counteroffer has been accepted.

 

How to read your Trade-in invoices

In your Trade-in invoice, you’ll find:

  • Total amount you paid to Back Market for your Trade-in orders for the month. This is “Total TTC en euros” (or GBP) and it includes:
    • Commission fees: “Commissions sur commandes déclarées payés du XXX au XXX inclus”.
    • Logistic fees: “Prestation logistique sur les colis reçus du XXX au XXX inclus”.
  • Total amount you paid to customers only for your Trade-in orders of the month. This is “Montant total payé pour les reprises, du XXX au XXX (inclus).”
    • This information is indicated on your invoice sheet but does not represent an amount Back Market invoiced you.
  • List of all Trade-in order IDs you paid during the past month. This can be found at the bottom of the invoice.
  • Invoice ID (also called bill_id) at the top: “Facture n°XXX”.


Pro tips:

  • Your Trade-in invoice is issued in French. We’re working on having it translated into English.
  • Value-Added Tax:
    • “Total HT” means amounts without tax. This is only what you have to pay if you’re not based in France.
    • If you’re based in France, you’ll see non-null VAT amounts “TVA”. They apply to commission and logistic fees and are summed up in the “TVA à 20%” row.
  • In case you failed to pay an order to a customer, the order will still show at the bottom of your invoice, and fees associated will be included in the amount invoiced. See “Chargebacks” below to understand what happens in such cases.

 

How can I see the Trade-in orders I paid in a given month?

There are two different ways to find this information:

  • If you just need the list of order IDs you paid during a month, download your Trade-in invoice for that month as indicated above and check the bottom of the invoice
  • If you need to have the details about the payments you made for each order of an invoice, we recommend you do the following. (Please bear with us as we’re working to improve this process!)
    • Go to your Trade-in Back Office > “Orders” > “All orders”.
    • Select “See more filters” and filter on all orders that were created from 3 months before the issuing date of the invoice. Select “Apply” > “Export in CSV”.
    • Open .csv file, filter on all orders, and use column “bill_id” to filter on orders taken into account in the corresponding invoice.
    • Amounts for each order here do NOT include commission and logistics fees but just the amount paid to the customer. Add those fees to each order to get to the full amount you paid during the month.

 

What if I have issues with an order to be paid?

Chargebacks

When you complete the payment of an order but the payment cannot be processed (e.g. insufficient funds, bank refused payment, etc.), Back Market pays the customer for you to maintain a good experience for them. This is what is called a “chargeback”.
As a result:

  • Debt is automatically generated for your account and can be refunded as follows:
    • Automatically: This debt is added to your next sales invoice and subtracted from the amount Back Market owes you. Your sales payout can then be negative.
    • Manually: You can pay by money transfer by using the Back Market IBAN provided on your sales invoice.
  • A fee of 7.5€ or £8 is also added to your next sales invoice for each order with a failed payment.
  • Your Trade-in service is provisionally turned offline in the country of the order.

 

Credit requests

A credit request is a way of making a goodwill gesture to a customer. A goodwill gesture means any gesture to compensate the customer. In our Trade-in program, it refers to a partial or full payment of an order to the customer that Back Market can make on your behalf.

You can track those credit requests from your Sales Back Office by going to “Money” > “Goodwill gestures”.

Learn about credit requests for Trade-in

 

How to get a refund for your orders where the counteroffer was declined?

When a counteroffer you make for an order is declined, you have to send the item back to the customer within 48 hours and post the tracking ID for the return in the order chat. You can ask for a refund of the costs to ship back the item in case you respected the process to do so.

Learn about how to get your shipping costs refunded and compensation per order
Title: Getting started with your Trade-in payments and invoices

Details__c:
Different ways of creating listings

In this section, you’ll upload the product listings available from your company. 

One listing equals one product or item that you wish to sell in Back Market.  

 

Note that you must provide a minimum of 5 listings, each with at least 1 product in stock. This requirement must be met for our Activation Team to proceed with activating your account.

 

You can create your listings in 3 different ways:

 

Manually from the Back Office: You can create new listings 1 at a time directly through the Back Office. This method is suitable for sellers with a smaller inventory or those who prefer to input product details individually for greater control. To do this, go to “Listings” > “Create new listing”.

 

Search the name of the product you want to sell:

 

 

 

Add the product or a similar product (color changes, capacity, etc.).

 

For more information on how to create listings visit here: Create my listing on Back Market's Back Office

 

In batches with a CSV file: For sellers with a larger inventory, creating listings in batches via a CSV file is a more efficient option. This method allows sellers to prepare a spreadsheet with all the necessary listing details and upload it to create multiple listings at once.

 

For more information on how to create listings with CSV file here: Create my listing with CSV files

 

Via API: This is the most automated method, suitable for sellers with technical capabilities or those using third-party software for inventory management. Listings can be created and updated programmatically through Back Market's API, facilitating real-time inventory synchronization and management.

 

Learn more about uploading listings via API here: Create my listing with API

 

To learn more about how to set up your Back Office account, go to the next article.

 

REMINDER: 

Your listings and your account will appear as “Offline” until your account will be validated by the Activation team.

 

Once you have completed all the sections of the onboarding, please send an email to activation-eu@backmarket.com

 

Need help? Don't hesitate to reach out to the Back Market team at activation-eu@backmarket.com
Title: #4 - How do I create listings?

Details__c:
Before you start

Prefer a visual approach? You can create and manage your listings directly through the Back Office, where you can easily fill in forms and see your changes in real time. This article is specifically for sellers who want to use API integration.

 

Creating Products via API

We highly recommend referring to our comprehensive API documentation for the latest information on creating listings: Back Market - API Guidelines.

 

Getting Started

To make API calls with our database, you will need to enter an access token in your header. If you want to know how you can get your token, you can read the article Get your Token.

 

You can create a listing with API on our marketplace by sending an HTTP POST request. The endpoint of the request is https://www.backmarket.fr/ws/listings.

 

Request Header

The header of your request should be (it's the same for all requests):

Content-type: application/json
Accept: application/json
Accept-Language: COUNTRY_CODE
Authorization: Basic YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN

 

Note that the listing will be activated in the country corresponding to the country code of your request. If you want to activate your listing in several countries, you have to make the same request for each country each time with a different country code.

 

Request Body

In the Body of your request, you must put the equivalent of the CSV file used to create listings.

 

There are mandatory fields and optional fields.

 

Mandatory fields for a listing:

 

  • the EAN or BACKMARKET_ID
  • the price
  • the quantity
  • the SKU
  • the state

For complete information on all fields (both mandatory and optional) to provide during listing creation, please refer to our API documentation: Back Market - API Guidelines

 

Example Request Body

Finally, the Body of your request should have this form:

{
    "encoding": "latin1",
    "delimiter": ";",
    "quotechar": "\"",
    "header": true,
    "catalog": "ean;category_id;title;description;brand;price_new;weight;image1;color;sim_lock;screen_size;model;storage;sku;quantity;price;state;\n8018417227790;2;iPhone 7 White 64 Go;This is the iphone 7 of state super shiny description;Apple;960;325;http://images.neteven.com/evenmarketplace/images_telephone/iphone7.jpg;White;SFR;4,7;iPhone 7;64;iphone7sku1;25;700;0;\n8018417227790;2;iPhone 7 White 64 Go;This is the iphone 7 of state bronze description;Apple;960;325;http://images.neteven.com/evenmarketplace/images_telephone/iphone7.jpg;White;SFR;4,7;iPhone 7;64;iphone7sku2;20;600;3;\n0190198067456;1;Old school phone title;This is an old school phone of state super shiny description;Nokia;310;325;http://images.neteven.com/evenmarketplace/images_telephone/nokia.jpg;White;SFR;2,2;Nokia 32xx;;nokia32xxsku1;25;200;0;\n0190198067456;1;Old school phone title;This is an old school phone of state bronze  description;Nokia;310;325;http://images.neteven.com/evenmarketplace/images_telephone/nokia.jpg;White;SFR;2,2;Nokia 32xx;;nokia32xxsku2;10;140;3;\n"
}
Title: Create my listing with API

No, unfortunately you can't do it by yourself. Remember that a product page is shared between all the sellers who put a listing on it, therefore you can't modify the attribute/title or description of an existing product.
