Details__c:
About your Trade-in address book

  • Your address book for Trade-in is accessible in the Sales part of your Back Office. This is where you’re able to create, edit, and delete specific rules for all your Trade-in orders.
  • UK ONLY – If you have several warehouses in the UK, you’ll be able to decide which one should be used for each item category.
  • EU ONLY – If you have warehouses in multiple EU countries, you’ll be able to decide which one should be used for each item category in each market.
  • If you don’t have a specific Trade-in address rule set up for your live markets and product categories, your default address will be used for trade-ins.

 

How to add a new address rule in your Back Office

  1. Go to your Back Office > “Options” > “Addresses”.
  2. Click on “Add a new rule”.
  3. Select the address you want to use.
  4. Select the process “Trade-In”.
  5. Select the market of the orders you want to target.
  6. Select the product categories you want to target. You can select specific categories or “All categories”. Note that selecting “All categories” will include all product categories you’re currently selling as well as future ones.


Once you’ve created your first address rule(s), they’ll be listed below your addresses. You’ll be able to amend or delete them anytime. 
Learn more about how to manage your address book
Title: How to set up your addresses for Trade-in orders?

Details__c:
PRICING

CCBM pricing is based on a per order-line-item model. CCBM costs will be issued every 10 days with your Back Market invoices, but the price-per-item will be fixed monthly (see indicator details below) for better financial planning. The price-per-item will include all costs — care, reverse logistics, packaging, etc. 

The CCBM pricing model was created with 3 seller-centric tenets in mind: 


1. Predictability: We’re giving you full transparency on all invoiced amounts — no hidden costs or fees! 
2. Simplicity: We’re making everything easier for you by consolidating multiple services and leveraging well-known and actionable indicators in the pricing grid
3. Engagement: We’re rewarding you for quality and sales volume 

The pricing is determined by the following 3 indicators:

1. Item category because weight impacts return shipping costs

  • Accessory, small, medium, and large item orders will be invoiced at different costs. The table below shows examples of each category, see the full list attached. Bulky items are excluded from crossborder returns, and will be invoiced like small items. Bulky items for France-France flows will be invoiced as large items beginning on November 5th. 

 

CategoryExamples
Accessory
(no automatic returns)		Smartphone Accessories, Earphones, MacBook chargers, Sim Card, Tablet Cases & Protections, etc. 
SmallSmartphones, Tablets, Apple Earphones, Cameras, Watches, etc.
MediumMacBook, Office laptops, Gaming computers, Fans, Video Game consoles, Chromebooks, etc. 
LargeMicrowaves, Air purifiers, Televisions, Desktops, PC monitors, Electric Bikes, Printers, etc. 
BulkyTelevisions, PC monitors, Electric Bikes, Ovens, Fridges, etc.



2. Quality (via the Long Term Contact Rate) to reward best-in-class sellers

  • The Long Term Contact Rate (LTCR) is an indicator used to estimate the number of customers’ requests in a given period. It is very closely related to a seller’s quality metrics. The LTCR is calculated as the proportion of contacts to orders within a 30-day period, see below: 

*After-sales tickets categorized with below reasons are excluded from LTCR computation:

 

  • Changed_Mind
  • Others
  • Delivery specifically related to PARCEL_RECOVERY_ISSUES, ADDRESS_ISSUE, and PICKUP_POINT_ISSUE

3. Volume to incentivize growth on Back Market and create a fair distribution

Each month, we will look at the LTCR on the last day of the previous month and the average sales revenue of the past 3 months to determine the options for price-per-item. The product category will then be used to define the final  price-per-item of each order-line-item. 

Below is the CCBM pricing grid, which was created using the above tenets and indicators. To read the grid, consider your average monthly GMV and average LTCR. For example, a EU-seller who makes more than 1 million € of GMV a month (on average over the last 3 months) and has an LTCR below 5% will be invoiced at (taxes excluded):

  • €0.99 for accessories
  • €1.99 for small products
  • €2.99 for medium products
  • €4.99 for large products

INVOICING

CCBM costs will be issued every 10 days with your Back Market invoice, and are effective every month starting the 6th of October, 2022. The CCBM price will be calculated using the seller's Long Term Contact Rate from the last day of the previous month and average sales revenue of the last three months. It is updated at the beginning of each month, and applicable for three invoicing periods until the 5th of the next month.

To monitor your costs, the Customer Care Insights Dashboard will display your Long Term Contact rate and tier. For more information, read the related article.
Title: CCBM Pricing & Invoicing

Details__c:
This article covers the “Generating your first Trade-in orders” module of the Trade-in Launch Phase. To complete this section and get 5 Trade-in orders added to your daily limit, you need to generate 15 orders. Learn more.


There are two different ways to manage listings from your Back Office:

By batch: This involves importing a .csv file. It’s recommended when you need to create, update, pause or reactivate listings for multiple models.

One by one: You can do this manually from the “Listings” tab of your Back Office. It’s recommended when you need to create or update listings for only a few models.
 

How to manage listings by batch with a CSV file

The most efficient way to create, update, pause, or reactivate multiple listings at once is by importing a .csv file in your Back Office. It only takes a few steps.

Here’s how to manage your listings with a CSV file.
 

Create listings in batches with a CSV file

Step 1. Download the catalogue file for your location (EU countries are now in 1 catalogue).

  • EU Trade-in catalogue
  • UK Trade-in catalogue

Step 2. Remove the products you don’t want to source.
Step 3. For each product you want to source, add a price (no need to add cents).
Step 4. Save your file in CSV format.
Step 5. Go to your Trade-in Back Office > “Listings” > “Import or export CSV file”. Select the delimiter used in your CSV file, and select your CSV file.
Step 6. Select the profile icon at the top right of the screen > “File library”. Check the status of your listing update. (Keep in mind it can take a few minutes for your listing update to appear in your File Library.)
 

Update your current listings in batches with a CSV file:

Step 1. Download this CSV import template and open it.
Step 2. Download your current listings from your Back Office by going to “Listings” > “Import or export CSV file” > “Export” > “Export file”. Go to the File Library and download the CSV file of your existing listings.
Step 3. Decide on new prices for your existing listings based on current prices of your listings and BackBox prices for them.
Step 4. After updating the price for each product you want to update, make sure these fields are filled in: 

  • Product_id
  • Listing_id
  • sku
  • grade_code: Based on Grade_code_table below*
  • market: en-es/de-de/fr-fr
  • price: No need to add cents here

Step 5. Copy and paste the previous fields in step 4 into the CSV template
Step 6. Save your CSV template file in format .csv and check with Notepad that you have the right delimiter.
Step 7. Go to your Trade-in Back Office > “Listings” > “Import or export CSV file”. Select the delimiter used in your CSV file, and select your CSV file.
Step 8. Select the profile icon at the top right of the screen, go to “File Library”, and check the status of your listing update. (Keep in mind it can take a few minutes for your listing update to appear in your File Library.)


Pause/reactivate listings in batches with CSV files

  • To pause listings in batches, set the listing prices to 0.00 €/£0.00 for any listings you’d like to pause.
  • To reactivate offline listings, simply update their prices using a CSV file. Your listings will automatically go live again, eliminating the need to activate them individually.

*grade_code table:
 

Gradegrade_code
Functional - Flawless FUNCTIONAL_FLAWLESS 
Functional - GoodFUNCTIONAL_GOOD
Functional - UsedFUNCTIONAL_USED
Functional - CrackedFUNCTIONAL_CRACKED
Not functional - UsedNOT_FUNCTIONAL_USED
Not functional - CrackedNOT_FUNCTIONAL_CRACKED


Learn more about Trade-in item grading here


How to manually create a new listing 

Create listings manually 

Step 1. Go to the Trade-in section of your Back Office > “Listings” > “Create listing”.
Step 2. Enter the name of the product you want to purchase and select “Search”.
Step 3. Find the exact product you’re looking to purchase with the help of filters.
Step 4. Choose the exact model you want and select “Buy product”.
Step 5. Select the market where you want to create the listing, enter the SKU (optional) and price for each grade you’re interested in, and select “Save & add new listing”.

Your listing will instantly go live!

Pro tips:

  • Before saving the listing, be careful to enter the exact price you’re willing to pay.
  • If you want to have BackBoxes, select “Win all BackBoxes” or “Get BackBox” to get the best offer for all grades or one specific grade.
  • The SKU field is optional and meant to help you do the matching of an item with the ID you use internally for it. You can set a different SKU for part or all of your listings, per item or item and grade.
  • You can also look for product IDs through our catalogs above, and use them to search for the items you want to add.

 

Update listings manually 

Step 1. Go to the Trade-in section of your Back Office > “Listings”.
Step 2. Find the item(s) you want to update by filtering by Model, Storage, Product ID, etc.
Step 3. Update the price of the item and the grade you want, and then select “Save”.

Your listing will update instantly!

Pro tips:

  • If your listing was previously offline, don’t forget to select “Turn online”.
  • To win the BackBox, just set your Price to win BackBox for a listing.

 

Take a listing online or offline manually

Step 1. Go to the Trade-in section of your Back Office > “Listings”.
Step 2. Filter for the product you want to update.
Step 3: Select “Turn online” to take a listing online. Or put price to 0.00 €/£0.00 to take the listing offline.

Pro tips:

  • If you want to take a listing back online, don’t forget to update the price if needed.

 

Why your Trade-in listings might appear offline 

If you have all your listings offline and can’t manage to turn any of them back online, it could be due to one of two reasons:

  • You have reached your Trade-in daily limit. When this happens, all your listings automatically go offline. They will be online again automatically at midnight.
To check it, you can go to your “Orders” dashboard and filter on your orders of the day to confirm you had orders for that day. You can also wait for the next day and check if your listings are online at the beginning of the day.
  • Your Trade-in service has been set offline. This can happen for quality or financial reasons. In both cases, you’ll be informed by email. In this case, you can’t generate new orders until your service is turned back on. Learn about requirements for getting back online

Still not sure why your service is set offline? Send us a request
 

Can't find a product in the Trade-in catalogue? 

You can request a new product here. If the product is approved, it should appear within 2 weeks maximum.
Title: Getting started with your listings

Details__c:
Here is a reminder of the important procedures to follow. This information will help you manage your transactions efficiently and resolve any problems quickly.

 

Tracking your refunds :

 

To check whether you have been reimbursed for an order, please go to your Back Office (BO), then to the ‘Money’ and ‘Seller Compensations’ sections. You can easily find all your refunds by using the order number. 

 

Reminder of the documents required to open a BackShip enquiry :

For forward delivery :

  • Lost/no tracking parcel :

If the tracking of your order shows no movement, we will need a stamped and dated proof of handover from the carrier to confirm that the item has been taken into their care. In the absence of proof of deposit (or if the carrier does not provide one), our team will contact the carrier to verify the presence of the package within their network. If no confirmation is given by the carrier, compensation will not be possible. 


Please note that the processing time for the case will naturally be extended due to this investigation.

 

For your returns:

  • Parcel lost on return :

If the tracking has moved and then stopped for 6 days, we will need the certificate of non-receipt completed by you ticking the case “The package is declared « lost » by the carrier.”

 

  • Parcel noted as delivered but never received :

We will need the completed certificate of non-receipt, as well as a police report if the proof of delivery is valid. This police report must include : product description, IMEI (if it's a phone), order number and tracking number.

 

Since September 2, 2024, we have a new procedure which stipulates that if a parcel is missing during a delivery with the carrier Chronopost, you must inform the deliverer, who will indicate this on the proof of delivery in the “reservations” box. Without this indication, we will not be able to proceed with a refund in the event of a missing parcel (in reference to the e-mail received on August 19, 2024).

 

  • Product received damaged :

Please provide photos of the packaging, the label on the parcel and the damaged product (with the damage clearly visible.)

 

  • Parcel received empty :

We will need the certificate of non-receipt, as well as photos of the packaging, the label and the inside of the parcel.

 

  • Packages that have not been tracked since the customer dropped them off :

We will need a completed certificate of non-receipt and proof of delivery from the customer.

 

!! Please note that for the certificate of non receipt, it is important to mention your postal address and not the customer's, as it is you who did not receive the product !!

 

Our quality charter specifies that you have 2 business days after receipt of a parcel indicated by tracking and confirmed by notification in the after-sales service to indicate that you have not received the parcel.


This rule applies to packages that have not been received, are damaged or are empty.
(Article 3.5.2 of our Quality Charter)

 

As for successful returns, the amount of the seller compensation for a delivery issue on a return shipment will correspond to the GMV of the product, minus the Back Market commission. For compensations related to "change mind" returns, the Back Market commission will be automatically refunded to you within a few weeks, without any additional action required on your part.
Title: Backship support guidelines

Details__c:
To help you easily check Back Market’s grading and testing requirements and train new employees on the best practices, we’ve created posters that you can download, print, and hang in multiple locations on your shop floor. 

Testing posters:
  • Smartphones and tablets
  • Computers
  • Apple earphones and watches

Grading posters
  • Smartphones and tablets
  • Laptops and MacBooks
 

Why is it important to use the Back Market quality posters?

These posters detail Quality Charter requirements for quick reference. By respecting the Quality Charter, you can tackle potential customer contacts before they even occur. Being proactive and ensuring our customers receive high-quality products has a lot of advantages:
  • Functional products increase customer trust in the refurbishing industry as a whole.
  • You’ll spend less time and money on customer service requests.
  • You’ll pay less overall on product returns and repairs.
Title: Quality requirement posters

Details__c:
Starting 1st April 2026, Back Market will apply a single blended payment fee of 1% to all orders, no matter how your customer pays. This replaces variable fees by payment method and makes your costs more predictable. This change is included in the updated General Terms and Conditions that took effect on March 30, 2026.

 

Back Market provides secure payment services through payment partners that operate under their own terms and conditions.

Available payment methods

What's changing

Before

  • Payment fees vary by payment method, which makes your monthly costs harder to predict.

  • Your invoices can show multiple payment-fee lines based on your customer's payment mix.

After (from 1st April 2026)

  • A single 1% payment fee applies to all orders, including PayPal and installment/buy now, pay later options.

  • Your Back Market invoice will show one consolidated payment-fee line item.

How the 1% fee is calculated

The 1% blended payment fee is calculated on the listing price for each sale.

Example: If your listing price is $200, the payment fee will be $2 (1% of $200).

What you need to do

You don't need to change anything. The updated General Terms and Conditions took effect on March 30, 2026. By continuing to use Back Market after that date, you agreed to these updates (including the new payment fee structure).

 

What you'll see on your invoices (from 15 April 2026)

  • One payment-fee line item

  • A 1% fee rate for all orders

FAQ

Where is this change documented?

It’s included in the updated General Terms and Conditions, effective March 30, 2026.

 

Why is Back Market making this change?

To make payment costs clearer and more predictable. Today, fees vary by payment method.

 

Does this apply to PayPal and installment/buy now, pay later payments?

Yes. The same 1% blended fee applies to all payment methods.

 

When does it start?

April 1st, 2026.

 

Do I need to take any action?

No. Only contact us if you want to refuse the updated Terms and Conditions.

 

Are payment services still provided through payment partners?

Yes. Back Market provides secure payment services through payment partners that operate under their own terms and conditions
Title: Blended payment fees: what is it about?

Details__c:
You will no longer have to ask Back Market customers to generate return shipping labels when:

 

  • The customer cannot upload their ID

  • The customer cannot disconnect their personal account (iCloud or Android)

What does this mean? 

If a customer is having trouble uploading their ID or disconnecting their personal account (iCloud/Google), this issue will be automatically forwarded to our Back Market agents to help the customer.

If the issue cannot be solved, Back Market agents will generate a return shipping label and will provide it to you in the chat. Then you’ll have to ship it within 2 business days.

 

We believe this will improve both your experience and customer experience.

Important:

  • You still have to provide return shipping labels in the chat for this process

  • Continue the current return process for rejected counteroffers
Title: Return process - Unresolved pending orders

Details__c:
API issues could be related to the failure of response when attempting to call a specific end point, or to wrong information received when calling one particular end point.

1. I receive a "Forbidden" or "Unauthorized" response

The issue could be related to a password change of your Back Office login details, as the token is directly related to this:

  • Password updated: The token is automatically modified (check our article “How to connect my token to Back Market's APIs or integrators ” for guidelines)
  • Password not updated: Your IP address may have been blocked by our firewall due to security reasons, and should therefore undergo a whitelisting process**. 


**In this case, please reach out to the SSC to send your IP address (you can easily find it by going to https://www.whatismyip.com/ ).

2. I have an issue with a specific end point

Our API Generics guide gives you instructions to help you integrate your database to our marketplace in order to manage your products, listings and orders on our platform.
If you are experiencing issues with a specific end point, please reach out to the SSC by providing:

  • The specific end point that is causing the issue
  • The result you get when calling the end point
  • The expected result (meaning, explain in your request what you are trying to achieve and why the result you are getting is not the correct one, in order to allow our team to propose an alternative solution)


The more complete the information you provide is, the more chances there are for our team to be able to handle your request in short delays.
Title: My API connection does not work anymore - What can I do?

Details__c:
Why is my invoice in French?

Invoices have to be issued in French due to legal reasons. But you can check out our article “My invoice step by step” to understand all the details. 
 

Why does my invoice amount differ from the amount in my Back Office wallet?

Your wallet in your Back Office is intended to give you a broad overview of your finances. If you want a more detailed report, you should refer to your invoices. 

Your Back Office wallet doesn’t take into account all the elements shown in your invoices, as the calculations are finalised on billing dates. 
 

When do I receive my payouts? 

Payouts are issued on the 6th, 16th, and 26th of each month. Each one covers the 10 days between the last payout date and the previous one. 

For example, on February 16, you’re invoiced for orders shipped between January 26 – February 6. 



Invoices are generated on the billing dates above and are available in PDF and CSV formats. You can find them in your Back Office under “Invoices” > “Past invoices”

 

Why did I not receive my payment?  

Some payments may take a little longer to process. Here's what to check if your payout hasn't arrived:

 

  • Wait up to 2 business days after the billing date, as slight delays can occur
  • Make sure your Hyperwallet account is fully set up and verified
  • Confirm that your bank details are correct and up to date in your Back Office

If you've checked all of the above and still haven't received your payment, please reach out to our Seller Support Center for further assistance.

 

Which orders are invoiced on each billing date?  

Pay attention to the dates under the columns titled “Start date (incl.)” and “End date (incl.).” You’ll be invoiced for the orders shipped during these dates.

Shipped orders means the orders were confirmed and marked as shipped in your Back Office.
 

Why can’t I find an order I should have been invoiced for?

You might assume that you’re getting paid for the latest shipped orders. But remember to check the following when looking for an order:

  • Check if your order was confirmed and marked as shipped in your Back Office
  • Check the shipped date

For example, if you confirmed an order on February 6 but shipped it on February 7, this order won’t appear on your invoice on February 16. It will show up on the following invoice issued on February 26.
Title: How does invoicing work at Back Market?

Details__c:
Overview

Back Market gives you the opportunity to have your parcels delivered to a Chronopost Collection Point, in addition to home delivery. This option is available exclusively through the BackShip service.


Prerequisites

Before activating this option, make sure you meet the following conditions:

 

  • You are subscribed to the BackShip service
  • Your orders are local orders (no cross-border shipping)
  • France: available now via Chronopost
  • United Kingdom: available from April 2025 for UK sellers

Why activate Collection point delivery?

Collection Point delivery offers concrete benefits for both you and your customers:

 

Benefit

Detail

💰 Lower cost

Up to 55% cheaper than home delivery (e.g. smartphones) → better margin for you

📦 Fewer failed deliveries

50% fewer failed deliveries compared to home delivery

🔒 Lower loss rate

Loss/non-receipt rate of 0.54% vs 0.93% for home delivery

🛒 Better conversion rate

Customers appreciate this option and convert more

 

How to activate Collection Point delivery?

Setup works exactly like any other BackShip delivery method - no additional configuration is required.

 

Follow this guide to add Collection Point as a delivery method: Set up BackShip delivery methods.

 

The customer experience

Once activated, Collection Point delivery appears as a delivery option on the Back Market website at checkout.

 

The customer:

 

  1. Selects the "Collection Point" option
  2. Chooses the nearest collection point to their registered address
  3. Completes their order as usual

When the parcel arrives at the collection point, the customer receives email and SMS notifications.

 

Size limitations

Not all parcels can be delivered to a collection point. Make sure your parcel meets the following requirements:

 

Criteria

Limit

Maximum weight

≤ 20 kg

Sum of all sides (2H + 2W)

≤ 250 cm

Maximum length

≤ 100 cm

 

⚠️ If these conditions are not met, the parcel will be refused by the collection point and will not be delivered.

 

What happens if the customer doesn't pick up their parcel?

The customer receives regular reminders to collect their parcel. Once the deadline below has passed, the parcel is returned to the sender (you):

 

Collection Point type

Storage period

Chronopost or La Poste agency

14 days

Chronopost partner collection point

8 days

 

Consequences for the seller:

  • If a customer service request was opened within the deadline, the order is refunded to the customer
  • The customer will need to place a new order if they still want the product
  • For any questions about your specific situation, open a ticket via the SSC

How does insurance work?

Insurance works the same way as for any BackShip shipment:

 

  • It is optional
  • All parcels are covered by "Ad valorem" insurance (up to the declared value of the product)

To find out more: How can I get reimbursed for a damaged package with BackShip?


Returns & billing

  • Returns: The standard process applies. The customer opens a customer service request within the required timeframe.
    • To find out more: Returns processing
  • Billing: Identical to other BackShip delivery methods.
    • To find out more: Understanding your BackShip invoices

Need help?

 

The BackShip team is available to assist you with any issue related to a Collection Point order.

 

Open a support ticket right here
Title: BackShip - Delivery to a Collection Point

Details__c:
What is the context ?

Trade-in customer satisfaction is key to building awareness about the circular economy while ensuring you have a robust, stable source of products. 

To improve the Trade-in experience, Back Market is actively working on initiatives to enhance the assistance brought to customers. Proactive Care intervention is one of them.

 

What are the objectives ?

  • Short-term: Increase customer satisfaction when they face painful situations
  • Mid-term: Reduce number of occurrences of those situations (and thus improve general Trade-in customer satisfaction) 

How are we doing this ? 

→ We’ve set up proactive intervention from Back Market Care team to help customers get out of painful situations. 

→ Our intervention aims at firstly providing reminders to you on related orders while you’re on track to be compliant with the deadline set in the Quality Charter. 

→ These interventions will only apply to most critical & painful situations identified by Back Market.

 

Below are the situations in which we’ll intervene:

 

  • Late order processing 
  • Undecided counter-offers (COs)
  • Parcels not sent back after counter-offer refused
  • On-hold orders with unanswered customer messages
  • Bypass of our processes
  • Transport issues

Please note that in all of these situations above, if, despite our reminders, you remain noncompliant with the commitments of the Quality Charter, Back Market may act in your place and offer a solution to the customer in accordance with the General Terms & Conditions for refurbishers and this charter.

 

Any costs generated and/or incurred by Back Market (return labels, refunds, etc.) will be covered by you. Back Market may also trigger the payment of an order at its initial price in your place. 

 

As the number of occurrences of the above situations decrease, we will prioritize and inform you of implementing additional situational remedies.
Title: Proactive Care Interventions

Details__c:
Protecting your on Time Delivery automatically 

Back Market automatically accounts for bank holidays when calculating shipping and delivery deadlines. This means one additional business day is added to your delivery promise, helping you maintain a strong On Time Delivery (OTD) rate – a key component of your quality metrics.

 

How it works

The system works automatically – you don't need to do anything.

  • Preparation time (seller's responsibility) and transit time (shipping provider's responsibility) are separated.
  • Bank holidays in both the seller’s and the customer’s countries are considered.

Example:

  • Order created on 14 July (French bank holiday) from France to Spain
    → We automatically add 1 business day to your preparation time.

 

  • Order with an estimated delivery date 19 Jan (US bank holiday) on the US market : 
    → We automatically add 1 business day to the estimated delivery date. 

This automatically adjusts your delivery promise, limiting any negative impact on your OTD. 

 

Managing planned absences

While Back Market handles bank holidays automatically, you are responsible for managing your own planned absences (vacations, business closures, etc.).

Put your listings offline when you cannot fulfill orders. This prevents:

 

  • New orders during your absence
  • Negative impact on your quality metrics
  • Customer service issues

Coming soon : We're developing a new feature that will allow you to set your absence dates directly in your Back Office. This calendar will automatically manage your listing availability during planned closures.

Please find below the list of bank holidays per market.
Title: Do federal holidays affect quality metrics?

Details__c:
So, you've recently received an Apple device from a Back Market customer that’s still locked to an iCloud account (FMIP ON), and/or data is still on the device, preventing you from having full access to it. No worries, we’re here to help! This guide will show you which steps to take next. 

Note: In accordance with our updated quality charter, after you receive the returned device, you must fully restore it and delete your customer’s data if they haven’t done so already. If the item is locked you must: Never ask for your customer’s credentials. Instead, Inform the customer/Back Market of the situation, and follow the steps in this article to resolve the situation. 


Follow these best practices:
 


1. Restore the device ALWAYS

Always start by restoring the phone. Restoring the device to its factory settings erases all personal data from the device and even removes the phone’s passcode. Note this action is completely harmless to the device (check how to do so on this article)
Also check out this article to learn how to restore multiple Apple devices simultaneously.
 


2. Connect the device to an Internet Connection

After restoring it to its factory settings, make sure the device is connected to the internet, so you can check if ‘’Find my iPhone’ is activated. This information is saved on Apple servers and therefore requires an internet connection to be updated.
 


3. Report a locked device as soon as possible

Once confirmed that ''Find my iPhone'' is activated, please report this to us within 48 hours.

It is important that you attach proofs on our customer service interface to show that the device is still connected to an account, in order to avoid complications.

Note for Trade-in : The deactivation of a iCloud/Google account on a device that you receive is one of the key elements that should be checked before proceeding with the payment. If the account is still connected to the device, you can put the order on-hold until the customer provides you with the necessary information or removes their account remotely.

 👉  Below are the different possible scenarios you may encounter and how to do in each situation to solve the issue:


1. Client erased data & FMIP is OFF

Step 1: Connect the device to an Internet Connection
Ensure that the device is connected to an internet connection before verifying whether FMIP is activated or deactivated. 

Step 2: Proceed to hard reboot the device
Once the device is connected to an internet connection, you can proceed to hard reboot the device. 
 


2. Client erased data but FMIP is ON

Step 1: Connect the device to an Internet connection
Ensure that the device is connected to an internet connection before resetting it to factory settings, because the process could be interrupted if it's not connected to the internet. 

Step 2: Contact the client to turn off FMIP
Once the device has been reset to factory settings and is still connected to an internet connection, check again if FMIP is still ON. If it is, please ask the client to deactivate the iCloud account following the remote procedure

Step 3: Hard Reboot the device
Once the above steps have been successfully completed, you may now proceed to hard reboot the device.


3. Client didn't erase data, but FMIP is OFF

If the client didn't erase their personal data the device can still be protected with a passcode thus preventing you from having full access to the device.
 


4. Client didn't erase data & FMIP is ON (device is function)

The client returned the device, and you realized that FMIP is activated on the device, and that the client didn't also erase their personal data. Follow the below steps:

Step 1: Connect the device to an Internet connection
Ensure that the device is connected to an internet connection before resetting it to factory settings. The process could be interrupted if it's not connected to the internet. Also, without an internet connection, the FMIP information is potentially not updated. Indeed, this information is saved on Apple servers and therefore requires an internet connection to be updated.  

Step 2: Contact the client to turn off the FMIP
Once the device has been successfully connected to an internet connection, and you confirm that the FMIP is still on,  please contact the client to turn off ''find my iPhone''

Step 3: Restore the device to its factory settings
Once FMIP has been turned off, you can proceed to restore the device to its factory settings, while it is still connected to the internet. 

To successfully restore the device, you will have to put the device in recovery mode. Also make sure you have the latest version of iTunes (on a PC) or an updated version of macOS
For more information on how to do a forced restore, check out the procedure here 
 


5. Client returned the device non-functional

The device was returned non-functional, and the client confirmed that they didn't erase their personal data nor turned off FMIP.  Please follow the below steps: 

Step 1: Repair the device
The first step to take is to repair the device to at least turn it on and connect it to the internet.

Step 2: Contact the client to turn off the ''find my iPhone''
Once the device has been repaired, please contact the client to turn off ''find my iPhone''

Step 3: Restore the device
The device is now functional and FMIP has also been turned off? Please proceed to restore the device. Restoring the device will reset the device to its factory defaults and reinstall the operating system. This process will also erase the client's personal data from the device.
 


6. Device is nonfunctional & irreparable

If the returned device is damaged beyond repair, or that the cost of repairing it is more expensive than the original price, in this case, we recommend you to either offer an exchange or a refund to the client.

Does the product have an exclusion of warranty? Please refer to this article  

/!\ Note that Back Market's terms of service prohibit the sharing of personal information. Please refrain from asking clients for their iCloud password.
Title: How to manage a product blocked with an iCloud account?

What are the new delivery requirements?

If you do cross the border, you are encouraged to use the express option for all cross-border flows. You can also add standard option for domestic flow with postal shipping provider to optimize price.

Details__c:
Our customers want their new product to be the way they expect it to be, which is why you need to make sure that the grade displayed on your offer matches the grade of the product the customer is receiving. Otherwise, you expose yourself to a high return rate and poor customer satisfaction. 

To help you ensure your grading fits our customer's expectations we have designed a toolkit with detailed guidelines for each grade of smartphones / tablets as well as MacBooks / laptops and newly added guidelines for Smartwatches.

 

Smartphones and Tablets

GradeBodyScreen
MintLike new. No scratches.Like new. No scratches.
Very goodExcellent condition. No scratches visible from a distance of 20cm/8 in (a bit longer than a standard-sized pencil).Excellent condition. No scratches.
GoodVery good condition. No scratches visible from a distance of 50 cm/20 in (about an arm's length).Excellent condition. No scratches.
FairGood condition. Light but plainly visible scratches.Good condition. Scratches that may be slightly visible when the screen is on.
StalloneFair condition. Visible signs of use, with scratches and/or dents that can be felt.Fair condition. Scratches that may be slightly visible when the screen is on.

 

MacBooks/iMacs, Laptops, and Desktop Computers

GradeBodyScreenKeyboard/Touchpad
MintLike new. No scratches. No sticker marks.Like new. No scratches.Like new. No scratches or signs of use.
Very goodExcellent condition. No scratches visible from a distance of 20 cm/8 in (a bit longer than a standard-sized pencil). No sticker marks.Excellent condition. No scratches.Excellent condition. No scratches or signs of use.
GoodVery good condition. No scratches visible from a distance of 50 cm/20 in (about an arm's length). No sticker marks.Very good condition. No scratches.Very good condition. Light scratches or minimal signs of use.
FairGood condition. Light but plainly visible scratches. For desktops and iMacs, plainly visible scratches on the bottom of the device. No sticker marks but decoloration is acceptable.Very good condition. No scratches visible when the screen is on.Very good condition. Light scratches or minimal signs of use.
StalloneFair condition. Visible signs of use, with scratches and/or dents that can be felt. No sticker marks but decoloration is acceptable.Fair condition. Light scratches that are invisible once the screen is on.Fair condition. Visible signs of use, with scratches and/or dents that can be felt.

Smart Watches

GradeBodyScreen
MintLike new. No scratches.Like new. No scratches.
Very goodExcellent condition. No scratches visible from a distance of 20cm/8 in (a bit longer than a standard-sized pencil).Excellent condition. No scratches.
GoodVery good condition. No scratches visible from a distance of 50 cm/20 in (about an arm's length).Good condition. No scratches visible when the screen is on.
FairFair condition. May have visible signs of use, with scratches and/or dents that can be felt.Good condition. No scratches visible when the screen is on.
StalloneFair condition. Visible signs of use, with scratches and/or dents that can be felt.Good condition. No scratches visible when the screen is on.

Other Products

GradeDescription
MintNo trace of use.
Very goodA few traces of use, but they’re invisible from more than 20 cm away.
GoodA few traces of use, but they’re invisible from more than 50 cm away.
FairA few traces of use visible from more than 50 cm away, but there’s no obstacle to use.
StalloneMarked traces of use, but there’s no obstacle to use.
 

 

Please note: Engravings are not permitted on products graded Premium, Excellent (Mint & Very Good), or Good. For products in Fair & Stallone grade, engravings are allowed provided the text does not contain:

 

  • Personal data
  • Discriminatory content
  • Hate speech
  • Obscene material
  • Any other inappropriate messages

The presence of such engraving does not void the customer's right of withdrawal.

 

As a reminder, customers have until the end of the withdrawal period to claim a physical appearance grade that has not been respected. You will then have to propose to the customer a commercial gesture (see table below), an exchange of the product, or the refund if they wish it (withdrawal period). We only display 3 grades to our customers (excluding Premium) while you can list the products into 5 esthetical states.


It is important to note that when in doubt it is always better to choose the lesser grade of two as it is economically damaging to over-grade products (for iPhones ). You can find a one-pager with the detail of the costs of over-grading attached at the bottom of this article.

NEW: 
Here are the fees that may be withheld in the event of a grade not corresponding to the order:

 

Grade ordered

Grade received by the customerGrade ordered: PremiumGrade ordered: ExcellentGrade ordered: Good
Excellent
Good10% of the product sale price5% of the product sale price
Fair15% of the product sale price10% of the product sale price5% of the product sale price
 
In case of the dirty item or item worse than fair, the Agent may propose 5% commercial gesture.
The minimum commercial gesture is 5 EUR/GBP/USD. To increase the probability that the customer will keep the device, for all partial refunds or goodwill gestures, the amount will be rounded up to the nearest 5€ or $5. For example, a refund of 7€ will be rounded up to 10€. This does not apply to partial refunds for late express shipping, which will be refunded at the exact shipping cost.
Title: Physical Appearance grades

Details__c:
Back Market provides, in your Back Office, a conversational assistant based on generative artificial intelligence (beta service). Its purpose is to help you find answers and guide you to the Seller Support Center and applicable documentation.

 

Purposes

We process data related to your exchanges with the assistant in order to:

 

  • Answer your questions and direct you to relevant resources (Seller Support Center, Seller Terms and Conditions, Quality charter)

  • Assess and improve response quality (e.g., analysis, usage statistics, identification of uncovered topics, etc.) and improve our help articles and support content.

 

How it works and its limitations (generative AI)  

Responses are generated automatically and may be inaccurate, incomplete, or not suited to certain situations.

 

The assistant does not replace the Seller Support Center or human assistance when needed. If in doubt, please contact our seller support service team.

 

This service does not provide personalized legal, financial, or professional advice. If in doubt, refer to the applicable contractual documents and/or contact our seller support service team or the appropriate professional advisor.

 

Data processed

Depending on how you use the service, processed data may include:

 

  • Your user identifier (or seller account identifier) and certain session-related technical information

  • The content of your messages and the generated responses

  • Metadata associated with the conversation (e.g., date/time, category, quality indicators, etc.)

 

Important: Please do not provide personal or sensitive data (including customer personal data), or information relating to third parties.

 

Retention

Conversations (questions and answers) linked to your user identifier are retained for up to 12 months maximum, then deleted or anonymized, unless a legal
Title: BackAI Assistant for Sellers (beta version) - Information about AI and data

Details__c:
As you might already know, some recent iPhone models display a notification when they’re repaired with non-original spare parts like screens, batteries, or cameras.

 

What does this mean for you?
At Back Market, we’re all about repairs and refurbishment – it’s the heart of what we do. So, no need to stress: you won’t be penalized for selling devices that show these notifications.

 

What about your customers?
Here’s the thing: not all customers are tech-savvy, and seeing these notifications might raise a few eyebrows (or support tickets). To save everyone the hassle – and avoid unnecessary complaints or returns – we recommend being upfront about these messages at the time of purchase.

 

Here’s how we’ve got your back:
We’ve created handy informational leaflets for all the countries we operate in. These are designed to reassure your customers and explain the notifications in simple, customer-friendly terms. Each leaflet even includes a QR code linking to a detailed article for those who want to dive deeper.

 

What should you do?

If you’re selling devices that show these notifications, we highly recommend:

  • Printing the leaflet that matches the customer’s country and language.
  • Including it in the package with the device.

This extra touch can go a long way in keeping your customers informed, happy, and confident in their refurbished tech purchase.

 

You can find each version of the leaflets at the bottom of this page. 

 

Pro tip: Make sure the leaflet is in your customer’s preferred language – it’s the little things that make a big difference.

By taking this small step, you’ll help educate your customers, reduce returns, and keep those satisfaction scores looking sharp. Win-win-win

 

🔋 Battery message issue

Any battery replacement by a third party generates warning messages (even with an OEM part).

You will see a notification on your Lock Screen for the first 4 days that you use your device, and in the Settings app for 15 days.

After that period, you can find the notification by going to Settings > General > About.

Those messages and the absence of battery health can cause SAV claims.

Those SAV claims will increase in the future as the volumes of XR and later increase and as those models get older and have their battery replaced.

     

📱 Screen message issue

Any screen replacement by a third party generates a warning messages (even with an OEM part).

You will see a notification on your Lock screen for the first 4 days that you use your device, and in the Settings app for 15 days.

After that period, you can find the notification by going to Settings > General > About.

Those messages and the absence of some functions can cause SAV claims.

Those SAV claims will increase in the future as the volumes of 11 and later increase and as those models get older and have their screen replaced.

Conclusion

We cannot prohibit the sale of these models because changing certain components is a normal process when reconditioning products. However, we strongly advise you to inform the customer when making a purchase.

Regarding the error messages related to the battery and the screen, please inform the customer that the message can be deleted after 15 days.
Title: Apple : Warning messages when replacing components

Details__c:
Back Market’s vision is to grow the renewed electronics market around the world so that consumers have no factual reason to buy new. Together with the sellers, we’ve succeeded in convincing over millions of customers to opt for a renewed device rather than a new one. 

Although an amazing achievement, there’s still room for growth. 

Customers are becoming increasingly selective about their purchasing decisions, and an overwhelming majority still prefer to buy new products from brands they know. In order for Back Market to continue to grow the share of the refurbished market versus new, we must continue to build customer trust and loyalty. 

There are 3 key factors to gaining consumer trust, all related to delivering an outstanding customer experience: 

 

  1. Selling top quality products (thanks to you!)
  2. Offering fast and trackable shipping
  3. Delivering exceptional customer support, thanks to Back Market agents managing your requests!

 

How do we ensure success in all 3 areas? 


Introducing Customer Care by Back Market (CCBM)! CCBM combines customer care with return logistics and packaging solutions to create a full service after-sales customer experience solution. CCBM will be launched in October 2022 to all Back Market sellers globally. 

By positioning Back Market as the main point of contact in after-sales claims, we seek to offer all customers a trustworthy and consistent experience regardless of product, seller, or country. Simultaneously, we’re offering you a valuable service bundle to save you time in after-sales tasks and allow you to focus on what you do best ; securing quality supply.

 

How does it works ?

  • Back Market Agents are the “middle man”, they answer the customer’s messages and will send you messages only if required by the resolution, please answer these messages within the next business day.
  • All customer requests must be exclusively handled by the agent.You can only manage after-sales services through the Back Office, and only the agent can respond to your customers' requests. Direct customer communication is prohibited.
  • Always use the preset buttons to communicate with the agent. When a message is required, provide complete, relevant, and effective responses to minimize the number of exchanges.
  • This mandatory service enable you to sell to any countries where Back Market is available and to deliver a best in class after-sale experience ( wide opening hours, chatbot, live chat, phone support).
Title: What is Customer Care by Back Market (CCBM)?

Details__c:
If you don’t have a specific Seller Success Manager at Back Market, it means you are capable of managing your account autonomously, your Back Office and the Seller Support Center have all the tools you need to succeed.

In your Back Office, you can:

  • Monitor your sales and quality performance
  • Update your offers and services
  • Find your invoices
  • Activate options for your account (e.g., shipping, payments, etc.)

 

Who should I contact if I have a question?

First, see our FAQ articles where the vast majority of questions from sellers are already answered.

If you don’t find the answers you’re looking for in the FAQs, use the contact form in the Seller Support Center. Specialists in each subject will answer your questions quickly.

 

What questions can the Seller Support Center answer?

For sellers like you, the Seller Support Center is the place to go for seeking help with service requests, reporting an issue with a listing, asking questions about your invoices, getting advice on how to increase sales, and asking questions about a Monitoring Period or other quality processes.

Topics related to your account performance can be accessed once you report that your request is not order-related under the “Account Performance” option listed in addition to Back Market Services, Finance/Legal, Integrations and Listings options. 

 

How can I grow my business?

Regularly check the level of your quality Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) in your Back Office to avoid falling into a Monitoring Period and having your sales potential limited.

You should be looking at your Quality Insights dashboard daily. This is where you can find detailed information about your return rate, shipping metrics, quality KPIs, and more.

Regularly check that you've won the BackBox on your flagship items as well as maintaining sufficient item inventory in your Back Office.

For more insights on how to succeed on Back Market, check out this FAQ article

                            

What can I do in my Back Office?

Your Back Office should become your main hub at Back Market if you want to improve your sales performance. 

Within your Back Office, you’ll find insights which will help you monitor your general performance. 

The Sales Insights dashboard allows you to focus on your goal and spend less time on non-sales tasks. The dashboard’s goal is to enable you to monitor your sales and have a better view of your global sales performance and turnover.

You’ll find more information on how to use this tool in this article

Along with Quality Charter, your Quality KPIs are 1 of the most important tools to help manage your item quality and customer satisfaction on Back Market. Find more information on how to monitor your quality in this article

 

What should I know about Monitoring and Last-Chance Periods?

Keeping track of your quality will help you avoid a Monitoring Period. This process has been created to help you monitor your weak quality KPIs — tracked in your Quality dashboard — to build strong long-term quality and enhance customer experience. 

Back Market will always let you know via email when you are entering a Monitoring Period. This email will also contain the name of the KPI that triggered the alert and the estimated deadline of the Monitoring Period. You can also find out if you’re in a Monitoring Period on the Insights tab of your Back Office

These Monitoring Periods aren’t negotiable, and the only way to get out of it is to improve your performance until the end of the period.

If you manage to improve the quality KPI for a full week, then the Monitoring Period will end.

If you fail to improve your quality KPI during this period, you’ll enter the Last-Chance Period before we proceed to close your account. This period will limit your sales to 30% of your average sales volume over the last 30 days.

 

What happens if my account is deactivated?

If your account is deactivated because of your performance, you’ll receive a warning email. In this email, you’ll find all the information related to the exit plan. 

If you contest your account deactivation, use the Seller Support Center forms and select “Contesting an imposed exit from Back Market”.

If you’d like to reactivate your account, wait 6 months after your last order and email activation-eu@backmarket.com

Account deactivation due to shipping issues can be avoided by subscribing to Back Market's BackShip service  
 

What’s a deferred payout?

Back Market can withhold deposits to cover any potential refunds for your items that are still under warranty. This may be triggered in 3 different situations: 

  • Your sales dropped too suddenly (i.e., as much as 90%)
  • Your Defective Rate is too high (i.e., more than 9%)
  • You have too many after-sales contacts


This money that has not been paid out to you yet is stored in your virtual wallet in your Back Office. If your last payout was on the 26th of the month for all sales made between the 6th and the 15th, your wallet will hold money from orders shipped between the 16th and the 25th. As soon as your risk decreases, we’ll release more of your deferred payout. 

In order to see your balance, visit the "Invoices" tab in your Back Office.

For more information, check out this FAQ article

It’s important to keep in mind that your deferred payout won’t be released until your sold items are no longer under warranty and your account has been deactivated.

 

How do I increase my daily sales limits?

Your daily sales limits will naturally increase as you continue to sell and your item quality remains high.

During a Monitoring or Last-Chance Period, sales limits are automatically monitored. Sales limits won’t be increased during this time. Sales limits are triggered once you enter a Last-Chance period and won’t be reversed until the period is resolved. 


Remember your main point of contact is the Seller Support Center (SSC). SSC agents have been professionally trained to answer your needs in the most efficient manner.
Title: I don’t have a dedicated Seller Success Manager (SSM), what should I do?

Details__c:
Jump to

What’s a battery drain test?
Why’s it important to test battery drain?
Testing conditions and measuring results
Equipment, tools, and software needed
How to set up a battery drain in Phonecheck’s Cloud Portal
 

What’s a battery drain test?

This test checks how a device’s battery performs under a heavy workload. During this test, you’ll check the battery by running intensive tasks and seeing how quickly it drains.
 

Why’s it important to test battery drain?

Testing can help you identify problems with the battery or other hardware components. When a battery isn’t functioning properly, it can cause a number of issues, such as reduced battery life, slow charging, and device shutdowns.

Testing battery drain is a valuable tool for ensuring your device is functioning properly and that you’re getting the most out of your device's battery. It can also help identify suspicious reprogrammed batteries.
 

Testing conditions and measuring results

To test, the device must be charged to 30–80%. It should never be 100% charged.

At the end of the test, you can judge the battery based on the results:

  • If the battery was drained 5% or less, you have a great battery.

  • If the battery was drained 5–10%, you have a good battery.

  • If the battery was drained 10–15% or more, you have a bad battery.
     

Equipment, tools, and software needed

To do this test, you’ll need:

  • A dedicated computer

  • Phonecheck 2.0 software USB hub

  • Charging cables

  • Phone that’s on the welcome screen
     

How to set up a battery drain in Phonecheck’s Cloud Portal

Step 1. Sign in to your Phonecheck Cloud Portal
Step 2. Go to “Customization” > “Test App Customization.”
Step 3. On the “Create Test Plan” page, choose the option that works best for you.
Step 4. Set the battery drain duration to 30 minutes. If you’d like to automate this test on all devices after Phonecheck testing, then choose “Auto Battery Drain after Test Results.” If you’d like to perform this test on a sample basis and dedicate a special station to it, choose “Auto Start Battery on Connection.”
Step 5. Type in a plan name (for example, “Battery Drainage Test”) and select “Create Test Plan” to save.


 


Step 6. Set up the profile to be assigned to any station desired via the “Assign App Customization” tab.
Step 7. Select “Enable.”
Step 8. Select “Update User/Master” to finalize the update.




Step 9. Restart Phonecheck for the station(s) that were updated in the Cloud. 


How to test your device

If you chose “Run Battery Drain”:
Step 1. Connect the device to the desktop. Phonecheck will activate the device and install the testing app automatically.
Step 2. Navigate through the various pop-up windows to close them, and then open Phonecheck.
Step 3. Perform functional tests through the testing app.
Step 4. Select “Drain Test.”
Step 5. Verify duration and set to 30 minutes if needed.
Step 6. Launch test.
Step 7. Look out for a flash on the screen. This happens when the test is complete.
Step 8. Connect the device to collect battery drain results.

If you chose “Autostart Battery Drain on Connection”:
Step 1. Connect the device to the desktop.
Step 2. Wait for Phonecheck to install the app.
Step 3. Wait for the battery drain test to automatically open. This will happen if it’s set up.
Step 4. Disconnect the device, and the battery drain will start.
Step 5. Look out for a flash on the screen. This happens when the test is complete.
Step 6. Connect the device to collect battery drain results.
Step 7. Complete device testing on the app.



Here’s what you’ll see when the test is complete.
Title: How to test battery drain on smartphones and tablets

Details__c: Back Market now applies a Service Fee  to customers on all orders. This fee is charged directly to customers at checkout. This is also called the Back Market Quality Assurance Fee. It aims at financing our work in the quality controls, monitoring of sellers’ performance, and it also supports the Back Market Innovation Lab. You are not responsible for this fee. When an order is refunded, Back Market reimburses the Service Fee directly, not the seller.
Title: Back Market Service Fee charged to customers

Details__c:
This article aims at helping you understand your invoice in detail. If you have doubts about our invoicing system in general, you can find all relevant information in our article “How does invoicing work at Back Market?”.
 

Step by step guide

The first page of your invoice always gives you a full overview of your final payment received, after deducting services fees and expenses. It also takes into account fluctuations of your warranty deposit. In simple terms, earnings are generated by your shipped orders and expenses are related to Back Market services and partial or full refunds of orders. 

Please find attached examples of invoices.

 

First section:

The first page of your invoice is a summary of all costs charged on the revenues of the previous ten days. This is why it is important to understand each line of the first page of your invoice.

  • Commission fee Back Market (Commissions BackMarket): sum of commissions retained by Back Market on all orders shipped during the period shown;
  • Fees payments in installments (Frais Paiement en x fois): service fees withheld due to payment methods Klarna and Oney;
  • BackCare fees (Frais Back Care): sum of costs related to the BackCare service;
  • BackShip Fees (Frais Back Ship): sum of logistical costs related to the BackShip service;
  • Adjustment of fees of the service... (Régularisation de frais de service... ): Always represents a cost recovery in favor of Back Market or the seller due to an oversight on your last invoice;
  • Total Back Market services fees (Total Prestations BackMarket): Subtotal of all paid Back Market services (sum of all previous points) 

 

Second section:


This section balances out the amount earned with the expenses incurred, in order to indicate the profit you generated from your sales after deducting all expenses:

  • Amount of orders shipped by the seller (Montant des commandes expédiées par le marchand): total sum of products sold over the given period;
  • Subtotal credited to the seller (Sous-Total crédité au marchand): total sum of products sold without deduction of services fees;

*** The subtraction of expenses from the total revenues then takes place:

  • Subtotal of fees related to Back Market Services (Sous-Total des frais lié aux prestations BackMarket): total sum of fees including BackCare, Klarna, Oney, Bakckship, regulatizations of fees (if any);
  • Subtotal of refunds made on behalf of the seller (Sous-Total des remboursements effectués au nom du marchand): list of refunded orders, including PayPal service charges on orders delivered and then refunded;
  • Return on deposit (Rendu sur dépôt): adjustment of your deposit, which is recalculated at each payment period, based on different risk criteria.
  • Subtotal of changes in security deposit (Sous-Total des variations de dépôt de garantie): amount of deposit returned or blocked according to our risk assessment.
  • Negative invoice balance (Réport facture negative): report of your previous invoice with a negative balance; the details of the amount displayed can be found in the previous invoice.
  • Amount of VAT collected on shipped orders: VAT collected by Back Market under Directive 2017/2455 applicable to online sales of goods to retail customers in the EU.
  • Amount of VAT collected on reimbursed orders: Adjustment of the VAT collected with regard to 2017/2455 directive following the reimbursement of the concerned orders in the appendices.

 

Debt recovery from accounts in GBP currency:

This scenario occurs only if you were selling in the one market in the past, and now you no longer do (it often concerns the UK market).

In this case, you might still incur some charges on orders that still have ongoing warranties. However, as you are no longer generating revenues in this market, your balance is likely to go negative. For this reason, any expenses that you may incur if you find yourself in this situation are charged to your invoice in EUR currency under the voice “Virements bancaires complémentaires”. Here is an example:

  • On the 16th of August you received your invoice in GBP currency, an amount of £1139.58 GB is due;

  • On the 16th of August, this amount is charged to your invoice in EUR currency following the currency converter OANDA 

Please note that this action is performed roughly once per month.

 

Third section:

A final overview of revenues balanced out with your expenses is shown in the bottom right:

  • Balance (Solde): final amount you will receive during the billing date concerned.

The following pages of your invoice aim at giving you all the details of the orders concerned.
Title: My invoice step by step

Details__c:
To build trust with the growing number of refurbished tech customers, it’s important to follow best practices for refurbishment when selling on Back Market. To make sure items sold on Back Market are high-quality and trustworthy, we’ve come up with a list of prohibited practices that you’ll need to keep in mind. Note that Back Market regularly makes mystery orders to confirm that all sellers aren’t using these prohibited practices.

To build trust with the growing number of refurbished tech customers, it’s important to follow best practices for refurbishment when selling on Back Market.

To make sure items sold on Back Market are high-quality and trustworthy, we’ve come up with a list of prohibited practices that you’ll need to keep in mind. Note that Back Market regularly makes mystery orders to confirm that all sellers aren’t using these prohibited practices.

 
Device partProhibited practiceWhat it looks likeRisks
GeneralIntellectual property infringement 
  • Putting the manufacturer's logo and name on an item or packaging
  • Putting the manufacturer's logo and brand name on non-original spare parts
  • Counterfeit
BatteryReprogramming a used battery
  • Changing battery information in any way (for example, capacity or health)
  • Fraud
  • Loss of customer trust
BatteryReusing a soft-cell battery
  • Dismounting and remounting a soft-cell battery can make dents and punctures
  • Safety risks
BatteryUsing incorrect adhesive
  • Using adhesive that’s not made to go on specific parts — in result, it won’t adhere to the other components and/or model (for example, no adhesive in the wireless charger or NFC anthem area)
  • Using battery adhesive without removable tabs — in iPhones, this will lead to device damage when the battery is removed
  • Safety risks
  • Can lead to battery or device deterioration
ScreenChanging screen technology
  • Changing screen technology (for example, LCD vs. OLED)
  • Loss of customer trust
ScreenPoorly-sealed screen and/or frame
  • Improperly-sealed screen and/or frame can lead to item damage (for example, missing dust protection can damage camera)
  • High defective risk
SpecsAdding new functionalities to the product
  • Creating physical SIM card openings and welding SIM trays for original eSIM products only
  • High defective risk
  • Conformity issue (will be refused as a counterfeit by OEMs)
  • Doesn’t qualify as refurbishment
SpecsMemory storage not compliant with IMEI or serial number
  • Changing memory (no NAND replacement) while the actual capacity doesn’t correspond to the IMEI/model number specification
  • High defective risk
  • Conformity issue (will be refused as a counterfeit by OEMs)
  • Loss of customer trust
SpecsColour not compliant with IMEI or serial number
  • Changing the back case colour to one different from the IMEI/model number specification
  • Conformity issue (will be refused as a counterfeit by OEMs)
  • Loss of customer trust
SpecsNot respecting OEM specification
  • Using compatible components that do not respect OEM specifications
  • Safety risk
  • High defective risk
AppearanceUsing protective skins and screen protectors
  • Using skins to cover the marks and scratches on devices (except for fair grade)
  • Loss of customer trust
Title: Prohibited refurbishment practices

Details__c:
If you've recently received an iPhone with a passcode from a Back Market customer, you can perform a factory reset to delete the passcode.

As noted in our updated Quality Charter, you're required to fully restore the device and delete the Back Market customer's data if they haven't done so already. If the device is locked:

  • Never ask for your customer’s credentials.
  • Follow the steps in this article to resolve the issue instead.


Want to restore multiple Apple devices simultaneously? Read this article to learn how


Make sure that you have a computer, either Mac or PC

This process requires a Mac or PC. If you're using a PC, make sure that it has Windows 8 or later, and that iTunes is installed. You also need a compatible cable to connect your iPhone to the computer. 
 

Start by turning off the iPhone.

  1. Unplug the iPhone from the computer if it’s connected.
  2. Drag the slider to turn off the iPhone, then wait a minute to make sure that it turns off completely.


Next, put the iPhone in recovery mode.

  1. Find which buttons you need to press depending on the model
  2. Press and hold the correct button for your iPhone while immediately connecting your iPhone to the computer. Don't let go of the button.
  3. Keep holding the button until you see the recovery mode screen on your iPhone, then let go.
  4. If you see the passcode screen, you need to turn off your iPhone and start again.


Next, restore the iPhone.

  1. Locate the iPhone in the Finder or in iTunes on the computer that it’s connected to.
  2. Choose Restore when you see the option to Restore or Update. Your computer downloads software for the iPhone and begins the restore process.

If the download takes more than 15 minutes and your device exits the recovery mode screen, let the download finish, then turn off the iPhone and start again.

  1. Wait for the process to finish.
  2. Disconnect the iPhone from the computer. 

This process restores the device to its factory settings, erases the client's personal data and removes the phone’s passcode. 

After the device has been restored, switch it on. If the display shows the image below, this means that the device is iCloud-locked. In that case, find the service request in the after-sales services platform, and select the “Report a problem” button. This will block an automatic refund, and allow our agents to help you.

Check out this article for more details
Title: Received an iPhone with a passcode?

Details__c: You will find attached below the Personal Data Best Practices Charter
Title: Personal Data Charter

Details__c:
Before you start

  1. The process for claiming a warranty exclusion is outlined in this article.
  2. Please also refer to our charter (section 1.1 Item quality control) and Ecodesign requirements of COMMISSION REGULATION (EU) 2023/1670 (article 1.2.1) that mention a “minimal resistance to accidental drop” with:
    1. Devices passing 45 falls without protection with a 1m height
    2. Recommended test procedure in Annexe 3 of Ecodesign requirements of COMMISSION REGULATION (EU) 2023/1670 (article 1.2.1)

Why do we review your warranty exclusion claims?

We review out-of-warranty claims to ensure fair warranty coverage and maintain customer trust in refurbished products. High standards and top quality are what keep customers buying refurbished. Below we have outlined the most commonly declared warranty exclusions that may be considered unjustified:

 

  • Broken back-case
  • Screen cracks
  • Detaching screen
  • Lens defects
  • Oxidation

Upon Lab review, warranty exclusions based on the above issues are likely to be rejected. When such exclusions are rejected, the devices will remain under warranty coverage and qualify for a refund, repair, or replacement.

 

Common unjustified warranty exclusions

Important: The images in subsequent sections showing valid and invalid warranty exclusions serve as guidance only. The final determination on warranty exclusion claims rests with Back Market Lab experts.

 

1- Broken back case

Why could this be covered under warranty?

 

High-quality back cases, whether original or refurbished, must withstand:

  • Small drops (30 cm).
  • Pressure or bending (100 lbs).

A low-quality back case breaks easily, often detaching in large pieces without a clear point of impact.

 

🔧 Quality hacks:

 

  • Test a sample of devices when receiving a new batch, we recommend 3 drops from a height of 30cm. (Drop Test video)
  • Low-quality back glass will shatter in big chunks (due to lower glass quality and potential missing glue). 

2- Screen cracks

Why could this be covered under warranty?

 

A well-refurbished screen using high-quality components will not develop:

  • Internal cracks (without external impact).
  • Discoloration due to poor screen quality.

🚨 Note: Damaged screen protectors do not qualify as proof for warranty exclusions, except for Samsung Fold and Flip models.

 

🔧 Quality hacks:

 

  • Warning:  On reglassed screen, the display tends to detach from the frame because of improper gluing.
  • Stick to the same screen specs:
    • OLED vs LCD
    • Soft OLED over hard OLED

3- Detaching screen

Why could this be covered under warranty?

 

A properly refurbished device will not have a detaching screen, even after light impacts or minor cracks.

  • Most common cause: Weak refurbishment (e.g., improper gluing, poor battery placement) can lead to screens detaching.
  • Risk: A loose screen can cause further damage or even safety issues.

🔧 Quality hacks:

 

  • We recommend properly cleaning the frame from sealing adhesive residues before reapplying it.
  • Warning: On reglassed screens, the display tends to detach from the frame because of improper gluing
  • Stick to the same screen specs:
    • OLED vs LCD
    • Soft OLED over hard OLED

4- Lens Defect

Why could this be covered under warranty?

 

A properly refurbished lens will:

  • Stay attached to the device.
  • Prevent dust from entering.
  • Avoid premature breakage (without external impact).

🔧 Quality hacks:

 

  • Be sure to align the back glass and the lens during refurbishment.
  • The lens must be correctly fastened and not only glued. 

5- Oxidation

When is oxidation not a valid warranty exclusion?

 

Back Market clearly informs customers that refurbished devices lose their waterproofing. However, to exclude a device from its warranty due to oxidation, you must provide clear evidence beyond:

 

 ❌ A red seal
 ❌ Small signs of water drops.

 

These proofs alone are not sufficient to determine if the device was not already oxidized before sale.

 

Proof that would exclude a device from its warranty would be images that show:

 

✅Rust
✅Corrosion

 

What if a customer is refunded despite the declaration of a valid warranty exclusion?

In some complex cases, our CCBM agents may refund the customer even if you believe a valid warranty exclusion was proven.

 

💰 If you have indeed provided evidence of a valid warranty exclusion, you will be able to obtain compensation.

To request compensation, open a case via the Seller Support Center.

 

Before submitting your request, ensure that:

 

✅ The warranty exclusion does not qualify under the non-valid warranty exclusions described above.

✅ You declared the warranty exclusion using the 3-click resolution button within 3 days (5 days for laptops) of receiving the device.

✅ Your claim for reimbursement is made within 3 months of the customer refund date (as per our T&Cs).

✅ You must:

      • Provide clear, high-resolution pictures showing the exclusion.
      • Add the IMEI or Serial Number (SN) of the device to the order when it is validated.
Title: Understanding Out-of-Warranty Claim Refusals

Details__c:
When a customer buys one or several listings from one specific Seller on Back Market, an order is created. An order is composed of one or several orderlines. An orderline is defined by a quantity of a specific listing from an order placed by a customer to a specific Seller (orderline contains also specific information about the customer: billing, shipping addresses, etc.). 

For example, if a customer buys 2 different listings owned by the Seller X and 4 same listings of the Seller Y, 2 orders will be created, one related to the Seller X and composed of 2 orderlines and one related to the Seller Y and composed of 1 orderline.


 

Actually, Sellers only have to manage orderlines. Orders are treated automatically when corresponding orderlines are treated.

Note that in most cases, an order is composed of only one orderline.
Title: Order vs. Orderline 🧱

Details__c:
What is the private copy levy?


In a nutshell, a private copy levy is a levy aiming at indemnifying the rights holders (authors, producers, artists, etc.) for the reproduction of their works on certain types of recording media. It is collected from manufacturers and distributors of recordable media. The levy is collected by collective management organizations (e.g. Copie France) acting on behalf of the rights holders.
 

Is Private Copy Levy limited to a specific country ?

No. So be careful, a private copy levy is likely to be due in any country if you are selling recordable media. For instance, it is due namely in Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Ireland, Italy, Netherlands, Spain and Sweden. For example, you will find below more information on the private copy levy applicable in : 

Belgium,  Denmark, Germany , Italy, Netherlands

Regarding France, on July 1, 2021, a decision of the Committee for the private copie levy remuneration (Commission pour la rémunération de la copie privée) came into force, and established a specific rate on refurbished items.  Please click here to see the details on this specific rate.
Title: Private Copy Levy
