Details__c:
What is the Wrong Product rate?

This rate reflects how well you manage your inventory and order preparation. If you don’t have the product your customer ordered in stock, you can propose an alternative by messaging a Back Market agent or cancel the order.

 

How is this rate calculated?

The number of orders supposed to be delivered in the past 30 days with a customer contact due to receiving the wrong product, including the wrong color, model year, memory storage, or accessory, (for example, USB C instead of lightning) divided by the total number of orders supposed to be delivered in the past 30 days, including cancelled orders.

 

How does this rate impact me?

The Wrong Product Rate impacts your ability to win the BackBox.

 

Where can I find my rate?

You’ll find your Wrong Product Rate and more details in your Quality Insights dashboard.
Title: Wrong Product Rate (over 30 days)

Details__c:
Overview of your wallet
 

Your wallet serves to equip you with all the details you need to grow your business. When you know the dates and total amounts of your payouts, you can plan better and smarter.

 Navigate to your wallet by going to the "Invoices" tab in your Back Office

Here you’ll find 3 useful features:

  • Your wallet balance
  • Your upcoming payouts
  • Your deferred payout




Your wallet balance is the amount you currently have. If you sell in markets with different currencies, you’ll see them listed here.

Your upcoming payouts section details the dates and amounts of your upcoming payouts.

And your deferred payout is the amount of money Back Market holds to cover any issues that might arise during your devices’ warranty periods. (Don’t worry, it’s not much.)
 

The details about your wallet balance


Depending on which countries you sell in, your wallet might show different currencies. It displays a certain percentage of your total sales. (The remaining amount is held as a deferred payout. Keep reading for more details.)


 

The details about your upcoming payouts


As you know, payouts are issued 3 times a month — on the 6th, 16th, and 26th.

On the 16th of the month, you’re paid what you sold between the 26th of the last month and 6th of the current month, minus what has been refunded between the 6th and the 16th of the current month.

On the 26th of the month, you're paid what you sold between the 6th and the 16th of the current month, minus what has been refunded between the 16th and the 26th of the current month.

For more details on your payouts or invoices, check out this article

In the upcoming payouts section, you’ll see your 2 future payouts. Again, if you sell in markets with different currencies, you’ll see them here too.



You’ll be able to track your selling periods and know exactly how much you’ve sold in different currencies. Please note that this section is updated everyday, so the numbers will fluctuate depending on your activity.
 

The details about your deferred payout


Here you’ll see the amount held back by Back Market. This amount serves to cover any issues that might happen during your items’ warranty periods.



If you want to know more about why we hold your deferred payout and how this number is calculated, check out this article
Title: Everything you need to know about your Back Market wallet

Details__c:
API stands for Application Programming Interface. An API is a software that allows 2 applications to share information with each other. 
 

This is relevant to you, because Back Market uses an API to help you connect your internal information with our marketplace.
 

These connections are made via something called an HTTP request — when 1 platform makes a request to pull information from the other platform. APIs handle lots of HTTP requests in order to show you the information you need from both platforms. 


When managing your Back Market catalog, you’ll be relying on HTTP requests to get and send information. Here are some examples:

  • A “POST” request allows you to create offers and orders.

  • A “GET” request allows you to get information about your offers and your orders (for example, the stock for an offer or the state for an order).

  • A “PUT” request allows you to update your offers and orders.


To manage your catalog via integrations, you’ll need to put an access token in your authorization header. Learn how to get your access token
 

Want to know more about using integrations with Back Market? We’ve got you. 


Learn how to create your product with API

Learn how to create your listing with API

Check out in-depth information about Back Market's API system
Title: What's an API connection?

Details__c:
This article explains the procedure to follow when you mistakenly send a different product than the one ordered, specifically when the item sent has a higher value than the originally purchased product.

 

Standard procedure (Customer returns the item)

1. Return request

  • The customer is asked to return the mistakenly sent device.

2. Seller obligation upon reception

  • Once you receive the returned device, you are obliged to ship the correct product (the one originally ordered and paid for) to the customer without delay.

3. Customer goodwill gesture

  • To acknowledge the inconvenience, the customer is provided with a 5% promotional code.

 

This is the preferred and standard resolution.

 

Exceptional case: Customer refuses to return the higher-valued item

If the customer refuses to return the higher-valued device, the following rules apply:

 

Warranty Handling

  • In the event of a warranty issue, you are obliged to repair the exact device currently held by the customer (i.e. the higher-valued device) and send it back to them.

Replacement Handling

  • If a replacement is required instead of a repair, you may send the device originally paid for by the customer (not the higher-valued item).

 

The customer will be clearly informed of these conditions before the case is closed.

 

Key points

  • Shipping the wrong item remains your responsibility.
  • Returning the higher-valued item and shipping the correct one is always the preferred outcome.
  • If the customer keeps the higher-valued device, repair obligations apply to that specific device.
  • Replacement rights remain limited to the originally purchased product.
Title: Special handling: Customer received a higher-valued item

Details__c:
Welcome to the Trade-in Launch Phase

Congrats, your Trade-in service is now activated! You’re all set to purchase devices with Back Market Trade-in. In this article, we’ll fill you in on everything you need to know for your Trade-in Launch Phase.
 

What’s the Trade-in Launch Phase?

The Trade-in Launch Phase follows the same concept as the Launch Phase you already went through to sell on Back Market.

This period is meant to provide you with all the knowledge you need to get off to a good start with our Trade-in service. We want to set you up for success with all the rules and tools you’ll need to achieve your goals while also providing high quality service to customers at the same time.

Your Trade-in Launch Phase consists of three trainings you’ll need to complete within a maximum of two months.

Each training completion will increase your daily limit by five.
And, if you’re based in France, Spain, or Germany, the completion of all 3 training segments will give you access to our entire EU market!
 

What does the training consist of?

Each training segment includes:

  • Reading some articles in the Seller Support Center (SSC)
  • Completing a few simple tasks to validate the training

 

Training segment 1: Generating 15 Trade-in orders

Objective: You’ll learn how to add your desired listings to your Back Office, how to keep your offers competitive over time, and how to get devices from customers.

Check out these articles in the SSC:

  • Getting started with your Back Office
  • Getting started with your listings


Training segment 2: Processing your first Trade-in orders

Pre-requisite: You’ll need to have completed the first training segment before starting this one. You’ll have 14 days to complete training segment 2 before we check.

Objective: You’ll learn how to use your Back Office to process your Trade-in orders on time, you’ll become familiar with our quality rules and have adjusted your processes to comply with them, and you’ll be in good financial shape to pay for your orders.

Tasks to validate training: 

  • Successfully process payment for five Trade-in orders
  • Have your quality of service compliant with these quality thresholds
    • Counteroffer Rate < 33%
    • Late Processing Rate < 15%
    • Late Response Rate < 15%

Check out these articles in the SSC:

  • Getting started with first-class quality
  • Getting started with your payments & invoices


Training segment 3: Being well informed and asking for help

Objective: You’ll learn how to receive communications from Back Market Trade-in, where to look for additional information when you need it, and how to ask the SSC for help.

Check out these articles in the SSC:

  • Getting started with seamless communication

Tasks to validate training: 

  • Submit a request to the SSC here to ask for advice on Trade-in. 
  • See the image below and fill in the areas highlighted in yellow.

 

 

How do I validate my Trade-in Launch Phase?

You have 2 months to complete these three training segments and the tasks they include.

If you haven’t finished in this time, you’ll still be able to use Trade-in, but your daily limit will remain capped. We highly recommend completing the trainings in this time so you understand all the rules and processes. This will set up you up for maximum success!
Title: Trade-in Launch Phase Presentation

Details__c:
Our goal is to make sure you’re able to cope with large numbers of sales while maintaining our quality expectations.
 
We’ve set up quality control points for you to target when you hit key daily sales numbers. If you can deliver the volume of sales while maintaining good quality metrics, only then will you be authorized to sell more.
 

What’s the daily sales limit?


After the launch phase, Sellers start with a daily sales limit of 25. 

 

Once you hit 20+ sales in a day and provided that your quality metrics (i.e., Defective rate over 90 days and Appearance rate over 30 days) remain below fixed targets (i.e. average rates of active Sellers), your limit will be increased to 50 sales per day. 

 

We check every 2 weeks. If we notice that you’ve hit the 20+ sales per day and the quality metrics are compliant, we increase the daily sales limit by an additional 25 sales per day. 

 

If you’ve reached your sales limit, the information will be available on the homepage of your Back Office.

 

What are the quality targets to hit to increase your  sales limits?

To unblock the growth of your daily sales, you need to respect the following targets: 

 

  • Appearance: below the average rate of active Sellers over 30 days
  • Defective: below the average rate of active Sellers over 90 days. 
  • To track your quality performance, check out your Quality Insights Dashboard.
Title: How can I sell more items on Back Market if there’s a sales limit?

Details__c:
Here’s what you need to know about getting your API token and setting up your integration. Just follow these steps. 
 

Step 1. Go to the Back Office “Options” tab. 

Step 2. Select “API & Integrations”.
 


Step 3. Select “Add new integration”. 

Step 4. Select your type of integration in the list.
 


Step 5. Enter your password to display your token.

Step 6. Copy the token and use it to set up your integration.
 


For more info, check out our API guidelines.

Keep in mind that:

  • Your API token will only be displayed once, so make sure to copy it. 
  • If you’ve lost your token or need to refresh it for any reason, you’ll have to delete your integration, refresh the webpage, and re-add your integration. 
  • If you change your token, you’ll need to enter the new one as soon as possible. Otherwise, you might run into issues and get blocked by our system.
  • The only person who can change tokens is the admin user of your account for security reasons. The admin user is the only one who can see the “Integrations” tab on the “Options” page.
Title: How do I connect my API token?

Details__c:
What’s the difference?

The Quality Charter (Appendix 1) lists up to 25 test categories you must verify before shipping products. Each category contains multiple checks. When you use approved testing software or do manual tests, you’ll see it runs 100+ specific function tests across those categories.

Why?

The Charter sets high-level categories. Testing software (and detailed manual testing) breaks each category into specific, measurable checks to ensure full quality control.

How it works

For the full breakdown of functions by product category, see the attached documents for smartphones, MacBooks, laptops, smartwatches, and gaming consoles.

 

Here’s how the Charter’s general requirements translate into detailed testing functions for smartphones:

 

Test Category

Functional tests

Buttons

Power button, Volume up, Volume down, Home button, Mute switch, Side button responsiveness

Cameras

Front camera, Rear camera, Camera focus, Flash, Video recording, Zoom, HDR mode, Camera aperture, Image quality, Night mode

Touch Screen

Touch response, Multi-touch, 3D Touch (iPhone), Digitizer accuracy, Dead pixels, Screen uniformity, Brightness levels, Color accuracy

Audio

Microphone, Earpiece speaker, Main speaker, Headphone jack (if applicable), Audio recording quality, Volume levels

Connectivity

Wi-Fi connection, Bluetooth pairing, GPS/Location services, Cellular network (3G/4G/5G), NFC, Carrier compatibility

Sensors

Proximity sensor, Accelerometer, Gyroscope, Ambient light sensor, Compass, Fingerprint sensor (Touch ID), Face recognition (Face ID), Heart rate sensor

Battery

Battery health percentage, Cycle count, Charging functionality, Battery capacity, Charging port, Wireless charging (if applicable)

Background Checks

IMEI verification, Blocklist status, SIM lock status, Device lock (iCloud/FRP), MDM status, Rooted/Jailbreak check

Important reminders

✅ All functions must be tested: Whether using testing software or manual checks, verify all applicable functions for the product you’re selling.

 

✅ Testing methods vary by product:

 

    • Smartphones, tablets, MacBooks, smartwatches: Use approved testing software (PhoneCheck, NSYS, BlackBelt 360, FutureDial, etc.).

    • Laptops and gaming consoles: Manual testing using the detailed test list is acceptable where software isn’t available.

✅ Test reports must be complete: Partial testing isn’t compliant with the Quality Charter.

 

✅ Documentation is required: You must provide the test report within 1 business day upon Back Market request.

 

    • Software testing: Provide the complete software test report.

    • Manual testing: Provide a documented checklist showing all verified functions.

✅ Tests must be recent: No older than 2 months at the time of sale.
Title: Understanding testing requirements: Charter categories vs. Detailed testing functions

Details__c:
The launch of Customer Care by Back Market (CCBM) on October 6, 2022, led to several updates to the Terms and Conditions and Quality Charter for sellers. Here’s what you need to know.
 

Jump to

Changes to the Terms and Conditions
Changes to the Quality Charter
​​​​​​​

Changes to the Terms and Conditions

Article 12: Quality Standards and Article 19: Rating and Product Classification
The CSAT (Customer Satisfaction) and Late Response Rate indicators are no longer used to assess your quality of processing of customer service requests, and they don't impact the BackBox algorithm or trigger monitoring periods any more.

Article 13: Customer Service
This article has been updated to reflect that you allow Back Market to manage customer service in your name and on your behalf. While back-and-forth messaging with your customers is to be eliminated, you're still able to communicate with Back Market agents any time. You’ll also have access to an online platform to manage your orders and obtain BackPack — Back Market-branded smartphone packaging. Learn more about getting packaging through BackPack

Article 14: Financial Terms and Conditions
This article has been modified to include the service costs of CCBM, as mentioned in the CCBM announcement. Financial terms for certain categories of accessories are also changing. Find a detailed breakdown of costs in this SSC article

Article 15: Invoicing and Payment
CCBM service costs are invoiced every 10 days, on a separate line of the sales invoices.

BackCare Terms and Conditions
This appendix has been deleted. Instead, CCBM is included in the sellers’ Terms and Conditions as part of the Back Market package.

BackShip Terms and Conditions
Back Market provides return labels for all orders after October 6, 2022, and Back Market agents escalate issues to shipping providers in case of return problems. As of April 1, 2023, you don’t have to pay any additional fees for returns from your customers (except for accessories and large or heavy items). You’re still able to opt for BackShip for shipping your items to your customers.

BackRepair and Fulfillment by Back Market
These services are not impacted in 2022.
 

Changes to the Quality Charter

Part 1: Responsibilities of the seller
You’ll no longer be able to communicate directly with your customer. You’ll communicate with Back Market agents exclusively. You’re still encouraged to proactively communicate any issue that could impact the customer experience so that the Back Market agent can propose a quick resolution.

Article 1.5: Legal guarantees and contractual warranty
The minimum warranty duration in Spain is 24 months now.

Article 3: After-sales
When an action is needed from you to process a service request (outside of return management), the deadlines to perform the action will start when the agent notifies you. You have 24 hours to respond to messages from Back Market agents.

Article 3.5: Managing item returns
Within CCBM service, prepaid returns labels are automatically provided in cases of return for "change of mind.” Back Market has developed new features — including 3-click resolution — to ease and speed up your interaction with after-sales service requests.

Instead of reporting on the actions you perform through messages, you’re able to use pre-configured buttons to perform most common actions in a few clicks. These required features were automatically activated as part of CCBM following their release.
Title: Summary of updates to T&Cs and Quality Charter linked to CCBM

Details__c:
While you're already familiar with our Sales BackBox, we're rolling out a revamped Trade-in BackBox as of June 2025.

 

Similar to sales, getting the Trade-in BackBox is no longer only about proposing the best price. The goal of the Trade-in BackBox is to give the final customer an offer at the best price with reduced counteroffer risk while rewarding refurbishers with stronger circularity on Back Market.

 

How is the Trade-in Backbox computed?

The Trade-in BackBox algorithm now integrates 3 metrics to determine your standing in the BackBox:

 

  1. Your price offer
  2. Your counteroffer rate: This key quality metric measures the percentage of orders for which you made a counteroffer. It can be viewed in your Back Office. Read all about the counteroffer rate here.
  3. Your circularity rate: This key performance metric measures how many Trade-in products you've bought and then sold on Back Market over the past 6 weeks. It is computed at the device level. It will be available in your Back Office shortly (Sales > Insights > Trade-in). Read all about the circularity rate here.


In short, the counteroffer rate and the circularity rate are now essential to winning the BackBox for you at the most competitive – lowest –  price.

 

What about your ability to set your prices?

On the Listings page of your Trade-in Back Office, you can set and change your prices. You will see: 

 

  • Your price: This is the current price you have defined for a specific product.
  • Price to win BackBox: This is the price you need to set to win the Trade-in BackBox. It can be lower or higher than the current BackBox price depending on your counteroffer rate and circularity rate.
  • BackBox price: This is the price currently offered to customers on the platform. It belongs to the seller with the ideal combination of price, counteroffer rate, and circularity rate. 

 

 

Similarly, if you are updating your prices through CSV or with the API, you will access the “price_to_win” information to be able to set your prices easily at your “Price to Win BackBox”, i.e. to easily get BackBoxes.

 

Good to know Q&A

Where can I find the price to win the BackBox? How do I know the prices of competitors?

For each product in each grade and each market, the Price to win the BackBox will always be displayed in your Back Office > Listings (see above screenshot) and on the CSV export of your listings (price_to_win).

You will not be able to know the price of competitors. But, you will be able to know the current BackBox price and see how your quality performance impacts your price to win.

 

If I win the Trade-in BackBox, will customers be offered my price?

Yes. If you win the BackBox, the price that will be displayed to the customer will be your price. Trade-ins for this product will be directed to you. 

 

My counteroffer rate or my circularity rate doesn’t seem to be good enough, I am not able to get any Trade-in BackBox, but I don't know what to do.

Don’t stress! Reach out to your Back Market contact and explain the situation. Together you will work on an adapted action plan to help you improve your counteroffer rate and/or resell more devices on Back Market.

 

[Circularity] What do I do if there is not enough demand for a device to sell it back on Back Market?

Your circularity rate is assessed compared to the average circularity rate of all sellers on the device you are willing to source. So in that case, low demand for a device will also impact other sellers in the same proportion. Read all about circularity rate here.

 

[Counteroffer rate] I’m doing low volumes. This can make my counteroffer rate highly volatile.

No worries, for you to not be impacted by the high volatility of your counteroffer rate when you have too low volumes (e.g. only 3 orders received in your last 30 days, and 67% CO rate with only 2 counteroffers), by default all refurbishers have 10 extra orders received added to the computation with the average CO rate of Back Market.
Title: Trade-in BackBox - How does it work?

Details__c:
Due to the high risks of counterfeit items and hazards to customer safety, Dyson products are a restricted category.

 

Here are the specific rules that apply to selling Dyson products on Back Market as of July 15, 2025 : 

 

  • For France only, the Dyson category is restricted to the Dyson company only. No third-party sellers are allowed to list Dyson products on www.backmarket.fr.
  • For other European countries (Germany, Spain, Great Britain, etc.), only sellers with a regular supply of authentic Dyson products will be eligible for the category.
    • All Dyson products must be listed exclusively in Dyson-specific categories, not in standard categories.
    • To sell Dyson products, you need manual approval. You can request access to list-in Dyson categories for all markets except France using the dedicated SSC form (Not-order related request > Listings > Sales > Applying to sell in the restricted item category > Dyson").
    • You need to have access to a regular supply of genuine Dyson devices to be able to list in Dyson categories.

Immediate category access revocation will apply across all countries if:

 

  • Counterfeit products are detected
  • Dyson Products are listed in France
  • Dyson Products are listed in standard categories

Potential category access revocation will apply across all countries if you actually don’t list regular supply in the Dyson categories.
Title: Restricted category - Dyson products

Details__c:
Starting April 2023, MacBooks are officially available for you to source through Trade-in — Back Market’s program that helps you buy old devices directly from customers. Here’s what you need to know.

Jump to

How do I create a listing to source MacBooks?
What specifications will you ask about when people are trading in their MacBooks?
How do I set a battery replacement price for my MacBook Trade-in listings?
​​​​​​​​​​​​​​How can I see if someone said their MacBook battery needs to be replaced?
​​​​​​​​​​​​​​What are acceptable reasons for a counteroffer on MacBooks?
Got more questions?
 

How do I create a listing to source MacBooks?

When Back Market customers want to sell their item through Trade-in, they’ll fill out a questionnaire to give details about their item. They’ll be instantly matched with an offer from a refurbisher (like you).

That means you’ll need to create a sourcing request (called a listing) that says what devices you’re looking for — this is what gives the device-owner an instant offer. You’ll need to indicate the quality levels you want and the prices you’d like to buy them at. Learn more about sourcing devices through Trade-in

There are 2 ways to create MacBook Trade-in listings — manually or via a CSV file upload.


Create a listing manually

Step 1. Determine which MacBook(s) you want to buy. You can decide which devices you want to buy by looking at different options in our MacBook catalog on Google Sheets. Select the “Catalog” tab and filter options by model, release date, screen size, and more. Look through the catalog on Google Sheets

Alternatively, if you know what model you want, you can search directly in the Back Office Trade-in search bar. Go to the Trade-in section of your Back Office > "Listings”. If you choose this option, you can skip Step 2 and start with Step 3. Note that you won’t need the product ID.

Step 2. Identify the product ID of the MacBook(s) you want. Pull the product ID listed in column A for the MacBooks you want to source. Please note that the product ID is called the Back Market ID in your Back Office.

Step 3. Create a listing in Back Office. Go to the Trade-in section of your Back Office > "Listings”. Enter the product ID in the “Back Market ID” box, then select “Search”. The MacBook you’re looking for should appear in the search results. Select "Buy item" and add the pricing for the grades you’d like to buy. If you don't want to source specific grades, enter 0.00 for those grades.

Step 4. Publish your listing. Select “Save listing”, and your listing will be created. Now sit back, and let the devices roll in.


Create a listing via CSV file upload

Setting up a CSV file upload takes a bit of work, but it’s a great tool to save time when you want to make a large number of listings.

Step 1. Set up your CSV file. Learn how to set your CSV file up for success and download a template via this article

Step 2. Decide which devices you want to buy. You can decide which devices you want to buy by looking at different options in our MacBook catalog on Google Sheets. Select the “Catalog” tab and filter options by model, release date, screen size, and more. Look through the catalog on Google Sheets

Step 3. Complete your upload. Import the file by going to your Back Office > "Trade-in" > "Listings" > "Import my listings”. Check that you’ve done everything correctly by reading our article on making CSV uploads
 

What specifications will you ask about when people are trading in their MacBooks?

We’ve defined a list of specifications to help you determine your offer prices. You’ll be able to see information about the device’s:

  • Processor

  • Memory

  • Storage

  • Keyboard layout

It’s important that you keep these specifications in mind when pricing your Trade-in listings. Note that charging cables and wall-plug chargers are not mandatory for MacBooks.
 

How do I set a battery replacement price for my MacBook Trade-in listings?

We added an additional step in the MacBook Trade-in questionnaire to ask Back Market customers about the battery health of their device. This will allow you to factor in the cost of battery replacements in your offer prices. Note that you’ll be able to make a counteroffer based on battery health if the MacBook’s battery doesn’t match the condition the Back Market customer stated.

You’ll be able to set the price of battery replacements for your MacBook Trade-in listings in your Back Office. You can add a cost deduction for the MacBook battery in the Back Office > “Trade-in” > “Options”.
 



 

How can I tell if someone said their MacBook battery will need to be replaced?

In the order description, you can see how the Back Market customer described their MacBook under “Customer assessment”. There, you’ll see if the battery is either normal or will need to be replaced.
 

What are acceptable reasons for a counteroffer on MacBook orders?

Read the full list of authorised counteroffer reasons for MacBooks via Google Sheets If you notice that a MacBook battery needs to be replaced but it wasn’t declared by the Back Market customer in their initial assessment, we recommend that you counteroffer with a price that takes the battery replacement price into account.
 

When can I put a MacBook Trade-in order on hold?

There are 2 reasons specific to MacBooks that may temporarily prevent you from processing a Trade-in order:

  • If the MDM status is active, the item may still be linked to the Back Market customer's business account.
  • If the firmware password is active. Learn more about firmware passwords via Apple

Pro tip: Sometimes the default keyboard when entering the firmware password is in QWERTY.

If you encounter either of these situations, you can put the order on hold. An automatic message will be sent to the Back Market customer with a clear explanation of the actions that need to happen to resolve the situation.
 

Got more questions?

Submit a support request via the Seller Support Center to get a quick answer.
Title: What should I know about Trade-in launching MacBooks?

Details__c:
I received a product blocked with an account (Google)

As soon as a customer service request is opened, an automatic message with instructions to follow to return the device is sent. This message contains a link to our customer FAQ article, where clear instructions on how to disconnect an account from a device are given. It can happen, however, that a customer fails to follow the return procedure correctly and sends you a product with an account still connected to it. 

Note: In accordance with our updated quality charter, after you receive the returned device, you must fully restore it and delete your customer’s data if they haven’t done so already. If the item is locked you must: Never ask for your customer’s credentials. Instead, Inform Back Market of the situation.

As there is potential for personal data breach here, it is important that you follow a few steps.

Rebooting and connecting the device to the internet after the deactivation of the account is necessary in order to complete the procedure of account deactivation.
 

Step 1

  • Provide the IMEI code. 
  • Or serial number of the device on our customer service interface directly as soon as you realize that the customer has failed to provide it before going ahead with the shipment of the product to your warehouse.

    This will allow you to check on a pertinent website whether the account has been successfully removed. 
    If this is not the case, you can provide a screenshot showing that an account is still connected to this IMEI as evidence and send it back to Back Market on the claim. Our customer service will share the instructions to follow in order to improve the resolution time of the case.

Step 2

Provide evidence directly on our customer service interface within 72 working hours from the reception of the product if you find that an account is still connected to the device.  As per Quality Charter regulations, you are expected to do a diagnosis of the device within this time-frame and this is one of the first checks you should do.  

Step 3

If the device is still user's blocked despite multiple interventions from our customer service, please consider sending back the device to the customer as an option" please consider sending back the device to them as an option. 

It is important that you continue to attach proofs on our customer service interface to show that the device is still connected to an account, in order to avoid any complications of the case. Keeping a device with the customer’s account on it is of no use to you as you will not be able to sell the device again, and equally painful for customers to wait weeks for their refund because they are not able to disconnect their account remotely from the device they sent back.
 

Recap of what is considered a proof:

  • Screenshot of a relevant website  showing that an account is still connected to this IMEI or serial number. 
  • Photo of the device in the section Settings → Account to show that an account is still connected to it.  
A simple link to a relevant website is going to serve its purpose at the moment, however, for the history of the case, it may be harder to understand exactly when the account was successfully removed. For this reason, we advise to always attach at least one of the two proofs mentioned above.


Note for Trade-in : The deactivation of a iCloud/Google account on a device that you receive is one of the key elements that should be checked before proceeding with the payment. If the account is still connected to the device, you can put the order on-hold until the customer provides you with the necessary information or removes their account remotely.
Title: How to manage a product blocked with an account (Android, Google,...)?

Details__c:
In cases of disputes related to shipping, it’s your sole responsibility to file a claim with your shipping provider and provide the necessary documents.

To streamline the customer experience, Back Market has simplified the process for customers by:

  • Allowing them 14 days to report cases of damage, empty packages, or incorrect products
  • Considering a package lost after 6 days without tracking updates, triggering a refund or the seller sending a new package 
  • No longer requiring the certificate of non-receipt (which will no longer be sent to you)

Upon notification from the agent following case assessment, you must initiate either a refund or shipment of a replacement product within 1 business day, based on the customer's selection. This obligation applies irrespective of the status, progress, or outcome of any shipping provider investigation.

 

This doesn’t mean that we’re relaxing our fraud detection though. We’ve increased detection methods with additional customer verification if we suspect fraud.

If the shipping provider rejects your claim due to the failure to provide the required documents, you may request compensation from the Seller Support Center, provided that you:

  • Submit your compensation request within 30 days of the order refund date.
  • Submit a request to the Seller Support Center by categorizing the request as "Non-Backship shipping or delivery".
  • Provide all information and documents requested by Back Market agents.
  • Prove that the shipping provider/insurer rejected your claim due to a lack of required documents, which should have been provided by Back Market.
  • Have used a delivery method requiring signature, PIN code, or QR code verification
  • Show that you have ad valorem insurance for the disputed parcel.


If you provide the proof that you held ad valorem insurance for the disputed parcel, compensation is capped to the amount specified in the insurance policy. 
If you are not able to provide proof of ad valorem insurance, compensation will be limited to 23€/$/GBP per Kg (e.g: If you shipped a 2Kg parcel, then you’ll get back 46€).

This compensation offered by Back Market doesn’t apply to cases of loss or damage for which the shipping provider is responsible.
 

Big investments to detect fraud and prevent fraudsters

We take abuse and fraud attempts very seriously. We’re committed to protecting you by conducting necessary checks with customers based on customer profiles defined by the type of order, order history, and reports received from our anti-fraud service providers.

We combat fake identities through external identity verification partners. These checks are used in a significant portion of our delivery incidents.

We cover the cost of identity verifications and external fraud prevention tools to protect you.

If you detect fraud with one of your packages, please reach out to the Seller Support Center. We’ll review the information, analyze the case, and if appropriate, offer compensation.

If the service provided by your shipping providers is unsatisfactory, check out our BackShip service.
Title: How to handle a shipping issue (lost, stolen, or damaged parcel)

Details__c:
Jump to
What’s the Defective Rate?
How is this rate calculated?
Where can I find my rate?
How can I improve my Defective Rate? 

 

What’s the Defective Rate?

This metric shows the technical issues that arise during and after the 30-day cancellation period. Technical issues should be avoided to make sure your customers are happy, trust the quality of devices sold on Back Market, and return to the platform for additional purchases. 
 
If your rate for a particular model is among the lowest-performing sellers on the platform, Back Market may suspend your listings of this model.


 

How is this rate calculated?

We divide the number of orders supposed to be delivered in the past 90 days that had at least 1customer contact for technical issues divided by the total number of orders supposed to be delivered in the past 90 days.



 

Where can I find my rate?

To see your rate, log into your Back Office > “Insights” > “Quality".
 
By selecting the “See details” button, you’ll access all the details, including any major technical issues causing contacts from your customers. 


 

How can I improve my Defective Rate? 

To improve your rate, you need to understand the root causes of your customer contacts.
 

While analyzing the data, determine whether the root causes are: 

  • Sourcing issues (either the item or the spare parts)
  • Testing problems (perhaps there’s a better software solution)
  • Human error (maybe your technicians could use more training)
  • Refurbishment process issues 
  • Or something else
Title: Defective Rate (over 90 days)

Details__c:
Purchasing qualitative components and accessories at a competitive price can be a challenge for Refurbishers.

 Yet using qualitative products allows sellers to:

  • lower their defective rate and return rate ↘️
  • raise their quality score, prices and customer satisfaction ↗️

For this reason, Back Market’s Innovation Lab has evaluated various suppliers, analyzing the performance, quality and security of their products.

This thorough review led to the selection of a portfolio of qualitative and certified components and accessories offered by European and American suppliers. The components and accessories that are already available for sale are:

  • iPhone batteries
  • iPhone screens
  • earphones and chargers for iOS & Android (smartphones & tablets)

We are working hard to rapidly include parts for other categories of devices (such as MacBook and iPad) and will of course keep you updated on our progress.

All necessary information on how to order are indicated in this article. If you still have questions, feel free to contact our team directly through the Seller Support Center, "Back Market Services" form.
Title: Components & Accessories recommended by Back Market

Details__c:
➡️ A new version of the address book has been released recently in Europe and in the United States. 

To find your address book, first, go to your Back Office under the 'Options' tab, then select the 'Address' tab to find the 'Address' section:


 

  • We also prevent you from deleting addresses that are used for the Trade-in service or BackShip pickup.
  • Same rule for return addresses: you cannot delete your return addresses.

New features are live:

You can now add the address rule for product return !

Below your addresses, you will see a section called “Address rules”.

Click on “Add a new rule”:

A panel will be displayed on the right-hand side of the page, with a multiple steps funnel:

  1. Select the address you want to use
  2. Select the Process you want to assign the address to - For now, only product return is available. But we will enrich the list in the coming weeks 😉
  3. Select the market(s) of the orders you want to target. You can select specific market(s) or “All markets”. This option will consider all markets you are online in, and the future ones.
  4. Select the product category(ies) you want to target. You can select specific a category(ies) or “All categories”. This option will consider all product categories you are currently selling, and the future ones.
  5. A Summary. So you review the scope of the address rule you are creating before validation.

You will be able to use address A for returns from France if the category is smartphone for instance, or use address B for returns from Spain, and use address C for all other scenarios.

Address Rules management:

Once you have created your first address rule(s), they will be listed below your addresses. You will be able to amend or delete them.

What’s next?

In the coming weeks, we are planning to enrich the list of processes, so you can build more automation and more control over your logistic processes. E.g. Return address for Trade-in orders, Pickup point for BackShip orders, etc...
Title: How can I manage my address book?
