Details__c:
What’s the STAR rate?

The Satisfaction Ticket After Resolution (STAR) rate is a KPI that measures customer satisfaction after Trade-in interactions. Think of this rate as a way for your customers to show you some love!

 

How does it work?

Seven days after a Trade-in interaction, Back Market sends a survey to your customer, asking them to rate their experience with you in 3 specific categories: speed, courtesy, and relevance.

Here are some ways to guarantee a sky-high rate every time:

  • Speed: 
    • Process all orders within 2 business days of receiving them (see LPR action plan for more details)
    • Respond to all customer inquiries within 24 hours (see LRR action plan for more details)
  • Courtesy: 
    • Ensure your communication is objective, helpful, and kind
  • Relevance: 
    • Follow all processes and regulations found in the Back Market CGU and Quality Charter
    • Avoid making unnecessary counteroffers (see CO Rate action plan for more details)
    • Confirm necessary counteroffers are fair to customers to avoid rejected offers (see Cancellation Rate action plan for more details) 

Your seller’s STAR rate is then calculated by dividing the number of positive survey responses by the total number of survey responses. This rate can be found on your dashboard and reflects the last 30 days of Trade-in interactions.

 

How can I improve my STAR rate?

You can boost your STAR rate by providing consistent, high-quality service. This means your customers will be more likely to leave positive reviews and return for additional devices in the future.

If you have any questions, get in touch with your Back Market representative.
Title: What's Trade-in's STAR rate?

Details__c:
PayPal Disputes

This article aims at providing you guidelines to follow in case a customer creates a dispute on PayPal. For general information on this payment method, you may discover more insights about how does Paypal work on Back Market in the recommended article.

Please note that these guidelines aim at helping you be proactive, however we cannot guarantee that PayPal will solve the dispute in your favor by providing the information mentioned here.


Overview on PayPal Disputes

When do customers open disputes?
Customers purchasing through PayPal have the right to dispute the transaction in case an issue occurs, see the PayPal guidelines on this topic.

For your information, generally on our platform customers tend to dispute the delivery of the product or that the device purchased does not match the description of the listing. Customers may also open a dispute if the refund of the order fails (to avoid this from happening, see the paragraph “Known issues, 2)” in our article PayPal: How does it work on Back Market? 


What is the role of Back Market when a customer disputes a transaction? 

Please note that Back Market is not able to influence PayPal's decisions in any way. Opening a dispute on a transaction is a solution customers may opt for if they experience an issue. We are not able to contact PayPal to provide advice though, as PayPal operates independently. 

In case of a PayPal dispute, please make sure you provide as much evidence as possible within the timeframe given by PayPal, in order to build a case in your favor.
 

What evidence should I provide to PayPal?

PayPal allows you to challenge what the customer declares within a specific timeframe, as mentioned in their guidelines. 

As soon as you are notified (generally on PayPal) that a dispute has been opened on a given transaction, reach out to PayPal directly and provide as much information as possible to prove that you have acted correctly. 

Some evidence that you should think of providing may be:

  • Extract of the conversation with the customer in cases of disputes on defective products
  • Details of shipments or links to the shipping provider's website in cases of disputes on non-received parcels
  • Any other evidence you may think of (screenshots from the Back Office to prove that the order exists on Back Market, etc.)


Use Case

For example, a customer ordered an iPhone 11 and has opened a PayPal dispute to claim that they never received the parcel. You, on your side, have all the elements to prove that you acted as expected. You can reach out to PayPal as soon as you are notified of the dispute creation and provide:

  • The tracking link showing that the parcel was sent from your warehouse to the customer’s address
  • The weight of the parcel, to show that it matches the expected weight of this kind of product
  • Proof of delivery with the signature of the customer
  • Screenshots from your database, showing that the device with this IMEI was shipped to the customer
  • Screenshots from our Back Office, showing that the order was placed through Back Market, or the conversation with the customer
  • ...

Such elements can really help you build the case in your favour and increase the possibility to win the dispute. 
 

What is the role of the SSC?

While we are always available to provide further insights or advice on specific cases, bear in mind that you need to seek any further assistance from PayPal directly in case you do not agree with the outcome of a specific case. As specified in our T&Cs, you need to rely on the Seller Protection Policy of PayPal.
Title: PayPal Disputes

Details__c:
Phonecheck now supports Apple Watch data collection and erasure. In this article, we’ll teach you how to do it.

Hardware requirements: 
  • All-in-one iBUS tool to connect the Apple Watch to Phonecheck
  • Lightning cable
  • Phonecheck station



Software requirements:
Apple Watch erasure is available for Mac only at this time (1.3.662.3). Apple Watch data collection is available for Mac and Windows.

Here are a few tips:
  • Apple Watch erasure on Windows will be added at a later date
  • Apple Watch erasure is user-guided
  • Apple Watch series 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, and series SE are supported
  • Apple Watch series 6, 7, 8, SE2, and Ultra aren’t supported

Apple Watch data collection includes:
  • Make
  • Model
  • Model number
  • Regulatory model number
  • Serial, storage
  • Color, software version
  • FMI status
  • Jailbreak 
  • Battery metrics




This will help you quickly know if an Apple Watch is iCloud locked and its battery health with no need to activate or synch it with an iPhone.

Here’s how you do this:
  1. Remove the cap protecting the connector (only available in series 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, and Series SE) with tweezers.
  2. Connect the Apple Watch to the all-in-one iBUS tool.
  3. The Apple Watch will show up on Phonecheck.
  4. Information will show up automatically.
  5. Report will be available in your console.
Title: How to connect Apple Watches to Phonecheck for data collection and erasure

Details__c:
What is the Appearance Rate?

This rate reflects how closely you follow Back Market grading standards, how well you clean your devices before sending them to your customer, and how well items are protected during delivery. 

 

How is this rate calculated?

The number of orders supposed to be delivered in the past 30 days with customer contact linked to physical appearance issues (for example, scratches, dirty items, etc.) divided by the total number of orders supposed to be delivered in the past 30 days, including cancelled orders.

 

How does this rate impact me?

This rate highly influences customer trust in refurbished items and impacts you as a seller, as a commercial gesture will be offered to your customer in case of grade discrepancy. 

The Appearance Rate also impacts your ability to win the BackBox and may lead to sales suspensions if some of your item categories are among the worst compared to the average seller on Back Market. If this is the case, your listings in this item category will be suspended for 1 week.

 

Where can I find my rate?

You’ll find your Appearance Rate and more details in your Quality Insights dashboard.
Title: Appearance Rate (over 30 days)

Details__c:
To understand how our marketplace works, it's very important to understand the differences between a product and a listing. 
 

  • A product refers to a device with several attributes (model, brand, color, weight, operator, processor, etc.)
  • A listing refers to the offer that a seller makes on this product.

Each seller can create a listing on an existing product in our database.

If the product you want to sell does not exist in our system, you can create it. After that, each seller will be able to create a listing on the product you have created.

You will be able to have various listings on the same product, just like other sellers: 



You can also differentiate between product and listing information directly on our website: 



 

Product information will never change, while listing information (such as price, warranty period, delivery options) may change over time.
Title: Product vs. Listing

Details__c:
In cases of disputes related to shipping, it’s your sole responsibility to file a claim with your shipping provider and provide the necessary documents.

To streamline the customer experience, Back Market has simplified the process for customers by:

  • Allowing them 14 days to report cases of damage, empty packages, or incorrect / missing products. Don’t worry, it is only to cover the edge cases where customers are not present the days following the delivery. We do not communicate this change proactively to the customers.
  • Considering a package lost after 6 days without tracking updates, triggering a refund or the seller sending a new package 
  • No longer requiring the certificate of non-receipt (which will no longer be sent to you)


This doesn’t mean that we’re relaxing our fraud detection though. We’ve increased detection methods with additional customer verification if we suspect fraud.


Delivered not received

If the shipping provider rejects your claim due to the failure to provide the required documents, you may request compensation from the Seller Support Center, provided that you:

  • Submit your compensation request within 30 days after the refund date (the shipment date applies if there is not delivery date).
  • Submit a request to the Seller Support Center by categorizing the request as "Non-Backship shipping or delivery".
  • Provide all information and documents requested by Back Market agents.
  • Prove that the shipping provider/insurer rejected your claim due to a lack of required documents, which should have been provided by Back Market.
  • Show that you have ad valorem insurance for the disputed parcel.

If you’re unable to prove that you have ad valorem insurance, compensation will be limited to the legal maximum. In any case, compensation will be capped at 400 € per parcel.

This compensation offered by Back Market doesn’t apply to cases of loss or damage for which the shipping provider is responsible.


Damage, incorrect / missing product

Please note that if the shipping provider rejects your claim because the customer reported the case after 3 days, you may request compensation from the Seller Support Center, provided that you:

  • Submit your compensation request within 30 days after the delivery date (the shipment date applies if there is not delivery date).
  • Submit a request to the Seller Support Center by categorizing the request as "Non-Backship shipping or delivery".
  • Provide all information and documents requested by Back Market agents.
  • Prove that the shipping provider/insurer rejected your claim because of the exceeded deadline. 

 

Big investments to detect fraud and prevent fraudsters

Here’s how we’re investing in fraud detection before it impacts your business: 

  • A task force of about 100 people to handle shipping incidents and identify potential fraud cases.
  • An algorithm based on automatic feedback from our anti-fraud service providers.
  • Scoring based on the type and order history of customers allowing us to better assess risk and be more cautious with high-value orders.
  • Additional identity verifications with photo IDs, Liveness detection and AI algorithms.

If fraud is suspected, we’ll ask the customer for a police report and live identity test before refunding the customer. 

We’re closely monitoring the delivery incident rate and have not seen an increase in refund requests since the launch of this new process. 

If you suspect fraud with one of your shipped orders, submit a request to the Seller Support Center so we can analyze the case. Note that parcels must be insured for you to be refunded.

If your shipping providers aren’t performing, explore our BackShip services.
Title: How to manage a transport issue (lost, stolen, damaged parcel) ?

Details__c:
At Back Market, our commitment to customer satisfaction goes beyond the sale. We expect sellers to uphold strict after-sales standards as outlined in our charter, ensuring a seamless experience for customers when things don't go as planned. Here's a breakdown of key compliance points and what happens when they aren't met.

 

Deadlines for repairs & replacements: Return compliance rate

Sellers must act quickly to resolve customer issues:

 

  • 3 business days to repair or replace (5 business days for laptops, MacBooks and home appliances) and upload the tracking number in the Back Office, starting from the day the returned product is received.
  • The product must be picked up by the shipping provider the next business day after repair or replacement.
  • Sellers must upload a valid tracking number for every return in the Back Office.
  • Tracking information must be entered before the shipping provider picks up the parcel.

Example:

 

If a seller receives a smartphone return on Monday, they must repair or replace it and upload the tracking number by Thursday evening. The shipping provider should pick up the product on Friday.

Tip: We highly recommend repairing and shipping products back as quickly as possible—sooner than the maximum deadlines!

 

Consequences of non-compliance

Slow handling times directly impact customer satisfaction, that is why we are tracking “Return compliance rate” that is negatively impacted by the following non-compliances :

 

  • No tracking
  • Wrong tracking
  • Late tracking
  • Late pickup

To protect our customers and our promise, Back Market enforces the following actions:

 

  • If deadlines are missed, our agents will issue a refund to the customer.
  • Frequent Non-compliance (above average):
    • 1st Notice: Seller receives an alert about a high rate of non-compliances.
    • 2nd Notice: If the issue persists, Back Market will deactivate the repair/replace option for the seller for at least 30 days until the seller can prove compliance.
      • This means customers will only be able to request a refund, and the seller must refund 100% of all returns.

We target 97% performance. If your performance falls below 90%, you will receive a warning email. If your performance remains below 80% for two consecutive months following two warnings, we will deactivate the "repair/replace" options. This means you will be required to refund returns for a minimum of one month and until you provide an action plan demonstrating how you will achieve 90% compliance. 

Multiple returns on the same product: Repeated returns rate

We closely monitor cases where a customer must return the same product more than once within 30 days (from the first return). These multiple issue returns are a key indicator of service quality.

 

  • Keep it below 5%: Sellers must keep their multiple issue return rate under this threshold.

If a product is returned twice or more, our agent will process a refund unless the customer specifically requests another repair.

If the rates are significantly above average:

 

  • 1st Notice: Seller receives an alert.
  • 2nd Notice: Repair/replace options are deactivated; only refunds will be possible, and all returns must be refunded.

Refund deadlines: On-time refund rate

Sellers have 2 business days after receiving a returned product to issue a refund. If the refund deadline passes, our agents will process the refund automatically.

 

How to track it and stay compliant

You receive monthly data on all these indicators, including details on the impacted orders. Keep an eye on emails from "Sellers Quality Back Market" to stay updated and take action if needed.
Title: After-sales seller performance: non-compliance & its impacts

Details__c:
You might want to add multiple users in your Back Office to avoid sharing your login information with every person who needs to access your Back Market account. And now it’s possible to add additional users.


Jump to
How do I add a new user to my account?
How do I select which pages the user can access?
How do I delete a user?


As the admin user, or primary user, you’ll be able to:

  • See existing users
  • Add new users
  • Delete existing users
  • Decide which pages users can access
  • Manage API tokens for the account as only the admin user has access to the integration page 

 



 

How do I add a new user to my account?

  1. Log into your Back Office > “Your profile”.
  2. Select the “User permissions” tab. 
  3. Select “Add new user”.
  4. Enter the user's first name, last name, and email address.
  5. This user will receive an email with a link to the Back Office. They will need to change their password before logging in.

 

How do I select which pages the user can access?

  1. Select a user profile on the user permissions page.
  2. Select the pages the user should have access to.


 




 

How do I delete a user?

  1. Select a user profile on the user permissions page.
  2. Select the “Delete” button next to the user you want to delete.

 

If you found this article clear and helpful, hit the like button to let us know.
Title: How do I add multiple users to my Back Office?

Details__c:
As part of our continuous effort to elevate the quality of refurbished devices available on Back Market, we’re introducing a new initiative designed to enhance our quality control measures. This initiative aims to promote high-quality products and boost overall customer satisfaction through third-party testing experts specializing in smartphone diagnostics and data erasure solutions.

Jump to
Mandatory use of vetted software for smartphone testing
Reported test results
How does this benefit me?
Q&A


Mandatory use of vetted software for smartphone testing

To ensure consistency and high standards, all sellers will be required to test their smartphones before shipping with one of the approved testing software solutions for smartphone diagnostics listed below.
 

NameApproval
PhoneCheckApproved
Blackbelt Smartphone DefenceApproved
FutureDialApproved
NSYSApproved
ICE (MannapovLLC)Approved
BlanccoApproved
M360Approved
DrFonesApproved
PiceasoftApproved

 

Disclaimer: Back Market has vetted certain software vendors for functional testing of refurbished phones. This vetting process focuses solely on the technical capabilities of the diagnostic software to perform functional tests on refurbished devices. It’s important to note that Back Market has not conducted any financial review of these software vendors. The responsibility for selecting and using these diagnostic tools remains with the Back Market sellers. Back Market is not liable for any issues arising from the use of these software solutions. Sellers are solely responsible for ensuring the quality and functionality of the devices they list on our platform.

 

Sellers should be aware that while these vetted software options have been identified as capable of performing necessary diagnostic tests, the ultimate choice and implementation of any testing software is at the discretion of the seller. Back Market’s vetting process does not constitute an endorsement or guarantee of the software’s performance or the vendor’s financial stability



Test results must cover all product functionalities, including at least all of Back Market’s quality-control checkpoints listed in Appendix 1 of our Quality Charter.

If you currently use a different test software that isn’t approved or if you test manually, you have until the end of December 2024 to comply with this requirement. 

In the meantime, you can reach out to your Back Market point of contact to explore how we can support you (such as with negotiated pricing) or if you want us to consider adding another software to the list.
 

Reported test results

To streamline the quality control process and reduce your administrative tasks, Back Market will work with testing software providers to collect testing results and device specifics for the smartphones you sell on our platform.

Using the IMEI numbers of the smartphones you sold (already collected to ensure safety and customer satisfaction), we’ll ensure that the product was properly tested before shipment. No personal data will be collected and the results will not be disclosed to external parties (with the exception of your customer).

We’ll use the gathered information solely for analysis, quality control, and after-sales solutions. All data will be securely stored on our servers and permanently erased following the expiration of all applicable warranties.
 

How does this benefit me?

  • Fairer competition: Quality Charter compliance ensures a level playing field for all sellers.
  • Higher-quality products: Sellers who consistently provide high-quality devices will be offered opportunities to win exclusive BackBoxes like Premium or New Battery offers, selling more and maintaining better prices.
  • More efficient and reliable testing: Thanks to the meta-analysis of our after-sales data, we’ll work with software providers on improved tests to spot potential failures and reduce defective occurrences.
  • Improved customer satisfaction: Thanks to this new guarantee that all devices have been thoroughly tested, customer satisfaction will increase and customer contact resolution will improve. 
  • Enhanced trust and credibility: Compliance with these new requirements will help you build trust with customers and the Back Market community.


Q&A

Questions related to the collection of testing results

Will Back Market access test results automatically from the approved software service providers? Or should sellers upload them like they do with IMEIs?Sellers will not have to upload anything more than the IMEI they already provide at the time of shipment.

Back Market will directly connect to the cloud of the approved software provider to retrieve the test results associated with the IMEI provided by the seller.
Will Back Market be able to access the test results for all phones sold on the platform?
 		No. Automatic access to test results will be granted by sellers to Back Market only for phones sold on the platform starting August 10, 2024, which is the date the updated Seller’s Terms and Conditions and Quality Charter took effect.

Rest assured, Back Market will never be able to access test results from phones not sold on Back Market. Back Market will not have access to the results of your whole inventory.
Does Phonecheck share seller  data without their prior approval? Is that legal?


How can Phonecheck get seller  data without approval?


What data is shared with BackMarket exactly? 


Is getting the test results a breach of GDPR?		None of the software approved providers have shared seller data so far. 

Back Market will only be able to access test results for smartphones sold on the platform starting August 10, 2024.

Back Market will only have access to test results that include the following data: device identification data (model, color, serial number, etc.), functional test results (for example, camera check OK/KO), and part status (battery health, genuine parts, etc.).

Back Market will not access any personal data nor sensitive information (such as supplier name, operator name, etc.). Therefore there will be no breach of GDPR.
When are sellers required to provide testing reports?Sellers are required to use vetted software by January 1, 2025. 
Back Market will start pulling test reports once the API integrations with approved software providers are built, but no sooner than August 10, 2024.

Sellers will not be asked to provide test reports for all orders themselves.

Be reminded that until the API connection with your provider is live, you can still be asked to provide some reports on request as per the existing Quality Charter.
What if a seller’s supplier uses one of the approved software? How would sellers give access to Back Market?For all software providers, the entry point to collect the test results will be the IMEI. On top of this, some software providers will require that the account owner who tested the device share some credentials or security keys. 

Note: The information needed and processed to collect them per provider will be shared to sellers as the different integrations are built.

Sellers are responsible to make sure that all smartphones sold on Back Market from August 10, 2024 can produce compliant test results from 1 approved software provider and that these test results will be directly accessible to Back Market. 

It’s highly recommended that sellers test the smartphones themselves. However, if a seller’s supplier agrees to grant Back Market access to their data and the seller can provide all due information to access such data (such as security keys or credentials), Back Market will proceed to the collection of testing results as for any other sellers. 

In this case, it’s the seller’s duty to ensure their suppliers consent to data sharing, and Back Market will hold the seller accountable in case of issue with testing result collection. 
How will Back Market know which software sellers use to be able to pull the data?Although today, Back Market doesn’t know the exact software used by each of seller, it has managed to get an initial sense of the most commonly used software through the analysis of: 
Reports shared by some sellers within customer service requests or directly with our Quality Team
Information gathered by Seller Success Managers during conversations with their sellers

For Phonecheck integration in particular (the first one built), as soon as the requirement is enforced, Back Market will run the database of smartphones sold after August 10 through the Phonecheck API and should be able to identify sellers using this solution more accurately. 

As Back Market starts integrating more approved software providers, sellers might be asked to provide the software they use through their Back Office. In such case, Back Market will communicate this request to sellers so that they can provide the information.
What if some devices that sellers  buy are already pre-tested by our supplier and they use a different software to test?It’s the seller’s responsibility to comply with the Quality Charter, and this includes the new policy around testing. In this situation, the seller should run all the quality control checks using approved software. 
Can sellers use their own test reports?Yes, under specific conditions. For sellers using their own built-in software, exceptions can be considered on a case by case basis, provided that all of these conditions are met: 
The seller’s sales should be high enough to make the integration worth it (that is, the same order of magnitude as the sales coverage of other vetted provider).
The functional coverage of the solution must be aligned with Back Market quality standards. 
The Seller should be able to share test results with Back Market (method to be discussed and agreed).

Sellers in this situation are encouraged to reach out to their Back Market point of contact.

 

Questions related to device testing

 

What tests must sellers run with the software?All functional tests available for the device must be run, including all the mandatory checkpoints listed in the Appendix 1 of the Quality Charter.
What if sellers run a full test and then only re-test 1 feature before shipment (for example, FaceID). How will Back Market know the product was fully functional? 

If a product was in a seller’s  stock for a long time, like 1 year, are sellers required to conduct a new test before selling it?		All devices sold on the platform should be able to produce at least 1 test report showing all functional tests successfully passed, as well as compliant battery status. 

This report should be no more than 2 months old at the time of sale (as per the existing Quality Charter). This means any device sitting in inventory for more than 2 months after the initial testing should be tested again prior shipment. 

If several reports exist for the same IMEI, Back Market will use the latest full report dated before the date of shipment provided that no later partial results contradict them.

Examples:
  • Situation 1: A report showing 100% functionality and compliance with our standards is dated 1 month prior to the sales >> OK
  • Situation 2: Situation 1 + a later report with only FaceID tested and functional is produced the day of the order >> OK
  • Situation 3: Situation 1 + a later report with only FaceID tested and not functional is produced the day of the order >> NOT OK
  • Situation 4: Situation 1 + a later report with only FaceID tested and not functional is produced after the shipment (for example, returns following a defective) >> OK

 

Questions related to the use of testing results

Is providing a test report required only in cases of defective devices received? Or is it for all smartphones sold?Back Market will access test results for all phones in order to: 
Proactively confirm smartphones are compliant with Back Market  testing requirements (all functionalities successfully tested, battery health compliant, etc.)
Leverage these test results in the customer experience (for example, to build reassurance post purchase)
Will these test reports be available for the customer support center?Yes, Back Market is aiming to share these test results. However the implementation date is not defined yet (not before 2025).  Different integrations should be built first.
Since sellers are being forced to use 1 of the Back Market approved testing software solutions, does this mean the reps have to start taking the reports as proof of functionality, that there is no issue with a device? Sellers have reps denying PhoneCheck test results as evidence that there is no issue with returns for repairs/replacements.Yes, test results can be used in the resolution of a service request (as it’s the case today).  

Test results are not indisputable proof that nothing is wrong with the product. There are some failures/issues that cannot be spotted by standard testing (for example, network issues related to RF weaknesses, rapid battery drainage linked to overconsuming parts, etc.).

Especially during the cancellation period, the test report cannot be used to refuse a return. Athougth it can be used to reassure the customer.

Also note that Back Market will be pulling test results before shipment and not after. Test results related to returns will still have to be provided by sellers.
Are you aware that a new battery percentage (that is, 100%) will not show up on many testing reports, as removal of update flex is necessary? Yes, Back Market is aware and knows that Apple will soon re-authorize SOH in the settings when a compatible battery is used (timeline still to be confirmed). This shouldn’t be an issue anymore in the near future.
Certain smartphones do not pull battery percentages. How will that work? Back Market knows this and will not use the SOH data when the model is not compatible with SOH reading.
How will Back Market handle the gaps in the software capability? Back Market approved software have, most of the time, very similar diagnostic capabilities. When/if a solution presents a flaw in their testing capability, sellers are encouraged to reach out to them but can also reach out to Back Market and we will raise the feedback to them and work with them to improve their coverage.
How long will the test results be stored?The test results will be stored until the end of all applicable warranties (commercial or legal warranties, as per Seller’s Terms and Conditions). After that, all associated data will be deleted.
As for pulling results, how will assessing grading work? Every company has different grading standards and that cosmetic difference may not be captured by testing software.The testing results Back Market will pull are related to functional diagnosis, the grading information when filled by the seller will not be taken into account. Grading will remain a declarative information of a listing. 

Sellers must comply with Back Market grading requirement and therefore match their internal grading standards to Back Market requirements when listing.

 

Questions related to the partnerships with software companies

Is this new policy is just a money grab for software providers?We know that testing represents a cost for sellers. We believe that software testing is helping sellers with their productivity, traceability, and quality, and represents a good investment versus manual testing. This is something confirmed by the fact that a lot of our sellers are already using a software for testing.

Among the providers vetted, we have different price ranges and different value propositions so that sellers can choose freely what is best for their need and cost structure. 
Does Back Market expect market prices to go up to cover this or the supply of devices to go down?As testing is a universal practice of the refurbished industry already required by all marketplaces for all products sold, and as the usage of testing software is a very common market practice (required by some buyers already), and although sellers remain free to define their prices,  this should not significantly impact prices or supply available. 
Have you negotiated a standard rate for Back Market sellers? Or are they free to charge sellers on their discretion?

Does Back Market negotiate special prices for sellers who want to get a testing software?		Back Market negotiated preferential rates with most approved providers that sellers can benefit from. 

In any case, sellers remain free of their choice of software provider among the vetted partners and Back Market is not involved in any way in the commercial relationship between sellers and software providers. 

Sellers are encouraged to negotiate their own terms and prices if they think they can get better deals.
Will Back Market take a commission on the license fee invoiced by testing softwares?No, Back Market does not take any commission. On the contrary our objective when we started building the partnerships with software providers has always been to better serve sellers and make this as easy as possible for them. 

This is why we have vetted so many providers, why we want to collect results directly from them instead of asking sellers to upload them, and this is also why we have negotiated preferential commercial conditions with each of them. 
If a seller uses software that isn’t approved,  can it be added  to the list?Back Market can consider the addition of other approved partners if:
Various sellers use the solution so that it makes sense to build the integration.
The functional coverage of the solution is aligned with Back Market quality standards.
The provider agrees to make the test results directly accessible to Back Market under the Quality Charter terms.

The updated list of approved software as well as software we’re in discussion with can be found in this article.
Will testing software be mandatory for other categories? Our vision to make testing the cornerstone of customer trust applies to the whole industry, and this is why testing is critical for all categories and why we have always had control checkpoints for all categories in the Quality Charter. 

That being said, test results collection applies only to smartphones at the moment. We’ll be considering the extension of this requirement to other categories in the future, but when the time comes sellers will be notified with enough time given to comply as we did for smartphones. 
In any case, we already strongly encourage all sellers in other categories to implement market standard traceability system with serial number tracking and standardized testing records.
Title: Smartphone testing: approved softwares and results collection

Details__c:
To ensure the highest quality and customer satisfaction, all laptops and computers must undergo comprehensive refurbishment, including thorough testing of hardware and software components, meticulous cleaning to remove dust and debris, and secure packaging to protect the devices during transit. Adhering to these standards guarantees that each product meets our rigorous performance and aesthetic criteria.

 

Note: 

OEM battery charge limiting features (Dell Battery Charge Configuration, HP Battery Health Manager, Lenovo Conservation Mode) artificially cap Full Charge Capacity in Windows battery reports, leading to perceived “low battery health”.

 

For any laptop, the battery might be reported as 80% or less, it may be a saving feature (not a defect). This intentional cap helps extend long‑term battery lifespan by limiting the maximum charge. If you want the battery to reach full capacity, disable Energy Saving / Battery Conservation / Battery Protection mode (name varies by brand). Then charge the laptop again and check the battery level.

 

Recommended Action - Mandatory Pre-Shipment: 

 

  • Disable ALL charge limits via respective BIOS/Vantage settings
  • Verify 100% charge capability

 

Jump to :

 

  • Gaming Computers
  • Gaming Laptops
  • Office Laptops
  • Desktop Computers
  • Notebooks & Ultrabooks

Gaming Computers

Refurbishing & Repairs- Replace faulty components (GPU, CPU, RAM, SSD/HDD, PSU, cooling).
- Apply high-quality thermal paste.
- Upgrade BIOS & firmware.
- Ensure PSU meets power requirements.
- Secure all cables and internal components.
Testing & Performance- Full POST test.
- Stress-test CPU/GPU with Cinebench, FutureMark, 3DMark (30 min).
- Memory diagnostics (MemTest86).
- Check storage health (CrystalDiskInfo).
- Verify motherboard ports (USB, HDMI, Ethernet, Wi-Fi).
- Benchmark gaming performance.
Cleaning & Aesthetics- Remove dust with compressed air.
- Deep clean case, remove scratches if possible.
- Secure all internal components.
- Replace or clean thermal pads.
- Ensure no rattling parts inside.
Packaging & Shipping- Use anti-static bags for internal components.
- Secure GPU & CPU cooler with protective padding.
- Use foam inserts/custom padding inside the case.
- Ship in a sturdy, double-walled box.

 

Gaming Laptops

Refurbishing & Repairs- Replace faulty parts (GPU, RAM, SSD/HDD, cooling system, battery).
- Apply new thermal paste to CPU & GPU.
- Upgrade BIOS & firmware.
- Check & replace worn-out keyboard, trackpad, and hinges.
- Ensure battery health is above 80%/85%.
Testing & Performance- Full POST test. - Stress-test CPU/GPU.
- Battery health test (charge cycles & capacity).
- Verify RGB, keyboard, trackpad, webcam, and speakers.
- Check all ports (USB, HDMI, audio, charging).
- Benchmark gaming performance.
Cleaning & Aesthetics- Deep clean chassis, keyboard, screen, and fans.
- Clean screen to remove smudges.
- Ensure no loose hinges or damaged frame.
- Remove dust from vents and cooling system.
Packaging & Shipping- Use shockproof packaging.
- Include original charger or compatible replacement.
- Label package as FRAGILE.

 

Office Laptops

Refurbishing & Repairs- Replace faulty parts (RAM, SSD/HDD, battery, keyboard, screen).
- Apply new thermal paste to the CPU. 
- Upgrade BIOS & firmware.
- Ensure battery health is above 80%.
- Check & replace worn-out ports, hinges, and fans.
Testing & Performance- Full POST test.
- Battery health test (capacity >80%).
- Stress-test CPU.
- Verify keyboard, trackpad, and webcam.
- Test all ports (USB, HDMI, Ethernet, audio, charging).
Cleaning & Aesthetics- Deep clean keyboard, screen, chassis, and ports.
- Clean screen to remove fingerprints.
- Remove dust from fans and vents.
- Ensure no loose parts or hinge issues.
Packaging & Shipping- Use padded laptop sleeves & anti-static bags.
- Wrap in bubble wrap & ship in a secure laptop box.
- Include original charger or compatible replacement.
- Label package as FRAGILE.

 

Desktop Computers

Refurbishing & Repairs- Replace defective parts (CPU, RAM, SSD/HDD, PSU).
- Apply thermal paste to the CPU. 
- Upgrade BIOS & firmware.
- Secure all internal connections.
- Ensure PSU is stable and noise-free.
Testing & Performance- Full POST test.
- Stress-test CPU.
- Memory diagnostics (MemTest86).
- Check storage health.
- Verify all ports (USB, HDMI, Ethernet, Wi-Fi).
- Boot test with pre-installed OS.
Cleaning & Aesthetics- Clean dust from internal & external parts.
- Ensure no major scratches or dents.
- Secure cables and connectors.
Packaging & Shipping- Use anti-static bags for internal components.
- Secure PSU & motherboard to avoid movement.
- Use protective padding inside the case
- Ship in a sturdy, well-cushioned box.

 

Notebooks & Ultrabooks

Refurbishing & Repairs- Replace faulty parts (RAM, SSD, battery, keyboard, screen).
- Upgrade BIOS & firmware.
- Apply new thermal paste to the CPU. 
- Ensure battery health is above 85%.
- Verify hinge and chassis integrity.
Testing & Performance- Full POST test. - Battery health test (capacity >85%).
- Verify keyboard, trackpad, webcam, fingerprint reader (if applicable).
- Test all ports (USB-C, Thunderbolt, HDMI, audio, charging).
- Boot test with pre-installed OS.
Cleaning & Aesthetics- Deep clean keyboard, screen, and chassis.
- Clean screen
- Ensure no visible wear on hinges.
- Remove dust from internal components.
Packaging & Shipping- Use padded sleeves & anti-static bags.
- Wrap in bubble wrap & ship in a secure, lightweight box. 
- Include USB-C/thunderbolt charger (if required).
- Label as FRAGILE.
Title: Technical requirements on Computers refurbishment

Details__c:
Starting 4 August 2025, annual T&Cs and Quality Charter updates will be enforced. 
You will find below the major changes compared to the last update (February 2025 for T&Cs and August 2024 for the Charter).
 

T&Cs updates: 

  • Design: We’ve redesigned our Sellers Terms and Conditions to make them more attractive and easier to read. It’s the same design as the Quality Charter.
  • Monthly subscription fees: Increase from 50 to 75€/£/$.
    • To allow BM to invest in enhanced seller support services, better marketplace infrastructure.
  • Deletion of Back Repair in the EU, and of Back Ship in the US
    • This update reflects the deletion of these services that has been in effect for several months. We're focusing resources on core marketplace features that directly impact seller success.
  • Launch Phase: Specification that it can be ended with a 1-day notice.
    • Focus on quality so that only high quality Sellers are onboarded
  • Trade-in Logistics fees (EU only): updates and increase of the logistics fees (+1€ or +2€), depending on the country of delivery of the product, i.e. the location where the Seller has their warehouse.
    • To reflect current market conditions and operational costs in different regions.
    • If the Seller is based in FR/DE/ES: Smartphones & Audio 11.90€ / Tablets 13.90€ / MacBooks & Consoles 14.90€
    • If the Seller is based in any other EU country: Smartphones & Audio 12.90€ / Tablets 13.90€ / MacBooks & Consoles 15.90€
  • Trade-in updates to BackBox: it now takes into account (i) the price offer, (ii) the Refurbisher’s counteroffer rate, and (iii) the Refurbisher’s circularity rate
    • Rewards to Sellers who maintain high circularity rates and competitive pricing, creating fairer competition for winning the BackBox position. 
  • Updates to the trade-in invoicing and payment terms article to account for trade-in
    • Clearer payment processes for trade-in transactions 
  • Updates to the deferred payout to take into consideration Trade-in
    • Better integration of trade-in payments with deferred payout structures
  • Back Market Personal Data Charter: Sellers can now find best practices for protecting Buyers' personal data in the Personal Data Charter.
    • Clear data protection guidelines help sellers maintain compliance with privacy regulations.

 

Quality Charter Sales part updates: 

General update: From now on, we will be counting only business days.

Article 1.1 Item quality control

  • Smartphones batteries in the US & UK must have initial capacities of at least 80% and no more than 700 cycle counts. Tablet, smart watches, and laptop batteries must have initial capacities of at least 85% and no more than 650 cycle counts.
  • MacBooks and iMacs must be equipped with MacOs 11 or higher
  • All Windows PC computers (laptops and desktops) must be reset with a Windows  11  license (a valid Windows 11  item key must have been provided or an OEM key validated during installation)
  • All iPhones and iPads must be restored to the latest supported iOS version released not more than 2 months prior to your customers’ purchases.

 

Article 1.3 Appearance: cleaning and assessing
Engravings are not permitted on products graded Premium, Excellent (Mint & Very Good), or Good. For products in Fair grade (Fair & Stallone), engravings are allowed provided the text does not contain personal data, discriminatory content, hate speech, obscene material, or any other inappropriate messages. Such engraving does not void the customers’ right of withdrawal.

 

Article 1.7 Accessories
For smartphones, tablets, and iPads: a compatible charging cable with surge protection is required.

  • We recommend using a cable of standard length, not exceeding 1 m (40 in), for quality reasons.
  • For ecological reasons, headphones and wall plugs are not mandatory when selling smartphones, tablets, and iPads.

 

Article 2.1 Shipping options
New table with preparation and transit times for all the categories:

  • Category S items must be shipped the same day if the order is placed before cut-off time
  • The default cut-off time is 1:00 PM. You may update this setting in your Back-Office to a later cut-off time if it better reflects your actual operations. For more information, consult the Seller Support Center.
  • We recommend using a secure delivery method (such as signature, PIN code, or QR code) for all orders over 150 €/£/$. Incidents during delivery are significantly reduced when these services are used.
  • We recommend you include the Back market packing slip

 

Previous Article 2.2 “Order preparation and shipment” was deleted, all the information is now available in article 2.1

 

Article 2.2 Serial number and IMEI code

  • You must provide IMEIs for smartphones and the serial numbers for iPads, tablettes, MacBooks and Laptops
  • These numbers must be added in the receipts as well

 

Article 2.3 Sending the receipt

  • The receipts must be issued in the customer’s language. If you fail to provide the receipt in the customer language the customer will be entitled to the goodwill gesture as if you have not provided the receipt
  • If you fail to provide a receipt containing the required information or proof of refund within 2 business days following Back Market’s reminder, the customer is entitled to a free cancellation of their order or a goodwill gesture valued at up to €50/$50/£50

 

Article 3.3 Delivery issues
In cases of shipping-related disputes on forward shipment, it is your sole responsibility to file a claim with your shipping provider and to provide all required documentation (unless you are using our BackShip service).
Back Market will:

  • Allow customers up to 14 days to report cases of damage, empty packages, or incorrect/missing products in specific cases (customer vacation, absent, etc).
  • Consider a package lost after 6 days without tracking updates.
  • No longer provide or require a certificate of non-receipt from customers.

The agent can ask your customer for a police report only when there is a doubt on delivery fraud and if you provide the delivery confirmation within 2 business days following the agent's request for a Proof of delivery.

Upon notification from the agent following case assessment, you must initiate either a refund or shipment of a replacement product within 1 business day, based on the customer's selection. This obligation applies irrespective of the status, progress, or outcome of any shipping provider investigation.

If your shipping provider rejects your claim due to failure to provide required documents, you may request compensation from the Seller Support Center, subject to the following conditions:

  • Submit your compensation request within thirty (30) days of the order refund date.
  • Provide all information and documentation reasonably requested by Back Market agents.
  • Submit proof that your claim was rejected by the shipping provider or insurer specifically due to missing documents that Back Market was responsible for providing.
  • Have used a delivery method requiring signature, PIN code, or QR code verification.

If you provide the proof that you held ad valorem insurance for the disputed parcel, compensation is capped to the amount specified in the insurance policy. 
If you are not able to provide proof of ad valorem insurance, compensation will be limited to 23€/$/GBP per Kg (e.g: If you shipped a 2Kg parcel, then you’ll get back 46€). 

 

Article 3.4.2 Seller contractual warranty: solutions offered
If your customer requests a repair or replacement, you have up to 3 business days from the receipt of the item to repair or replace it and upload the tracking number in the Back Office. The item must be shipped back to the customer no later than the end of the 4th business day—the sooner, the better. For laptops and home appliances, you have up to 5 business days to complete the repair or replacement and upload the tracking number, and the item must be shipped the next business day at the latest. You can provide a refund if a repair or replacement isn’t an option.

In the EU, if a customer submits a technical service request within 6 months of receiving their product and the battery capacity is below 80%, you must replace the battery in addition to performing any other necessary repairs. If you are unable to complete the repair promptly, you must offer a replacement device or a refund.

In the US and UK, the same obligation applies, but the period during which the customer can submit a technical service request is limited to 3 months from the date of receipt.


Article 3.4.3 Multiple-defect policy
If your multiple defects rate is deemed too high by Back Market, we reserve the right to deactivate the repair and/or replacement options for your account. For more detailed information, please refer to the Seller Support Center

 

Article 3.4.4 Seller contractual warranty exclusions
The seller contractual warranty doesn’t cover the following cases:

  • When the product is oxidized or broken
  • When 1 or more of the components of the product have been manipulated by the customer or a third party not authorized by you or Back Market 

However, products with swollen batteries cannot be shipped due to the risk of explosion. Therefore, if a swollen battery is suspected, our team will diagnose the issue and instruct your customer to visit a local repair shop. The repair shop will follow provided guidelines, and costs will be pre-approved according to market standards, not exceeding the value of the device.

If the device is repairable, you are responsible for covering the invoice from the repair shop, regardless of whether the battery is ultimately found to be swollen. If the device cannot be repaired, it will be returned to you with the battery removed, at your expense, and the customer will receive a full refund. In the event that a certified OEM workshop determines the device poses a safety hazard and that battery removal is too dangerous, the customer will be refunded in full and the device will not be returned.


Article 3.5.2 Reception of the product and applicable deadlines
If your customer requests a repair or replacement, you have up to 3 business days from the receipt of the item to repair or replace it and upload the tracking number in the Back Office. The item must be shipped back to the customer no later than the end of the 4th business day—the sooner, the better. For laptops and home appliances, you have up to 5 business days to complete the repair or replacement and upload the tracking number, and the item must be shipped the next business day at the latest.

If you repeatedly fail to meet the repair or replacement deadlines—after receiving at least two warnings from Back Market—we may deactivate the repair option for your account. For more detailed information, please refer to the Seller Support Center.

For parcels that look damaged:

  • Do not refuse the parcel. Instead, express reservations on the proof of delivery when you receive the parcel.
  • Take photos of the outside of the parcel, including a photo with the label visible. Open the parcel and take photos of the content (video works as well).
  • Go to the AfterSales conversation related to the order, and use the preset buttons to declare a problem. Choose "Damaged package and product in transit" and include the photos.
  • The agent will take over the case. If the device is refunded to the customer, you will receive a compensation corresponding to the repair cost of the damage, as stated in the SSC.

 

Article 3.5.5 Order refunds
In cases where accessories are missing (not returned by the customer), you must issue a full refund and submit a refund request through the Seller Support Center.
You can’t deduct the following costs from the refund:

  • Restocking fees
  • Return costs in case your customer refuses the package
  • Missing accessories

 

Article 3.5.7 Declaring a warranty exclusion
If you believe a warranty exclusion applies, you must:

  • Report the warranty exclusion on the Customer Care after-sales platform via the action buttons within 2 business days in case the customer requests a refund and within 3 business days in case the customer requests repair or replacement for all categories. (Reporting a warranty exclusion by message will not work. You must report the exclusion via the action buttons.)
  • Document and explain the warranty exclusion within the same deadline for it to be valid.
  • Return the product to your customer only after the confirmation of Warranty exclusion by Back Market and within 2 business days after this confirmation. Paid repairs are forbidden in this case.

You must use the preset buttons to report any issues with returns by the deadline or the order can be automatically refunded. Any declaration done without using the preset buttons will not be accepted.

If your customer disputes the warranty exclusion, or if Back Market rejects the exclusion due to doubts about its legitimacy, you will be required to proceed with the repair or replacement within the next 2 business days, or issue an immediate refund, as if no warranty exclusion had been applied.

If there is any doubt regarding the authenticity of the device and no IMEI (or serial number) has been provided in the Back Office, Back Market will side with the customer.

For more information refer to the article “Declare and manage a warranty exclusion” in the Seller Support center.

 

Article 3.6 Goodwill gestures
To increase the probability that the customer will keep the device, for all partial refunds or goodwill gestures, the amount will be rounded up to the nearest 5€ or $5. For example, a refund of 7€ will be rounded up to 10€. This does not apply to partial refunds for late express shipping, which will be refunded at the exact shipping cost. 

 

Article 3.6.2 In case of missing or defective accessories
If an accessory is not listed in Appendix 2, and its value is less than 40 EUR/GBP/USD and no more than 10% of the item's price, the Agent may offer a commercial gesture based on the current market price, without needing to request the exact amount from the seller.

 

Article 3.6.3 In case of appearance grade discrepancy

 

Grade ordered

Premium

Excellent

Good

Grade received by the customer

Excellent

Good

10% of the product sale price

5% of the product sale price

Fair

15% of the product sale price

10% of the product sale price

5% of the product sale price

 

The minimum commercial gesture is 5 EUR/GBP.
In case of the dirty item or item worse than fair, the Agent may propose 5% commercial gesture.


Quality Charter Trade-in updates

Article 1.4: Trade-in grades
Engraved MacBook or side with stickers are considered as cracked (grade 3 or grade 5, depending on functional state).

 

Article 2.2.2: Packages received empty or non-received

  • In case of packages marked as delivered but not received, or received empty, you now need to provide directly in the chat and right away the Certificate of non-receipt.
  • For damaged package received, if you have a listing for the product in the received grade, you must do a counteroffer and the counteroffer price must, at least, either match the listing price for that grade or be 80% of the current Backbox price.

 

Article 2.2.3: Cleaning and performing checks
In the event that you receive the wrong model with higher market value than expected or multiple devices:

  • Always inform the Back Market customer by message (this includes indicating to the Back Market customer the model of the product actually received)
  • Make a counteroffer for the wrong model either in line with your listing prices if you list this model, or with the product's market value. 

 

Article 2.4.1 Deadline for processing trade-in
If you’re unable to process the Trade-in order by the deadline, once the Back Office clearly warns you about not meeting processing deadlines and you are late on processing an order, Back Market will proceed with the payment of the initial offer price on your behalf. You must track the orders at risk in your Back Office.

 

Article 2.4.2 Conditions of Trade-in

  • If you’re unable to carry out any action to process the order (validation, customer action required, unprocessable item, transport issue), you must inform Back Market without delay and justify your reasoning. If you don’t notify Back Market, the order may be considered as not yet processed and payment may be triggered.
  • Specific rules for products bought in grade 4 & 5 (see grade table in 1.4) :
    • If IMEI can't be found: you must pay the initial offer price
    • You can put on hold the order (for iCloud locked) or refuse a product (for blocklisted/rooted/counterfeit/greylisted) only if you are able to prove the issue to the customer (with IMEI check report or alternatives).  

 

Article 2.5.3 Making a counteroffer
You can now access the list of accepted testing softwares in our Seller Support Center HERE to help you enhance the accuracy and quality of your counteroffers.
There is one unique list of accepted softwares for both your Sales and your Trade-ins.

 

Article 2.8 Recovered product return
If you’re unable to return products by the deadline (1 business day), Back Market reserves the right to pay the initial offer price  on your behalf.
Title: Summary of updates to T&Cs and Quality Charter 2025

Details__c:
In this guide we will see how to handle a cancellation of an order after validation, and we will also answer all the main questions around that topic.

What happens when a customer wants to withdraw?

1- We ask the customer the reason why
  • Customers who faced delivery issues or have received a faulty or non-compliant item and wish to retract will automatically receive a return label generated by Back Market. This return will be at your expanse as historically indicated in the quality charter. 
  • Customers who have received a compliant item and simply change their mind will not receive an automatic return label. They will have to send back the parcel on their own based on your guidelines. 

2-  We open a customer request.

Each withdrawal request is followed by a customer request, whatever the cancellation reason is. 
 

  • Issue with the package/device : we open a claim & post the return label on the claim. We also post a message informing you about the customer’s preference (refund or swap).  You have the possibility to speak to the customer at all time. 
  • Simple change mind : we open a claim & inform the customer that he needs to send the package at his expanse. Please make sure you provide the return address to the customer.

How do I recognize Back Market’s packages?

We automatically provide Backship return labels. 

The order number is written on the return label. For colissimo and DHL the ref client is always in the middle of the ref client (order id) <pk-merchant>-<order-id>-<timestamp>:

Are all the customers eligible for an automatic return label?

Only packages <30 kg are eligible for an automatic return label. For all the heavy packages, the withdrawal process remains the same & you need to send a return label / organize a pickup when the customer is eligible.
 

What happens if the return label fails to be edited?

Please provide your return label as usual & fill the return tracking information.
Title: Cancellation of an order after validation

Details__c:
The importance of customer care

Providing good customer service is a core value of Back Market, so we want to help you better understand how to manage your after-sales services in accordance with Back Market’s Quality Charter and vision.

It’s important to understand that every interaction has a significant impact on the perception of your business and Back Market, and the quality of customer service will have a significant impact on whether a Back Market customer will use the service again or recommend it to others. 

Back Market customers who experienced delays or a lack of courtesy during their conversations with refurbishers are twice as dissatisfied and twice less likely to return than customers who had quality experiences and interactions.


After-sales service processes

Find orders that need your attention in your Back Office by going to "Trade-in" > "Orders" > "Pending customers"



 

Trade-in customer care guidelines

Communication standards

  • Remain polite and professional in all situations, never use inappropriate language under any circumstances, and never blame the Back Market customer or accuse them of bad behavior.
  • Make sure all communications are in accordance with the Back Market Quality Charter.
  • Always follow procedure when handling a customer request.
  • Provide complete, relevant, and effective answers to minimize the number of exchanges.
  • Address the Back Market customer directly and try to be as clear as possible when providing an explanation or making a request. Understand that they may not have the same technical knowledge as you.
  • Follow through on your promises. If you tell the Back Market customer you’ll do something, make sure it happens and happens on time.
  • Never forget that you’re speaking on behalf of your business and have been entrusted by Back Market as a partner to provide high-quality service.
  • If you need help from Back Market in addressing a situation, please don’t do so in the chat, but rather contact the Seller Support Center here





 

24-hour response time

  • You’re required to respond to all messages from Back Market customers within 24 hours. 
  • Any response after 24 hours will be considered a "late response" including messages that don’t require follow-up.
  • According to our policy, you must have the final word in every conversation in order to control your Late Response Rate (LRR).
  • Your Late Response Rate is a key indicator monitored by Back Market. If you have an unacceptable amount of late responses over a 30-day period, you’ll be entered into a quality control process. Learn more about the Late Response Rate and Monitoring Periods
  • It’s important to be proactive and fast on all customer requests in order to avoid delays. Back Market reserves the right to intervene in order to accelerate support, in the case of non-compliance.
     
 

Free returns

  • If a Back Market customer declines your counteroffer, you must return the item within 2 business days and follow the mandatory Trade-in free return process in accordance with the Quality Charter.
  • If you process your free returns correctly by following the process in the link above, Back Market will refund you for your returns at the end of each month.
  • Please be respectful when communicating with a Back Market customer in the case of a return, remaining transparent and sharing all information needed as soon as you have it, such as return tracking information.

Note that if you’re still unsure about how to handle an after-sales situation, please contact the Seller Support Center here or reach out to your Back Market point of contact for additional support.
Title: How to perform customer service management for Trade-in

Details__c:
Disclaimer: Note that Back Market doesn’t provide legal advice, and that it’s your responsibility to remain up to date with existing regulations. For additional information, contact your legal advisors.
 

What’s EPR? 

Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is an environmental policy that makes the party who first places an item on the market subject to EPR requirements. This party is also known as the “producer.” They’re responsible for the entire lifecycle of the item, from design to end-of-life disposal, including waste collection and treatment). Under EPR regulations, the producer must mitigate the environmental impacts of their items throughout the entire item lifecycle by paying a fee. 
 

How does the EPR system works in France? 

The French government has introduced a new regulation called Loi AGEC (Loi anti-gaspillage pour une économie circulaire) to promote a circular economy and reduce waste. 

Under this regulation, marketplaces such as Back Market are required to prevent and manage waste and ensure that sellers selling in the French market have a unique identification number (IDU).

There are many different categories of EPR: Batteries, Packaging, Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE), and others. Each of these categories has 1 or more Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) who’s responsible for registering the producers, providing them with a unique IDU number and collecting fees. 

Sellers are responsible for registering with the relevant Producer Responsibility Organization. 
 

Am I a producer under French regulations? 

A producer isn’t the same as a manufacturer. The producer is the one who first puts an item for sale in France. 
 

What items are subject to EPR? 

Several categories of items sold on Back Market are concerned. 

The main ones are:  

  • Packaging 
  • Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) 
  • Batteries 
  • Paper for user manuals (note that you may need to register even if you’re below the 5-ton limit)

With some exceptions, you must get a separate IDU number for each category.

For example, if you’re selling phones and tablets, you need to register with 3 different Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs): 1 for Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE), 1 for Batteries, and 1 for Packaging. This means you’ll get 3 different IDU numbers. 

Here are the Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs) associated with each EPR category: 

EPR CategoryProducer Responsibility Organizations (PROs)
Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE)https://www.ecosystem.eco
https://www.ecologic-france.com
Batterieshttps://www.corepile.fr/
https://www.screlec.fr/
Packaginghttps://www.citeo.com
https://www.leko-organisme.fr
Paperhttps://www.citeo.com


Depending on the type of items you sell, you may need to contact other Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs) who can handle other categories of items. 
 

How do I register? 

Note that these steps apply only to France. You’ll need to register separately for other markets such as Germany or Austria. 

To register, here’s that you need to do: 

  1. Contact a Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) for each category
  2. Get a registration number for each category, and in some cases, pay fees that can vary from 0 € to several hundred euros 
  3. After you’re registered with a Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO), they’ll contact the ADEME and give you your IDU number
  4. Add your IDU numbers to your Back Office > “Business profile” > “Legal details” 

If you need assistance, some service providers offer support services like Avask, or Take-e-way  

Note that these providers are independent of Back Market. 
 

Is registration mandatory in France?

Yes. If you’re not registered and still want to sell in France, Back Market will pay the fees on your behalf and will charge you. This will be more expensive than registering for EPR on your own. Back Market isn’t able to benefit from reduced EPR fees when declaring on your behalf.
 

What do I need to do after registering? 

Once registered, you must: 

  • Add your IDU number for each category depending on the type of items you sell. 
  • Report the quantity of items that you sold in France to the relevant Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs). 
  • Pay fees to the Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs). 

The fees are calculated by each Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) according to sales, categories of items, and the volume of items put on the French market. 
 

What numbers should I add to my Back Office? 

You’ll have to give Back Market your EPR number for each category. Please note that if you have several accounts you must fill in your EPR number for each account. 

Back Market will verify your numbers once submitted in your Back Office.

The format of the numbers no matter the category is FRXXXXXX_XXXXXX. 

Please note that your Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) registration number isn’t the same as your IDU number for each category. You need to add your unique IDU number for each category in your Back Office > “Your business profile” > “Legal details”



Click “Add details” 



Choose your category and add the corresponding IDU number. 

I’m buying items from an upstream supplier. What do I need to do? 

If your upstream supplier is located in France and is registered with a Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO), your supplier is required to share their IDU number with you. You’re required to add your supplier’s IDU number to your Back Office. If you have more than 1 supplier, you’ll need to provide all IDU numbers.  

If your upstream supplier isn’t located in France, you’re considered to be the producer for EPR purposes, and you’re required to register with a Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) and add your IDU number to your Back Office. 

If both of these situations apply to you, you need to add the following to your Back Office: 

  • The IDU number of your upstream supplier located in France
  • Your own IDU number 


What if my supplier doesn’t give me their EPR number? 

If your upstream supplier doesn’t want to give you their EPR registration number, you must register with a Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) and provide your EPR registration number to Back Market. Back Market will pay the EPR fees to the relevant Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) on your behalf and will invoice the fees to you. 
 

What happens if I don’t add my IDU number to my Back Office? 

Back Market will pay the EPR fees to the relevant Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) on your behalf and will invoice the fees to you.
Back Market will charge you quarterly installments based on your sales in France over the previous quarter.

Since February 2024, Back Market is also charging a non-refundable 50 € admin fee per quarter. If you declare and pay the fees directly to the agency and have provided valid IDU numbers in your Back Office or you’re exempt from EPR fees, you won’t be charged.

If you’re registered with a Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) for only certain categories, Back Market will only invoice the fees for the categories where you’re not already registered. 

If you register with a Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) and provide Back Market with your EPR number, Back Market will stop charging you EPR fees on the next quarterly installment.

Here are example fees for the following 3 categories, applicable starting 1st July 2024, provided by Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs): 

EPR SECTOR SMARTPHONESLAPTOPS TABLETS HEADPHONES
WEEE3.34 cents0.63 cents0.63 cents 0.20 cents
Batteries 0.30 cents 0.30 cents0.30 cents 0.30 cents 
Packaging0.1461 cents 0.1540 cents 0.1540 cents 0.1592 cents 

 

I only sell refurbished items. Am I exempt from EPR in France? 

Certain refurbished items can be exempt from Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) in France, notably if they were already put on the market in France and were already subject to the eco-fee in France. If you only sell refurbished items that meet these conditions, you’re not required to register for the Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) category. Contact the Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) to determine if this exemption applies to you.

If you purchase your products outside of France (whether secondhand or new), you’re subject to the EPR fee for the WEEE category as you sell products on the French market. 

If you’re exempt from Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE), you must certify this by checking the “I’m exempt from WEEE” box in your Back Office. If you’re exempt, Back Market will not charge you the Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) fees. 



However, you’re still required to register for the Batteries and Packaging categories and add your IDU numbers for these categories in your Back Office.

I want more information about EPR requirements in France. Where can I find this? 

If you want information about a particular EPR category, you can check the Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) website. 

You can also reach out to Taxually, Avask or Take-e-way if you need more assistance or information about EPR categories. Please note that these providers are independent of Back Market. 

Can Back Market share my EPR data? 

Back Market must establish and uphold a record that contains details about sellers who are selling in France. This includes identification details, VAT numbers, EPR IDU numbers, the quantity of items covered by EPR schemes by category. This register can be shared to the authorities upon request.
Title: Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) :  obligations in France

Context:
Your seller account may sometimes reach a critical financial situation when your wallet has a negative balance, and you owe Back Market an amount of money.
Most of the time, a negative balance is offset on the following invoices by your latest sales on the platform, but the amount of debt can also keep growing. In this case, an intervention is needed to clear the invoices and settle the financial situation of your account.

Process:
As soon as you cannot count on your sales to offset your debt, you need to repay your debt to Back Market following the process below.

  1. Check your latest invoice in your Back-office and the amount due to Back Market
  2. Send a bank transfer of this amount to the bank account attached below
  3. Specify “your PK number and the invoice number” as the reason for payment

Details__c:
When do you have to offer a goodwill gesture?

Goodwill gestures must be mandatory when the service is not in accordance with what is advertised to the Buyer when they place their order, in particular, in the following cases.

 

In all these cases, the Buyer is entitled to refuse the goodwill gesture and request an exchange for a compliant item or a refund of their order, in which cases the return costs shall be borne by the Seller. 

 

In addition, in the event of the Seller’s non-reaction following the Buyer's complaint or a proposal that does not comply with the rules below, Back Market reserves the right to apply the goodwill gestures detailed in the tables below. 

We would like to remind you that all the criteria mentioned in the articles below are taken into account by Backbox's algorithm. 

Also, on these types of after-sales claims, when the goodwill gesture is accepted by the customer, the CSAT is on average 0.3pt higher and the claim is resolved on average 50% faster.
 

Delay in shipping or delivery

If your customer’s delivery is delayed by 1 or more days, whether due to delays on your part or a shipping provider’s, your customer is entitled to a refund equivalent to the shipping cost.
 

Physical Appearance discrepancy

In the event your customer challenges the appearance grade of their product during the cancellation period, the agent will offer them a choice between the following options:

 

  • A goodwill gesture according to the amounts listed in the tables
  • Return the product for a refund or replacement.

The minimum commercial gesture is 5 €/£/$ .

In case of the dirty item or item worse than fair, the Agent may propose 5% commercial gesture.



 
 

Missing or defective accessories

In the event of a missing or defective standard accessory, the agent will offer your customer a choice between the following options:

 

  • A goodwill gesture according to the amounts indicated below
  • The missing accessory

If an accessory is not listed below, and its value is less than 40 €/£/$ and no more than 10% of the item’s price, the Agent may offer a commercial gesture based on the current market price, without needing to request the exact amount from the seller.

 

If your customer chooses to have a new accessory shipped to them, you must share the package’s tracking number. This rule also applies to the accessories included by the customer in case of a return for repair/replacement and that were either not sent back to the customer after or that were sent defective. 


Wrong Product color

The agent may offer your customer a minimum goodwill gesture of 5% of the product 
 

The computer has not been properly reset

  • Missing or inactivated Windows license: clear and detailed instructions are not sent within one (1) Business Day of Buyer notification, the Buyer may be refunded the price of a new Windows 10 Home or pro license (as described in the Product description) or 20% of the device's sales price, whichever is lower.
  • Incorrectly reset Windows PC or macOS computer: If clear and detailed reset instructions are not sent within one (1) Business Day of Buyer notification, the Buyer may be refunded the cost of a clean installation at a third party repair shop up to the lesser of €100 for Europe, $120 for the US or 20% of the device's sales price.

 

Costs that may be retained by the Seller

When returning for a refund, if the Buyer does not return any of these accessories, the Seller may deduct the following amounts from the full refund.

If the Buyer has to return the same Product a second time due to a fault or a defective Product, the Seller can not charge the customer any of those amounts for any missing accessories.
Title: Proposing a goodwill gesture to avoid returns
