Details__c:
Roles & responsibilities  

Structurally, deliveries are under the responsibility of the seller. This means the responsibility as a seller do not end once shipment is done, but once product has been received compliant to the order.

Back Market on the other end gives the framework into which the Seller operate - shipping delays, available delivery methods, quality standards, etc. - but ultimately the Seller get to chose their shipping provider, the delivery fees and the way they want to handle their deliveries.

From a customer standpoint this means each product description has its own estimated delivery time and shipping costs and each product of an order made on the site is shipped separately (no group purchase):
 

In a delivery, there are 4 roles. Most of the time, the Seller is both responsible for the transport and sender (or recipient).

  • The responsible for the transport is the only one conversing with the shipping provider. Any request for information / change / modification (either requested by the sender or recipient) shall be handled through the responsible for the transport. This includes changing address, delivery instructions, request additional information on delivery status, etc
     
  • The sender is responsible for packaging the product, this is essential to limit the risk of damage during transport
     
  • The recipient is responsible for declaring any issue (damaged product, stolen product etc.) within 72h after delivery is complete (in case the delivery is made to a pick-up point the 27h start after recipient has picked the parcel)
     
  • The shipping provider is responsible for delivering the parcel undamaged, on-time to its recipient according to the delivery instructions provided by the responsible for the transport.


Delivery options available to Seller

Delivery managed by Sellers

The first option for a Seller is to handle deliveries all by themselves. They chose their shipping provider and contract with them directly, they edit the delivery label, they organize the shipment and manually enter the tracking number in BO. In this case the Seller is responsible for the transport
 

Backship

The 3rd option (not available in the US) is for the Seller to benefit from Back Market transport service BackShip. This service allows the Seller to issue pre-folded transport labels and benefit from competitive prices with Back Market's partners: Colissimo and DHL Express. In this case Back Market is responsible for the transport

See detailed responsibilities in case of a delivery issue here


Delivery standards at Back Market

    You will find on the image below the shipping requirements needed per category of products.

    The default cut-off time is 1:00 PM. You may update this setting in your Back-Office to a later cut-off time if it better reflects your actual operations. 

     

    • You need to have one free shipping option 

    • We advise you to add a customer’s signature on your shipments 

    • We advise you to insure your shipments 

    • Seller shall ensure the tracking number is loaded in the BO for customers to be able to track their package (in case of a return, at least the tracking number and shipping provider shall be shared within the S.A.V.)

Details__c:
When you create a new product, it is systematically sent in validation if it meets specific criteria. 

This process consists in checking that the product you are selling complies with our quality standards, that it doesn't already exist in our catalog, and that it meets all requirements to be validated. 

As long as your product is not validated, your listing won't be published online. You can find these listings in the section 'Offers > In validation':




 

If your listings have been pending validation for more than 48 hours, and before contacting our team, please check the following elements: 

 

➡️ Your product is approved for sale at Back Market. Please make sure that:

 

  • Your product is an electronic device and fits the Back Market DNA: it is not a spare part, accessory or health device. If it is a product bundle, it must be authentic and sold by the brand as well.
    💡 For more details, you can check this article: The different products available for sale on Back Market.


 

 

  • It is impossible to fully guarantee product reliability (e.g. health products)
  • The product prevents the customer from benefiting from a "like new" experience due to incompatibility issues related to older operating systems
  • The product has too many specificities or defaults and generate too many returns (e.g. some hardware, LG products)
  • The product’s current market price (delivery included) is higher than the selling price. For example, video games prior to 2015 are not accepted. 

➡️ Your product has been created correctly. Check that : 

  • The product pictures comply with our quality standards. The photos have a white background, and look professional. No accessories not provided with the device and no advertising or brand name is visible on the picture. 


 

  • All mandatory fields specific to the product and your listing have been filled in correctly: Stock higher than 0, delivery mode set, no contradictory attributes on the product page, etc.
  • The price new of your product: this must be based on the value of the device. It is your responsibility to fill it in each time you create a product. If your selling price is higher than the price new, your listing will not be validated. 

⚠️ We remind you that if the stock of the listing is equal to 0, the product will not be validated by our team. 

The validation process can take up to 48 hours. If it's still not validated after this period, and you've checked all elements listed above, please contact us through the dedicated contact form.

💡 You can also check if the products is blocklisted in this article: The different products available for sale on Back Market.
Title: Why is my listing under validation?

Details__c:
Why Sweden?

Want to sell more inventory, faster? We’ve made it easiest than ever to unlock the potential of the Swedish market: 

  • Market opportunity: We currently have fewer sellers listing their products in Sweden, but the refurbished electronics market is large and growing.
  • Category opportunity: You may be offering a product no other seller is offering to customers in Sweden, creating a higher likelihood of winning the BackBox and shifting your inventory, faster.
  • Fewer listings, less competition: 3 times less daily listings and competitor per BackBox compared to Finland and 6 times less daily listings and competitor per BackBox compared to France.
  • Higher out of stock %: 67% Out of Stock rate for top device categories compared to 1% in France, and 3% in Finland


Why open Sweden now? 

    Automated currency conversion

    Automatic currency conversion (EUR to SEK) is now available in your pricing rules  in the Back Office, meaning less risk of pricing errors and order cancellations

    The pricing rule is calculates SEK price = your primary market price x daily conversion rate (See example below) 
    Set and forget; the prices will be automatically converted every 24 hours 



     

    How to open your Swedish market

    1. First, your Seller Success Manager will activate your Swedish Market

    2. You will need to set your shipping method for Sweden in the Back Office 

    • Go to ‘Options’ 
    • Go to ‘Shipping’ 
    • Add your desired shipping settings for Sweden

    3. Your pricing rule will be automatically set to the SEK price = your primary market price x daily conversion rate. You can manually edit this rule by adding a ‘fixed amount’ for each listing, if desired.

    • Go to ‘Options
    • Go to “Pricing’ 
    • Add a ‘fixed amount’ if you wish to add a buffer to each listing price for shipping costs, for example


    FAQ

    Do I need to manually open the Swedish market?

    We (your SSM) will do this for you but you will have to add the shipping option yourselves. 


    What if we don’t want to be live in Sweden?

    You are not obliged to be live in Sweden and if you don’t activate it, it will remain closed. You can change your mind at any time and follow the instructions listed in this document.
     

    With the automatic currency conversion, can I still update prices manually? 

    Yes, you can do this as usual by updating your listing prices as normal and by deactivating our pricing rule. 
     

    How often will Swedish prices be converted?

    Every 24 hours to begin with however we are working on creating more frequent price updates soon.
     

    How will it work with my pricing software? 

    For now, we do not have an API endpoint supporting the currency conversion
Title: Why Sell in  Sweden?

Details__c:
What is it?

This article is a visual guide for sellers in order to show them the best practices when it comes to packing a good. This guide will be used as a visual chart with pictures of the recommended practices in order to avoid any damaged parcel during transportation.
 

General advice 

Starting September 15, 2023, you must use cardboard box packaging when shipping smartphones, tablets, and laptops. It’s prohibited to send these items only in bubble wrap and envelopes.
For all other items, we recommend you use cardboard boxes as well.

Don’t forget that BackPack for smartphones is included in Customer Care by Back Market.
Check out our offers in this article


Packaging requirements

Weight

Consider the strength and durability of the packaging box an appropriate box should be able to securely hold the weight of the content being shipped

Size and Shape

Items should not touch the outer wall of the box Odd-shaped or rounded packaging may need extra attention

Form of Contents

Special packaging materials and packing techniques are required for shipping liquids and powders

Value of Contents

Extra cushioning and protection may be required for high-value goods

Fragility 

Extra cushioning and protection are required for fragile items Apply special handling label

Regulations

Some regulated items may require specialist packaging

 

External Packing Material

Material 

Always use high quality corrugated cardboard boxes for parcels. For fragile or heavy items, double-wall or tri-wall constructions are recommended. Avoid reusing old boxes, as they may have lost some of their rigidity

Strength

Look out for the manufacturer stamp, which tells you information about the construction type and strength of a box. Ask your box supplier for more information as required

Size

Under-filled boxes may collapse and over-filled boxes may burst. Prevent this by choosing a box with the right size for the content being shipped

 

Internal Packaging technique

Bubble wrap, airbags, cardboard and foam peanuts/pellets are all commonly used as packaging material.

It is an essential par of the packaging routine, adding protection inside the box will reduce the risk of damaged parcels during transportation

 

Leave No Empty Space 

  • It is important to completely fill empty space within the box to avoid contents moving during transit, which can cause damage to both contents and box.
  • Place void filler on the base of the box, then around the item you are shipping.
  • Ensure there are no gaps or movement.
  • When choosing void filling materials, always ensure that they would provide sufficient support for the weight of the shipment contents. Some materials may sink or deflate under the weight of the content, which may eventually leave empty space inside the box

6 cm Distance

  • Always place items being shipped in the center of the box, with at least 6 cm of separation from any external walls or corners 
  • Take this into consideration when determining the size of the box relative to the size of the contents 


Basic Packing Method

Here are the basic packing methods suitable for most non-fragile articles / Low protection level 

  • We recommend preparing a double wall box, but if using a single wall, ensure your box has suitable strength for the weight of its contents.
  • Items that may be affected by moisture and staining should be placed into a strong plastic bag or container for extra protection.
  • Ensure your items are placed centrally in the box with at least
  • 6 cm of separation from any of the external walls and corners.
  • If shipping multiple items within a single box, ensure each one is wrapped individually and well separated from one another.
  • Fill any void space to prevent items moving within the box during transport.


Box-in-box Method

The box-in-box method utilizes a second outer box to offer additional protection. It is useful for the transportation of more fragile items / High level of protection. 

  • Both an outer and inner box will need to be prepared.
  • We recommend using the original manufacturer's packaging as the inner box where possible. Alternatively, the inner box should be packed using the basic packing method outlined above.
  • The outer box should be at least 14 cm larger in all dimensions than the inner box to provide sufficient over pack protection. We recommend a double wall box but if using a single wall, ensure your box has suitable strength for the weight of its contents.
  • Place approximately 6 cm of void fill in the base of the outer box before centrally placing the inner box inside.
  • Restrict any movement of the inner box by filling void space on all sides before sealing the outer box ready for transportation.


Seal the shipment

Recommended tapes

  • Polypropylene tape (brown plastic tape)
  • Vinyl adhesive tape (electrical tape)
  • Fiber-reinforced paper tape (duct tape)

Avoid using the following: 

H-taping

  • Apply 1 strip of tape along the center seam
  • Apply 2 strips of tape across both edge seams
  • Apply more strips of tapes for heavier shipments
  • Repeat the H-taping so that both the box's top and bottom seams are sealed


Applying Shipment Label

  • Fix the shipment label securely on the top surface of the shipment box. This will help us keep your shipment in an upright position as much as possible during transit
  • Ensure the label is wholly visible on one surface and that the label does not cover any seams.
  • Do not let other labels, tapes or paperwork cover the shipment label.
  • We never recommend using an old box, but if you do, ensure all outdated shipment labels are removed prior to use.
  • Placing a spare label inside the package will help identify your shipment if the original label becomes detached or damaged.
  • If you need to apply other labels to your shipment, please avoid placing them on the same surface as the shipment label.
  • Use a plastic self-adhesive clear window pouch to hold any loose documents that are required to be placed outside the box.
Title: Packaging Guide

Details__c:
Process for a device blocked by a lock code or a client account (iCloud, Google,...)

It happens that a customer did not reset his device correctly and sent you a product that is blocked by an user account or a lock code. In regard to our quality charter, after receiving the device you have to restore it completely if the customer didn’t do it already.

In this article we will explain to you the guidelines to follow, to be able to manage this kind of situation. The following actions aim to restore a device and therefore remove all codes that may block it.

Please remember to never ask the customer for his personal logins.

The steps to follow : For an iCloud Account

If you’ve recently received an iPhone with a passcode from a Back Market customer, you can perform a factory reset to delete the passcode.

Please note that this won’t remove iCloud, but it’s an essential first step to erase the customer’s data.

 If the device is locked:

  • Never ask for your customer’s credentials.

  • Follow the steps in this article to resolve the issue instead.


Want to restore multiple Apple devices simultaneously? Read this article to learn how

Make sure that you have a computer; either a Mac or PC

This process requires a Mac or PC. If you're using a PC, make sure that it has Windows 8 or later, and that Apple Devices App is installed. You also need a compatible cable to connect your iPhone to the computer.
 

Start by turning off the iPhone.

  1. Unplug the iPhone from the computer if it’s connected.

  2. Drag the slider to turn off the iPhone, then wait a minute to make sure that it turns off completely.

Next, put the iPhone in recovery mode.

  1. Press and hold the correct button for your iPhone while immediately connecting your iPhone to the computer. 

Buttons to press:

  1. For iPhone 8 or later, including iPhone SE (2nd generation and later): Press and quickly release the volume up button. Press and quickly release the volume down button. Then, press and hold the side button until you see the Connect to computer screen.

               

  1. For iPhone 7, iPhone 7 Plus, and iPod touch (7th generation): Press and hold the top (or side) and volume down buttons at the same time. Keep holding them until you see the Connect to computer screen.

               

  1. iPhone 6s or earlier, including iPhone SE (1st generation), and iPod touch (6th generation) or earlier: Press and hold both the Home and the top (or side) buttons at the same time. Keep holding them until you see the Connect to computer screen.

                      

2. Don't let go of the button.Keep holding the button until you see the recovery mode screen on your iPhone, then let go. If you see the passcode screen, you need to turn off your iPhone and start again.

            

Next, restore the iPhone.

  1. Locate the iPhone in the Finder or in iTunes on the computer that it’s connected to.

  2. Choose Restore when you see the option to Restore or Update. Your computer downloads software for the iPhone and begins the restore process.

  3. If the download takes more than 15 minutes and your device exits the recovery mode screen, let the download finish, then turn off the iPhone and start again.

  1. Wait for the process to finish.

  2. Disconnect the iPhone from the computer. 

This process restores the device to its factory settings, erases the client's personal data and removes the phone’s passcode.

After the device has been restored, switch it on. If the display shows the image below, this means that the device is iCloud-locked. In that case, find the service request in the after-sales services platform, and select the “Report a problem” button. This will block an automatic refund, and allow our agents to help you.


 

 

In specific cases where:

A.Client erased data & FMIP is OFF

Step 1: Connect the device to an Internet Connection

Ensure that the device is connected to an internet connection before verifying whether FMIP is activated or deactivated.

Step 2: Proceed to hard reboot the device

Once the device is connected to an internet connection, you can proceed to hard reboot the device.

 

For iPhone X, 11, 12, 13, or later

 

  1. Press and hold either volume button and the side button until the power-off slider appears.

       

  1. Drag the slider, then wait 30 seconds for your device to turn off. If your device is frozen or unresponsive, force restart your device.

  2. To turn your device back on, press and hold the side button (on the right side of your iPhone) until you see the Apple logo.

How to Force Restart

 

  1. Press and quickly release the volume up button.

  2. Press and quickly release the volume down button.

  3. Press and hold the side button.

  4. When the Apple logo appears, release the side button

                
 

B. Client erased data but FMIP is ON

Step 1: Connect the device to an Internet connection

Ensure that the device is connected to an internet connection before resetting it to factory settings, because the process could be interrupted if it's not connected to the internet. 

 

Step 2: Contact the client to turn off FMIP

Once the device has been reset to factory settings and is still connected to an internet connection, check again if FMIP is still ON. Use the 3-click solution tool to declare a Problem: “Account locked”, this will trigger an email sent to the customer with instructions to deactivate it. 

 

Step 3: Hard Reboot the device by following the process outlined  above

Once the above steps have been successfully completed, you may now proceed to hard reboot the device. Note that Back Market's terms of service prohibit the sharing of personal information. Please refrain from asking clients for their iCloud password.

 

If the customer is unable to disconnect the FMIP, the BM agent will continue to try to help them to deactivate it. If after 7 calendar days the device is still connected: you can return the device to the customer.

 

C. Client didn't erase data, but FMIP is OFF

If the client didn't erase their personal data, the device can still be protected with a passcode thus preventing you from having full access to the device.

To successfully restore the device, you will have to put the device in recovery mode  recovery mode (insert link to recovery mode above when suggestions are accepted)

Also make sure you have the latest version of iTunes (on a PC) or an updated version of macOS.

 

D. Client didn't erase data & FMIP is ON (device is functional)

The client returned the device, and you realised that FMIP is activated on the device, and that the client didn't also erase their personal data. Follow the below steps:

Step 1: Connect the device to an Internet connection

Ensure that the device is connected to an internet connection before resetting it to factory settings. The process could be interrupted if it's not connected to the internet. Also, without an internet connection, the FMIP information is potentially not updated., This information is saved on Apple servers and therefore requires an internet connection to be updated.  

 

Step 2: Contact the client to turn off the FMIP

Once the device has been successfully connected to an internet connection, and you confirm that the FMIP is still on,  Use the 3-click solution tool to declare a Problem: “Account locked”, this will trigger an email sent to the customer with instructions to deactivate it. 

 

Step 3: Restore the device to its factory settings

Once FMIP has been turned off, you can proceed to restore the device to its factory settings, while it is still connected to the internet.  Note that Back Market's terms of service prohibit the sharing of personal information. Please refrain from asking clients for their iCloud password 

 

If the customer is unable to disconnect the FMIP, the BM agent will continue to try to help them to deactivate it. If after 7 calendar days the device is still connected: you can return the device to the customer.

 

E. Client returned the device non-functional

The device was returned non-functional, and the client confirmed that they didn't erase their personal data nor turn off FMIP.  Please follow the below steps: 

 

Step 1: Repair the device

The first step to take is to repair the device to at least turn it on and connect it to the internet.


Step 2: Contact the client to turn off the ''find my iPhone'' feature

Once the device has been repaired, Use the 3-click solution tool to declare a Problem: “Account locked”, this will trigger an email sent to the customer with instructions to deactivate it.


Step 3: Restore the device

The device is now functional and FMIP has also been turned off? Please proceed to restore the device. Restoring the device will reset the device to its factory defaults and reinstall the operating system. This process will also erase the client's personal data from the device. Note that Back Market's terms of service prohibit the sharing of personal information. 

Please refrain from asking clients for their iCloud password  

If the customer is unable to disconnect the FMIP, the BM agent will continue to try to help them to deactivate it. If after 7 calendar days the device is still connected: you can return the device to the customer.


F. Device is nonfunctional & irreparable

If the returned device is damaged beyond repair, or that the cost of repairing it is more expensive than the original price, in this case, we recommend you to either offer an exchange or a refund to the client.

Does the product have an exclusion of warranty? Please refer to this article.
 

Delays to Respect

Time to process a return from its reception

  • If your customer received a wrong item, you have 1 business day to send the correct item

  • If your customer asked for a refund,  you have 2 business days to issue the refund

  • If your customer asked for a repair or replacement, you have 3 business days to ship an item back to them

  • If you receive a damaged item, you must inform your customer within 2 business days

  • If the device remains locked after 7 business days, you can return it to the customer
Title: Process for a device blocked by a client account (iCloud, Google,...)

Details__c:
The Trade-in Quality Dashboard provides you with detailed visibility of your performance and remains your best ally in understanding and expanding your buyback business. 

Given that this information is crucial to the management of your business, we have had to make it much more visible and accessible. This led to the introduction of the Quality Insight project, which involves integrating all your quality indicators directly into your Back Office.

This will allow you to review your Trade-in quality performance whenever necessary without having to wait for the Quality Dashboard email. By giving you better access to your indicators, we are also giving you more autonomy so that it is easier to improve these rates. You may apply specific filters and export the data in this tab.
 


Where can I access Trade-in Quality Insights?

Directly in your back-office by accessing the 'Insights' tab. You will see a 'Trade-in Quality Dashboard' section where you can find your recovery quality indicators and details, thanks to the interactive interface.

 

Is the website sending you to a login page instead of showing you your dashboard?

This means that cross-website tracking of your web browser is deactivated. No worries, here's how to solve the problem:

The tool allows you to access all Trade-in quality indicators (more details here)

These quality indicators are the same as those found in your Quality Dashboard history. The display may be different. However, the method for calculating all indicators remains the same.

 

How does the dashboard work?

Reminder: Quality indicator rates are calculated on orders received within the last 30 days. For each indicator, you need to be lower than the Back Market average.


Main page

On the main page, for each indicator, you will be able to access:
  • The exact definition
  • Your current rate (vs the Back Market average) with the associated volumes. If the rate is green, it means you are above or equal to the Back Market rate. On the other hand, if the rate is red, it means that your rate is lower than the Back Market average.
  • The evolution of this rate for 30 days
  • To the action plan to improve your rate
  • The button to access a detailed explanation of the performance. By using this button, you will be redirected to a much more detailed page. This option is very useful and should be used for in-depth analysis.

Detailed page

(1) Overview of KPI 📊

Hover your mouse over the indicators to view more details. This can be very helpful if you want to get a holistic view of how the rate has evolved over the last 30 days.

(2) Daily breakdown 🧮

The indicators are calculated from a defined cohort (30 days). In this visualization, you have a detailed daily breakdown of all events. You will be able to determine if some days are more impacted than others.

(3) Adapted Visualizations

For each KPI, appropriate visualizations have been incorporated to assist you in understanding your quality performance. Do not hesitate to use the filters in order to obtain relevant information.

Example: This view shows you the counteroffer rate per model. This rate can be compared with the Back Market average.


 


(4) Details of impacted orders 🔎

The list of impacted orders can be found at the end of each KPI. Important information about these orders is embedded in this table. You can easily access this order by clicking on “View Order”




Dashboards can be exported, and we recommend you do so, especially the last one that gives you more visibility on orders that affect your rate.


How to download raw data?

  • Click the "Download" button in the lower right corner of your screen.
  • Select "Crosstab".
  • Select the small "Raw data" square. You can select CSV or Excel extension
  • Click "Download". Wait a few seconds and the file will automatically download to your computer.
 

How to return to the main page?

Detail pages open in a new tab. If you want to go back to the overview, just switch tabs.

 

We would like to hear your opinion!

We care about your opinion! You can provide us with your comments in less than two minutes in this form or by leaving us your opinion on the bottom left right in the performance tab. Thank you in advance for your feedback.
Title: Trade-in Quality Insights Dashboard

Details__c:
Regular pickup is a benefit of BackShip. You can schedule a shipping provider to pick up packages daily at your chosen time and location.

Benefits

  • No more drop offs at collection points
  • More time for quality testing
  • Improve your On-Time Delivery rate
  • No additional costs


 

Which shipping providers are available and what are their requirements for regular pickups?

 

Shipping providerRegular pickup availableRequirements
DHL ExpressYES1 package / day
UPS (standard and express)YES1 package / day
Correos ExpressYES1 package / day
ChronopostYESMinimum 3 packages / day
DPD UKYESMinimum 100 packages / month
DPD DEYESMinimum 100 packages / month
ColissimoNo regular pickup yet

 

How do I set up a recurring regular pickup?

 

Submit a support request via the Seller Support Center by following these steps:
  1. Is the question order related? > No
  2. What does your question concern? > Back Market services
  3. Which service > BackShip
  4. What do you want to do? > Set up a regular pickup
  5. Click on the link to fill in your request and then submit it
  6. Wait until our team gets back to you shortly


Setting up regular BackShip pickups usually takes approximately 7 business days.


About automatic API call to shipping providers

With DHL Express, Correos Express, and UPS, once you confirm an order, this integration automatically schedules a pickup with the shipping provider for the next business day. To modify a pickup or inform us about a shipping provider that failed to pick up your packages, submit a support request via the Seller Support Center .
Title: BackShip - How to set a regular pick up

Details__c:
BackPricer works 24/7 to optimize your listing selling prices in real-time to make sure you never lose BackBoxes. 

Here’s how it works: Set your minimum and target prices, and BackPricer will find the best possible price for you (based on your quality score), either by hitting your target price or by finding the optimal price between your target price and your minimum price at which you can win the BackBox.


Jump to
How do I activate and deactivate BackPricer?  
How many times per day can I activate or deactivate BackPricer?  
Is BackPricer available on integrations?  
If I use an integration with a re-pricing tool and I want to use BackPricer, will BackPricer override the integration's re-pricing tool?
How should I define my target and minimum prices?  
Is it possible that a bug in BackPricer could send prices spiraling?
What happens when a listing runs out of stock?
Can a seller still win the BackBox even if they’re not the lowest price?  
How does BackPricer interact with the BackBox algorithm?  
Does BackPricer work in real-time?
What happens if I enter a target price that’s too high?
What happens if I enter a minimum price that’s very low?


 

How do I activate and deactivate BackPricer? 

To activate or deactivate BackPricer for all listings, you can use the toggle in your Back Office > “Options” > “BackPricer”.  

When BackPricer is activated, it will apply to all listings where a minimum price and target price are set. When BackPricer is deactivated, the minimum price values of all listings are erased. The target prices remain the only price values.  

To activate BackPricer for a specific listing, simply enter a minimum price. To deactivate BackPricer for a specific listing, delete the minimum price.

 



To activate or deactivate BackPricer through a CSV import:

  • The CSV should contain a “sku” column with the sku reference number of your listing
  • The CSV should contain a “backpricer” column with a “TRUE” or “FALSE” value for each row, specifying if it should be activated for the given listing
  • The CSV should contain a “min_price” column with the min_price value to use for the given listing 
  • If you are in an international context, you should also provide a “lang” column with the country it should be applied to (like “en-us”, “fr-fr”, or “fr-be”)
  • Save your CSV file using the CSV comma-delimited format
  • On the Back Office, go to “import” > “update listing” > “select your file” > “test BackPricer” > “Open” > “Update”
  • You can check that the import was successful using the “Notification” page, or looking directly at your listing page

 


 

To activate BackPricer through an integration, the “min_price” field is available through the following endpoints: 

  • Create listing integration
  • Update specific listing integration
  • Update several listings integration

To get your minimum prices through an integration, the “min_price” field is available through the following endpoints: 

  • Get my listings
  • Get specific listing  

 

How many times per day can I activate or deactivate BackPricer? 

You can activate or deactivate BackPricer for all listings at the same time once a day. Or, you can activate or deactivate BackPricer for a specific listing as often as you want, just by adding or removing the minimum price for that listing.

Note that even without BackPricer activated, you can still win the BackBox by setting the right target price.

 

Is BackPricer available on integrations? 

BackPricer is currently available on SellerCloud and coming soon to other integrations within the next few months.
 
 

If I use an integration with a re-pricing tool and I want to use BackPricer, will BackPricer override the integration's re-pricing tool?

Integrations with re-pricing tools will still be available. The main difference is that an integration’s re-pricing tool is limited to 2 updates per hour, while BackPricer updates prices in real-time. 

 

How should I define my target and minimum prices? 

The target price is the equivalent of the price field when BackPricer is deactivated. You should set your target price to the amount at which you want to sell the listing. You should set your minimum price as low as you’re able to go to win the BackBox without losing money. In this way, you will win more BackBoxes and sell more items, maximizing total revenue.  

The minimum and target price for listings must stay within a maximum range of 8%. This means your target price can’t be more than 8% higher than the corresponding minimum price.

💡 What happens if you go over the limit?

  • Via Back Office: You won’t be able to save the price change, and an error message will pop up.
  • Via Bulk Upload (CSV/API): The BackBox competition tool will automatically adjust your target price to exactly 8% above the minimum price.

To avoid errors and keep your listings performing at their best, please make sure your pricing aligns with this policy. 

Is it possible that a bug in BackPricer could send prices spiraling?

No, this situation can’t happen. The selling price will always be the higher price in the range between the minimum price and the target price, depending on the competition. 

Let’s say seller A and seller B are both selling a product with a target price of 100 €, but seller A’s minimum price is 80 € and seller B’s minimum is 89 €. Who wins the BackBox assuming both sellers have equal quality rates?

Seller A wins the BackBox with a selling price of 88 €. The winner will be the seller with the lowest minimum price, and the selling price will be set just below the minimum price of the closest competitor. To win the BackBox, seller B should set a minimum price of 79 €.



 

What happens when a listing runs out of stock?



If seller A runs out of stock, and there are no other competitors except seller B, then seller B wins the BackBox with a selling price of 100 €. 

The selling price is the higher price in the range between the min price and the target price. Without competition, it is equal to the target price.

 

Can a seller still win the BackBox even if they’re not the lowest price? 

Yes! For example, if seller A adds a minimum price of 400 €, and seller B adds a minimum price of 420 € but has better quality rates than seller A, seller B can win the BackBox. 

 

How does BackPricer interact with the BackBox algorithm? 

The BackBox algorithm remains the same with or without BackPricer. Minimum and target prices set by a seller aren’t the only criteria to win the BackBox — seller and product quality are still taken into account. 

 

Does BackPricer work in real-time? 

Yes! Every time a minimum price or target price is updated by a seller, Back Market recalculates the BackBox winner. 

 

What happens if I enter a target price that’s too high?

If your target and minimum prices are too high, you’ll lose competitiveness and will have less of a chance of winning the BackBox.

If your target price is too high but your minimum price is relevant, you’ll remain competitive, as BackPricer will select the most competitive value compared to the competitors in the range between the minimum price and the target price.

 

What happens if I enter a minimum price that’s very low?

If you enter a minimum price that’s very low but your quality rates are good, you’ll have a high probability of winning the BackBox. Your selling price will be set just below the minimum price of the second competitor, not equal to your own minimum price.

Also, there’s a maximum price range between the minimum and target prices. If a seller accidentally sets a very low price (like 1 €), it will not be taken into account and the BackPricer will be deactivated.
Title: How to use BackPricer?

Details__c:
Smartphones are designed for specific markets, which can lead to significant differences. 

 

Major Market - Specific Differences

  1. CE Marking: EU-specific smartphones have a visible CE marking, while those made for other markets, do not always bear such visible CE marking. US-specific smartphones rather bear a FCC mark.
  2. Functionality Variations: Some smartphones may lack features like eSIM, find my people, etc.

Here is the example of the minor functionalities differences for iPhones (starting from iPhone 14): 

 

 

Seller Expectations

In accordance with the Sellers Terms and Conditions and Quality Charter, the devices you sell must comply with the laws and regulations of your customer’s country of residence. Your devices must be 100% functional and thoroughly tested.

 

Addressing Customer Complaints on Imported Products

Absence of CE Marking & no Functional Differences

All devices sold on Back Market must comply with the safety requirements acknowledged by the CE marking.

 

  • The agents will first guide the customer to locate the CE marking.
  • In case the CE marking is not found:
    • During the withdrawal period (30 days after delivery): customer will be offered a refund or replacement;
    • 30 days to 6 months after delivery: agent will inform the customer of the seller's guarantee on safety and a refund shall be processed if customer requests it;
    • > 6 months after delivery: cases will be reviewed individually to verify claim legitimacy, assessing if there's an attempt to bypass out-of-warranty (OOW) devices.
  • Degradation: If the returned product is degraded, the seller can reduce the refund proportionally to account for depreciation.

Functional Differences

SIM Type Issues : customer received a phone with a different SIM type than expected

 

  • When the smartphone received is e-SIM only : Agent will offer to cover the cost of changing their SIM card type (credit request, seller-paid, €10).
  • When the smartphone received is non e-SIM phone, or if customers insists :
    • During the withdrawal period (30 days after delivery): customer will be offered a refund or replacement;
    • 30 days to 6 months after delivery: customer will be offered a refund or replacement;
    • > 6 months after delivery: cases will be reviewed individually to verify claim legitimacy, assessing if there's an attempt to bypass out-of-warranty (OOW) devices.

 

Other Minor Differences (e.g., FaceTime audio, WiFi, satellite emergency feature, missing Samsung pay)

 

  • Agent will first explain that these minor differences do not affect major functionalities. Agent will also offer an alternative, such as using WhatsApp for audio calls. 
  • If customers insist, they will be offered a €20 partial refund to keep the device.
  • If customers still insist:
    • During the withdrawal period (30 days after delivery): customer will be offered a refund or replacement;
    • 30 days to 6 months after delivery: customer will be offered a refund or replacement;
    • > 6 months after delivery: cases will be reviewed individually to verify claim legitimacy, assessing if there's an attempt to bypass out-of-warranty (OOW) devices.

Degradation: As with CE marking issues, sellers can apply a proportional reduction to the refund if the product is degraded.
Title: Navigating Market-Specific Smartphones: A Guide for Sellers

Details__c:
In addition to France, our home country, Back Market is present in 14 other markets: Italy, Portugal, Austria, Belgium, Sweden, Slovakia, Greece, Finland, Spain, Ireland, Germany, the Netherlands, UK, and the US. In these newer markets, your chance of winning BackBoxes is higher due to less competition.

Our analysis shows that a seller who opens in Germany and Spain, in addition to France, will double their monthly sales on average. But what if you don't have a competitive shipping contract to deliver internationally? Or what if you don't know how to manage returns from abroad? To provide you with a turnkey solution to these questions, we created BackShip.
 

What is BackShip?

BackShip is a service that allows you to issue shipping labels directly from your Back Office in 1 click. The service allows you to benefit from competitive prices with our international shipping partners, including DHL Express, Chronopost, Colissimo, DPD, UPS, and Correos Express. And with delivery guaranteed within 24 hours of shipment, you’ll be able to increase your customer conversion rate. 

Using BackShip with an API is also available! Sellers who use BackShip can create shipping labels automatically after confirming their orders via an API. To learn more about creating BackShip shipping labels in bulk, read this article


Can I still use BackShip if I don’t sell internationally?

Absolutely. Keep reading for the benefits of using BackShip domestically and internationally. 
 

What are the benefits of using BackShip?

  • Access to new countries with international delivery to all of Europe within 1 click 
  • Competitive shipping prices negotiated by Back Market 
  • Access to more customers and increased sales by selling in more countries
  • Improved quality score to increase your margins and win more BackBoxes
  • Labels directly downloaded within 1 click in your Back Office
  • Automatic package pickup scheduling with DHL Express, UPS, Correos, Chronopost, and DPD
  • Optional package insurance 
  • Worry-free shipping with Back Market agents opening and leading shipping provider investigations


For more information on BackShip, continue reading here
Title: Discover BackShip — BackMarket’s shipping service

Details__c:
Many sellers leverage Finale to manage their inventory as they sell on the mobile phones on Back Market and potentially other selling channels such as eBay and Amazon.

Finale has the ability to manage your BackMarket inventory with two common configurations. The first configuration allows for a direct connection between your Back Market listing and Finale. Back Market orders are automatically synced to Finale where it will make the proper stock adjustments.

With the Back Market inventory management solution, any quantity changes resulting from a stock operation (such as receiving a PO shipment or shipping out an order) will be updated to your Back Market listing within five minutes to prevent overselling.


 

If you are leveraging ShipStation for your fulfillment needs, Finale Inventory’s Back Market integration makes both inventory management and shipping easier than ever.

Many retailers who utilize the Back Market platform are also multichannel e-commerce sellers who leverage ShipStation to fulfill orders from many marketplaces (e.g. eBay and Amazon) and to manage the warehouse shipment process of those orders using ShipStation, marking the orders as shipped when done.


 

If your company has this shipping process, Finale can be quickly added to your ShipStation integration to keep track of your stock on hand without changing anything about your existing shipping process. Using ShipStation's API, Finale communicates with ShipStation behind the scenes to watch the orders that are marked complete, and seamlessly records the transaction to remove the items from inventory.

Benefits of Finale's Back Market Inventory Management Solution

There are many benefits to using Finale Inventory as your Back Market inventory management solution.

Auto Update your Back Market listings. Finale will manage customer orders from Back Market and other channels. Finale will keep track of all stock levels and will auto-update stock levels back to stock quantities to Back Market and across other channels every 5 minutes. Click here to see a tutorial video.

Support allowing multiple SKUs for a unique Product ID. When you are selling the same phone on multiple listings, Finale has the ability to easily associate multiple alias SKUs to a single Finale product ID.  For example, you could easily list an iPhone X 64 GB in "Very Good" Condition for the "Fair", "Good" and "Very Good" Condition.

Additionally, when you are selling products on different marketplaces (e.g. Amazon and eBay), you may be forced to have different SKUs for the same product. Many customers manage this unnecessary complexity with Excel worksheets. Finale Inventory handles this alias SKU mapping with a couple of mouse clicks to properly keeps tracks on your inventory.

High Transaction Volumes Support. Conduct a large number of monthly transactions? No problem. Finale Inventory can support up to 1M transactions per month, and we are scaling our architecture to support even higher volumes. High transaction customers should be considering leveraging our integrated turnkey mobile barcode solution to decrease warehouse operations times from receiving and picking while improving accuracy.

Restock Forecasting.  Running a growing business is hard.  There’s a lot you have to keep up with.  Leveraging the sales velocity of a product, Finale will automatically calculate dynamic reorder points based on your inputs – supplier lead time, desired safety stock, and expected sales growth — to make smarter, faster-purchasing decisions.

Restock forecasting from Finale gives you one less thing to worry about. When your inventory gets low, we’ll send you an alert letting you know it’s time to send out a purchase order. You can email the purchase order directly from Finale, and when you receive your shipment with Finale, Finale will update all your channels with the will be updated stock quantities.

Multiple Shipping Location Support. Easily manage your inventory across multiple shipping warehouses from Finale Inventory.

Avoid Stock Outs. Keep your business humming along by creating product count thresholds to know what needs to be reordered to avoid costly stock-outs.

Status Change Automation. When products are back-ordered, orders containing those products are typically marked as "On Hold" in ShipStation. When those products are received in your warehouse and recorded in Finale, Finale will automatically change the order status from "On Hold" to "Waiting" in ShipStation.
Title: How do I configure my BackMarket account with Finale Inventory?

Details__c:
Use multiple addresses for product returns:

To ease your logistic processes and automate efficiently your return label generation, we have built a new address book.

Goal: To provide more flexibility and granularity to reduce your pain in terms of product returns processes.

How to access your address book?

To find your address book, first, go to your Back Office under the 'Options' tab, then select the 'Addresses' tab to find the 'Address' section:

You will see all your addresses

  • Pro tip: Name your addresses ! 
    • If you use the CCBM service, we strongly recommend that you give an explicit name to your addresses. This way, the Back Market agents from CCBM will know directly which address to use while generating a return label.
    • E.g. Address for BuyBack orders, Address for UK returns ... 

What did we change?

Use an address “as default”:

It can sometimes happen, you receive product scenarios that are not yet covered by a price.

E.g. Selling in a new market or a new category of product.

In this case, no worries: You can define a default return address.

This address will be used for every edge case not covered by the address rules you created. 

Set up your “Address rules”!

You can now define a specific return address based on the category of the product and/or the market of the order. E.g. I want my smartphones orders to be sent back to my address A, and my other orders to be sent back to my address B.

How to create an address rule for product return?

Below your addresses, you will see a section called “Address rules”. Select 'Add a new rule' 

Click on “Add a new rule”:

A panel will be displayed on the right-hand side of the page, with a multiple steps funnel:

  1. Select the address you want to use
  2. Select the Process you want to assign the address to - For now, only product return is available. But we will enrich the list in the coming weeks
  3. Select the market(s) of the orders you want to target. You can select specific market(s) or “All markets”. This option will consider all markets you are online in, and the future ones
  4. Select the product category(ies) you want to target. You can select specific a category(ies) or “All categories”. This option will consider all product categories you are currently selling, and the future ones
  5. A Summary. So you review the scope of the address rule you are creating before validation

Address Rules management:

Once you have created your first address rule(s), they will be listed below your addresses. You will be able to amend or delete them.

Smart management of rules priorities:

You can combine multiple address rules to design the return process that fits your needs. In some cases, some rules scope might overlap. In this case, we have designed a priority matrix, preventing errors and making sure that your parcel always reaches the right destination.

e.g. If you have created the following address rules:

  • Use Address A for orders from France and all product categories
  • Use Address B for orders from France and Smartphone only

What’s next?

In the coming weeks, we are planning to enrich the list of processes, so you can build more automation and more control over your logistic processes. E.g. Return address for BuyBack orders, Pickup point for BackShip orders, etc...
Title: Receiving product returns to different addresses

If you need to report an issue with a return, keep in mind that you need to do so quickly. If you don’t take any action on the return service request, Back Market will automatically issue the refund after 2 business days (the period of time you have to either issue a refund or report a problem, outlined in the Quality Charter).


Jump to

How do I report a problem?

How do I report a problem?

The best way to report your issue is by selecting “Report a problem” from the preset buttons on the service request. You’ll find the request in the “Item returns” section of the Back Office.

You’ll be able to select your issue from a list of common problems, or select “problem reported couldn’t be identified” if your issue isn’t listed. Then, you’ll be able to share more information with an agent and send documentation of the problem.
 
 

Should I contact a Back Market agent directly?

You can contact a Back Market agent with questions about a potential issue, but keep in mind that this will not be recognised as an “action” taken on the return. That means a refund will be issued within 2 business days, as usual. If there’s a problem, you should report the issue using the preset buttons instead of sending a message to an agent.

Learn more about managing your returns and reporting problems with this quick tutorial.

Details__c:
As refurbishers, you receive customers’ trade-in devices before paying for them. Therefore, Back Market must take specific precautions to protect Trade-in customers against failed and refused payments.


This article details our various processes to protect Trade-in customers and introduces you to the new KPI they’re based on —Trade-in Exposure.

 

One main KPI: Trade-in Exposure

Trade-in Exposure is the KPI used at the core of all finance processes to protect customers’ payments.

 

Your Trade-in Exposure is evaluated by comparing your Sales and Trade-in activities and quantifying the amount of Trade-in orders for which the payment can’t be guaranteed. It is computed on Tuesdays before each payout by subtracting your full Sales Wallet from your estimated amount of trade-ins. 

 

Written as a formula, that’s: 

 

Trade-in Exposure = Estimated Future Costs (Trade-in) - Wallet (Sales) 

 

Where:

  • Wallet (Sales): Total amount of your sales not yet paid to date, accessible through your Back Office.
  • Estimated Future Costs (Trade-in): The estimated amounts you are committed to pay, based on all the Trade-in orders you generated and/or received in the last 20 days, taking into account ratios of your orders sent, with counteroffers, and paid.

 

Process 1: Trade-in Deferred Payout

The Trade-in deferred payout is additional to your Sales deferred payout and is separate from the amount retained based on your sales volume.

 

Also known as a “Trade-in Security Payment”, it aims to retain money from your payout to guarantee the payment of your trade-in orders. The main factor to calculate it is your Trade-in Exposure.

 

The Trade-in Deferred Payout will help you run and grow your Trade-in activities while having lower Sales activities.

 

Learn more about Trade-in Deferred payout 

 

Process 2: Weekly limit adjustments

Your Trade-in daily limit is checked weekly to see if it requires adjustments.

 

If your Trade-in Exposure is too high, and the payment of your Trade-in orders can no longer be guaranteed, your Trade-in daily limit will be decreased instantly to reduce this risk. 

 

This reduced daily limit is set so that, should you keep having the same amount of Sales, your Trade-in Exposure will be back at an acceptable level within 3 weeks.

 

You will always be notified by email of any daily limit reduction.

 

Please note that:

  • 3 weeks after receiving your first notice, your Trade-in limit will be reassessed and set definitely for your account.
  • During this period of 3 weeks, your Trade-in limit may be readjusted, increased, or decreased, depending on the evolution of your Trade-in Exposure.

 

Learn more about weekly limit adjustments

 

How can you reduce your Trade-in Exposure?

Reducing your Trade-in Exposure is your best bet for avoiding:

  • Trade-in Deferred Payouts on your account
  • Reductions to your Trade-in Daily limit

Boosting your Sales on Back Market is what will reduce your Trade-in Exposure. Here are a few tips:

  • Refurbished and resell the devices you sourced through Trade-in on Back Market.
  • Open additional categories and markets on Back Market, including those not yet available with Trade-in.
Title: Trade-in processes to protect customers’ payments

Details__c:
Your listing may be offline for different reasons. Before contacting our team, please check the following information: 

 

❌ Your listing is online and hasn't been archived. 
If it has been archived, you can publish it again at any time in the 'Listing' > 'Archived' section of your Back Office. All you have to do is click on 'Publish offer'. 

 

🔢 Your listing has a stock higher than 0.
If your listing has no stock, it will be automatically deactivated. 

 

📜 Your listing has a price, a comment and has free shipping option.
This must be applied to all countries where you want to sell. 

 

✅ Your product is authorized for sale and is not blocklisted. 
You can see which products are concerned in this article: The different products available for sale on Back Market.

 

📶 Your shop has been activated and is online. 
Please also make sure that your daily sales limit has not been reached. If this is the case, and have any questions about it, please contact your Account Manager. 

 

💲 Your selling price is neither too high nor too low.
In fact, your selling price cannot be higher than the price new of the product. Likewise, if your selling price is 85% lower than the new price of the product, your listing will be deactivated. 

 

💡 Once a new listing is created, it must be validated by our team. This process can take up to 48 hours. If after this period, the listing is still in validation, please check the following article: Why is my listing under validation?
Title: Why is my listing offline?

Details__c:
What is ESD?


ESD stands for electrostatic discharge. It’s the sudden flow of electricity between 2 objects. The rapid discharge of static electricity can cause significant and expensive damage to components.

It’s often referred to as the “invisible foe” in the electronics industry because you can’t detect it. 

Industry experts estimate that an average of 8–33% of electronics are damaged due to static discharge. Others estimate the actual cost of ESD damage to be in the billions of dollars annually.

 

How can I make sure that ESD doesn’t affect my devices?


We’ve created a guide to help you implement an effective ESD control program. It will help you increase your repair yields, reduce field failures, lower return costs, and improve your customers' trust in buying refurbished devices.

Check out the guide here
Title: Quality Assurance: ESD Control - What is it and how to implement it?

Details__c:
If you’ve recently received an Android device with a passcode from a Back Market customer, you can perform a factory reset to delete the passcode.

As noted in our updated Quality Charter, you’re required to fully restore the device and delete the Back Market customer’s data if they haven’t done so already. If the device is locked:
  • Never ask for your customer’s credentials.
  • Follow the steps in this article to resolve the situation.

  
Start by opening the recovery menu.

  1. If the device has a physical home button, for example S6 or S7, press and hold the volume up, home, and power buttons. 
  2. When you feel the device vibrate, release the power button.
  3. The device will vibrate again and a menu will appear. 
  4. Release the other buttons.
  1. If the device doesn’t have a home button but does have a separate power button, for example S8, S9, and S10/S10+, press and hold the volume up, Bixby, and power buttons until the device vibrates and a logo appears.
  2. Release the other buttons.
 
  1. If the device doesn’t have a home button or a separate power button, for example Note10, Fold, and Z Flip, press and hold the volume up and side buttons until the device vibrates and a logo appears.
  2. Release the other buttons.

 

Then, select “Delete All User Data.” 

  1. Once the recovery menu has launched on your device, use the volume up and volume down buttons to select "Delete all user data" or "Wipe data/factory reset." 
  2. Press the power button to select.

 

Then, use the volume up and volume down buttons to confirm your selection.

  1. Depending on the device, you may need to select "Yes", "Yes - delete all user data," or "Factory data reset." 
  2. Press the power button to select.

 

Then, the device will process the factory reset.


Once complete, you’ll see "Data wipe complete" at the bottom of the screen. 
 

  1. Press the power button to select "Reboot device." It may take a few minutes for your device to reset. 
  2. Wait while it reboots. It will power on automatically when it’s ready to be set up.
  3. When it switches on, you’ll be prompted to go through the steps to set up the device as if it were the first time used.


This process restores the device to its factory settings, erases the Back Market customer's personal data, and removes the phone’s passcode in almost all cases. 

Note for Trade-In : If after resetting a Trade-In Android phone, you are requested a code, you can ask the customer for it by explaining the product has already been reset. You have to send a proof that the item has been reset.

After the device has been restored, switch it on. If you’re prompted to enter credentials like an email address or password, this means that the device has an active Google account still attached to it. In that case, find the service request in the after-sales services platform, and select the “Report a problem” button. This will block an automatic refund, and allow our agents to help you.

Check out this article for more details
Title: Received an Android device with a passcode?

Details__c:
Disclaimer: Note that Back Market doesn’t provide legal advice, and that it’s your responsibility to remain up to date with existing regulations. For additional information, contact your legal advisors.
 

What’s EPR? 

Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is an environmental policy that makes the party who first places an item on the market subject to EPR requirements. This party is also known as the “producer.” They’re responsible for the entire lifecycle of the item, from design to end-of-life disposal, including waste collection and treatment). Under EPR regulations, the producer must mitigate the environmental impacts of their items throughout the entire item lifecycle by paying a fee. 
 

How does the EPR system work in Germany? 

The German government has introduced a new version of the Electric and Electronic Equipment Act (ElektroG), the German Packaging Act (VerpackungsG), and the Batteries Act (BattG). 

Under this regulation, platforms such as Back Market are legally required to prevent and manage waste and make sure that its partners selling in the German market have an EPR registration number for Packaging (from July 2022), Batteries (starting July 1, 2023), and Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) (starting July 1, 2023). 

There are many different categories of EPR: Batteries, Packaging, Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE), etc. Each of these categories have 1 or more Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) who’s responsible for registering the producers and providing them with EPR registration numbers and collecting fees.

Partners are responsible for registering with the relevant organizations. If not compliant, Back Market will have to suspend them from selling in the German market. 
 

Am I a producer under German regulations? 

A producer isn’t the same as a manufacturer. 

Packaging 

As a retailer, you’re considered a producer if you’re the first to place packages filled with goods on the German market on a commercial basis.

Regarding primary packaging (packaging for the item itself), if your supplier has already put the packaging on the German market, your supplier may be considered the producer and you may provide your supplier’s registration number. 

Regarding secondary packaging (shipment packaging), all sellers are considered producers and must register, report, and pay the fees. 

Check out this ZSVR infographic for more details


Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) and Batteries 

You’re considered a producer if:

  • You import an item subject to EPR requirements in Germany. 
  • You sell an item subject to EPR requirements in Germany but are not established in Germany (in this case, you may need to appoint a representative in Germany).
  • You manufacture an item subject to EPR requirements in Germany.

 


What items are subject to EPR? 

Several categories of items sold on Back Market fall under these requirements, including:

  • Packaging 
  • Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) 
  • Batteries 

With some exceptions, you must get a unique EPR registration number for each category.

For example, if you’re selling phones and tablets, you need to register for 3 different EPR categories: Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE), Batteries, and Packaging. This means you’ll get 3 different EPR registration numbers. 

Here are the Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs) you need to register with for each category: 

EPR categoryProducer Responsibility Organization (PRO) to register with 
Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE)https://www.stiftung-ear.de/en/home
Batterieshttps://www.stiftung-ear.de/en/home
Packaging
https://lucid.verpackungsregister.org/
 


Depending on the type of items you sell, you may need to contact other Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs) who handle other categories of items.
 

How do I register? 

Note that these steps apply only to Germany. You’ll need to register separately for other markets such as France or Austria. 

Packaging  

You’re responsible for both primary and secondary packaging, so here’s that you need to do to register for Packaging:

Register with the central packaging agency Zentrale Stelle Verpackungsregister-ZSVR through the Lucid Packaging Register. After you register, the ZSVR will provide you with your EPR registration number that begins with “DE” and is followed by 13 digits. 

You need to fulfill the system participation requirements, so you’ll have to enter into a system participation agreement with 1 or more authorized recycling systems (see here for a list).  By providing your Lucid registration number to the authorized recycling system of your choice, you’ll be able to get a dual membership certificate. 

Once you’ve gotten your Lucid registration number and the dual system certificate of membership, you must add them to the “Legal details” section of your Back Office. Keep reading for Back Market's collection process. 

If you need more information, see ZSVR's FAQs  


Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE)  

From July 1, 2023, Back Market will be legally required to make sure that you comply with EPR obligations in the Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) category. You must register with the EAR Foundation, who will issue you a number.

If you don’t already have these registration numbers, we suggest you apply now as the process may take a few weeks. Register with the EAR Foundation now

Once you’ve gotten your registration number, add it to the “Legal details” section of your Back Office. Keep reading for Back Market's collection process. 

Need more information? See the Stiftung EAR website


Batteries

From July 1, 2023, Back Market will be legally required to make sure that you comply with EPR obligations in the Batteries category. You must register with the EAR Foundation, who will issue you a number.

If you don’t already have these registration numbers, we suggest you apply now as the process may take a few weeks. Register with the EAR Foundation now

Once you’ve gotten your registration number, add it to the “Legal details” section of your Back Office. Keep reading for Back Market's collection process. 

Need more information? See the Stiftung EAR website

If you need assistance, some Back Market partners offer support services: 

  • For Packaging, check out Reclay and get 5% off when you use promo code BACK5ACT
  • For Packaging, Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE), and Batteries, check out Take-e-way 

Note that these providers are independent of Back Market. 
 

I don’t have a business entity in Germany. What do I need to do? 

If you don’t have a business entity established in Germany, you may be required to appoint an authorized representative to register on your behalf. Get more information here  
 

Is registration mandatory in Germany?

Yes. If you don’t comply with the EPR requirements for Packaging, Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE), and Batteries, Back Market will have to suspend you from selling in the German market. 

Make sure you add valid EPR registration numbers for all categories in Germany in the “Legal details” section of your Back Office.
 

What do I need to do after registering? 

Once registered, you must : 

  • Add your EPR registration number for each category depending on the type of items you sell. 
  • Make sure your EPR registration numbers are publicly available on the official databases (Lucid for Packaging and the EAR Foundation for Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) and Batteries. 
  • Report the quantity of items that you sold in Germany. 
  • Pay fees to the Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs). 

The fees are calculated by each Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) according to sales, categories of items, and the volume of items put on the market. 
 

What numbers should I add to my Back Office? 

You’ll have to give Back Market your EPR number for each category. Please note that if you have several accounts you must fill in your EPR number for each account. 

Back Market will verify your numbers once submitted in your Back Office.

The format of each EPR number is as follows: 

  • Packaging: DEXXXXXXXXXXXXX (13 numbers)*
  • Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE): DEXXXXXXXX (8 numbers)
  • Batteries: DEXXXXXXXX (8 numbers)

*In addition to the number for the Packaging category, you’ll also need to add your dual system membership certificate in the “Legal details” section of your Back Office. 

See screenshot below:



Click “Add details.”



To upload your packaging certificate, choose “Packaging.” 



Finally, choose your category and add the corresponding EPR registration number. 
 

I’m buying items from an upstream supplier. What do I need to do? 

Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) and Batteries categories
 
If you’re buying items from an upstream supplier and you’re not producing or importing items that fall under these categories, you need to be sure that your upstream supplier is registered with the EAR Foundation. In such cases, you’ll need to add the EPR numbers of your supplier. 

For the Packaging category 

For the primary packaging, you can add your upstream supplier’s EPR number if your supplier is considered a producer. 

For the secondary packaging, you’ll always be the producer, so you need to have your own Packaging EPR registration number. 
 

What if my supplier doesn’t give me their EPR number? 

If your upstream supplier doesn’t want to give you their EPR registration number, you must register with a Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) and provide your EPR registration number to Back Market. Otherwise,  Back Market will suspend you from selling in the German market. 
 

I only sell refurbished items. Am I exempt from EPR requirements in Germany? 

If you buy second-hand products from Germany: you don't need your own number if you don't sell them under your own brand.
In this case, you do not need to submit a registration number, but you must certify under your own responsibility that you are exempt.

If you buy products outside Germany to sell on the German market, you are considered to be an importer and must therefore obtain a WEEE registration number and send it to Back Market.

 

Where can I find more information about EPR obligations in Germany? 

If you want information about a particular EPR category, you can check the Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) websites (see above for details). 

For Packaging, check out ZSVR’s FAQs here

For Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) and Batteries, check out Stiftung EAR’s FAQs here  

We know EPR requirements are complicated. Back Market has a network of compliance partners who can help.

Reach out to Reclay and get 5% off when you use promo code BACK5ACT

Or check out Take e-way to learn more
Title: Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR): Obligations in Germany
