Details__c:
Since January 2024, the Back Office is offering a new feature on returns where we can track your ability to repair or replace. You can either communicate this availability proactively to your SSM who can activate or deactivate your repair/replace abilities, or you will be notified when your refund rate on returns for repair/replace are above 90%. 

This means that if you are not able to repair or replace returned items, the customers will be able to choose a “remote solution” or a “refund” option instead - after having gone through our proposed Self-Care Remote Solution guides. 

If a customer selects “refund”’, then the return item from that moment on will be treated as a ‘refund’ request, where your intervention will be very limited. 

Changes also apply to the deadlines mentioned in the charter

Whether the request was made within or outside the withdrawal period, all ‘Refund’ requests must be handled within 2 business days after delivery at the seller’s warehouse. All problems with a return (Account Lock, Out of Warranty, Delivery Issue etc.) must be reported via pre-set buttons before the 2 business day deadline to block the refund request.

In other words, the already existing rules set for refund requests during the withdrawal period, will now also apply to the new refund requests that are created after the withdrawal period has expired. 

With this new process, we expect to:
  • Decrease your intervention into claims: There is no iteration required on your part unless you need to ‘report a problem’.
  • Increase customer satisfaction thanks to a better transparency on what they will be offered
 
New Resolution Grid:
Title: New Seller Resolution Options

Details__c:
To avoid unnecessary contact from customers, you will no longer receive cancellation requests for orders that have not yet been validated. 

Any cancellation request made by a customer on an order that has not been validated yet will be approved automatically. The customer will be refunded and no commission will be charged. Here is how the feature will work.

  • If you manage orders directly in Back Market's Back Office: Nothing could be simpler. The canceled orders will automatically disappear from your "Orders" tab. 
  • If you manage orders using your own API connection: An error message saying “The new status of the order line (XXXXXX) does not respect the status hierarchy” will warn you if you try to validate a canceled order.   
  • If you manage orders using a channel manager's API connection: The same error message saying “The new status of the order line (XXXXXX) does not respect the status hierarchy” will warn the channel manger. We have notified your channel manager of this message & have asked them to take it into consideration. If you still receive canceled orders from your channel manager's API feed, we recommend you take the following actions:

1- Ask your channel manager to immediately validate the orders when they are uploaded. If the order is immediately validated, it can no longer be canceled by the customer.
2- Ask your channel manager to take our status update messages into consideration and not push any order with a "400 bad requests" status.

  • If you manage orders via an FTP connection: Back Market does not handle FTP error messages. We, therefore, ask that you check the status of the orders in your Back Market Back Office before validating. You will then see if an order has been canceled or not before shipping.
Title: Cancellation of an order before validation ❌

Details__c:
If you find this article useful, hit the like button at the end to let us know.

 

Need to know how to create a product? You’re in the right place. 

 

But before you go any further, we’ve got to warn you — a product and a listing are 2 different things on Back Market. Check out this article to learn about the difference between a product and a listing 

 

Also keep in mind that we try to keep a clean, organised catalogue for customers — that means there are rules for publishing products. If your product is stuck in validation, it’s likely not compliant with these rules. Check out the rules and see why your product might be stuck in validation

 

Now let’s get to it. 

 

Jump to 

How do I create a product manually?
How do I create a product via CSV?
How do I create a product via API?
What specifications does my product need to have?
What are the guidelines for photos? How do I add a description?

 

How do I create a product manually?

Creating products manually involves doing so in the Back Office. We’ve gathered everything you need to know in one place. Learn how to create a product manually (from the Back Office) 
 

How do I create a product via CSV?

Creating products via CSV allows you to create a large quantity of products at one time. Learn how to create a product via CSV
 

How do I create a product via API?

You can also create products through your API integration. This process allows you to create a large number of products at the same time, as well as synchronise them on different marketplaces. Learn how to create a product via API

 

What specifications does my product need to have?

Product specifications, both mandatory and optional, are essential for high quality product pages. 

 

What are the mandatory specifications?

  • Back Market ID (BMID)
  • EAN
  • Model
  • Title
  • Description
  • Brand
  • New price (price of the product when it’s new)
  • Weight
  • First image

 

What are additional mandatory specifications that might also appear based on product type?

Some items will have mandatory specifications that apply only to their device type. For example, smartphones also require that you include:

  • Storage
  • SIM lock
  • Color
  • Dual SIM capabilities
  • Screen size

 

Optional specifications

There are several optional specifications for products, but we strongly recommend filling them out. Including as much detail as possible helps build customer confidence and increases conversion. It also helps your listing get a better Google Quality Score, leading to a better referencing of your products. 
 

What are the guidelines for photos?

Images are an integral part of product details. These images will be the first elements visible to Back Market customers, and they’ll also be the first thing to appear on external referencing pages (like Google Shopping). 

 

The images you upload should give a full overview of your product. They should also match Back Market standards, which includes that your photos:

 

  • Are professionally taken
  • Are taken on a white background
  • Don’t include personal branding
  • Don’t have accessories visible if they aren’t included with the device
  • Are in JPG format
  • Are greater than 500 x 500 resolution

 

 

How do I add a description?

Descriptions are essential for building customer confidence and sharing the details of your product. Here’s how to add a description:

  • Convert your text into HTML including line breaks (aka including “<br />”). Convert your text with TextFixer’s simple tool
  • Make sure special characters are properly converted.
  • Insert your HTML description by selecting "<>" in the “description” section.
  • Select “Ok” to officially add your description.
  • Modify your text with bold text and italics afterwards, directly from the “description”' section.
Title: How to create a product

Details__c:
How is this rate calculated?

This rate is calculated based on all orders cancelled by the seller in the previous 7 days.

The number  of orders cancelled by you in the last 7 days divided by the total number of orders supposed to be delivered in the past 7 days, including canceled orders. To avoid such cancellations, always update your stock and prices.
 


How does this rate impact me?

Order cancellation is a very negative experience for the customer. Only 1 of 2 customers will place an order again after their initial order was cancelled. If Back Market notices a large increase in the number of orders you’ve cancelled within  a short period of time, Back Market reserves the right to suspend your account until we receive clarification and a solution for your situation. 

 

Where can I find my rate?

You’ll find your Order Cancellation Rate and more details in your Quality Insights dashboard.
Title: Order Cancellation Rate (over 7 days)

Details__c:
Back Market holds a percentage of your funds to cover any issues that may arise with your sold products that still have an active warranty. 
As mentioned in our article Deferred Payout, the amount withheld keeps evolving due to the factors that affect your deposit. However, you can easily keep up with any changes directly from your Back Office > Invoices > Your wallet, thanks to the dedicated section in the right-hand side of the page.



You will still have access to your Back Office, and therefore your invoices, to keep up with the detail of refunds issued for specific orders. The evolution of your deposit is reflected in the first page of your invoice:
  • Subtotal of changes in security deposit (Sommes retenues à titre préventif): amount of deposit returned or blocked based on our risk assessment.
  • Return on deposit (Rendu sur dépôt): adjustment of your deposit, which is recalculated at each payment period, based on different risk criteria.


Why is all my money withheld if I am no longer selling on Back Market?

If you have exited the platform, and you are offline, the entire amount of your wallet is withheld as a deposit because we have to be able to cover any issues that may arise with your sold products that still have an active warranty. 
Any compensations issued at your favor for specific orders are integrated in your deposit calculation, therefore you will not receive a payout. 
 

When will my money be paid back out to me?

  • After 6 months, if your deferred payout is greater than €2000, it will be paid out in 10% increments, unless your estimated future refund amount is too high. Any remaining amount will be paid out 13 months after you have exited the marketplace  (except for sales in Spain and Portugal, where the corresponding deposit will be paid out 24 months after you’ve exited the marketplace).  
 
  • After 6 months, if your deferred payout is less than €2000, it will be totally paid out only 13 months after you have exited the marketplace (except for sales in Spain and Portugal, where the corresponding deposit will be paid out 24 months after you’ve exited the marketplace).
Title: I don't sell on Back Market anymore, when can I get my deposit back?

Details__c:
Hyperwallet is our new payment partner, this change allows us to continue our growth and international expansion. Integrating Hyperwallet enables us to have access to even more payment methods to reach and satisfy even more customers.

This very good news comes with an extra effort on your side, though. To continue receiving the payout of your Back Market's sales, you have to pass the Hyperwallet KYB process. It's pretty simple, you just have to provide us a little information (personal information of Business contacts, directors and beneficial owners for example) and legal documentation (Passport, driver's licence, government ID or article of incorporation for example) directly in your Seller back office.

Please find attached the T&Cs of Hyperwallet.

What do you need to provide? 

  • Personal information (name, date of birth, etc.), contact information and home address of Business contacts.
  • Business information about your business (legal name, business registration number, business contact information, business address).
  • Personal information (name, date of birth, etc.), contact information and home address of directors.
  • Personal information (name, date of birth, etc.), contact information and home address of beneficial owners (person who controls or owns more than 25% of the shared, voting rights and other interests).

Which related documents do you need?

  • Business contacts: Passport / Driver's licence / Government ID.
  • Proof of business: Articles of incorporation.
  • Beneficial owners: Passport / Driver's licence / Government ID.
Title: Hyperwallet: What is it?

Details__c:
When you want to sell a product that doesn't exist in our catalog yet, our new service BackList is here to help. It allows you to create a new type of product on Back Market, so you can start uploading your listings in no time.

Jump to

How do I create a new type of listing with BackList?
How do I get a CSV template for my request?
How do I make sure my request is processed quickly?
 

How do I create a new product with BackList?

To create a new catalog entry via BackList, just submit a request via the Seller Support Center, or reach out to your Back Market point of contact.

Make sure to include a CSV file with more information about the product(s) in question. If you’re submitting a request through the Seller Support Center, you can upload the CSV to your open ticket under “My existing requests”.

The information you need to share will depend on the category of product you want to sell. Keep reading for more details about creating a CSV file with the correct information.

Once your request has been processed, your Seller Success Manager will send you the new Back Market product ID (BMID) for each product. You’ll be able to update your stock and pricing using the new BMIDs. Processing time depends on how many products you want to add to the catalog, but it should usually take around 3 business days.
 

How do I get a CSV template for my request?

We make it easy to know what information you need to share in your CSV file. Just log on to your Back Office > “Options” > “Batch Listing Creation” > “New items listings”.

You’ll be prompted to choose what kind of category your product falls into. Finally, select “Create CSV file” and you’ll be able to download a CSV template that best suits your new product.

If you don’t think your product is represented by a category that’s listed in the drop-down or you have a concern, don’t worry. Just submit a request via the Seller Support Center, or reach out to your Back Market point of contact.
 

How do I make sure my request is processed quickly?

To make sure your request is processed in a timely manner, make sure:

  • All required product specifications are included in the CSV file.
  • There are no duplicates in the CSV file.
  • The products you want to sell are not blocklisted or prohibited. Learn which products are prohibited on Back Market
Title: How to connect to our free listing creation service?

Details__c:
With Back Market, you can source products directly from Back Market customers through your Back Office.

Jump to
How to join Trade-in
Step 1: Set up Trade-in in your Back Office.
Step 2: Complete the SEPA or BACS mandate.
Step 3: Complete the Trade-in quality test.
Step 4: Get ready for your first orders.

 

How to join Trade-in

If you’d like to subscribe to Trade-In, fill out this form. 

Our team will start your onboarding within 2 days and remind you of the mandatory steps you need to complete to get onboarded. If you haven’t heard from our team in over a week of applying, please fill out the form again.

No need to wait for our confirmation email though. You can complete all steps of your onboarding before then.
 

Step 1: Set up Trade-in in your Back Office.

In your Back Office, go to the “Trade-in” section at the top right of the page > “Options” to complete the following:

  • Add your ACH bank transfer information.
  • Add your credit card details.

If you still see the red "X" next to the "One-click payment setup" field, don't panic. Your card has been added. You'll see a green "X" once the first payment has gone through.

The default payment method will be your credit card unless you check ACH debit. If a card payment fails, the payment will be taken from the ACH information provided.

Next, in your Back Office, go to the “Sales” section at the top right of the page > “Options” > “Addresses” to set up the address where you would like to receive your Trade-in purchases.

If this address is the same as your default one for Sales, you don’t have to do anything.

If it’s another address, add it by selecting “Add new address”, then add Address rules at the bottom of your screen. Read about more details in this article

 

Step 2: Complete the SEPA or BACS mandate.

If you want to source devices in the European Union, complete the SEPA mandate online here.

If you want to source devices in the UK, complete the BACS mandate online here.

It’s legally required that we have the right documents to pay Back Market customers via direct debit. This is mandatory.

 

Step 3: Complete the Trade-in quality test.

Complete the Trade-in quality test

This test integrates the main rules you need to comply with once you are live on Trade-in and start receiving orders.

We also strongly recommend that you carefully review our Quality Charter here for all detailed guidelines you’ll have to respect.
 

Step 4: Get ready for your first orders.

In your Back Office, go to the “Trade-in” section at the top right of the page and:

  • Add your first listings by going to “Listings” and creating your listings manually or by batch-uploading via CSV file. Read more about this here
  • Fill in the cost of MacBook battery replacement by going to “Options” and going to the bottom of the page. (Note that this cost is deducted from your listing price when the Back Market customer specifies this issue as part of their assessment.)
Title: How to onboard with Back Market Trade-in

Details__c:
If you stopped using Trade-in and want to start using it again, you’re in the right place.


Jump to 

If you stopped using Trade-in due to quality issues
If you stopped using Trade-in due to a refused payment​​​​​​​
If you stopped using Trade-in for any other reason

 

If you stopped using Trade-in due to quality issues

You can join Trade-in again if you:

  • Are still selling items on Back Market
  • Received your exit notice at least 3 months ago

If both are true, you’ll just need to provide an explanation of the changes you’ve implemented to tackle the original quality issue(s). Submit your request via this form. 

We’ll update you once your request is confirmed. In the meantime, feel free to start updating your listings and financial information to speed up the process.

You’ll receive a notification once you have access to Trade-in again, and your daily limit will be set at 5 items. Learn how to increase your Trade-in limit

 

If you stopped using Trade-in due to a refused payment

You’ll need to contact your bank to find out the reason for the refusal and solve it as soon as possible.

We’ll give you access to Trade-in again as soon as you figure out your bank situation and make sure that you don’t owe Back Market any money.

You’ll receive a notification once you have access to Trade-in again, and your daily limit will be set to where it was before the issue.

 

If you stopped using Trade-in for any other reason

You can subscribe again as long as you’re still selling items on Back Market. Submit your request via this form.

We’ll update you once your request is confirmed. In the meantime, feel free to start updating your listings and financial information to speed up the process.

You’ll receive a notification once you have access to Trade-in again, and your daily limit will be set to 5 items. Learn how to increase your Trade-in limit
Title: How do I start using Trade-in again?

Details__c:
If you use our BackShip service, you know delivery labels are automatically created when you confirm an order. However, there are a few situations when you may want to manually create a label:
  • The automatically-generated label didn’t work or the label is unavailable.
  • You want to ship multiple items within 1 order, but you need an additional delivery label.
  • You use your own delivery labels and want to use the BackShip service occasionally.
 

How do I create a label?

To create a label, log into your Back Office > “Orders” > “Create BackShip label”



Enter the order number and select a shipping provider. All other fields will be filled in automatically.




If you use BackShip for all of your orders, make sure you include the parcel in the next scheduled pickup. If you have no scheduled pickup or you’re not a regular BackShip user, drop off the parcel at the appropriate collection point.
Title: How do I manually create a BackShip shipping label?

Details__c:
Why this new feature?

Many carriers deliver on Saturdays and Sundays, but we've historically shown delivery times in business days only. You can now reflect weekend operations in your delivery promise so customers see earlier, more accurate delivery dates.

 

A significant opportunity

Our analysis reveals an important gap:

 

  • Current situation: There's a notable difference between our delivery promise and actual delivery times
  • Weekend operations: A significant portion of deliveries already happen on weekends
  • Impact on sales: Our A/B tests have demonstrated that reducing the delivery promise by one business day leads to a measurable increase in conversion rate

What does this mean for you?

As a seller, you can now align your delivery promise with your carriers' actual capabilities. If your shipping provider operates on Saturday and/or Sunday, you can now indicate this in your configuration.


❌ Without weekend delivery:

 

  • Order placed: Thursday morning
  • Carrier delivery: 48 hours
  • Customer sees: "Delivery Monday" (4 calendar days)

✅ With weekend delivery:

 

  • Order placed: Thursday morning
  • Carrier delivery: 48 hours
  • Customer sees: "Delivery Saturday" (2 calendar days)

🎯 Impact: Your product appears 2 days faster → Customers are more likely to buy from you!

 

Concrete Benefits:

 

✅ Shorter and more accurate delivery promise displayed to customers

✅ Better conversion: customers are more likely to buy when they see reduced delivery times

✅ More sales thanks to a competitive advantage on delivery speed

✅ Increased transparency: what you promise matches what you actually deliver

 

How to configure weekend deliveries in your BackOffice?

It's very simple! For each shipping configuration to a destination country where your carrier works on weekends:

 

1. Access your Back Market BackOffice

2. Navigate to your shipping configurations

 

 

3. Select the configuration affected by weekend deliveries

 

 

4. Enable working days: check Saturday and/or Sunday according to your carrier's operating days

 

5. Save your changes

 

Our recommendations:

 

✅ Check with your carriers which days they actually operate for each destination
✅ Enable this option only for routes where you're certain the carrier works on weekends

 

Frequently asked questions :

Q: What if my carrier only delivers on Saturday, not Sunday?
A: No problem! You can enable Saturday only. The system is flexible.

 

Q: Can I disable it later if needed?
A: Yes, you can enable or disable weekend deliveries at any time in your BackOffice.

 

Q: Which carriers typically deliver on weekends?
A: Royal Mail, Colissimo, DPD, Chronopost, it depends on your contract. Check with your specific carrier.
Title: Weekend Deliveries: Boost your conversion rate with accurate delivery promises

Details__c:
Why use a recommended shipping provider ?

Benefits for your customers

Shipping providers recommended by Back Market are integrated with our advanced tracking system. In practice, your customers benefit from:

 

  • Centralized tracking: all delivery information directly redirected by email or my customer order page, without redirection to the carrier's website
  • A consistent experience regardless of the carrier used

Benefits for you

By using these providers, you significantly reduce:

 

  • Customer inquiries related to tracking ("Where is my package?")
  • Disputes due to lack of information
  • Your customer service workload

Important: Not all shipping providers share the necessary tracking data with us. Only recommended providers guarantee this complete integration. An untracked order will automatically be considered as non delivered, therefore delivered late. 

 

Impact on your performances 

Your choice of shipping provider directly affects key metrics that determine your success on Back Market:

 

On Time Delivery & Backbox

Your late delivery rate impacts your Backbox score : A reliable carrier with consistent delivery times can boost your visibility in search results.

Conversely, frequent delays will penalize your ranking and reduce your sales opportunities

 

Incident Rate

Not all carriers are equal: some experience significantly higher rates of delivery incidents (stolen packages, lost parcels, "delivered but not received" claims)

A high incident rate increases: Customer complaints and negative reviews, Refund requests and disputes, Your operational costs and time spent resolving issues. 

 

Secured deliveries: a strong recommendation

For all orders exceeding €/£/$150, we strongly recommend using a secured delivery method:

 

  • Signature required
  • PIN code
  • QR code verification

Proven results: Delivery incidents are significantly reduced with these services.
Title: Which shipping providers should I use for my shipments?

Details__c:
Your daily Trade-in limit is the maximum number of Trade-in orders you can reach in a single day. This limit is reset every day at 12:00 AM GMT +1 or 7:00 PM EST.
 

How do I increase my daily order limit?

Every week, on Wednesday, Back Market checks your account to see if you’re eligible for an increase of your limit for up to +30 daily orders/day.

 

To be eligible for a daily order limit increase, you need to comply with the following requirements:

 

  • Reach your daily limit on a regular basis (5+ times in last 30 days)
  • Do enough Sales to allow for a limit increase without leading to a too high Trade-in Exposure. 
  • Have no Monitoring period on your quality Key Performance Indicators

How will I know when my daily order limit increases?

When you’re eligible for a daily order limit increase, we’ll let you know by email. 

By default, your daily order limit will be increased 24 hours after you’re notified unless you specifically ask not to by filling up this form.
 

How do I avoid my daily order limit decreasing?

Your daily Trade-in order limit is fully within your control.

 

First, make sure your Trade-in Exposure remains null or low, by maintaining or boosting your Sales on Back Market.. Learn more about Trade-in Exposure and associated processes.

 

Then, make sure you have no ongoing Monitoring Period. Learn more about Monitoring Period and thresholds.

 

If there are major issues with your account and you’re not responding, your limit could decrease without an alert.
Title: How do I increase my daily Trade-in limit? And how do I avoid decreasing it?

Details__c:
Unfortunately, E-commerce doesn't get the chance to leave for holidays. However, we do have some tips for you in case you are taking some much-needed away time.
First and foremost, you need to put your stock to 0, so that they go offline. Therefore, please go to your Back Office, on the tab 'Offers'. You have two ways to put your stock offline:

  • Put your whole stock offline thanks to a CSV file. 
  • Put your stock offline manually directly in the offers tab.

On top of that, your team must be able to take care of our customers. The last thing we want is for them to have to wait for an answer because you're enjoying the sun on the beach. We advise you to brief them before leaving so that this remains under control.
Lastly, please liaise with your Back Market contact about your vacations, so that we can take this info into account.
You'll find below a list of '2024 Bank Holidays' for which your quality score will not be impacted. 


Now that you know everything, off you go! Enjoy your holidays!
Title: Is there a "Holiday" Mode on Back Market?

Details__c:
Sellers’ undertakings regarding VAT vary depending on the different situations that may apply.

In case of imports of Products from a country outside the EU:
  • For orders less than or equal to 150€ (excl. VAT and shipping costs): The Seller undertakes to affix Back Market IOSS number, as communicated to the Seller by Back Market, on the package containing the Products and to communicate this number by any appropriate means to their transporter. Back Market shall collect the VAT due on the sale and repay it to the tax administration. This sum will therefore be deducted by Back Market from the sums to pay out to the Seller, on each decade.
 
  • For orders over 150€ (excl. VAT and shipping costs): 
    • In general, the Seller acknowledges and accepts, for each sale, that they are fully liable for the customs duties and taxes due for the importation of Products into the territory of the European Union. The Seller undertakes to pay them in their entirety and to integrate them into the selling price of the Products. In no way shall customers be responsible for paid customs duties or taxes.
    • As an exception, when the Product is imported in France, Back Market shall collect the import VAT and repay it to the tax administration. As a consequence, the Seller undertakes to (i) process with custom clearance of the Product in France, and (ii) affix on the package containing the Products Back Market intra-community VAT number, as communicated by Back Market to the Seller and to communicate this number by any appropriate means to their transporter. Given this payment by Back Market, the Seller accepts that the amount of importation VAT will be deducted by Back Market from the sums to be paid out to the Seller, every ten days.
Title: Requirements regarding VAT in case of imports

Details__c:
This Ethics and Compliance Charter supplements the Seller Terms & Conditions (“Seller T&Cs”) and the Quality Charter. In the event of a conflict, the provisions of the Seller T&Cs will prevail on both charters. 

 

All terms starting with a capital letter have the meaning given to such terms in the Seller T&Cs, accepted by the Seller. 

 

Any Seller who wishes to sell Products on the Back Market Platform undertakes to comply with the principles set out in this Charter. In the event of a violation of this Charter, Back Market reserves its right to suspend or close the Seller Account as stated in the Seller T&Cs, which will prevent the Seller from using the Platform, without prejudice to the other penalties that Back Market may seek or that may be pronounced against the Seller. 

 

This Charter may change and you will be informed of any amendments by email or by any other digital means (such as Back Office notifications) sent by Back Market at least one month before such amendments take effect. You are free to accept the amended charter and undertake to comply with the updated Charter or to discontinue using the Platform in accordance with the Seller T&Cs. 

 

Have questions about this Ethics and Compliance Charter?
Reach out to: legal-sellers@backmarket.com

 

Part 1. The 10 Principles of the United Nations Global Compact 

 

In the interest of improving our approach to social responsibility, Back Market has committed to, and asks Sellers to commit themselves to respecting the following 10 principles established by the UN Global Compact regarding human rights, international labor laws, the environment, and the fight against corruption: 

 

Human Rights

 

Principle 1: Sellers should support and respect the protection of internationally proclaimed human rights.

 

Principle 2: Sellers should make sure they are not accomplices to human rights abuses.

 

International labor standards 

 

Principle 3: Sellers should uphold the freedom of association and the effective recognition of the right to collective bargaining.

 

Principle 4: Sellers should uphold the elimination of all forms of forced and compulsory labor.

 

Principle 5: Sellers should uphold the effective abolition of child labor.

 

Principle 6: Sellers should uphold the elimination of discrimination in respect of employment and occupation.

 

The environment

 

Principle 7: Sellers should support a precautionary approach to environmental challenges.

 

Principle 8: Sellers should undertake initiatives to promote greater environmental responsibility.

 

Principle 9: Sellers should encourage the development and diffusion of environmentally friendly technologies.

 

Fighting against corruption

 

Principle 10: Sellers should work against corruption in all of its forms, including extortion and bribery (see part 2 of this Charter for more on corruption).

 

Part 2. Anti-corruption
and anti-money laundering 

 

Back Market pays special attention to the fight against fraud, corruption, influence peddling, and money laundering, and asks Sellers to commit to respecting the principles described below. 

 

Sellers’ Commitments

 

The Seller undertakes, both with regards to themselves, their employees, agents, representatives, shareholders, members, and persons acting on their behalf, that they will conduct their business in strict compliance with all applicable local and international laws and regulations on corruption, money laundering, and terrorism (hereinafter, the “Regulations”). 

 

In particular, the Seller will not engage in the following practices:

  • Corrupt practices meant to promote or give a direct or indirect advantage to any individual or legal entity (whether a public or private person) for the purpose of carrying out, or abstaining from carrying out, an act that falls within their duties ;
  • Money laundering practices meant to conceal the origin of property or revenue of the perpetrator of a crime or offense or to help in the placement, concealment, or conversion of a transaction with respect to the direct or indirect proceeds of a crime or offense ;
  • Deceptive or unfair practices meant to deliberately deceive, intentionally conceal facts, or avoid legal or regulatory obligations, for the purpose of obtaining an illegitimate benefit.
Title: Ethics and Compliance Charter for Sellers

Details__c:
If, for instance, you are a German seller willing to sell in France, you would need to get your Back Office set-up for this (thanks to your SSM) and then, for your listing you would need to follow this tutorial.

Good to know💡

First, please note that some fields that those are the fields, for the same listing, that can be different from one country to another:

  • the description of the product
  • the original price/price new
  • the title
  • the price
  • the warranty delay

 

The quantity would remain the same for all countries, though. For instance: it is not possible to have a quantity of 2 in Spain and 0 in France for the same listing.

 

Here, we will detail three methods of putting your listing live abroad:
 

On the back Office manually

Go to the listing page and then click on "Edit offer" :

Then, just click on the flags to select the countries you want to sell your listing to. The mandatory fields to fill are:

  • price
  • translated description
  • warranty
  • shipping mode

You can also check in "View details" why your product is not live in some countries. Our alert system will indicate you if your product is waiting for validation or if there are missing fields.
 

Using CSV Import

If you want your product to be available in several countries, you have to select 'all' in the 'Language' field of 'Import my offers'.

Be careful, you also have to add the 'lang' column to your CSV file. In this column, you have to fill the country code of the country where you want to sell your product. Thereby, if, for example, you want to sell your product in 3 countries you will have to put 3 lines for this product in your CSV file, each with a different country code in the lang column.

 

Via API

Using API, you can just change the country-code directly in the header of your request.

You can find more information on our Technical documentation here: https://doc.backmarket.fr/#getting-started.
Title: How to put my listing live in other countries?

Details__c:
At Back Market, we understand that time is limited. As a seller, you need to have information that quickly shows a holistic view of your sales with the ability to easily dive deeper into any data point—without requiring much time or effort. 

That is why we continue to develop insights for you, and we are more than happy to launch a Sales Insights Dashboard that is accessible through the back office. 

The Sales Insights dashboard will allow you to focus on your goal and spend less time on non-sales tasks. The objective of the dashboard is to enable you to monitor your sales, and to have a better view of your global sales performance and turnover. You can choose a time period for which you want to do your analysis. You can also choose a specific country or product on which you would like to run your analysis.

In summary, the dashboard will answer these 3 questions:

  • What is my business performance in the last weeks/months/years?
  • What are my top/flop selling products?
  • What are my best performing markets?

How to access the Sales Insights Dashboard?

From the Back Office, click on the 'Insights' tab. You will see the 'Business Dashboard' section, where you will be able to dive deeper into your sales. 

Troubleshooting: If you see a connection page instead of your Dashboard, that means that cross-site tracking is disabled. No worries, here is what you should do to get access to your Dashboard:

If you use Safari, you have two options:

  • Use another browser (Chrome or Firefox for example).
  • Enable cross-site tracking in Safari. Here is the way to change your system preferences in Safari.

If you use Chrome or Firefox, and it still does not work:

  • Turn cookies on.

How does the dashboard work, and how do I interpret my data?

Growth Margin Value and Orderline Volumes

This dashboard allows you to monitor your sales evolution. The darker line represents the current year while the lighter line represents the previous year (if you are analyzing your current year's performance). The dashboard also allows to analyze your weekly, monthly and quarterly performances. Doing this comparison will allow you to know how you are performing compared to the previous periods. 

On the left, you have an overall summary of your sales. The first part shows you your sales for the current year, the second part is your turnover for the previous year, and the third part represents your turnover from two years ago.

GMV Variance at product level

This dashboard compares the GMV of the current year with that of the previous year. It also shows you which products are having the highest growth, which allows you to have a clearer view on your bestsellers.

 

Market Breakdown

The Market breakdown dashboard highlights your best performing markets. It splits your turnover by country and shows you your average selling price and their sales volume.

 

GMV Details

The GMV Details dashboard gives you a detailed view of your bestsellers. It also allows you to do a more in-depth analysis on a particular model or on a product category. The dashboard also gives you the possibility of analyzing the evolution of your sales and the evolution of your prices over the year.


 

How do I download my raw data?

  • Click the "Download" button in the lower right corner of your screen.
  • Select the view you want to download
  • Select CSV or Excel extension
  • Click on "Download". Wait a few seconds and the file will automatically download on your computer

We need you!

Please take a few minutes to fill this form and give us feedback. You can also use the  feedback button on the 'Performance' tab in the Back Office. We're looking forward to hearing from you. 😍
Title: Business Insights Dashboard

Details__c:
To create a product from your Back Office, you can proceed by two different ways:

  • Create a product by duplicating an existing product in our catalog
  • Create a product from scratch

 

To create a product by duplicating an existing product in our catalog

 

  1. On your Back Office, go to 'Listings' or click on the following link: https://www.backmarket.fr/bo-seller/listings-new
  2. Click on 'Create a listing':


 

  1. From this page, you can use the search bar to see if the product you are selling already exists in our catalog or not:



You'll need to specify the name of the product and its main characteristics. You can also use the search filters located on the left side of this page. This will help you refine your search and get access to the most similar products available.

  1. Once you have identified the most similar model, all you have to do is click on the blue "Duplicate this product" button: 

 

💡 If you see that the product already exists in our catalog, you can click directly on 'Sell yours'. 



 

  1. In order to duplicate the product, you will have to generate a new EAN, just as for any new product. You will also have to modify the attributes that differentiate your product from the duplicated product:



 

  1. Once the changes are made, click on 'Create my product'.
  2. All you have to do is fill in the information specific to your listing (SKU, Grade, Selling price, Quantity, Delivery option)
  3. Then, validate. Congratulations, your product has been created 🎉

 

To create a product from scratch

If the product you are selling doesn't exist in our catalog, or if no similar product exists in order to duplicate it, you will have to create your product from scratch. To do this, you'll have to: 

  1. On your Back Office, go to 'Listings' or click on the following link: https://www.backmarket.fr/bo-seller/listings-new
  2. Click on 'Create a listing':



 

  1. If the product you want to create does not exist, and you want to create it yourself from scratch, click on 'Add new item to the catalog': 

 

 

  1. Choose your product category:


 

  1. Then, you'll only have to fill in the mandatory attributes, indicated by the red asterisk: *. For large appliances, the dimensions are also mandatory.

 

⚠️ To obtain complete and qualitative product sheets, do not hesitate to fill in as many optional fields as possible.

 

  1. Click on 'Create my product'. 
  2. All you have to do is fill in the information specific to your listing (SKU, Grade, Selling price, Quantity, Delivery option)
  3. Then, validate. Congratulations, your product has been created 🎉


💡 Once your product is created, it must be validated by our team before your listing is put online. This can take up to 48 hours. 
 

➡️ Please refer to the article What Items does Back Market restrict or prohibit to know what product or category of product you can list on our Platform.
Title: Create my product on Back Market's Back Office

Details__c:
In order to always have an up-to-date pricing in every of your market, we launched the Back Market pricing tool. It enables you to create price rule which are automatically updating prices depending on the prices in your reference market.

 

First, go to your Back Office under the 'Options' tab:

Then select the 'Pricing' tab to find the 'International Pricing Tool' section:

 

Check that you reference market is correctly set up

This market will serve as a reference for your price rule, so be sure to take your main market as a reference! As the market where you are doing the more sales, it's also the market where you will be the more competitive. Aligning your price with that market is the best mean to boost your sales.

 

Create your rules

  • Chose the country to apply this rule and select 'Add another rule'

1. Select a product category to apply this rule (tips: use the "all categories" to be more scalable)

2. Define a rule for this category

The formula is as followed:

price in reference market * multiplier + value

 

Example:

- in Germany, the price of my smartphone is 400€

- for my French price rule I add 2% because my customer transactions are mainly made through Paypal so I apply a multiplier of 1.02

- and my shipping provider is invoicing me 5€ more to deliver from the Germany to France, so I increase every French prices by 5€

- in France, my smartphone will have a price of 413€

Price rule created! It will update automatically every 24h.

Good to know: you can always change the prices of your product manually to win backboxes if you want, the new price will simply be erased by the new price rule at the time of price rule update.

You can also deactivate the automatic update via the green button. This will disable the rule on existing listings, but not on new listings, on which the rule will continue to apply automatically.
Title: Create a price rule per country  🌎

Details__c:
Once you have online listings on your preproduction account, you can start to place orders to test the order flow.

Step 1

  • Go to this website, which is a replica of our Back Market website with the database of the preproduction back office.  
  • Search one of your product in the search bar and buy it like a normal product, as if you were a customer. During the checkout, you can put any values (on the name, address, etc.) but it needs to correspond to our format requirements.
  • On the payment page, choose a credit card and click on 'CLICK HERE TO GET TEST CARD NUMBERS' at the bottom of the page to get test card numbers.
  • Choose one card and confirm the payment to finalize the order. To make sure that the payment works well - it is best to use a US Visa Credit Card. You will find it in the link we mention just above.

Step 2

Your order will soon appear in the order tab of your back office. Now you can start testing the delivery flow, the cancellation, refunds, customer claims etc.
Title: How do I place an order on my preproduction account?

Details__c:
In this article, you'll learn how to restore multiple Apple devices simultaneously using Apple configurator.


Step 1: Download Firmware files

In order to optimize the restore time, we highly recommend you to download firmware files (known as IPSW files) in advance. You can find an updated list here.

You can find the downloaded files in the following places:
  • On a Mac (using configurator): /Users/**USERNAME**/Library/Group Containers/K36BKF7T3D.group.com.apple.configurator/Library/Caches
  • On a Mac (using iTunes): /Users/**USERNAME**/Library/iTunes/iPhone Software Updates
  • On a PC: C:\Users\USERNAME\AppData\Local\Packages\AppleInc.iTunes_nzyj5cx40ttqa\LocalCache\Roaming\Apple Computer\iTunes\iPhone Software Updates


Step 2: Download and Install Apple Configurator 2

You can download and install Apple Configurator 2 via this link.


Step 3: Configure Apple Configurator 2

To configure your Apple configurator 2, please follow the instructions below:
  1. Go to the “view” menu in the toolbar and click on list
 
  1. Right-Click on the header and select battery to see the battery level in real time. 

        



Restore Multiple Devices

Select All devices in Edit menu and (in the Action menu or by right-clicking) select Restore.
 


If the IPSW (iOS restore file) is not present in the Mac it will automatically download the latest version of iOS. 


⚠️  Bonus : Automatically restore all plugged iOS devices

  • Simply download, install & launch this App. 
  • Configurator MUST be installed 
  • It will automatically restore all plugged devices in the background.
  • If the IPSW (iOS restore file) is not present in the Mac it will automatically download the latest version of iOS.
 

​​​​​​​You can see the status of the restore process at the top left of your screen in the “Automator” menu.
Title: How to restore multiple Apple devices simultaneously?

Details__c:
Each time you upload a CSV file, it might take up to 2 minutes before we process the file.

Once the file is processed, you can see the results in the 'Notifications' part of your Back Office, under the profile icon on the top right.

Then you will see a line called 'Product import completed'

When you click on it, it will tell you the number of products and/or listings created. If there are any errors, download a detailed "error report" by clicking on the link.

You will get an Excel file which is a modified version of the file you imported with additional columns. The first column is called "Error".  There is an error in each line with a "yes" in the "Error" column. The origin of the error is described in the last column of the file.
Title: Error management in CSV files

Details__c:
Each seller can create a listing on a product to sell it on Back Market. It corresponds to the listing you are selling, taking into account different criteria, such as its condition, its stock or its selling price.

Products sold on Back Market are refurbished, and their aesthetic state can vary a great deal. In order to make the most informed choice, each listing is defined by one of the following aesthetic grades:

  • Mint: Almost like new.
  • Very good: Nearly invisible scratches.
  • Good: Scratches invisible at more than 10 cm.
  • Fair: Scratches visible at more than 10 cm.
  • Stallone: This product survived many battles, but not without scratches.

These 5 grades are the ones you have to fill in when you create your listing. Once the listing is validated, these 5 grades are recalculated in order to display only 3 of them on the customer view: 


 

For more information about our grade system and calculation, you can consult this article: Back Market's 3 Customer Grades

On the other hand, a listing has mandatory and optional fields.

These fields are the same for each listing, regardless of the product category:

  • The product ID (the BMID)
  • THE EAN 
  • The SKU
  • The quantity
  • The selling price
  • Aesthetic grade
  • The warranty period
  • The country of sale
  • Refurbisher comment

These fields can be modified at any time from your Back Office.

💡 For more information about the listings' update, you can consult the article Update my listing on the Back Office
Title: Listing - definition

  3. ShipStation “API Key” & “API Secret” 

We will go through how to locate all of these items, then tell you how to send them to us.


Backmarket Token

To make sure we can update your quantity and prices on BackMarket using REST API, you need to locate your BackMarket Token.

Here are the steps to find it:

➡️ Go on your Back Market Back Office 

➡️ Go to the Options Tab > Integrations

➡️ Enter your password and get your token

We need this token to make sure we can get the Quantity and Prices of your Inventory in SkuVault.

Here are the steps: 

  • Go on the following link: https://dev.skuvault.com/reference#gettokens

  • SkuVault TenantToken:

Provide us the link and the “passphrase” if you set one, and we would then encrypt and save your values in our Database to start the connection.

Details__c:
What’s a screen stress test?

A screen stress test mimics the usage of a smartphone under a high load (like gaming or photo editing). 

Why’s this test important?

The screen stress test helps detect screen issues that could occur when the customer uses their smartphone for gaming or photo editing. It’s also one of the best ways to determine whether or not a refurbished screen is in working condition. 


Equipment, tools, and software needed

  • Heating mat 
  • coconutBattery or 3UTool to monitor the temperature of the device 
  • A testing software (PhoneCheck or Blancco) or a manual touch screen test
 

Test parameters and specification

  • Test conditions:
    • Device must reach 40 – 45 ° C
  • Parameters tested and pass / fail specs:
    • Perform usual screen tests (with something like PhoneCheck) and check for failures 


Scope

The procedure below applies to all smartphone screens that are being replaced — especially iPhone 11s (and above) that feature screens with a “transplanted IC.”
 

Test procedure

  • Place the device (screen facing down) on the heating mat.


 
  • Set the temperature to 45° C and the timer to 10 minutes.
  • Wait for 10 minutes.
  • Control the device’s temperature using coconutBattery or 3UTools.

  • Connect the device to PhoneCheck or another testing software.
  • Perform the usual screen tests or manual tests if no testing options are available.


 
  • Look for the test result.

Screens that pass the touch test will be considered operational and ready to sell.
Title: Testing Standard Operating Procedure: screen stress test

Details__c:
Summary:

Two-factor authentication (2FA) is now required for anyone logging into the Back Office. 2FA adds an extra layer of protection to your Back Office account by requiring two forms of identification when logging in:

 

  1. Your usual Back Office credentials (username and password)
  2. A 6-digit code sent to your registered email

Incorporating this additional security measure significantly reduces the risk of unauthorized access, even if someone has your password.

 

What is the purpose of 2FA?

2FA enhances security by making it much harder for unauthorized users to access your account. Even if cybercriminals obtain your password, they would still need access to your email to complete the login process. 
This protects your sensitive business information and maintains account privacy in today's digital environment, where cyber threats are increasingly common.

 

How does two-factor authentication (2FA) change my Back Office login process?

Adding 2FA does not significantly increase your login time, but it does significantly enhance your security.

Here's the new process:

 

  • Enter your login credentials on the Back Office landing page (as usual)
  • After successful credential verification, a pop-up screen will appear requesting a 6-digit code
  • Check your registered email for the authentication code
  • Enter the 6-digit code in the pop-up screen
  • You'll then be fully logged into your Back Office

 

Can I use my phone number to authenticate?

For the time being, we are requiring all authentications to be sent to your Back Office login’s email address. However, authentication via text message is planned in the future as an additional option.

 

What happens if I don’t have access to the email address listed on my Back Office login?

If you don't have access to the email linked to your Back Office login, don't worry. Please reach out to your Back Market point of contact to help you update the admin user’s email or request a new user creation. If you have questions, please reach out to your dedicated Back Market point of contact.
Title: Two Factor Authentication (2FA)

Details__c:
Archiving a single listing

Here’s the process for archiving a single listing:

  1. Log into your Back Office.
  2. Go to the Listings page.
  3. Find the listing you want to archive by searching for a particular SKU.
  4. Select “Archive offer”.
You can check that the listing was archived by going to the “Archived” tab and searching for the SKU.

 

Archiving multiple listings

Archiving multiple listings at once can be useful if you want to temporarily remove listings from the platform or make them inactive.

Since this action can only be done by an admin, submit a support request via the Seller Support Center.

  1. Log into the Seller Support Center. 
  2. Select “Submit new request”.
  3. Select “No” when asked if your question is order related.
  4. Select “Listings” when asked what you need help with.
  5. Select “Sales” when asked which service do you need help with.
  6. Select “Batch archiving listings” when asked what you specifically need help with.

To save time, provide the product IDs for the listings you want to archive when submitting the support request.
Title: How do I archive my listings?

Details__c:
Set-up with InfiPlex Inventory Management and Order Management

  • Connect your Back Market Seller's Account:
    • back_market_api_key - This is your API Key used to connect to Back Market. From your Back Market dashboard, click on the Support link in the top menu, then click on the Technical Support link in the left menu. EU customers will need to open a Customer Service Support Ticket to request their API Token.
    • back_market_api_region - This is the region where you sell your Back Market products. If you need to connect with both regions, please contact support to have that set-up.
    • back_market_pull_orders - Set to YES. This controls if orders are pulled from Back Market.
    • back_market_send_tracking_numbers - Set to YES. This controls if tracking numbers are sent back to Back Market for backmarket.com orders.
    • back_market_sobm_enabled - Set to YES. This controls if inventory levels are sent to Back Market.
    • back_market_sobm_pricing_enabled - This controls if pricing is sent to Back Market on SKUs set as Sell On Back Market. Default is set to NO.
    • back_market_pricing_multiplier - Default is 1. All prices sent to Back Market will be multiplied by this value. For example, if this value is 0.8 and a product is $75, then 75 * 0.8 = 60, so $60 will be sent to Back Market for that specific product.
    • Save - Click the Save button to save the settings.

Get Back Market Token



InfiPlex Settings

Adding Back Market SKUs

You can use the Tools > Product Upload tool to add SKUs to your OMS. You can easily download your SKUs from Back Market using the instructions below. You can then use the data from that file to build your product upload file into the OMS.

  • Log into your Back Market Back office
  • Go to the Offers' page
  • Click on Export My Offers
    • This will add a message to your Notifications' page for the download
  • Go to your Notifications page
    • Click on the new Product Export message
    • Click the Download link to download the file

Details__c:
Since January 3rd, a new process has been put in place for products that are pending validation and cannot be validated by our teams. 

⚠️ As a reminder, all new products you create must first be validated. This validation phase takes between 24 and 48 hours. If the created products do not meet our validation criteria, they will remain stuck in validation. 

From now on, you will receive a notification from your email address informing you of the reason for the non-validation of your product. 
For example: 


 

Once informed of this non-validation, the products will be deleted from our platform. You will then have to recreate them taking into account the remarks. For example: 

- No picture size 500x500 available (Image) : you will need to provide higher quality images, greater than 500x500
- Missing information/Attribute impossible to find (processor) : you will need to provide the processor reference of the device

Even though the products are deleted, the listings will still be visible from your Back Office, in the 'Validation' section. You will be able to archive them and modify the SKU reference of each of them if you wish to use them again. 

On the other hand, if you are notified that the product is blacklisted, as below: 
Product not accepted on Back Market website (Blacklisted)
you can directly archive this offer, it will not be validated.

💡 For more information about products available for sale on Back Market, you can check this article.
Title: New process for products stuck in validation

Details__c:
Back Market requires all sellers who want to sell on the marketplace to provide the following documents and information: 
  • Individuals need to provide: 
    • Full name, residential address, email address, mobile phone number, and date of birth 
    • Registration number (in the trade register or equivalent in their country of registration) 
    • Physical business address 
    • Applicable VAT regime and intra-community VAT number or any other applicable VAT identification number or proof of VAT registration, their bank details, 
    • Bank account identification form or any equivalent document
    • Copy of valid identification card 
    • Direct debit mandate and confirmation of bank activation 
    • Self-certification that they commit to sell only devices that comply with the applicable rules of European Union law
 
  • Legal entities need to provide: 
    • Full name, email address, and telephone number of the representing individual on the marketplace 
    • Corporate name and address of registered office 
    • Registration number (in the trade and companies register or the trades register or equivalent in their country of registration) 
    • Applicable VAT regime, their intra-community VAT number or any other applicable VAT identification number or proof of VAT registration 
    • Bank details
    • Copy of certificate of registration not older than 3 months 
    • Copy of identification card of any individual directly or indirectly holding more than 25% of capital and/or voting rights, copies of identification cards of individuals who are directly or indirectly legal representatives, and/or copy of declaration of beneficial owners
    • Copy of current articles of association
    • Bank identification statement in the name of the seller or any equivalent document
    • Direct debit mandate and confirmation of activation with bank
    • Self-certification that they commit to sell only devices that comply with the applicable rules of European Union law
For tax purposes, to establish if Back Market can collect and pay VAT for the seller, the seller must provide their place of establishment and, for each transaction, dispatch location, and destination of products, plus all the supporting documentation that may be requested by Back Market.
Title: Documents and information required for onboarding

Once you have selected the dates, you can click on 'Export my orders'. A pop-up will open. Please choose the format and language that suits you best:

Here’s how to do it: Submit a support request via the Seller Support Center and select "Account performance" or reach out to your Back Market point of contact and tell them you want to receive a copy of the catalog. Make sure you specify the item category name and ID.

Keep in mind that we’re only able to provide a copy of the catalog for item categories that you’re already selling. You’ll get the mandatory item specifications, not the GTIN, MPN, or images.

Details__c:
Back Market is a marketplace dedicated to the sale of electronic refurbished products. However, we authorize the sale of new products in our accessory category. Certain accessories, essential for the proper use of our products, cannot be reconditioned.

Note, however, that this remains an exception, and is subject to strict rules, which are detailed as follows:
 

(1) Eco-friendly indicates a product that is sustainable / recycled / recyclable / biodegradable / made from natural materials.

In addition, some accessory categories are restricted. This was implemented either for security reasons or to combat counterfeiting:

(2) Cases & sleeves -> send the eco-label certificate of your product to your Account Manager

(3) AirPods and chargers (wall plug) -> send them to our lab for whitelisting (verify the process with your account manager)
Title: Refurbishment requirement for accessories

Details__c:
No, you can't delete a listing, But you can either deactivate it either archive it to clean your catalog display.

To deactivate a listing, you can change the stock of this listing to 0. This will set the listing offline.

If you want to archive a listing you just need to click on 'Archive offer'

To see all your archived adds you just need to go on 'Archived'


 
If you want to publish an archived offer then click on 'Publish offer'.
 

 
If you want to deactivate a lot of listings, you can send the list of the corresponding SKU to us.
Title: Can I delete a listing/SKU?

Details__c:
All of our products are structured under different categories. We have three levels of categories, defined as follows:

  • Category 1 (e.g. High-tech)
  • Category 2 (Tablets, Computers & co, Audio, Entertainment, etc.)
  • Category 3 (E-reader, Laptop, Video Games, etc.)



 

Category 1 is considered the parent category of our products, which is divided into sub-categories, categories 2 and 3. Category 3 determines the attributes (mandatory or optional) to be filled in during product creation.

 

➡️ Please refer to the article The different products available for sale on Back Market to know what product or category of product you can list on our Platform.
Title: Category - definition

This identification number is the ID issued by the companies, trade or commerce registry (or equivalent) in your country. Details__c:
Starting January 13, 2022, consoles will be split into 3 categories :

  • Video game consoles
  • Gaming consoles (previous generations)
  • Gaming consoles (retro). 

The following rules will apply per category:
(You can find more info about gaming consoles generations here)

  • Video game consoles : The following models will be accepted : PlayStation 5 Digital Edition - PlayStation 5 - Xbox Series X - Nintendo Switch OLED - PlayStation 4 Pro - PlayStation 4 - PlayStation 4 Slim - Xbox Series S - Xbox One S - Nintendo Switch Lite - Nintendo Switch - Xbox One - Xbox One X - Wii U - PS Vita (from 8th generation to now)
  • Gaming consoles (previous generations) : The models between Dreamcast (1998) and Mega Drive 4 will be accepted (2009) (from 6th generation to 7th generation)
  • Gaming consoles (retro) : All the models before Dreamcast will be accepted
Title: New Video Game Console categories
